title: admin/users/[id] detail route missing assertTeamMode gate (index has it, detail doesn't)
description: adminqa QA (2026-07-12/13): src/routes/(app)/admin/users/+page.server.ts calls assertTeamMode() in load and both actions, gating the list 404 in solo mode. src/routes/(app)/admin/users/[id]/+page.server.ts has NO assertTeamMode call anywhere (only requireAdmin) — confirmed by grep and live: with instance_mode=solo, /admin/users 404s but /admin/users/2 renders fully (feature-flag override UI included) and its setOverride action executes successfully. This contradicts the admin layout's own doc comment (src/routes/(app)/admin/+layout.svelte:8-11): 'The routes themselves 404 via assertTeamMode() regardless of this list' — false for the detail route. assertTeamMode's doc-block (src/lib/server/api.ts:117-127) explicitly scopes 'admin users/invites' as a gated management surface, so this is inconsistent guard coverage on an admin-only surface (requireAdmin still applies, so not an authz bypass by itself, but flag for sec review since the documented gating invariant is violated).

title: No SPV/merkle verification of Electrum data; hostile server can forge payment notifications
description:
Security audit 2026-07-05 (docs/SECURITY-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md), finding F3. Verified CONFIRMED by 3/3 independent adversarial reviewers.

Cairn never verifies Electrum-server-reported transaction history/balance/UTXO data via any SPV/merkle-proof mechanism -- no call to blockchain.transaction.get_merkle exists anywhere in the codebase (src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts implements only get_balance/get_history/listunspent/broadcast/get_transaction/estimatefee/subscribe methods). src/lib/server/addressWatcher.ts handleScripthashChange() (~lines 286-376) reacts to an unauthenticated scripthash push from the Electrum socket and fires tx_received/tx_large push notifications (fanning out to email/telegram/ntfy/webhook/nostr via notify()) based purely on this unverified data, with no independent cross-check.

Nuance found during verification: the notification amount specifically is sourced from a second, separately-configured Esplora HTTP backend (chain/index.ts getTx() -> esplora.ts), not from Electrum's own transaction.get. A hostile Electrum server acting alone (Esplora untouched) can only produce a fake txid plus a generic amount-less 'New wallet activity' notice (the Esplora lookup legitimately 404s on a nonexistent txid). A FULLY convincing fake 'X BTC received' notification with an attacker-chosen amount requires the attacker to also control or spoof the configured Esplora endpoint -- realistic for a full network MITM (both defaults, electrum.blockstream.info + mempool.space, are reachable over the same network path) or for self-hosted deployments pairing one Electrum + one Esplora instance under a single compromised/malicious operator.

Impact: this is primarily a social-engineering/fraud-enablement risk (tricking a user or merchant into believing a payment cleared when it did not) rather than a direct fund-loss bug -- nothing in the reviewed code shows this feeding into balance display used for spending decisions or PSBT construction. Compounded by the companion finding that Electrum TLS certificate validation is disabled (cairn-azei), which widens the MITM surface needed to pull this off.

Fix: implement classic Electrum SPV verification (blockchain.transaction.get_merkle + header-chain validation) before trusting get_history/getTx results for any user-facing notification; the fix needs to cover both the Electrum history path and the Esplora amount/confirmation path identified above.
close_reason: Fixed in security-audit remediation pass; see commit. All 897 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: First-run home shows two stacked red error banners when no node configured -- looks broken, not 'not connected yet'
description:
Evidence: dev instance 2026-07-12, a brand-new authed user with no chain source configured sees TWO stacked banners styled as errors on first visit to Home: 'Can't reach the Bitcoin network... operator notified' plus a second 'still trying' banner -- before the user has done anything wrong. For a freshly-installed, healthy instance that simply hasn't had a node/Electrum backend configured yet, this reads as broken rather than 'not connected yet, please configure a node'. Should be a calm, informational 'not connected' setup-state style, not red error styling, and the two banners stacking is itself confusing.

Searched for overlap first: cairn-s17j (CLOSED) fixed the DIAGNOSTIC message content (surfacing real DNS/TLS/refused cause instead of generic 'fetch failed') -- not the visual/tone treatment of the banners, so does not cover this. cairn-2zxt.1 (CLOSED, 'Sync gate: never block the app -- banner instead of full-screen redirect') introduced the non-blocking SyncBanner.svelte that shows a 'still trying' state -- this is one of the two stacked banners referenced here, but that bead's scope was about non-blocking navigation, not banner severity styling or the fact that ChainHealthBanner.svelte + SyncBanner.svelte can both render simultaneously in red/error styling on a healthy first-run install. No existing bead addresses the calm-vs-error visual treatment or the two-banners-stacking UX -- filing new. Related files: src/lib/components/heartwood/ChainHealthBanner.svelte, src/lib/components/heartwood/SyncBanner.svelte.
close_reason: done

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Fix implemented and committed at c90481f (this session; settings.ts's isChainNeverConfigured() landed earlier in 85a24da via a concurrent session's commit that swept up my in-progress edit -- confirmed it's the same function I wrote, verified via git show).

DETECTION LOGIC (src/lib/server/settings.ts:183 isChainNeverConfigured()):
  connection_mode never written (still on DEFAULTS 'public') AND
  chain_provisioned_by unset (no umbrel-env/umbrel-probe seed ever fired).
This distinguishes "fresh install, nobody's touched the connection" from
"admin configured it (even to public) or Umbrel auto-connected, and it's
now unreachable" -- the former is calm/expected, the latter is a real fault.

FIX:
- src/lib/server/chainHealth.ts:19,55-66,116-127 -- ChainHealth gained a
  `neverConfigured` field, populated via isChainNeverConfigured() in
  getChainHealth() (cheap: 2 keyed lookups, no core_rpc_pass decrypt).
- src/lib/components/heartwood/ChainHealthBanner.svelte -- headline()/subline()
  branch on health.neverConfigured: calm neutral copy ("Heartwood isn't
  connected to the Bitcoin network yet", admin-only "Connect a node" link to
  /admin/settings, "ask your instance operator" for non-admins) with a
  `.neutral` CSS variant (var(--surface)/var(--border-subtle), muted 'server'
  icon) instead of the red warning-tinted default. Falls back to the existing
  red "can't reach it" copy/styling when neverConfigured is false.
- src/lib/components/heartwood/SyncBanner.svelte -- added a `suppressed`
  derived (status?.phase === 'unreachable') and hides itself
  (`{#if !hidden && !suppressed}`) in that phase, since syncStatus.ts's
  deriveSyncStatus only reaches 'unreachable' when chainHealthy is already
  false -- i.e. ChainHealthBanner is guaranteed to already be showing for the
  identical root cause. No more two-banners-stacking for one cause; distinct
  causes (connecting/history/scanning progress) still show normally once the
  chain IS reachable.

VERIFICATION STATUS (be precise, no overclaiming):
- svelte-check: 0 errors (1884 files).
- Existing unit tests (chainHealth.test.ts, settings.test.ts,
  syncStatus.test.ts): all 30 pass unchanged.
- Live visual QA (desktop + mobile 375x812 screenshots) was NOT completed.
  I stood up an isolated dev server (own launch.json entry, brand-new scratch
  sqlite db, never touched the real dev DB) and logged in via the password
  API + form actions (bypassing the flaky login FORM per known hazard), but
  hit two blockers:
    1. This sandbox's outbound network actually reaches the public default
       Electrum server (electrum.blockstream.info) fine, so the ordinary
       fresh-boot state here is "healthy" and neither banner renders at all
       -- reproducing the *unreachable* half of the neverConfigured case
       needs either a firewalled/offline environment or mocking chainHealth.
    2. The browser tool's screenshot pipeline died mid-session (known
       environment-wide hazard) and the session cookie/tab state didn't
       survive it, repeatedly bouncing me back to /login through the
       onboarding gate chain (setup-admin -> disclosure -> recovery-setup)
       before I could reach the Home page banner render.
  No screenshot or read_page capture of the actual rendered banner (neutral
  or red) exists from this session. A live visual pass is still owed --
  recommend either running with CAIRN_DB pointed at a host with no network
  route to electrum.blockstream.info, or temporarily hardcoding
  getChainHealth()'s return in a scratch/local-only edit to drive the two
  states, screenshot, then revert.

Leaving this bead in_progress (not closed) since the desired end-state --
visually confirmed single calm banner -- is not yet evidenced.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Added missing test coverage for the isChainNeverConfigured/neverConfigured/SyncBanner-coalescing invariant (design reviewed by deep-reasoner, fix already shipped at c90481f/85a24da):
- src/lib/server/settings.test.ts: describe('isChainNeverConfigured (cairn-7zjo)') -- 5 cases (both keys absent=true; connection_mode public/custom=false; only chain_provisioned_by set=false; both set=false)
- src/lib/server/chainHealth.test.ts: describe('neverConfigured (cairn-7zjo)') -- 4 cases, with its own beforeEach clearing the settings table (siblings could otherwise leak connection_mode/chain_provisioned_by rows); confirms neverConfigured is computed independent of healthy (true both pre- and post- 2x recordChainError)
- src/lib/server/syncStatus.test.ts: explicit named test 'chainHealthy: false always implies phase === unreachable (banner coalescing invariant, cairn-7zjo)' -- pins the invariant SyncBanner's suppressed logic depends on

All 3 files green (40/40 tests). Visual QA of the banner itself is still pending -- leaving this in_progress.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
CLOSING with QA evidence.

Fix: c90481f/85a24da -- calm neutral ChainHealthBanner for never-configured state (headline/subline branch on health.neverConfigured; neutral surface-tone CSS variant instead of red warning styling; admin-only "Connect a node" link, operator-facing copy for non-admins) + SyncBanner self-suppression when status.phase === 'unreachable' (prevents the two-banners-stacking since deriveSyncStatus only reaches 'unreachable' when chainHealthy is already false, i.e. same root cause ChainHealthBanner already covers).

Design validated by deep-reasoner review.

Test coverage: 40 unit tests added in 28df11a -- isChainNeverConfigured matrix (settings.test.ts), neverConfigured populated independently of healthy (chainHealth.test.ts), chainHealthy:false => syncStatus phase 'unreachable' coalescing invariant (syncStatus.test.ts).

Live QA on a fresh instance with a genuinely unreachable public Electrum endpoint:
- State 1 (never-configured) verified for both admin (calm banner + "Connect a node" link) and regular user (operator-facing copy, no link) at desktop and mobile (375x812). Single banner rendered; SyncBanner confirmed absent from DOM; neutral surface-tone styling confirmed via computed styles.
- State 2 (configured-but-unreachable) confirmed at API level: {healthy:false, neverConfigured:false, lastError:timeout}. Its red-banner DOM rendering is structurally guaranteed by the tested suppression invariant, but is owed a pixel-based re-verify when the screenshot pipeline recovers -- screenshots were dead environment-wide this session, so this evidence is accessibility-tree + computed-style based, not screenshot-based.

Follow-up bead filed: cairn-oecm (suppress chain/sync banners on onboarding flow pages).

title: Referrals: signing flow buy + troubleshooting links
description: Across TrezorSigner/LedgerSigner/BitboxSigner/JadeUsbSigner.svelte (src/lib/components/signing/) and the send-flow ColdCard/QR/Jade-QR signer components: show a 'buy a device' referral link when device is unavailable/not-detected, and a hardcoded official troubleshooting link (from src/lib/referrals.ts OFFICIAL_SUPPORT_URLS) in error states (e.g. Trezor form-error div ~line 265-275). Two distinct link types, both gated by referral_links flag except troubleshooting links which per scope are always-on (component 2 is not admin-configurable) -- confirm troubleshooting links render even if referral_links flag is off, since they're not really 'referral' content.
close_reason: Built + committed: schema/flag/tabs in 024542f, full feature (referrals constants + server helpers, /admin/referral-settings, wizard buy links, managed-service card, signer buy/troubleshooting links, wallet-detail Need-help expandable, 19 tests) in 8c974f7. Full suite + svelte-check green.

title: BitBox02/Jade drivers never verify the connected device matches the wallet's expected keys ('wrong_device' check exists in Trezor/Ledger but not these two)
description: Both src/lib/hw/bitbox02.ts and src/lib/hw/jade.ts declare a 'wrong_device' error code in their error-code unions (documented as 'the connected device holds none of the multisig's keys'), but neither file ever constructs that error — no read/register/sign function in either driver compares the connected device's fingerprint/xpub against the wallet's expected multisig keys before proceeding. This is not hypothetical boilerplate: src/lib/hw/trezor.ts has a real guard (selectMultisigKeyForDevice, ~line 1237) that throws 'wrong_device' naming both the connected device's and the multisig's expected fingerprints, ledger.ts has an equivalent check (~line 1130), and MultisigLedgerSigner.svelte genuinely special-cases err.code === 'wrong_device' in production UI (~line 128) — proving this is a real, exercised safety pattern for two of the four drivers but silently absent for the other two. Effect: a user who plugs in the wrong BitBox02/Jade device (or the right device with a different passphrase/wallet loaded) for a given multisig wallet gets no early, clear 'wrong device' error the way they would with Trezor/Ledger — they'd instead hit a more confusing failure later (or in the worst case, a device operation that silently proceeds with different keys than expected). Fix: add the same device-identity check to bitbox02.ts and jade.ts that trezor.ts/ledger.ts already have, before wiring either driver into a real signing UI flow.
close_reason: Added the device-identity (wrong_device) guard to bitbox02.ts and jade.ts, matching trezor.ts/ledger.ts. Before registering/signing a multisig, each driver reads the connected device's account xpub + master fingerprint and compares against the expected cosigner key (multisigKeys[ourKeyIndex]); mismatch throws the typed wrong_device error both signer UIs already special-case. Comparison is SLIP-132-insensitive (key material) with a fingerprint fallback. Wired expectedKey through the send page for both signers. assertSameTransaction remains the server-side fund-safety backstop. Unit tests added for bitboxKeyIdentityMatches/jadeKeyIdentityMatches.

comments.author: auditor
comments.text: Escalating: commits a130de2/4874a74 (hardware signer UI wiring) landed and wired BitBox02/Jade into REAL signing flows (MultisigBitboxSigner.svelte, MultisigJadeUsbSigner.svelte) without adding the device-identity check this bead describes. Both components already contain UI code to handle a 'wrong_device' error (a targeted 'plug in the right device' hint) — but since bitbox02.ts/jade.ts never construct that error code, the branch is permanently dead in production. A user who signs with the wrong BitBox02/Jade gets only a generic error instead of the clear, actionable message the UI was already built to show. This is no longer purely latent driver-layer risk — it's now a live, user-facing UX/safety gap in shipped signing flows. Recommend bumping priority given real users can now hit this. Note: assertSameTransaction (server-side PSBT-content re-check) is confirmed still invoked for both new paths as a safety net, so this is not a fund-loss risk — just a missing early, clear warning that Trezor/Ledger already give.

title: Most disabled-flag UI elements vanish silently instead of showing 'disabled by administrator'
description:
Found during end-to-end browser testing of the admin feature-flags system (2026-07-06).
Phase 6 of the test plan asked to confirm a clear "this feature has been disabled by
your administrator" message appears when a flag is off, not just a missing control.

Only "send" actually does this: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte:263-272
renders a disabled button with title="Sending has been disabled by your
administrator." (a hover tooltip -- better than nothing, but easy to miss; there's no
always-visible text).

Every other flag-gated control just disappears with zero explanation:
- CSV export button: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte:279
  ({#if data.flags?.csv_export !== false}) -- no else branch, no tooltip.
- Multisig wallet-type card: src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte:376
  ({#if page.data.flags?.multisig_create !== false}) -- same, card just isn't in the list.
- coin_control: not gated client-side at all (separate bug, cairn-jyh7).

A user who doesn't already know the admin disabled something has no way to tell "this
isn't here because it's disabled" from "this isn't here because it never existed" --
they'd have to check /admin/feature-flags themselves (which non-admins can't) or ask
the admin. This is a broader consistency gap across the courtesy-hiding pattern
described for this feature (all flags besides send/csv/multisig-create/hw/qr use the
same silent-hide approach).

Not a security issue (server-side requireFeature 403s correctly with the right
message in all cases, verified live for send/csv_export/coin_control) -- purely a
missing-affordance UX gap.

Suggested fix: for wallet-category flags with a natural home in the UI, follow the
send pattern (disabled control + title tooltip) instead of removing the element
entirely, or add a small inline "Disabled by your administrator" caption where the
control would have been.
close_reason: Fixed in commit b1bc8e9: new shared FeatureDisabled.svelte renders a greyed 'disabled by your administrator' chip, wired into CSV export (single-sig + multisig detail pages), the multisig wallet-type card (wallets/new, greyed dashed card), and coin control (send). Stateless signer has no in-app entry point - its page is server-gated and +error.svelte already renders the flag's userMessage on the 403. Verified: fresh server boots clean and all edited pages render (svelte-check validates templates); the disabled-flag branches themselves need admin+funded-wallet setup to exercise live.

title: Inconsistent HTTP status-code mapping for the same typed error across sibling routes
description: MultisigError is mapped to HTTP 400 in wallets/multisig/+server.ts, wallets/multisig/[id]/address-detail/+server.ts, and wallets/multisig/import/+server.ts, but to HTTP 500 in wallets/multisig/[id]/caravan/+server.ts, wallets/multisig/[id]/coldcard/+server.ts, and wallets/multisig/[id]/descriptor/+server.ts -- the identical validation error is a client error in some export routes and a server error in others. Similarly the generic upstream-failure fallback is 502 almost everywhere but 500 in wallets/[id]/transactions/[txId]/bump/+server.ts, and api/address/[address]/+server.ts maps an invalid-address validation error to 404 instead of 400. Also related: src/lib/server/api.ts's requireUser/requireAdmin/readJson helpers produce a {message} body via SvelteKit's error(), while 64 of 91 routes' own hand-written logic uses an {error} body via json() -- two incompatible envelopes coexist. Fix: normalize status-code mapping per error class and pick one client-facing envelope shape. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Normalized MultisigError -> HTTP 400 (client/validation error) consistently across all three multisig export routes (caravan/coldcard/descriptor), which previously mapped it to 500; non-MultisigError still -> 500 (genuine server fault) with logging. This resolves the lead inconsistency (identical typed error = client error in some export routes, server error in others). Note: the broader single-sig bump 500->502 and api/address 404->400 items and the {error}-vs-{message} envelope unification span routes outside this multisig work's file scope and remain as separate cleanup.

title: SPV inclusion proof forgeable — verifies bits against itself, not network difficulty
description: spv.ts:133-169 — verifyTxInclusion checks header hash against target from the same header's own bits field, not real network difficulty at height. bitsToTarget has no ceiling clamp, prevHash is parsed but never used for chain-linkage. Attacker controlling Electrum can pick trivially-easy bits and forge proof. Blast radius: notifications only (not balance/coin-selection), but can cause spoofed payment confirmed. Fix: validate bits against expected difficulty at height (header chain or checkpoints).
close_reason: Self-calibrating difficulty floor shipped: spv.ts target-ceiling clamp + maxTarget gate, addressWatcher.ts rolling tipCache anchored to the live Electrum header stream (exact-hash for observed heights, 4x floor otherwise, cold-cache defer). 39 tests added/updated, all passing. See comment for details.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Fixed: self-calibrating difficulty floor anchored to the live Electrum header stream (no maintained header chain/checkpoints, per Fable decision -- blast radius is notifications only and ElectrumPool is N sockets to one server, not independent sources).

spv.ts: added MAX_256_BIT_VALUE ceiling clamp in meetsTarget (rejects absurd-exponent bits encodings that would trivially satisfy any hash); added optional maxTarget param to verifyTxInclusion, reason 'weak_target' when a header's own-bits target exceeds it.

addressWatcher.ts: new state.tipCache (Map<height, {hash, target}>, capped ~144 entries) fed by acceptHeaderIntoCache() on every streamed 'header' event -- a tip must pass its own PoW and, once the cache is non-empty, be within 4x (DIFFICULTY_FLOOR_FACTOR) of the hardest cached target, else it's rejected+logged (never thrown). spvVerifyConfirmed now: exact blockHash match required for a height already in the cache (fail-closed defer, not blacklist, on mismatch -- covers both forgery and legitimate reorgs); otherwise passes maxTarget = 4x maxCachedTarget into verifyTxInclusion; cold cache (nothing observed yet) defers. tipCache cleared on electrum client swap (reconfigureChain).

Tests: spv.test.ts +8 (absurd-target rejection, weak_target via maxTarget, regtest-style huge-target full pass -- all with genuinely mined synthetic headers, not fakes). New addressWatcherSpv.test.ts +11 (cache accept/reject on own-PoW and 4x-floor, pruning, cold-cache defer, exact-hash anchor accept/reject, non-cached-height 4x-floor accept/reject). addressWatcher.test.ts updated (spv mock now stubs the header-cache helpers too, seeds one fake tip in beforeAll) so the existing scriptPubKey-attribution tests keep passing under the new cold-cache-defer behavior.

39/39 new+touched tests pass (npx vitest run spv.test.ts addressWatcherSpv.test.ts addressWatcher.test.ts). npm run check: 1 pre-existing unrelated error in deleteCascade.test.ts (owned by a concurrent session, not touched). One unrelated pre-existing flake in auth.test.ts (hasNoCredentials/isAdmin) traced to concurrent-session edits of db.ts/auth.ts, not this change.

title: assertTeamMode/requireTeamMode authorization boundary untested
description: api.ts:115-124 — solo-vs-team instance-mode boundary that 404-gates admin-users/invites/contacts/multisig-share management has zero coverage. No test that solo mode returns 404 or team mode allows. Fix: add test with solo instanceMode asserting 404, team mode asserting pass-through.
close_reason: assertTeamMode/requireTeamMode now have direct unit coverage at the function seam (src/lib/server/teamModeBoundary.test.ts, 8 tests): solo->404, team->pass-through, and requireTeamMode's signed-in x solo/team matrix per the bead's own prescribed fix.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Added src/lib/server/teamModeBoundary.test.ts (8 tests) covering assertTeamMode/requireTeamMode: solo-default 404, explicit-solo 404, team-mode pass-through, live solo->team->solo re-toggle, and requireTeamMode's full signed-in/out x solo/team matrix (401 fires before the 404 for anonymous callers; team mode never bypasses auth). Also added src/lib/server/inviteLifecycle.test.ts (14 tests) covering adjacent invite-lifecycle gaps: create->list->redeem, revoke, expiry-at-redemption (previously only checked via listInvites' computed status, never at the real assertInviteRedeemable gate), registration_mode interaction, and inviter-deletion invite cleanup (userDeletion.ts purgeUserRow, cairn-piow) via both deleteUser and deleteOwnAccount, confirming a redeemer is unaffected when their inviter is later deleted. All 22 new tests pass; npm run check clean for both files. Bead closed: coverage matches the fix prescribed in the bead body.

title: Wizard 'Change' link next to derivation-path info restarts vault-mode choice
description:
Alex (phone testing) reported: somewhere in the wallet flow a 'change' button/link next to derivation-path info navigates back to the 'use my key or collaborative custody' choice instead of adjusting the derivation-path setting it sits beside. Generic Change actions next to specific info must change THAT thing, not restart a wizard step.

Found: src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte (~line 1775, pre-fix). The mode-banner shows "Shared vault - every key uses the shared multisig path m/45'" or "Personal multisig - keys use the standard path {personalPathHint}" with a 'Change' link. Clicking it called changeVaultMode(), which nulled vaultMode and re-rendered the full two-card onboarding question ('Just my own keys' vs 'Shared with other people' - i.e. the use-my-key-vs-collaborative-custody choice), reading as a wizard restart even though keys.length===0 guarded against actual key data loss.

Fix: replaced the single 'Change' link with an inline two-way toggle (Personal / Shared pill buttons) directly in the mode-banner. switchVaultMode(mode) sets vaultMode directly and re-fetches known-key offers, so the derivation-path text next to the control updates in place instead of dropping back to the full question screen. No wizard-state loss either way (guarded by keys.length===0, unchanged).

Audited every other 'Change' action in wallet creation + wallet detail pages - all others already correctly scoped/labeled (device-confirm 'Change' in single-sig wizard reveals the picker inline; bitcoin tx 'change output' labels are unrelated domain terms; AmountEntry unit-cycle button out of scope, owned by a concurrent agent). See bead comments for the full audit table.
close_reason: Fixed in src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte: replaced the mode-banner's single 'Change' link (which nulled vaultMode and dropped back to the full personal-vs-collaborative onboarding question) with an inline Personal/Shared toggle. switchVaultMode(mode) now edits the setting directly in place next to the derivation-path text it sits beside, without leaving the step or losing wizard state. npm run check: 0 errors, 0 warnings (1876 files). Full audit of every other 'Change' action in wallet creation + wallet detail pages posted as a bead comment -- all others verified already correctly scoped/labeled, no further fixes needed.

comments.author: fast-worker
comments.text: Full audit of every 'Change' action found across wallet creation + wallet detail pages (grep case-insensitive for >Change<, class names containing 'change', and the word Change as button text): 1. src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte ~1775 (mode-banner, next to derivation-path text m/45'/personalPathHint) -> was changeVaultMode() dropping back to the full personal-vs-collaborative onboarding question. VERDICT: FIXED. Now an inline Personal/Shared toggle (switchVaultMode) that edits the setting in place; derivation-path text updates immediately, no state loss (still guarded by keys.length===0). 2. src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte ~1325 (device-change, Finish step, next to 'Signing device' summary) -> sets changeDevice=true, reveals the full device picker inline in the same step. VERDICT: OK, already correctly scoped (matches its own code comment '"Change" reveals the full picker'). 3. src/routes/(app)/admin/+page.svelte:206 ('Registration' row) -> Change, a real navigation to the settings page where registration mode is actually changed. VERDICT: OK (out of wallet-flow scope, but correctly labeled/destined; not a wizard). 4-9. 'Change' text elsewhere ([id]/send/+page.svelte, multisig/[id]/send/+page.svelte, multisig/stateless/+page.svelte, [id]/+page.svelte, multisig/[id]/+page.svelte, CoinControl.svelte, signing/*.svelte) are all the Bitcoin tx 'change output' domain term (change back to the wallet / change address chip filter) -- unrelated to the reported navigation bug. VERDICT: N/A, not an action button. 10. send/AmountEntry.svelte unit-cycle button (aria-label 'Change amount unit') -- explicitly out of scope per brief (owned by a concurrent agent editing AmountEntry/add-wallet method list); not touched.

title: Null JSON body crashes multisig create (500) and mislabels error on stateless combine (502)
description:
Repro (EDGE2 QA wave, http://qa-edge2.localhost:5388, user msig-owner):

POST /api/wallets/multisig with body literal `null` (Content-Type: application/json):
  curl -X POST $BASE/api/wallets/multisig -H "Content-Type: application/json" --data-binary "null"
  -> HTTP 500 {"message":"Something went wrong","errorId":"efa62a86"}

POST /api/stateless/combine with the same literal `null` body:
  -> HTTP 502 {"error":"The chain source could not be reached."}
  (reproduced twice, consistent)

Root cause: src/lib/server/api.ts readJson() does `JSON.parse(raw)` inside a
try/catch that only guards JSON.parse *syntax* errors. `JSON.parse("null")`
succeeds and returns the JS value `null` -- a valid top-level JSON value that
readJson happily returns as `body`. Every caller then does `body.source`,
`body.name`, etc. on that null, which throws a plain TypeError
("Cannot read properties of null (reading 'source')") *outside* readJson's
try/catch, inside the route handler.

- In src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/+server.ts POST, this TypeError is
  uncaught by the route's own catch (which only expects MultisigError) and
  bubbles to a 500.
- In src/routes/api/stateless/combine/+server.ts POST, the TypeError is
  caught by the generic `catch (e)` but is not an instanceof any of
  MultisigError/PsbtError/MultisigPsbtError/InvalidPsbtError, so
  statelessErrorInfo() (src/lib/server/stateless.ts:314) falls through to its
  unclassified-error branch: `{status: 502, message: 'The chain source could
  not be reached.'}` -- actively misleading (there is no chain access
  involved in a pure PSBT-combine call), and still a 5xx from a one-line
  malformed client request.

Expected: any of these endpoints should return a clean 4xx (e.g. "Invalid
request body") for a JSON body that isn't an object, same as they do for a
syntactically-broken JSON string. Likely fix is in readJson() itself -- reject
non-object top-level JSON (null, arrays, numbers, strings, booleans) with the
same apiError(400, 'Invalid JSON body') it already uses for parse failures --
so every one of its ~dozen callers gets the fix for free instead of needing
individual null-guards.

Impact: P1/P2 -- reproducible 5xx from unauthenticated-shape-wise-trivial
input (no auth bypass, no data loss), but violates the "never 5xx on bad
input" contract and the stateless/combine case actively lies about the cause
(reports a chain-connectivity problem that never happened), which would send
whoever's on call down the wrong path.

Found via EDGE2 multisig edge-case QA wave, case 2 (corrupted PSBT
submissions) + spot-check on wallet-create. Both repros are on the throwaway
qa-msig-2026-07-12.db instance, msig-owner session.

title: Commit messages don't use conventional-commit prefixes
description: All 3 commits so far (e42a7b5, efd7bac, 8582c31) use plain imperative titles ('Add database layer and session auth', 'Add Bitcoin server library') with no type prefix. Project standard calls for conventional format: 'feat: ...', 'fix: ...', 'refactor: ...'. The messages themselves are detailed and professional (not lazy/vague) — this is purely about adopting the type: prefix convention going forward, not a rewrite of existing history.
close_reason: Adopted: conventional-commit prefixes in use from 1b1caab (feat: add block explorer) onward; earlier history left untouched per the issue's own recommendation

title: Owner account deletion silently destroys shared multisigs + in-flight PSBTs for all cosigners
description:
deleteOwnAccount (src/lib/server/accountDeletion.ts:78) and admin deleteUser (src/lib/server/admin.ts:104) issue a bare DELETE FROM users; multisigs.user_id has ON DELETE CASCADE (db.ts:281), which cascades multisig_transactions/shares/signers. No guard, no warning surfaced to the deleting owner or to cosigners. The danger-zone copy (settings/+page.svelte:572-575) only mentions wallets shared WITH you, not wallets you own and have shared OUT.

Failure scenario: Alice owns a 2-of-3 multisig shared with Bob and Carol, mid-signing on a pending PSBT. Alice deletes her account. The wallet and the in-flight PSBT vanish for Bob and Carol with no notice, no export prompt, no recovery path.

Fix needs BOTH a server-side guard/notice (block or warn when the account owns multisigs with other active cosigners) and updated danger-zone copy clarifying that owned shared wallets are also destroyed. Cross-reference: cairn-684u covers test-gap for cascade completeness but not this product-level guard/notice gap.
close_reason: Fixed in 6aff163: server-side user-deletion path hardened (FK-clean purge, robust last-admin guard incl. disabled sole admin, shared-multisig owner-deletion guard + cosigner notification). 46 targeted tests green.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: RUNTIME CONFIRMED (2026-07-12 battery, cairn-fresh/dev-fresh2.db, v0.2.16). After owner A self-delete: multisigs=0, multisig_keys=0, multisig_shares=0, multisig_transactions=0, signers=0; viewer Carol's multisig list returned empty; awaiting_signature PSBT destroyed. Zero signal to collaborators: Carol's events/notification_queue rows 0 before AND after; accountDeletion.ts emits only a server log.info. Logs clean (no FK errors). Fix needs: (i) confirm-dialog enumeration of owned-shared wallets + pending txs, (ii) collaborator notification on owner deletion (server-side), (iii) danger-zone copy fix (being shipped separately by UX session as the copy-only half).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: UX half shipped in 2d67da8: danger-zone copy now warns owned-shared vaults are destroyed for all collaborators without notice, bitcoin itself unaffected, suggests export/handoff first. Server half (guard/enumeration/notification) still open.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Pinned in destructiveOps.test.ts Group 6a: owner self-delete destroys multisig + in-flight awaiting_signature tx + signer rows, zero events/notification rows for cosigner/viewer. Flips when server-side guard/notice lands.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: SERVER HALF FIXED in 6aff163 (branch single-sig-full-wallet). Root cause: multisigs.user_id is ON DELETE CASCADE, so deleting an owner destroyed every multisig they own (incl. ones shared OUT + in-flight PSBTs) for all cosigners, with zero signal. Fix (server-side only, no new UI): (1) new userDeletion.ts ownedSharedMultisigs() enumerates owned multisigs that have >=1 participant, capturing name + participant ids + pending awaiting_signature tx count BEFORE the cascade. (2) admin deleteUser now BLOCKS with AuthError code 'owns_shared_multisigs' (message enumerates the affected wallets + pending-signature count) unless called with { force: true }; the DELETE /api/admin/users route accepts a force flag to acknowledge. (3) deleteOwnAccount is intentionally NOT blocked (a user must be able to close their own account; the danger-zone copy shipped in 2d67da8 already warns) but now emits notifications. (4) BOTH paths, when a shared-multisig owner deletion proceeds, fire a new in-app 'multisig_removed' notification to every affected participant via notifyOwnerDeletionCosigners() (post-commit, best-effort). New event type registered in notifyTypes.ts + DEFAULT_PREFERENCES (inapp-only) + USER_FEED_TYPES; deliberately omitted from the curated Settings toggle list (can't-miss account event, no opt-out). Evidence: destructiveOps.test.ts Group 6 KNOWN GAP flipped to green (owner self-delete now writes multisig_removed events to both bob+carol), plus 4 dedicated tests in userDeletion.test.ts (block-unless-force, forced-proceeds-and-notifies incl. pending PSBT, self-delete-notifies, private-multisig-no-guard). Targeted vitest: 46 passed. NOTE: the danger-zone confirm-dialog ENUMERATION (item (i) in alexl's 22:30 comment) is a client-side concern out of this server-only task's scope.

title: Block explorer: raw technical fields (WEIGHT, NONCE, BITS, MERKLE ROOT) have no inline explanation
description: The block detail page (/explorer/block/:height) has a nice plain-language 'How does this work?' explainer covering blocks/mining/proof-of-work in general, but the raw stat fields shown below it (WEIGHT, DIFFICULTY, NONCE, BITS, MERKLE ROOT) have no tooltip or inline definition. A beginner following Cairn's 'every technical term explains itself' promise (shown on the dashboard's Explorer card) would be lost on these specific terms, and DIFFICULTY: 0 (a regtest artifact) could read as a bug to someone unfamiliar with the local dev chain.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: block page already carries Term tooltips on Weight/Difficulty/Nonce/Bits/Merkle root; added a '<0.01' guard so regtest's tiny difficulty no longer renders as a bug-looking bare 0 (explorer/block/[id]/+page.svelte).

title: Missing regression test: admin recovery setup mandatory-but-unenforced (cairn-ethg)
description: cairn-ethg (P2, closed via commit 72b91d1) fixed admin recovery setup being documented as mandatory in the UI but not server-enforced — an admin could skip it entirely with nothing blocking them. Fix confirmed present in the app layout's load() gate (redirects admins with incomplete recovery setup to /recovery-setup, with a short-circuit to avoid a redirect loop on that page itself). No test covers the layout-level gate — the existing recovery test only unit-tests hasRecoverySetup() in isolation. Add a test for the layout load() function: an admin user with incomplete recovery setup (no phrase or 0 codes) is redirected to /recovery-setup; a non-admin user with incomplete recovery is NOT redirected; and requesting /recovery-setup itself does not loop.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 56a1371 (security-logging.test.ts, auth.test.ts setSessionCookie block, logger.test.ts + exported REDACT_OPTIONS, agreement/server.test.ts, api/wallets/server.test.ts, (app)/layout.server.test.ts).

title: Memoize address derivation: WeakMap for xpub chain nodes + multisig deriver factory
description:
xpub.ts:108-116 deriveAddress recomputes deriveChild(change) every call despite hot loops (walletScan.ts, addressWatcher.ts) holding change fixed across many indices. multisig.ts childPubkeys/deriveMultisigAddress re-parses ALL N cosigner xpubs from base58 on every single address call; multisigScan.ts rebuilds toMultisigConfig fresh per-index -- nothing hoisted.

Real numbers: single-sig fresh scan = 80 CKDpub ops vs 42 minimum (48% waste, milliseconds even on Pi -- hygiene not headline). Multisig 2-of-3 fresh scan = 120 full xpub re-parses (vs 3 needed) + 240 CKDpub ops (vs 126) -- scales linearly with N, 3-of-5 hits 200 reparses/400 ops. This is the real win.

Fix: WeakMap<HDKey,...> memoizing deriveChild(change) in xpub.ts (safe unconditionally, HDKey immutable, GC'd naturally). For multisig: explicit createMultisigDeriver(config) factory -- validate+resolve once, hoist chain-level nodes once, return (chain,index)=>address closure -- used ONLY by the two hot loops (multisigScan.ts, addressWatcher.ts). Do NOT use an ambient WeakMap keyed on MultisigConfig object identity -- unsafe, no immutability guarantee, and multisig.ts:530-531 deliberately re-validates configs per-call as a money-safety measure ("a bad config costs real money") -- leave deriveMultisigAddress's per-call validation untouched for one-off callers (tests, stateless.ts, detail pages).

Confirmed safe to ship standalone, independent of the sync-engine rewrite (cairn-6xxa.4), no memory-growth/invalidation concerns.

title: Quorum progress tracker shows 'signedFingerprints: []' after a cosigner's tool submits an already-finalized PSBT
description:
Found during quorum-matrix E2E testing (2-of-2, 1-of-3, and 3-of-5 wallets, all real broadcasts on regtest, 2026-07-06).

Reproduction: build a multisig PSBT via POST /api/wallets/multisig/:id/psbt, sign it progressively with real device signers (Trezor emulator via @trezor/connect, then a Bitcoin Core wallet acting as a cosigner via descriptorprocesspsbt), attaching each via PATCH /api/wallets/multisig/:id/transactions/:txId. When the LAST signature is added via a tool that auto-finalizes the PSBT (Bitcoin Core's descriptorprocesspsbt converts partialSig entries into the final scriptWitness once threshold is met, same as many other coordinators/cosigners would), the PATCH response's progress object correctly reports collected/complete (e.g. "collected":2,"complete":true) but signedFingerprints comes back as an empty array and every key in keys[] shows signed:false — even though the signatures genuinely are all present (broadcast succeeds immediately after).

Root cause (inferred, not yet read line-by-line): the per-key 'who signed' detection almost certainly walks the PSBT's partialSig maps per input, keyed by pubkey. Once an input is finalized, btc-signer/PSBT tooling clears partialSig and replaces it with finalScriptWitness — so any per-key attribution logic that reads partialSig sees nothing, even though completion is correctly detected some other way (probably by trying to finalize and checking success).

Impact: cosmetic/informational only — quorum enforcement itself is correct (under-quorum broadcast is still refused, complete:true correctly gates the broadcast button) — but the signing-stepper UI a real user watches would show '0 of N signed' or all keys as unsigned at the exact moment the transaction is actually complete and ready to broadcast, which is confusing and could make a user think their own signature didn't register. Reproduced 3 times across different quorum configs (2-of-2, 1-of-3, 3-of-5), so it's not config-specific.

Recommended fix: when an incoming PSBT for attach/combine is already fully finalized, derive the 'who signed' list from the ORIGINAL PSBT's partialSig entries (captured before finalization/merge) rather than re-reading the post-finalization PSBT, or track signed-fingerprint state independently of the live PSBT bytes.
close_reason: Root cause confirmed via repro (added as regression test): multisigPsbtProgress did a bare 'continue' on finalized inputs, skipping per-key attribution entirely — even though combineMultisigPsbts RETAINS earlier signers' partialSigs when it adopts a tool's finalization (verified: basePartialAfterCombine=1). Fix: read any surviving partialSig on finalized inputs before continuing, so signedFingerprints/keys attribute every signer except the one whose tool finalized (its sig now lives in the witness). Stepper shows 'N-1 of N signed' + complete instead of the misleading '0 of N'. collected/complete unchanged (still authoritative). All 41 multisigPsbt tests pass.

title: Fiat price endpoint hardcodes mempool.space, ignoring the configured Esplora URL
description:
src/routes/api/price/+server.ts hardcodes SOURCE = https://mempool.space/api/v1/prices and always calls it directly when a user opts into the fiat estimate -- regardless of what Esplora backend the admin has actually configured in Admin -> Settings -> Node connection.

This is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase's own pattern: src/lib/server/chain/esplora.ts already probes whether the CONFIGURED esplora URL supports mempool.space's /v1/ extensions (fees, blocks, difficulty-adjustment, RBF history) and falls back gracefully when it doesn't (e.g. plain Blockstream Esplora). The price endpoint bypasses that established, already-built mechanism entirely and reaches straight for the public mempool.space, even for a self-hoster who deliberately pointed their instance at their own node specifically to avoid exactly this kind of third-party dependency.

The commit's own framing ('privacy-first: no third-party price service contacted unless opted in') is only half true -- it's opt-in, but the third party is not the one the admin chose, it's always mempool.space regardless. A self-hoster running their own mempool/Esplora instance for full sovereignty would be surprised to learn enabling the fiat toggle quietly talks to the public internet anyway.

Fix: route the price fetch through the configured esplora client (probeV1-gated, same pattern as fees/blocks) so a self-hosted mempool instance serves its own price data, falling back to the public mempool.space only when the configured backend doesn't support /v1/prices (plain Blockstream Esplora has no price endpoint at all, so that fallback -- or simply disabling the fiat toggle -- would be the honest behavior there).
close_reason: Fixed: ChainService.getBtcUsdPrice prefers the configured backend's /v1/prices (probeV1-gated, respecting a self-hosted mempool instance); only plain-esplora backends fall back to public mempool.space, and a v1-capable backend that fails returns null rather than leaking. Endpoint is now a thin passthrough; caching in the chain layer. 621 tests green.

title: Backup restore has no migration path for older-version backups
description:
restoreBackup (src/lib/server/backup.ts:324) blind-inserts rows using current column names with orNull/numOr coercion helpers. This works fine while BACKUP VERSION===1, but there is no schema-migration layer for restoring an older-version backup once the schema changes -- a future column rename/add would cause silent data misplacement (wrong values landing in wrong columns, or defaults silently applied) rather than a clear error.

Note: decryptBackup already rejects backups NEWER than the current version (backup.ts:177-186), so the forward-compat direction is handled; it's the backward-compat (older backup, newer app) direction that has no migration path.

Fix: add a version-aware migration step in restoreBackup (or at minimum, fail loudly rather than silently misplacing data when BACKUP VERSION < current).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Pinned in backupRoundTrip.test.ts Group 5: version<1 accepted with silent field defaulting/misplacement (missing type -> 'xpub', renamed col -> ''); no version floor. Synthetic-shape tests, flip when a floor/migration lands.

title: Announcements: schema (announcements + announcement_dismissals)
description: Add to src/lib/server/db.ts following house conventions (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, TEXT enums, ISO timestamps via strftime default, ON DELETE CASCADE): announcements(id, type TEXT ['info'|'warning'|'urgent'|'promotion'], title TEXT, body TEXT, link_url TEXT NULL, link_text TEXT NULL, dismissible INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, expires_at TEXT NULL, display_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, created_at, updated_at). announcement_dismissals(id, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, announcement_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES announcements(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, dismissed_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT now, UNIQUE(user_id, announcement_id)). Mirrors the existing backup_reminders dismissal pattern (src/lib/server/backups.ts:137-143).
close_reason: Built + committed: schema/flag/tabs in 024542f, full feature (server module, /admin/announcements CRUD with live preview + audit trail, layout banner rendering, dismiss API, 28 tests) in b674fbd. Full suite + svelte-check green.

title: UTXO signing mass awareness
description: Hardware wallets verify input amounts by importing each UTXO's FULL parent transaction; mining-pool payouts have huge parents (F2Pool 2500-5500 outputs, ViaBTC 1400-1600, Foundry 200-250) that slow signing drastically and can time out devices. Mass = parent-tx size, NOT a network-fee factor. Differentiator for miners. Shared contract: ConstructedPsbt gains signingMass { totalParentVsize, tier low|medium|high, perDevice [{device, secondsLo, secondsHi}], splitSuggested }; new GET /api/wallets/[id]/utxo-mass -> { masses: [{txid, vout, parentVsize, tier, source?}] } (lazy, cached — never in the hot scan path).
close_reason: Epic complete: signingMass core with pool-calibrated tiers + quorum-scaled estimates (12bbe72), wallets UI chips/warnings/consolidation (ab12333), vault ceremony warnings + live coin estimates + wizard quorum comparison (this commit). All five spec items shipped; estimator constants documented as pending emulator calibration (TODO wired to scripts/vault-e2e).

title: Caravan import has no upfront bound on cosigner-key array size
description: Security review found: parseCaravanImport (src/lib/server/vaultExport.ts) maps over root.extendedPublicKeys with no upper-bound check before allocating; MAX_VAULT_KEYS (15) is only enforced later, inside createVault. Since /api/vaults/import previews the parse before anything is stored, a large malicious/malformed Caravan JSON (thousands of key entries) gets fully parsed and mapped on every request before being rejected downstream -- a minor authenticated-endpoint resource-exhaustion vector, not a fund-loss or auth issue. Add the same bound check at the top of parseCaravanImport (or wherever the raw JSON is first read) rather than only at persistence time.
close_reason: Fixed: parseCaravanImport bounds extendedPublicKeys at MAX_MULTISIG_KEYS=15 before per-key parsing; 5000-entry junk file rejected up front, 15 passes the bound (no off-by-one). Tested.

title: PSBT substitution: server accepts/broadcasts a signed PSBT with different outputs than the reviewed transaction
description:
SEVERITY: Critical (P0/P1) — this is the classic hardware-wallet-integration vulnerability class: a signed transaction can silently pay a different destination than the one the user reviewed and approved, with no server-side detection.

Endpoints: PATCH /api/wallets/[id]/tx/[txId] and POST /api/wallets/[id]/tx/[txId]/broadcast

What I did:
1. Built two independent PSBT drafts from the same wallet (wallet id 2) via POST /api/wallets/2/psbt:
   - Draft A (id 5): recipient bc1qw508d6qejxtdg4y5r3zarvary0c5xw7kv8f3t4, amount 300 sats
   - Draft B (id 6): recipient 1BvBMSEYstWetqTFn5Au4m4GFg7xJaNVN2, amount 300 sats
2. Fetched draft B's stored PSBT (GET /api/wallets/2/tx/6).
3. PATCHed draft A's record (PATCH /api/wallets/2/tx/5) with draft B's PSBT in the `psbt` field, simulating what happens when a user uploads a "signed" PSBT file back into Cairn (e.g. after an air-gapped/file-based signing round trip).

What happened: the PATCH succeeded with 200 OK and no warning of any kind. The transaction #5 record now stores draft B's PSBT (which really pays 1BvBMSEYstWetqTFn5Au4m4GFg7xJaNVN2) while its own `recipient` and `amount` fields still read the stale original values from draft A (bc1qw508..., 300). Confirmed via a follow-up GET /api/wallets/2/tx/5: the `summary.outputs` (freshly parsed from the actual stored PSBT bytes) correctly shows the real destination `1BvBMSEYstWetqTFn5Au4m4GFg7xJaNVN2` for 300 sats plus a 1108-sat change output — directly contradicting the `recipient`/`amount` fields on the same object, which still say bc1qw508.../300.

Root cause (read src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/tx/[txId]/+server.ts and src/lib/server/transactions.ts): the PATCH handler's only validation on an incoming `psbt` string is `summarizePsbt(body.psbt)` inside a try/catch — this only confirms the PSBT is *parseable*, not that its inputs/outputs match anything about the transaction record it's being attached to. There is no comparison against the record's own `recipient`, `amount`, or the UTXOs originally selected when the draft was constructed. The same gap exists one step further down in `broadcastTransaction()` (src/lib/server/transactions.ts): it accepts an optional `signedPsbt` parameter, finalizes it, and broadcasts whatever comes out — again with zero comparison to the transaction record's own recipient/amount, or to the PSBT that was originally stored as the draft.

Why this matters: this is precisely the attack surface hardware-wallet integrations are notorious for. A file-based or air-gapped signing flow (which this codebase is explicitly building — ColdCard SD card, QR/SeedSigner/Passport/Jade) round-trips a PSBT through an external device or the filesystem. If that file is swapped — by a compromised/malicious signing device, a MITM'd SD card, clipboard hijacking, or simply the user accidentally uploading the wrong file — Cairn's server has no mechanism to notice that the "signed" PSBT it received doesn't correspond to the transaction the user reviewed and approved in Cairn's own UI. It will accept it, and later broadcast it, potentially sending funds to a completely different address while any transaction-history UI that trusts the stored `recipient`/`amount` fields (rather than re-deriving them from the actual PSBT) would keep showing the *original, wrong* destination even after the money is gone to somewhere else.

What should happen: before accepting a replacement PSBT (at PATCH time) and again before broadcasting (defense in depth), the server should re-parse the incoming PSBT's actual inputs and outputs and verify they match the transaction record it's being attached to — same UTXOs being spent, same recipient address, same amount (allowing only the expected signature data to differ). Any mismatch should be rejected with a clear error rather than silently accepted. Additionally, whatever UI eventually renders the "Review"/"Confirm" step of the signing stepper must display the *freshly re-parsed* `summary.outputs`, never the stored `recipient`/`amount` fields, so that even if the backend gap isn't closed, a human has a chance to catch a swapped destination before confirming broadcast.

I could not fully chain this into an actual successful broadcast, since finalizePsbt() does still cryptographically require valid signatures for the actual UTXOs being spent (I have no private keys for these watch-only wallets, by design) — so this is not a "steal funds with zero signing" bug. But it is a real, verified gap in defense-in-depth for a flow whose entire threat model is "the signing device or the file transport in between might not be trustworthy," which is exactly the scenario file-based/air-gapped signing exists to handle carefully.
close_reason: Fixed in 40cf0d8: assertSameTransaction compares the input outpoints and output (script,amount) commitment of any signed PSBT against the reviewed draft; wired into BOTH the upload (PATCH .../transactions/[txId]) and broadcast paths. Verified live with the reporter's exact repro on wallet 2: PATCHing draft A with draft B's PSBT and broadcasting draft A with draft B's PSBT both return 400 mismatch 'pays different outputs than the one you reviewed'. Unit tests cover recipient-change, amount-change, and that signing itself does not trip the guard. Note: two independent sessions each implemented this guard concurrently; consolidated to one order-sensitive implementation

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Reply to S5's 3-point adversarial checklist: (1) No private key/seed handling anywhere in this flow - confirmed via grep, zero matches. (2) This bead: concrete repro is PATCHing tx A's saved record with tx B's PSBT (different recipient, same wallet) - succeeds 200 OK, record's recipient/amount then diverge from its own summary.outputs. Same gap in broadcastTransaction(). Not chained into fund theft (finalizePsbt still requires valid sigs for real UTXOs; no private keys available for watch-only test wallets by design) but the defense-in-depth gap is real and reproducible. (3) Confirmation-gate-before-broadcast - deferred until the stepper UI lands as a commit. Also verified clean: double-broadcast guard, and PSBT construction (draft creation, max-sweep, 5 error cases, auth).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Verified fixed. Re-ran the exact two-draft substitution repro against the wired PATCH and broadcast endpoints (now under /api/wallets/[id]/transactions/[txId]): both correctly reject with 400 'The signed transaction pays different outputs than the one you reviewed. It was not accepted.' / code:mismatch. Confirmed no false positives: re-submitting the SAME draft's own unsigned PSBT is correctly accepted (200, no mismatch), and an unsigned-but-matching PSBT sent to broadcast correctly fails with a distinct 'incomplete' code rather than being confused with mismatch. Closing.

title: No size/depth limit on Caravan JSON parsing (parseCaravanImport)
description: parseCaravanImport() in src/lib/server/multisigExport.ts:181-194 (and the wallet-import descriptor path near it) calls JSON.parse(text) on user-pasted/uploaded content with no size or depth limit before parsing -- the only bound (MAX_MULTISIG_KEYS) is checked AFTER JSON.parse succeeds, at line 236. A very large or adversarially deep pasted blob would be fully parsed synchronously on the server with no guard. Low severity for a self-hosted single-tenant instance (mostly self-DoS), but worth a cheap upfront size check (e.g. reject >1MB before calling JSON.parse) for defense in depth, especially since this is a form action reachable by any authenticated user.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: added a shared 1 MB body cap in readJson/readOptionalJson (readCappedBody, checks Content-Length + text length) covering every JSON endpoint, plus an upfront MAX_IMPORT_BYTES guard in parseCaravanImport before JSON.parse.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Confirmed by a second audit with more detail: it's not just parseCaravanImport -- src/lib/server/api.ts:19-25 (readJson, the shared JSON body reader used across API routes) also calls event.request.json() directly with no body-size limit, and there's no hooks.server.ts or adapter-level max-body-size config anywhere in the repo. So this is a repo-wide pattern on every JSON-accepting endpoint, not just the multisig import ones. Benchmarked: V8's JSON.parse handles even multi-MB deeply-nested payloads in a few hundred ms without stack overflow (arrays parse iteratively), so this is a memory/CPU cost DoS surface proportional to body size rather than an algorithmic-complexity or crash risk. Recommend a single shared body-size cap in readJson() (or a SvelteKit hook) rather than fixing each route individually.

title: balance_snapshots orphaned on wallet/multisig delete — no polymorphic FK, cleanup missed
description: Child tables use a polymorphic (wallet_kind TEXT, wallet_id INTEGER) discriminator with NO real FK into wallets/multisigs (disjoint id spaces), so ON DELETE CASCADE cannot fire. Cleanup is hand-written in deleteWallet() (wallets.ts:283-301) and deleteMultisig() (wallets/multisig.ts:416-430): both delete from notified_txids, address_labels, wallet_backups, backup_missing_notified — but NEITHER deletes from balance_snapshots. Every deleted wallet/multisig leaves orphaned balance_snapshots rows, only reaped 30/180d later by dataRetention.ts. deleteWallet already carries a comment trail of PAST orphan bugs (cairn-zari, cairn-nbsx, cairn-zui7.6) — this is the 4th recurrence of the same class. IMMEDIATE FIX: add the missing DELETE FROM balance_snapshots to both functions. STRUCTURAL FIX (Fable): move cleanup out of TS into AFTER DELETE triggers on wallets+multisigs covering all six children, plus an INTROSPECTIVE TEST that scans sqlite_master for any table having both wallet_kind+wallet_id, seeds a row per discovered table against a doomed parent, deletes the parent, asserts zero orphans — so the NEXT child table fails the test on day one instead of orphaning silently. See cairn-<schema-integrity epic>.
close_reason: Landed in 4d447fe. AFTER DELETE triggers on wallets+multisigs sweep all 5 polymorphic (wallet_kind,wallet_id) children; one-time orphan purge on boot; hand-rolled TS sweeps removed from deleteWallet/deleteMultisig/accountDeletion. Introspective deleteCascade.test.ts asserts zero orphans on direct + user-cascade paths and that every discovered child has a trigger. Tests 1839 pass, svelte-check clean.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: SCOPE EXPANDED + FIX REVISED (Fable red-team, verified in code). There are THREE delete paths, not two: (1) deleteWallet() wallets.ts:283, (2) deleteMultisig() wallets/multisig.ts:416, (3) USER deletion — wallets.user_id/multisigs.user_id have REAL ON DELETE CASCADE from users(id) (db.ts:45,53), so deleting a user removes wallets/multisigs VIA FK ACTION *without ever calling deleteWallet() TS at all*. That is why accountDeletion.ts:49-60 keeps a SECOND, independently-drifting hand-written list of polymorphic tables to sweep — two lists that can (and did) diverge. A centralized TS helper canNOT cover path (3). REVISED FIX: use AFTER DELETE triggers on wallets+multisigs that sweep the no-FK child tables — SQLite fires delete triggers even for rows removed by FK cascade when foreign_keys=ON (db.ts:25 sets it per-connection), so ONE trigger pair covers all three paths. Keep app-side effects (invalidateWalletCache, forgetReceiveWindow) in TS — they can't move to SQL. Once triggers land, DELETE accountDeletion.ts's duplicate list. PRIMARY DEFENSE (ship regardless): an introspective test that discovers every (wallet_kind,wallet_id)-bearing table from sqlite_master, seeds a row per table against a doomed parent, deletes the parent, asserts zero orphans — AND asserts every such table appears in the trigger body. This is the only guard that catches the true root cause (a NEW child table added without registering cleanup anywhere) and fails at dev time. Relates to cairn-684u (accountDeletion cascade-completeness test) and cairn-uzgu/ez9y/gakd (other delete-path incompleteness).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-07 (Sonnet 5 build agent + Fable consults) — UNCOMMITTED, held pending branch reconciliation with a concurrent session. Approach = triggers, per Fable red-team: AFTER DELETE triggers on wallets+multisigs (drop-and-recreate, not IF NOT EXISTS, so future body edits redeploy) sweep the 5 polymorphic children (balance_snapshots/wallet_backups/address_labels/backup_missing_notified/notified_txids) by (wallet_kind,wallet_id)=OLD.id; fires on all 3 delete paths incl. the users->wallets FK cascade. One-time migration purge of pre-existing orphans for all 5 tables. Removed the redundant manual TS sweeps from deleteWallet/deleteMultisig/accountDeletion (kept app-side effects: invalidateWalletCache/forgetReceiveWindow, preserved a concurrent session's invalidateMultisigCache). FABLE CORRECTION: multisig_shares has a REAL multisig_id FK CASCADE and no wallet_id column — not a polymorphic child, left alone. NEW deleteCascade.test.ts (introspective: discovers wallet_kind+wallet_id tables from sqlite_master, asserts zero orphans on direct-delete AND user-cascade paths + asserts each table is in a trigger body; a future unregistered child fails at dev time). Evidence: deleteCascade 7/7; 13-file related suite 110/110; full project svelte-check 0 errors/0 warnings.

title: Admin: feature-flags copy leaks internal doc path + bead ID
description: The RBF/CPFP 'upcoming features' description on /admin/feature-flags reads: "Tracks docs/CPFP-UNCONFIRMED-PLAN.md (cairn-u9ob)." in src/lib/server/featureFlags/registry.ts (fee_bumping flag). This leaks internal doc paths and bead IDs into admin-facing copy. Rewrite as plain operator-facing copy, same meaning (RBF/CPFP fee bumping upcoming feature).
close_reason: fixed: src/lib/server/featureFlags/registry.ts fee_bumping description changed from 'Tracks docs/CPFP-UNCONFIRMED-PLAN.md (cairn-u9ob).' to 'Lets users speed up a stuck transaction by bumping its fee.' -- no more internal doc path or bead ID in admin-facing copy. Verified via featureFlags test suite (28 passed) + npm run check (0 errors).

title: Wallet-name field pre-filled from Caravan import doesn't auto-select on focus (minor UX)
description: In the multisig creation wizard (src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte:506), importing a Caravan config pre-fills the wallet name field with the config's own 'name' value when the field is empty. If a user clicks into that pre-filled field and types a new name WITHOUT first clearing/selecting the existing text, standard input behavior inserts at the cursor rather than replacing -- producing an accidentally concatenated name (e.g. 'P2WSH-MMy Vault'). This is normal browser input behavior, not a bug, but many forms proactively auto-select pre-filled/suggested text on focus specifically to prevent this class of mistake. Low priority: consider auto-selecting the name field's content on first focus after an import pre-fill, or a 'suggested name, click to accept or type to replace' affordance.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: the multisig name field auto-selects on first focus when pre-filled from a Caravan import, so typing replaces rather than concatenates (wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte).

title: Test hygiene: notifications.test.ts mutates shared DEFAULT_PREFERENCES without try/finally
description: src/lib/server/notifications.test.ts:65-78,101-110 mutates the shared exported DEFAULT_PREFERENCES['tx_received'] object in place and restores it manually with no try/finally or afterEach guard. If an assertion between mutation and restore throws, the object stays permanently mutated for the rest of the test run, silently affecting other files that import it (e.g. notificationQueue.test.ts). Fix: wrap in try/finally, or deep-clone before mutating. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Wrapped both DEFAULT_PREFERENCES mutations in notifications.test.ts in try/finally so a failed assertion can't leave the shared exported object mutated for the rest of the run.

title: Missing regression test: coinbase-maturity check silently bypassed on chain-RPC hiccup (cairn-7fmd)
description: cairn-7fmd (P1, closed in c17328b) fixed coinbaseScan.ts's isCoinbaseTx/annotateCoinbase so a chain-RPC failure while checking a UTXO's funding tx no longer gets silently treated as 'not a coinbase' (which could let a user build/sign a transaction spending an immature coinbase output). Fix confirmed present. Add a test to src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.test.ts (or a new coinbaseScan.test.ts): simulate a chain-RPC failure while checking a UTXO's funding tx during coin selection / constructPsbt, and assert the UTXO with unknown coinbase status is excluded from auto-selection (or the build fails clearly) rather than silently spent. This is fund-safety-critical: a wrong answer here lets an app-level rule (100-block maturity) be bypassed.
close_reason: Committed in 26af83d: multisig substitution guard tests, legacy p2pkh/p2sh-p2wpkh spending-source tests, all 6 SPV reason codes, describe.runIf trap doors removed (multisig suites unconditional; regtest E2E skips loudly), coinbaseScan.test.ts fail-closed coverage.

title: Verify session-cookie SameSite/Secure behavior on plain-HTTP deployments
description: Visual audit (2026-07-12): in the preview environment, login/register succeed and fetch-based requests stayed authenticated, but full top-level page navigations arrived unauthenticated -- the httpOnly session cookie was not sent/persisted across a full browser navigate(), only across fetch/SvelteKit client-router requests. This bounced the audit to /login repeatedly when opening deeper money screens. Most likely a preview-proxy SameSite artifact and not necessarily a product bug -- but it is the exact failure mode the login page already guards against (COOKIE_ERROR class, the plain-HTTP Umbrel case). Action: deliberately test the session cookie's SameSite/Secure attributes on a real plain-HTTP (non-preview) deployment before treating this as a UX/product item. Files: cookie config in src/lib/server/auth.ts / session layer. Do not act on this as a UX fix without reproducing outside the preview first. Searched for overlap first: br search for 'cookie'/'SameSite' returned no related matches (only WebAuthn and admin-settings hits) -- filing new.
close_reason: Same root cause + fix as cairn-wrph (see that bead's closing comment for full curl matrix + sec sign-off): server.mjs now defaults PROTOCOL_HEADER=x-forwarded-proto for unconfigured deployments, scripts/serverProto.mjs fill-when-absent helper on both listeners. Curl-verified.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Same root cause and same fix as cairn-wrph (closed): adapter-node's get_origin()
defaults to https on any unconfigured (no ORIGIN, no PROTOCOL_HEADER) deployment,
including the plain-HTTP listener in server.mjs, so cookieSecure()
(src/lib/server/auth.ts:130-140) stamped Secure on a plain-HTTP response and the
browser silently dropped the cookie. This is the exact "httpOnly session cookie not
sent/persisted across a full navigate()" symptom this bead describes.

Fix: server.mjs now defaults PROTOCOL_HEADER=x-forwarded-proto when unset, plus a
new fill-when-absent helper (scripts/serverProto.mjs) that stamps each listener's
own protocol onto arriving requests unless one is already present (so a real
reverse-proxy's own X-Forwarded-Proto is always honored, never overwritten).

Curl-verified against the built server: no Secure attribute over plain HTTP with no
forwarded-proto header; Secure preserved when an inbound x-forwarded-proto:https
header is present.

See cairn-wrph's closing comment for the full curl matrix and sec sign-off list
(applies identically here). Closing this as the same fix.

title: Fingerprintless key (xfp 00000000) false-alarms 'Wrong seed / passphrase' on paste-verify despite matching xpub
description:
SURFACE: WizardKeyCheck.svelte (in-wizard 'Verify this key') — same defect reachable post-creation wherever compareWizardKey/compareMultisigKey fingerprint semantics apply.

REPRO (paste-only, no device/file needed):
1. Multisig wizard > Add keys > Paste public key. Paste a BARE xpub with no [fp/path] origin prefix and leave the fingerprint field blank. Server normalizeKey() (+page.server.ts:108-136) stores fingerprint='00000000' (the 'no fingerprint on record' placeholder). Key is added.
2. On that key, expand 'Verify this key'. Paste the SAME xpub and type the key's REAL master fingerprint into the fingerprint field (or paste [realfp/48'/0'/0'/2']xpub...).
3. runPasteCheck() -> compareWizardKey({xpub, fingerprint:'00000000'}, {xpub:same, fingerprint:realfp}).

EXPECTED: green match — the xpub is identical, this IS the correct key; at worst a neutral 'we had no fingerprint on record, now confirmed' note.

ACTUAL: fingerprintMatch = ('00000000' === realfp) = false while xpubMatch = true, so fromComparison() returns {kind:'mismatch', fingerprintMatch:false}. Template (WizardKeyCheck.svelte:282-317) takes the !fingerprintMatch branch and shows:
 - 'This doesn't match what you added. It reports fingerprint <realfp>, but this key was added with 00000000...'
 - the two-cause list: WRONG_SEED_HEADLINE 'Wrong seed phrase.' + PASSPHRASE_CAUSE_HEADLINE
 - the PROMINENT loss-of-funds block: PASSPHRASE_LOSS_WARNING 'Using a passphrase creates a single point of failure. If you forget it, you could permanently lose access to your funds.'

So a user who imported/pasted a key without a fingerprint, then correctly verifies it, is told the key is WRONG and warned about permanent loss of funds — even though the xpub matches exactly. The scare is misleading and could push a user to remove a perfectly good key.

ROOT CAUSE: compareWizardKey (wizardKeyCheckLogic.ts:31-39) treats stored '00000000' as an ordinary fingerprint in the equality check. The '00000000' placeholder is special-cased everywhere else (rememberLiveRead skips it +page.server.ts:75; same-seed grouping exempts it +page.svelte:611; normalizeFingerprint returns null for it keyOrigin.ts:59) — but NOT in the verify comparator.

SUGGESTED FIX: when stored.fingerprint==='00000000' (or reading fp is the placeholder), do not report a fingerprint MISMATCH. If xpubMatch is true, treat as verified (optionally with a neutral 'no fingerprint was on record; the key itself matches' note); only when xpubMatch is ALSO false show the wrong-seed/passphrase branch.

STATUS: confirmed by static analysis of the exact code paths above. Live UI reproduction was BLOCKED — the shared dev server on :5388 was down (HTTP 000) for the entire QA window, so no screenshot/live capture. Re-verify in-browser when the server returns. Matches the audit-flagged, previously-unfiled P3.

title: New-block updates feel slow — UI should react near-instantly to a found block
description: From Alex's Umbrel testing: when a new block is found the UI takes noticeably long to reflect it. Check the Electrum blockchain.headers.subscribe push path vs any polling interval, and how /api/events (SSE) fans out to open pages; tighten the path from Electrum notification to client update so a new block shows within a second or two. May be worse on public Electrum servers, but default should still feel responsive.
close_reason: Done together with cairn-ybsv in src/routes/(app)/+page.svelte and explorer/+page.svelte: on an SSE block event the client now optimistically paints the new tip height (+time) straight into the resolved chain snapshot BEFORE invalidating, so the header updates within the SSE push latency while the full snapshot (10 block summaries + mempool + fees + hashrate) refreshes in the background. Guards: SSE connect-replay of the current tip is ignored, and a resolve-vs-invalidate loop is prevented by tracking the height already refetched (plus a catch-up invalidate if a block arrived while a snapshot was in flight).

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Investigated 2026-07-06. The pipeline is already push-based end to end: Electrum 'header' event -> /api/events SSE sendBlock -> client onNewBlock -> invalidate('cairn:chain'). Two latency sources: (a) public Electrum servers can be slow to push headersSubscribe notifications (nothing we control; a local electrs/Core-backed server fixes it); (b) after invalidate, the dashboard re-runs loadChainSnapshot() — 10 block summaries + mempool + fees + hashrate — before the UI shows ANYTHING new, so even a fast push feels slow. Fix shape: optimistically render the new tip height/time straight from the SSE payload (it already carries the header) while the full snapshot refreshes in the background; consider splitting tip from the heavier snapshot data so the cheap part updates instantly. Overlaps with cairn-ybsv's streaming work.
