title: Drop BIP-48 p2sh:1 origin mapping — require m/45' for bare legacy P2SH multisig cosigner keys
description: bitcoin/multisig.ts:260-266 — BIP48_SUFFIX maps bare-legacy p2sh to suffix 1' (m/48'/0'/0'/1'), the SAME slot as p2sh-p2wsh. BIP-48 only defines 1'(P2SH-P2WSH) and 2'(P2WSH); it defines NO path for bare legacy P2SH — the ecosystem uses m/45' (Fable, high confidence: Coldcard exports p2sh_deriv:m/45' alongside p2sh_p2wsh_deriv:m/48'/0'/0'/1'; Sparrow default P2SH = m/45'; Caravan/unchained = m/45'/0'/0'; Electrum = m/45'/0. Trezor uses 48'/coin'/acct'/0' — suffix 0', never 1'. Nothing emits 1' for bare P2SH). HARM (beyond nonstandard descriptor): (a) accepting 1' MASKS exactly the wrong-key paste this validator exists to catch — a 1' key IS someone's nested-segwit account key, so it reuses an account across script types AND hides the paste error; (b) if the key really came from m/45', an EXPORTED fabricated [fp/48h/0h/0h/1h] origin poisons seed recovery (re-derivation from the recorded path yields a different xpub, funds not found); (c) Trezor firmware validates script-type-vs-path at signing and trips 'forbidden key path' on a 1' origin over a bare-P2SH input. FIX (pure deletion — BIP-45 is already accepted unconditionally at line 305): remove the p2sh:1 mapping, require m/45' for legacy P2SH. KEEP P2SH import (Caravan round-trip is the interchange standard; legacy Unchained/Caravan m/45' vaults exist) but do NOT offer bare P2SH for NEW wallet creation. NOTE: SIG_PUSH_BYTES=73 was reviewed and is CORRECT (74 budgets for non-relayable high-S sigs) — no change.
close_reason: Landed in 4d447fe. Removed BIP-48 p2sh:1 mapping; bare P2SH import-only (fresh creation throws in hw/common + wizard disabled). validateCosignerKeyPath requires m/45' or Trezor 0', rejects 48'/.../1' on create, tolerates it on import with a warning. Ingestion-only — existing 1'-suffix wallets still load/derive/spend (raw-insert regression test). Full 12-file battery 433/433, svelte-check 0/0.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-07 (2 Sonnet build agents + Fable consults) — UNCOMMITTED. Grew from a 'pure deletion' into a coherent multi-file change once the build agent found Cairn's OWN HW drivers emit the rejected path. (1) Core: removed p2sh:1 from BIP48_SUFFIX; validateCosignerKeyPath rejects 48'/.../1' for bare p2sh, accepts m/45' + Trezor's 48'/.../0'; clear plain-language error. (2) Regression exposed + fixed (Fable Option B): bare legacy P2SH REMOVED as a creation option (wizard shows it disabled 'import only'; hw/common.ts multisigAccountPathIndexes now throws for p2sh instead of deriving the wrong ...1' account key — the actual latent bug). Do NOT fix drivers to derive at m/45' (would change the xpub). (3) SAFETY VERIFIED: validation is INGESTION-ONLY (validateMultisigKeyPaths create+import, vaultIntent add-key); load/view/SPEND never re-validate, so existing 48'/.../1' wallets keep working — locked in by a new raw-SQL-insert regression test (getMultisig->toMultisigConfig->deriveMultisigAddress->constructMultisigPsbt all succeed). (4) FABLE import-leniency = Verdict A: IMPORT tolerates a stored 48'/.../1' legacy-P2SH label WITH A WARNING (true provenance; rejecting would push a re-export at m/45' = different xpub = wrong addresses). Threaded {mode:'create'|'import'} through the validator; create throws, import warns; wired through createMultisig/parseCaravanImport(returns warnings[])/2 import routes/stateless; vaultIntent stays strict. Caravan export still emits each key's true stored path. Fixed 2 stale fixtures. Evidence: 4 core multisig files 169/169; full 12-file battery (incl. hw drivers + wizardProgress) 433/433; svelte-check 0/0. Two follow-ups filed.

title: Test gap still open: HW driver signing entry points (Trezor, Jade) untested beyond pure logic
description: The 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit noted: 'pure logic is excellent across all four drivers, but the actual device-interaction entry points (signPsbtWithLedger, signPsbtWithTrezor, Jade's full sign flow including its PIN-server HTTP relay) are largely untested beyond BitBox02's registration-order case. Jade is weakest — essentially untested beyond pure path/descriptor helpers.' Re-verified 2026-07-06: grep confirms signPsbtWithTrezor has no matching test in trezor.test.ts, and Jade's full sign-flow (including the PIN-server HTTP relay) remains untested. This is money-moving code (the actual PSBT-signing call to hardware). Add tests mocking the device-transport layer for signPsbtWithTrezor's happy path + a representative error case, and for Jade's sign flow including a mocked PIN-server HTTP exchange.
close_reason: Committed in 81181db: Trezor warn-not-swallow tests, signPsbtWithTrezor + signPsbtWithJade (scripted device + PIN-relay fetch) coverage, sign-method list extracted to pure signMethods.ts with unit tests (capability-false renders disabled-with-reason, never vanishes).

title: Electrum serial resubscription and missing unsubscribe
description: client.ts:338-357 — resubscribe uses for...await (serial) not Promise.all. No unsubscribeScripthash() exists anywhere — scripthashSubs set only grows. Multi-wallet instances pay N sequential round-trips on every reconnect. Fix: use Promise.all for resubscription, add unsubscribe method, clean up subs when wallets are removed.

title: Add-wallet wizard loses all progress on page reload (Umbrel: loops back to method chooser)
description: Reported by Alex testing v0.1.1 on Umbrel: paste xpub -> validate -> confirm derived addresses -> wizard resets to the 'choose your method' step instead of advancing to Name. Root cause: the wizard keeps every bit of progress in ephemeral Svelte state, and Umbrel's app_proxy auth wall can force a full-page reload mid-wizard; a remount resets to step 0 (or the method chooser when the URL carries ?restore=1). The wizard's own step logic was verified correct against the deployed 0.1.1 image. Fix: sessionStorage progress snapshot + resume-after-reload with a Start-over escape hatch (wizardProgress.ts + tests), plus PROXY_AUTH_ADD false in the store compose to remove the reload trigger.
close_reason: Fixed and shipped as v0.1.2: sessionStorage progress persistence + resume note with Start-over (private commit 409de91, public 8861c22, 13 unit tests in wizardProgress.test.ts); store commit e1786aa pins image 0.1.2 and Alex's 34ff99d sets PROXY_AUTH_ADD false. Verified on the deployed 0.1.2 image: mid-wizard reload resumes at Preview with addresses intact; update path 0.1.1->0.1.2 tested with data preserved.

title: Mobile: wallet/vault detail page titles collapse to 0px width (flexbox shrink bug)
description:
Pages affected: /wallets/[id] and /vaults/[id], at mobile viewport width (tested 390x844, iPhone-ish)

What I did:
- Resized the browser to 390x844 and navigated to a wallet detail page, then a vault detail page.

What happened:
- On /wallets/1 ("Native SegWit wallet"), the <h1> title is squeezed down to ~26px wide, showing only "N.." before being clipped by ellipsis, while the "Native SegWit" type badge and orange "Send" button next to it keep their full size.
- On /vaults/4 ("E2E cairn-a4k Vault"), it's worse: the <h1> renders at literally 0px width — the vault name is completely invisible. Only a single stray "g" character (the tail end of "Vault") peeks out between the "Send" and "Download backup" buttons. Confirmed via DOM inspection: getComputedStyle on the h1 shows width=0, white-space: nowrap, overflow: hidden, flex-shrink: 1, min-width: auto (the default).
- Root cause: the page header is a flex row containing the title + type/quorum badge + action buttons (Send, Download backup, Export history, Delete). None of these siblings shrink, so the browser's default flexbox behavior (min-width: auto on a nowrap text element) squeezes the title down to fit whatever space is left over — which on a narrow viewport is zero or near-zero.

What should have happened:
- The page name/title must always stay legible. On narrow viewports the header should wrap the action buttons to a new row (or collapse them into a menu) rather than let the title itself shrink to nothing. At minimum the title needs a sane min-width or the flex item needs `flex-shrink: 0` / `min-width: 0` handled properly with its own truncation budget separate from the buttons.

Priority: P1 — a user cannot tell which wallet or vault they are looking at on a phone, which is a serious usability problem for a Bitcoin app where "am I looking at the right wallet" matters a lot. Reproduced cleanly on two different detail pages (wallet and vault), so this is a shared layout pattern, not a one-off.
close_reason: Fixed on both detail pages: head-title takes flex-basis 100% below 640px so actions wrap below. Browser-verified at 390x844 (wallet h1 252px, multisig h1 176px — previously 26px/0px — buttons wrapped below) and at 1280px (single row unchanged). Done in the foreground: subagent spawning is blocked by the monthly spend limit.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Verified fixed: both /wallets/1 and /wallets/multisig/4 (formerly /vaults/4) now render the full title on its own line at 375px and 390px width, with badges/buttons wrapping to a second row instead of squeezing the title. Retested during the final polish QA pass.

title: Wallet-creation wizard: browser back button exits the wizard instead of going back one step
description: On /wallets/new (v0.2.0), the wizard has 3 steps: Key, Verify, Finish. The step transitions do NOT push new browser history entries -- the URL stays /wallets/new for all three steps. Consequence: the in-app 'Back' link inside the wizard correctly steps back one screen (Verify -> Key, Finish -> Verify) and preserves entered data, but the browser back button bypasses the wizard state machine entirely and jumps to whatever page was open before /wallets/new (in testing: straight to /wallets), discarding the user out of the flow. Reproduced from both step 2 (Verify) and step 3 (Finish) -- both browser-back to /wallets, skipping intermediate steps. Browser forward then correctly restores to the step you left (sessionStorage resume state works), but a user who instinctively hits the OS/mouse back button to correct a mistake on one wizard step gets bounced out of the whole flow instead. Fix: push a history entry (or at least a popstate handler) per wizard step so browser back matches the in-app Back link's one-step-at-a-time behavior. This is likely the same underlying pattern behind Alex's broader 'back takes me too far' reports -- worth checking the multisig wallet wizard and any other multi-step flow (recovery setup, etc.) for the same gap.
close_reason: Fixed in 8541ebb, merged (both single-sig /wallets/new and multisig /wallets/multisig/new, per Alex's follow-up comment confirming the same gap in both). Neither wizard changes the URL per step, so browser Back previously bypassed the flow entirely. Fix mirrors each step into the history stack via SvelteKit shallow routing (pushState/replaceState with a new wizardStep PageState field, src/app.d.ts): forward transitions push a new entry, in-app Back now delegates to history.back() so both Back paths are identical and can never disagree, and a popstate listener applies the step from history.state instead of letting the browser navigate away. Single-sig seeds the full 0..step history stack on mount (a sessionStorage resume can land mid-wizard); multisig always starts at step 0. Verify/Finish clamp back to Key if required data is missing; the Done view is left alone (terminal, driven by createdId not step). Verified via careful manual review of the push/pop separation (pushState never fires popstate, so no loop) plus npm run check 0/0 and the full suite (1682 passed/1 skipped, including 16 existing wizard unit tests).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Confirmed the same gap exists in the multisig wallet wizard (/wallets/multisig/new, steps: Protection -> Add Keys -> Review -> Confirm -> Done). Reproduced: advanced from Protection to 'Add Keys' (step 2) via the in-app Continue button, URL stayed at /wallets/multisig/new (no per-step history entry, same pattern as the single-sig wizard). Browser back button then skipped the entire wizard AND the wallets list, landing straight on the dashboard (/) -- worse than the single-sig case since the entry point this time was one hop further from Home. Same fix applies: push a history entry per wizard step in both wallet-creation wizards.

title: Missing regression test: broadcast route error paths lack a test for the log.error call (cairn-obb follow-up)
description: cairn-obb (P1) reported no server-side logging anywhere; the close reason noted 3 of 4 fix items landed but flagged remaining single-sig routes with bare catch-and-respond blocks, specifically src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/transactions/[txId]/broadcast/+server.ts. A same-day follow-up commit (d5f9c73) already added the missing log.error() call to that route, but no test exists asserting it fires. Add a +server.test.ts (or extend an existing route test) for both the single-sig and multisig broadcast routes: mock the broadcast call to throw, and assert both a 502-class response AND a log.error() call with request context (walletId/txId) are produced.
close_reason: Committed in 8603ac6: multisigShares.test.ts (first coverage), contacts.test.ts share-revocation, notifications.test.ts stage isolation, broadcast route malformed-JSON 400 + log.error tests, walletsDraft.test.ts buildDraft/coin-control coverage.

title: Trezor Connect may hit the same first-click Vite optimizer churn
description: @trezor/connect-web is loaded via the same lazy dynamic-import pattern that broke Ledger (Vite discovers the dep graph mid-session on first click, re-optimizes, kills the in-flight import with 504 Outdated Optimize Dep). Found while fixing cairn-ivq; Trezor left untouched there. Fix: add the @trezor/connect-web lazy graph to optimizeDeps.include in vite.config.ts and verify the first-click path on a cold dev server. Low risk in prod builds (no optimizer).
close_reason: Fixed: @trezor/connect-web added to optimizeDeps.include with a comment tying it to the Ledger incident. Prod builds unaffected (no optimizer).

title: Single-key wallets never store master_fingerprint — all hardware-wallet signing is broken
description:
POST /api/wallets (src/routes/api/wallets/+server.ts) only accepts { name, xpub, deviceType }.
createWallet() in src/lib/server/wallets.ts (line ~138-166) inserts a row with no
master_fingerprint — the column exists in the wallets table and is read by
config/descriptor export routes, but is NEVER written by any wallet-creation path.
This is true even for wallets created via a real hardware-wallet "Connect" flow
(Trezor/Ledger/BitBox/Jade) — the wizard client (src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte)
discards the origin fingerprint that TrezorConnect.getPublicKey() etc. return
(e.g. `descriptor: "wpkh([73c5da0a/84h/0h/0h]xpub.../<0;1>/*)"`), keeping only a
cosmetic `deviceType` label string. The "Paste public key" flow never asks for
one either.

Downstream impact (src/lib/server/transactions.ts ~line 297-303):
  const origin = wallet.master_fingerprint ? { fingerprint: ..., path: ... } : null;
origin is always null for single-key wallets, so constructPsbt() (src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts
line ~509) never sets bip32Derivation on inputs (guarded by
`if (originPath && fingerprint !== null)`).

Every hardware-signing code path depends on bip32Derivation to know which
derivation path to sign with. src/lib/hw/trezor.ts's trezorSignRequestFromPsbt
(line ~173-178) explicitly anticipates this and throws:
  "This transaction is missing the key-origin information the Trezor needs to
   sign. Re-create it from a wallet set up with its master fingerprint."
But there is currently NO way to "set up a wallet with its master fingerprint"
in the product — the field is never collected or stored anywhere.

Reproduced end-to-end this session: fetched a real xpub from a Trezor emulator
(trezor-user-env, BIP39 "abandon...about" test vector, fp 73c5da0a), imported it
into Cairn via "Paste public key" (Native SegWit), built a real send transaction
against a funded regtest wallet, downloaded the unsigned PSBT via
GET /api/wallets/:id/transactions/:id/file, and confirmed with a script that
parses the PSBT (@scure/btc-signer) that bip32Derivation is entirely absent from
the single input. Root-caused via code inspection (not just the paste-key path —
createWallet has no fingerprint parameter at all, so device-connect wallets are
equally affected).

Impact: every single-key wallet's hardware-signing flow (Trezor, Ledger, BitBox02,
Jade — anything that isn't the "Generic wallet/file" external-signer passthrough,
which doesn't need bip32Derivation since it hands off to Sparrow/Electrum/ColdCard)
is broken and will throw trezor.ts's "missing key-origin information" error (or the
equivalent in ledger.ts/bitbox.ts/jade.ts) on every attempt to sign, for every
single-key wallet in the product today.

WHY THIS IS THE FOUNDATION (per Alex, 2026-07-06): without a master_fingerprint on
every key, a PSBT cannot carry bip32Derivation, and without bip32Derivation a
hardware wallet cannot know which key/path to sign with — full stop. This is not
one bug among several equally-weighted collaborative-custody findings; it is the
precondition for hardware-wallet signing to work AT ALL. The BIP-48 path-hygiene
work in cairn-1kc3.* (cosigner path validation) and the dual-path upload UX in
cairn-fdlf.* (see docs/MULTISIG-DERIVATION-AUDIT-2026-07-06.md) both assume PSBTs
already carry correct, complete key-origin data — that assumption is false today
for every single-key wallet. Multisig wallets are NOT affected by this specific
bug (multisig_keys / MultisigSignKey already stores and validates fingerprint
correctly, confirmed in cairn-c9op's investigation) — this bug is scoped to the
single-key `wallets` table / createWallet() path only. Fix this first; treat
BIP-48 hygiene and upload-UX work as building on top of it, not parallel to it.

Duplicate reports merged: this same bug was independently rediscovered twice more
in concurrent QA sessions against the same live instance and filed as cairn-tu0i
(closed as a dup) and cairn-c9op (closed as a dup, 2026-07-06) — both had identical
root-cause analysis. This bead (cairn-alw8) is the canonical one.

Fix sketch: capture the origin fingerprint (and ideally the full key-origin path)
at wallet-creation time — from the device response when connecting a real device,
and as an optional field on the "paste public key" form (many wallets export a
descriptor like `[fingerprint/path]xpub...` rather than a bare xpub, which could
also be accepted and parsed) — and persist it to wallets.master_fingerprint /
derivation_path so constructPsbt can populate bip32Derivation.
close_reason: Fixed: key origin (master fingerprint + derivation path) is now captured at wallet creation from every source (device connect, ColdCard export, pasted [fp/path]xpub descriptor, optional XFP field, backup restore) and persisted to wallets.master_fingerprint/derivation_path, so constructPsbt emits bip32Derivation and hardware signers can sign. Verified by end-to-end test: createWallet -> constructPsbt -> trezorSignRequestFromPsbt succeeds (and bare-xpub regression still throws). 1468 tests green, svelte-check clean. No manual HW-emulator signing run (Trezor Connect needs a browser popup); unit/integration coverage includes the real driver layer.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
FIXED (single-sig key-origin capture, all layers):

- src/lib/hw/keyOrigin.ts: new parseKeyOriginInput / normalizeFingerprint / normalizeOriginPath — accepts bare xpub, [fp/path]xpub key-origin form, h/H hardened markers, wpkh(...)/sh(wpkh(...)) descriptor wrappers, /<0;1>/* suffixes, #checksums. Lenient parser (never throws); all-zero fingerprint placeholder treated as unknown.
- src/lib/server/wallets.ts: createWallet() now takes fingerprint/derivationPath, ALSO parses an origin embedded in the xpub string (embedded wins), validates loudly (non-empty garbage throws a friendly error instead of silently dropping — silently dropping would re-create this bug), and persists both columns. Activity event detail gains hasKeyOrigin boolean for admin-log diagnosis.
- src/routes/api/wallets/+server.ts: POST accepts { deviceType, fingerprint, derivationPath } and descriptor-form xpub.
- Wizard (src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/): device connect (Trezor/Ledger/BitBox02/Jade) and ColdCard file import now KEEP the fingerprint+path the readers already returned (they were being discarded — the root cause); paste form accepts [fp/path]xpub in the key field plus an optional 8-hex master-fingerprint field for bare keys; QR descriptor scans pick the origin up via the same preview parse; restore-from-backup re-attaches masterFingerprint/derivationPath from the config file; Preview step shows the captured fingerprint badge; origin survives reloads via the wizardProgress sessionStorage snapshot.
- No DB migration needed: master_fingerprint/derivation_path columns already existed (db.ts lines ~108-112); they were just never written.

Verified (unit/integration tests, all green — 1468 passed / 1 pre-existing skip; svelte-check 0 errors 0 warnings):
- createWallet stores origin from explicit fields, from descriptor form, treats 00000000 as unknown, rejects malformed input, and stores null for bare xpub (regression guard).
- END-TO-END in wallets.test.ts: createWallet("[73c5da0a/84'/0'/0']zpub...") -> wallet row -> origin built exactly as transactions.ts buildDraft does -> constructPsbt -> psbtHasKeyOrigin true AND the real trezorSignRequestFromPsbt (the driver that used to throw "missing the key-origin information") accepts it, deriving address_n [84',0',0',0,0]. Inverse test pins the pre-fix state: bare-xpub wallet -> origin-free PSBT -> driver throws /key-origin/.
- psbt.test.ts: new explicit regression test that origin:null emits NO bip32Derivation on any input/output (constructPsbt itself was already correct — the bug was purely that origin was always null upstream).

Manual HW-emulator note: trezor-user-env stack is partially up on this machine (bridge 21325, ws 9001, electrs 60401, bitcoind 18443) but Trezor Connect requires a browser popup, so no headless manual signing run was performed — verification is via the unit/integration tests above, which exercise the real driver translation layer.

NOT touched: multisig paths (unaffected; MultisigSignKey already stores fp), transactions.ts, psbt.ts (verified correct, read-only). Unblocks cairn-fdlf.1 — the single-sig key-add flow now has a real key-origin capture seam: acceptReadKey(xpub, method, {fingerprint, path}) in +page.svelte, and the deviceRead.ts readers already return the full DeviceKey {xpub, fingerprint, path}.

title: Explorer search: live-suggestion dropdown visually overlaps/collides with 'Recent' search history row
description:
Page: /explorer (search box)

What I did:
- Typed a partial query ("127") into the Explorer search box while a "Recent:" search-history row was already showing below it (from earlier searches in the session).

What happened:
- The live-suggestion dropdown ("Block 127") visually overlaps the "Recent: ..." pills row directly underneath it, producing a garbled look — a small dark chip with cut-off text ("Blo...") appears to sit half on top of, half beside the "Recent" label, rather than either replacing it cleanly or pushing it down.
- Inspected via computed styles: `.live-suggest` (the suggestion box) is `position: absolute; z-index: 20; background: rgb(44,39,36)`, with a bounding rect spanning the full search-box width (248–1132px) and covering y=360.8–404.3. Meanwhile `.recent` (the search-history row) is `position: static; z-index: auto; background: transparent`, sitting almost exactly in the same region (y=364.8–390.8). Despite `.live-suggest` computing to a full-width solid-background box that should cleanly cover `.recent`, the actual rendered paint only shows a small "Blo" chip — suggesting the visible content inside `.live-suggest` doesn't fill its own bounding box, letting the transparent `.recent` row bleed through around it.
- Reproduced cleanly on a fresh, non-crashed page load (confirmed this is unrelated to the each_key_duplicate crash filed separately as cairn-5n3b).

What should have happened:
- The suggestion dropdown should either fully and cleanly cover the recent-searches row while open (solid background across its whole box) or the two should be laid out so they never occupy the same space (e.g. recent-searches hidden while suggestions are showing, or suggestions positioned below the recent row).

Priority: P3 — purely cosmetic, doesn't block search functionality (selecting the suggestion or pressing Enter still works), but looks broken/glitchy on a page every user will use constantly.
close_reason: Fixed: .recent now renders only when !liveLoading && !liveResult, making it mutually exclusive with the absolute .live-suggest dropdown — the overlap is structurally impossible. Verified by inspection + npm run check (0 errors); did not exercise in-browser because the concurrent account-recovery workflow is rewriting auth code and a login mid-flight can't be trusted.

title: Multisig finalization: full cryptographic signature verification (belt-and-suspenders)
description: cairn-vo6z (fixed on track/signing @ f6a6837, merged 5f5aa72) validates multisig finalization structurally: script binding + DER SIGHASH_ALL trailing byte + quorum count. A crafted DER-shaped-but-cryptographically-invalid signature still passes this structural check and only dies at broadcast (self-inflicted failure, no fund loss risk since network relay/consensus rejects it). Follow-up: verify signatures cryptographically against the descriptor's actual pubkeys (ECDSA/Schnorr verify) rather than relying on structural shape alone, for full belt-and-suspenders assurance ahead of broadcast. Related: cairn-u2r5's SIGHASH_ALL enforcement on the single-sig path has the same structural-only residual.

title: Copy-to-clipboard affordance is invisible on touch devices
description: src/lib/components/CopyText.svelte: the copy icon is opacity:0 by default and only reaches opacity:1 on :hover. Touch devices have no hover state, so every copyable value in the app (addresses, tx ids, block hashes — this component is used everywhere) gives mobile users zero visual indication it's tappable. Verified live: on a 375px viewport the copy icon is confirmed opacity 0 with no other affordance. Also has no :focus-visible styling, so keyboard users tabbing to it get a focus ring around an invisible icon. Fix: give the icon a low-opacity baseline visibility (not fully hidden) instead of hover-only, and add a proper :focus-visible state matching the app's global focus style.
close_reason: Fixed in 6f1ce4f: copy icon has a 0.45 baseline opacity (full on hover/focus) so touch users see the affordance, plus a focus-visible accent ring on the button

title: BitBox02 and Jade hardware wallet support
description: Two new devices following the established signer contract. BitBox02 (Shift Crypto): WebHID, touch confirmation, Bitcoin-only firmware supported, native PSBT signing. Jade (Blockstream): BOTH modes per Alex — QR air-gapped (likely already works via the generic BBQr/UR signer; verify + add device-specific instructions) and USB via Web Serial. Bastion has working implementations of both (public/js/bitbox.js, public/js/jade.js — jade via Web Serial + jadets lib; both devices register multisig quorums in a 16-slot on-device store and return signed PSBTs directly, no server seam). Device picker cards + per-device instructions + wizard key-import + multisig registration flows + tests.
close_reason: Epic delivered: BitBox02 (full) + Jade USB (full) drivers, signers for single-sig and multisig send flows, device-picker/wizard integration, WalletDeviceType extension, WASM build support. Jade-QR (BC-UR) split into its own follow-on bead per the plan.

title: Login succeeds server-side but session cookie doesn't stick -> silent nothing-happens loop; show a clear error
description: The v0.1.1 Secure-cookie bug showed the failure mode: POST /api/auth/login/password returns 200 + Set-Cookie, the browser drops the cookie (e.g. Secure flag over plain HTTP), and the login page just loops with no feedback. Defense-in-depth fix on the login client: after a successful login response, verify the session actually stuck (an authenticated probe); if not, show a clear error like 'Login failed - your connection may not support secure cookies. Contact your administrator.' instead of silently reloading the form.
close_reason: Fixed: the login page now probes /api/auth/me after a successful password or passkey login; a definite 401 (cookie didn't stick) shows 'Login succeeded, but your browser did not keep the session cookie — the connection may not support secure cookies. Contact your administrator (on a plain-HTTP server, ORIGIN or PROTOCOL_HEADER needs to be set).' instead of silently looping. Network-blip probe failures give the login the benefit of the doubt. Verified in the browser: normal login unaffected; stubbed 401 probe renders the error.

title: Electrum TLS connections never validate server certificates (rejectUnauthorized hardcoded false)
description:
Security audit 2026-07-05 (docs/SECURITY-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md), finding F2. Verified CONFIRMED by 3/3 independent adversarial reviewers.

src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts (~lines 146-153): when connecting via TLS to the configured Electrum server, rejectUnauthorized is hardcoded to false with no configuration path (env var, settings field, or cert-pinning option) to enable real certificate validation. ElectrumClientOptions exposes only host/port/tls(boolean)/timeoutMs -- no verification-mode or pinned-fingerprint field exists anywhere. This applies identically to the default public server (electrum.blockstream.info) and to any admin-configured 'custom' server, including a self-hoster's own trusted node reached over an untrusted network path.

Impact: any network-level attacker able to intercept the path to the configured Electrum host (hostile Wi-Fi, compromised router/DNS, malicious VPN/Tor exit, compromised ISP) can perform a full TLS MITM using a self-signed certificate, and Cairn accepts it silently. This is an integrity/availability risk, not primarily a confidentiality one (the in-code comment 'no secrets, only public chain data' is true for eavesdropping but ignores active tampering): a MITM on this channel can feed Cairn forged balances/UTXO sets/history/fee estimates, or intercept and lie about blockchain.transaction.broadcast results (see companion finding on unverified broadcast txids). No private keys or PSBT signing material transit this channel, bounding the severity below direct fund theft.

Fix: default to rejectUnauthorized: true, with an explicit opt-out (documented, off by default) for admins who knowingly run a self-signed server; consider certificate/public-key pinning (TOFU: pin fingerprint on first connect, alert on change) as a stronger alternative that still supports self-signed setups safely.
close_reason: Fixed in security-audit remediation pass; see commit. All 897 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: Remove empty vaults route skeletons left by the vault-to-multisig rename
description: src/routes/(app)/vaults/** and src/routes/api/vaults/** are zero-file directory skeletons left by the vault-to-multisig rename (commit 78a2864). Not live routes; delete the empty directories. Trivial, but currently the only dead surface in src/routes.

title: CSV formula injection in transaction history export
description: historyExport.ts:50-52 — csvField does RFC-4180 quoting but no formula-injection guard. User-controlled Label column (via PUT /api/wallets/[id]/labels, 120 chars, no char restriction) written verbatim. A label like =HYPERLINK("http://evil","x") executes when exported CSV opened in Excel/Sheets. RFC quoting does not neutralize leading =,+,-,@. Impact bounded: self-set label. Fix: prefix any cell matching /^[=+\-@\t\r]/ with a leading apostrophe.
close_reason: Duplicate of cairn-mf68 (same defect: historyExport.ts:50-52 CSV formula injection via user-controlled Label field, no formula-prefix guard). mf68 filed 2026-07-08T01:41:14Z, b1rg filed 2026-07-08T01:48:47Z (~7 min later) — closing the later-filed as dup per 2026-07-12 beads audit finding. Fix the CSV escaping once under cairn-mf68.

title: Portfolio balance chart can show duplicate x-axis date labels
description:
Dashboard "Balance over time" chart (src/lib/components/portfolio/BalanceChart.svelte)
picks up to 5 x-axis ticks by evenly-spaced INDEX into the filtered points array
(xTicks derived, ~line 132-144), then formats each with formatDate() which only
shows month+day (no time, no year) — src/lib/components/portfolio/BalanceChart.svelte:118-121.

When a wallet's balance-history series is sparse (few data points), two distinct
ticks can land on data points from the same calendar day, producing two
adjacent x-axis labels reading the exact same text (e.g. "Jul 4" ... "Jul 4" ...
"Jul 6"), which looks like a rendering bug even though each label is technically
correct for the point it's attached to.

Reproduced on the live QA instance (2026-07-06) after importing a fresh
single-key wallet with a small backfilled history — both the "1M" and "ALL"
ranges showed this ("ALL" screenshot: ticks read "Jul 4", "Jul 4", "Jul 6").

Not a data-correctness bug — a display/UX issue: identical adjacent tick labels
give the reader no signal that these are two different points in time.

Fix sketch: when two chosen ticks fall on the same calendar day, either skip the
redundant one, or fall back to a date+time format for that pair, or space ticks
by roughly-even time delta rather than by raw array index so sparse/clustered
data doesn't produce duplicate-looking labels.
close_reason: Fixed in src/lib/components/portfolio/BalanceChart.svelte xTicks: ticks are now spaced by roughly-even TIME across the visible range and snapped to the nearest data point (index spacing bunched ticks on sparse series), then deduped — a tick landing on the same data point or the same calendar-day label as the previous kept tick is skipped rather than reformatted, so every rendered label is distinct. svelte-check clean, full suite green.

comments.author: fable
comments.text: still visible post-merge 2026-07-12, garbled overlapping date labels bottom-left at desktop width, cosmetic P3

title: Portfolio: cache-first from wallet_snapshots, stop bypassing scan-lane guardrails
description:
getPortfolioDetail (portfolio.ts:386,409) calls scanWallet/scanMultisig directly with no lane arg -> defaults to 'interactive' lane; one uncapped Promise.all over every wallet+multisig; never imports walletSync.ts so never touches scanLimit (capped at SCAN_CONCURRENCY=2). Writes only to balance_snapshots, never reads/writes wallet_snapshots -- dashboard and /wallets run two independent full-scan pipelines for the same balances. Dashboard fires GET /api/portfolio on every mount -> N wallets past their 60s ScanCache window = N simultaneous interactive-lane gap scans, recreating the exact storm cairn-2zxt just fixed one page over.

Fix: new getPortfolioDetailFromCache(userId) reading wallet_snapshots/multisig snapshots synchronously (mirrors listCachedPortfolio on /wallets); relocate recordSnapshot/backfillSnapshots into doWalletScan/doMultisigScan's background pass; dashboard fires POST /api/portfolio/refresh on mount (existing endpoint, matches /wallets SWR pattern).

Must fold in 4 correctness fixes: (1) exclude shared multisigs from the owner-only balance chart (avoid contradicting hero card), (2) attribute multisig backfill rows to the multisig owner not the triggering viewer, (3) gate recordSnapshot on zero failed/aborted items, not 'some cached snapshot exists' (else charts flat-stale points through a multi-day outage), (4) tipHeight=0 (persisted on a scan-time tip lookup hiccup) must fall back to max tipHeight across snapshots or confirmations freeze at 0.

Needs Alex's explicit sign-off on one copy change: scannedCount meaning shifts from "responded within 10s this request" to "has ever synced" -- the unreachable-count chip copy should become something like "not yet synced" so it doesn't imply false real-time reachability.

Confirmed low-risk, standalone (sole consumer is routes/api/portfolio/+server.ts; send/PSBT path untouched). Fable-recommended: do this FIRST if only one week available.
close_reason: Already fixed by commit 50a274e (Release v0.2.7) which is an ancestor of this branch -- verified the scan-lane bypass, uncapped Promise.all, and per-mount rescan storm described in this bead no longer exist in current code. See comment for file:line evidence. Closed with --force because the tracking epic cairn-6xxa (dependency_type=blocks) is still open; this is a leaf verification, not blocked on the epic's other work.

comments.author: build-agent-b7to
comments.text:
Verified against current code (branch single-sig-full-wallet, HEAD 6a4e10e): the bug this bead describes was already fixed by commit 50a274e (Release v0.2.7, 'fix dashboard's uncached live-scan path'), which is already an ancestor of this branch.

Confirmed by reading the code, not just the commit message:
- routes/api/portfolio/+server.ts:24-33 -- GET is now a synchronous readPortfolioSnapshot() read, zero Electrum calls. No more per-mount scan storm.
- routes/api/portfolio/refresh/+server.ts -- POST drives walletSync.refreshPortfolio(), the same coalesced pass the wallets list uses.
- walletSync.ts: doWalletScan/doMultisigScan call scanWallet/scanMultisig with { lane: 'background' } (lines 415, 492); refreshPortfolio -> runPortfolioRefreshPass is capped at SCAN_CONCURRENCY (= DEFAULT_BACKGROUND_LANE_SIZE, walletSync.ts:402) via createLimiter, most-stale-first, with an abort-on-connect-failure guard.
- refreshWalletSnapshot/refreshMultisigSnapshot go through singleFlightThrottled (walletSync.ts:311-346) with THROTTLE_MS=20s, so concurrent dashboard mounts coalesce into one background refresh instead of stacking rescans.
- buildPortfolioAggregate() rebuilds the persisted PortfolioDetail from the per-wallet snapshots the pass already wrote (no second scan pipeline) and writes it via writePortfolioSnapshot (portfolioSnapshot.ts).
- routes/(app)/+page.svelte:91-121 -- client reads the cached snapshot first (instant paint), then fires one POST /api/portfolio/refresh in the background and refetches on success. Response shape (PortfolioDetail) unchanged.
- The bead's 4 folded-in correctness fixes are also already present: assemblePortfolio only records a snapshot when scanned.length === walletCount (portfolio.ts:491, fix 3); buildPortfolioAggregate iterates only listWalletRows/listMultisigs (owned), excluding listSharedMultisigs, for both the chart and backfill attribution (walletSync.ts:729-761, fixes 1+2); tipHeight is Math.max across all per-wallet/multisig snapshot tipHeights rather than a single scan-time lookup (walletSync.ts:734,751, fix 4).

getPortfolioDetail (portfolio.ts:522, the live-scanning, uncapped-Promise.all, interactive-lane function the bead flags) still exists but is dead in production -- its only remaining caller is portfolio.test.ts. Confirmed via grep across src/.

Not done (out of scope for this bead per its own text, needs Alex's sign-off): the scannedCount/'N unreachable' chip copy in +page.svelte:422-424 still reads as real-time reachability, even though scannedCount now means 'has a persisted snapshot' rather than 'responded within 10s this request'. Left untouched -- did not make that copy call unilaterally.

No code changes made. Full suite: npm test -> 2031 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failed. npm run check (svelte-check) -> 0 errors, 0 warnings.

title: parseXpub accepts depth-0 master key without warning
description: No BIP-32 depth validation anywhere in xpub.ts/multisig.ts; importing a master xpub as an account silently exposes the whole seed's watch surface. Privacy/scope risk, no funds-loss. Pinned in hostileXpubs.test.ts on branch test/qa-wave-2026-07-12, worktree C:\dev\cairn-testqa.

title: guard attachMultisigSignature's synchronous atomicity with a comment/lint
description: Track D proved it race-free ONLY because it has zero awaits (sync combine re-reads persisted PSBT); any future await inside (e.g. async sig verification per cairn-anrs) opens a read-modify-write race and must add withLock. Add a warning comment when next touched. Evidence: src/lib/server/concurrencyMultisigRace.test.ts on branch test/qa-wave-2026-07-12, worktree C:\dev\cairn-testqa.

title: Orphaned sign-session roster code (getRoster, RosterMember, RosterStatus) -- wire in or delete
description: src/lib/server/multisigRoster.ts:111,198-234's getRoster() function and RosterMember/RosterStatus types are documented as "what the sign-session view renders" but no route or page calls them anywhere -- the only caller is the function's own test. getSignableMultisig() in wallets/multisig.ts:167 implements a different, simpler per-transaction gate against the same table instead. Looks like a sign-session roster UI that was built but never wired to a route (possibly related to the collaborative-custody work). Fix: either wire getRoster() into the multisig sign-session page, or delete it and its test. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Wired getRoster() into the multisig send page (Unit resolution: 'wire in', not delete). The send loader reconciles the frozen roster against the live PSBT and, for SHARED wallets only (>1 roster member), passes a per-person roster to the sign step, which now renders a 'who signed / who is waiting' list beside the existing per-key chips. Solo wallets unchanged. getRoster/RosterMember are no longer orphaned — they power exactly the collaborative-custody sign-session view they were built for. Typecheck clean.

title: Non-admin invite minting + unauthenticated invite revocation
description: admin/invites/+page.server.ts:12-55 — create action guards only with assertTeamMode (instance-mode check, not identity). Any authenticated non-admin can mint invite codes. revoke action never touches locals at all — fully unauthenticated, can revoke any invite by sequential id (signup DoS). Contrast with correct api/admin/invites/+server.ts which uses requireAdmin. Fix: add requireAdmin(event) to both actions.
close_reason: FIXED. Layer 1 (per-action identity guards, identity-first before assertTeamMode): admin/settings save+testElectrum+testEsplora now 'if(!locals.user?.isAdmin) return fail(403)'; admin/users userAction+setDisabledAction call requireAdmin(event) before any mutation; admin/invites create+revoke call requireAdmin(event). Layer 2 (defense-in-depth backstop): hooks.server.ts isAdminMutationRequest() blocks non-GET/HEAD /admin* from anon(401)/non-admin(403), logged at warn, decodes the path (decodeURI) so /%61dmin bypass is closed. Regression tests: 25 tests across admin/{settings,users,invites}/page.server.test.ts assert anon+non-admin denial AND the mutation mock is never called + positive admin case; hooks.server.test.ts pins the predicate incl. encoded-path cases. Full suite green (1744 pass), npm run check clean. Independent re-audit (19 action files + ~45 /api handlers) confirmed no other missing-auth surface.

title: Admin Overview: skeleton loading placeholders exposed to accessibility tree
description: src/routes/(app)/admin/+page.svelte's nodeLoading state renders literal placeholder digits ('000,000', 'ring 000 forming — 0,000 of 2,016 laid', 'host.example:50002', 'Electrum · public servers') inside elements with the .skeleton CSS class (src/app.css:538). The class visually hides the text (color:transparent, shimmer background) but does NOT set aria-hidden, so screen readers and accessibility-tree tooling read the fake placeholder values as real page content while data.node is still streaming in. Fix: add aria-hidden=true to skeleton placeholder nodes in the hero/backend/server rows.
close_reason: fixed: src/routes/(app)/admin/+page.svelte -- added aria-hidden=true to the 4 skeleton placeholder nodes shown while data.node is streaming in (hero-height '000,000', hero-ring 'ring 000 forming...', Backend 'Electrum · public servers', Server 'host.example:50002'). The .skeleton CSS class (src/app.css:538) already hides these visually (color:transparent, shimmer) but had no aria-hidden, so screen readers / accessibility-tree tools were reading fake placeholder data as real content. Streaming/loading logic itself untouched -- markup-only fix. Verified via admin route test suite (46 passed) + npm run check (0 errors).

title: Rename-recovery migration drops child tables without verifying they're empty too
description:
src/lib/server/db.ts (~lines 193-207): the empty-shell rename-recovery migration (45c8381, a genuine and well-designed fix for a real near-miss data-loss incident -- recovered a live install's 6 multisig wallets, 21 keys, 11 transactions) only checks rowCount('multisigs') === 0 before dropping multisig_keys, multisig_transactions, ledger_multisig_registrations, AND multisigs. The comment claims it 'only ever discards provably-empty tables', but only the parent table's emptiness is independently verified -- the children are assumed empty because the parent is.

In the current schema this is very likely always safe in practice: PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON is set at line 14, well before this migration runs, and the child tables presumably FK-reference multisigs(id), which under normal SQLite operation makes it schema-impossible for a child to have rows while its parent has none. But this is a DROP TABLE -- irreversible -- in a migration whose entire purpose is preventing exactly this kind of data loss, so relying transitively on FK enforcement rather than an explicit, independent emptiness check on each child table is worth tightening. Add the same rowCount(t) === 0 guard to each child table before dropping it, matching the parent's already-explicit check, so the safety claim in the comment is actually true rather than assumed. Low priority -- defense in depth on an already-safe-in-practice path, not a live bug.
close_reason: Fixed in 5a09851: the empty-shell recovery now independently verifies every shell table (parent + all children) is empty before the irreversible DROP, aborting with a warning if any child holds rows while the parent is empty. Comment updated to describe the now-verified behavior.

title: No pino redact config -- add defense-in-depth for secrets/xpubs/PSBTs/mnemonics
description: src/lib/server/logger.ts:184-192's pino() call has no redact option configured at all. The only redaction anywhere (redactPath() in hooks.server.ts) covers only txids/addresses in URL path segments, not log-call metadata objects. No current call site is known to log a secret, but nothing would stop a future logger.error call from writing a live SMTP password, xpub, PSBT, or recovery phrase into data/logs/cairn.log, which /admin/logs then serves over HTTP. Fix: add a redact config covering password/pass/passEnc/token/secret/accessToken/xprv/mnemonic/phrase/psbt style fields as defense-in-depth. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed in 7ebd68c

title: Heartwood app-open confirmation screen shows generic placeholder icon, not the app's actual icon
description: 0.2.15 smoke test, local umbrelOS test env (ghcr.io/getumbrel/umbrelos:1.7.4, heartwood-bitcoin app id, v0.2.14 and v0.2.15 both observed). When opening the app from a fresh browser session, Umbrel's 'Log in with Umbrel' gate (http://<host>:2000/?origin=host&app=heartwood-bitcoin&path=%2F) renders a generic gray placeholder icon (assets/app-icon-placeholder.svg) next to the Umbrel logo instead of Heartwood's actual purple icon. Confirmed reproducible across a fresh page load and a reload. The backing API call GET /v1/apps?app=heartwood-bitcoin returns only {"id":"heartwood-bitcoin","name":"Heartwood"} — no icon field — even though the store manifest (heartwood-app-store/heartwood-bitcoin/umbrel-app.yml) sets icon: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AlexM223/heartwood-app-store/main/heartwood-bitcoin/icon.svg, and that URL is directly reachable (curl 200 OK) from inside the test container. NOT ROOT-CAUSED: could not confirm whether this is Umbrel-core behavior for ALL apps at this pre-auth gate (no other app was installed in the test env to compare), or specific to how this manifest supplies its icon (remote raw-GitHub URL vs. the icon.svg/icon-256.png files that already exist locally in the heartwood-bitcoin store directory but aren't referenced by umbrel-app.yml's icon: field). Screenshot on file (session artifact). Suggest: compare against another installed app's open-gate rendering on a real device, and consider whether umbrel-app.yml should reference a local icon path instead of/in addition to the remote URL.
close_reason: Root-caused to upstream umbrelOS behavior (containers/app-auth's legacy pre-auth login gate, umbrel 1.7.4 source): it ignores manifest.icon entirely and hardcodes a guess at getumbrel's official-store-only gallery URL, so every community-store app shows the gray placeholder at this one gate regardless of manifest correctness. Heartwood's manifest icon: is correct and works everywhere else (main app store, post-login). Nothing to fix in our manifest/store repo. See comment for file:line evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
ROOT-CAUSED via umbrelOS source (github.com/getumbrel/umbrel @ tag 1.7.4, matches test env ghcr.io/getumbrel/umbrelos:1.7.4).

CONCLUSION: (a) Umbrel-core limitation affecting ALL community-store apps at this specific pre-auth gate. Nothing to fix in heartwood-app-store's umbrel-app.yml -- icon: field is correct and already used properly everywhere else.

EVIDENCE:
1. The gate at :2000/?origin=...&app=... is served by the legacy 'app-auth' Express container (containers/app-auth/, wired in packages/umbreld/source/modules/apps/legacy-compat/docker-compose.yml), NOT the modern umbreld/tRPC app-store code.
2. Its GET /v1/apps handler (containers/app-auth/routes/auth.js:105-113) calls appUtils.getBasicInfo(), which is hardcoded to return ONLY {id, name} from the manifest (containers/app-auth/utils/app.js:7-20) -- it never reads manifest.icon at all. This exactly matches our observed API response.
3. The frontend then papers over the missing icon by unconditionally constructing a guessed URL (packages/ui/app-auth/src/login-with-umbrel.tsx:161-164): icon set to `https://getumbrel.github.io/umbrel-apps-gallery/${appId}/icon.svg` whenever appId is present. It ignores manifest.icon entirely and never falls back to it -- there is no null-coalescing to the real value here.
4. getumbrel.github.io/umbrel-apps-gallery is Umbrel's own asset mirror populated only for OFFICIAL getumbrel/umbrel-apps store entries. Community-store apps (like heartwood-bitcoin, from AlexM223/heartwood-app-store) have no directory there, so the guessed URL 404s -> broken image -> AppIcon's gray placeholder fallback. This affects every community-store app uniformly; official apps only "work" here by coincidence (the guessed URL happens to resolve for them).
5. Contrast: the real, authenticated app store/dashboard (packages/umbreld/source/modules/apps/app-repository.ts:177-186) does it correctly -- icon: app.icon with gallery URL only as fallback when the manifest omits icon -- i.e. it prefers the manifest's icon: URL (ours works fine there) and only falls back to the gallery guess when a manifest doesn't define one. So Heartwood's icon renders fine post-login and in the main app store grid; only this one legacy pre-auth screen is broken, and it's broken the same way for every community app.

ACTION: no manifest/store-repo change can fix this (the code path doesn't consult manifest.icon or any local path convention at all, for any app). This is an upstream umbrelOS bug in containers/app-auth. Closing per Alex's rule (close reason: upstream Umbrel behavior, not ours) -- worth an upstream issue/PR to getumbrel/umbrel someday (fix: have auth.js's getBasicInfo also return manifest.icon, and have login-with-umbrel.tsx prefer that value over the gallery guess, same pattern app-repository.ts already uses) but out of scope for this repo.

title: RecipientCombobox renders duplicate DOM ids when multiple instances mount
description: Batch sending mounts one RecipientCombobox per recipient row; each hardcodes id=recipient and id=recipient-listbox internally, producing duplicate DOM ids (invalid HTML, aria-activedescendant ambiguity). Parameterize the ids via a prop (e.g. id={base}-N). Found by the batch-send build agent; functionality currently works.
close_reason: Fixed: RecipientCombobox takes an id prop (default 'recipient') deriving listbox/option ids; send page passes recipient-{row.key} per row and the single-row label targets it. check 0 errors.

title: notified_txids rows orphaned forever: no FK to users/wallets/multisigs, never cleaned on delete or factory reset
description: src/lib/server/db.ts ~L606-616: CREATE TABLE notified_txids has user_id INTEGER NOT NULL and wallet_id INTEGER NOT NULL but NEITHER column has a REFERENCES/FK clause (every other user_id column in db.ts uses REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE). Grepped the whole codebase (addressWatcher.ts is the only writer) and there is no DELETE FROM notified_txids anywhere — not in deleteUser(), not in wallet/multisig deletion paths, not in admin.ts resetInstance(). Effect: every row this table has ever written for a deleted user, wallet, or multisig becomes a permanent orphan. resetInstance() (src/lib/server/admin.ts) explicitly documents itself as a full factory reset ('delete every user, session, wallet, invite and setting') and the Settings UI (admin/settings/+page.svelte) tells the admin 'nothing else survives' — but notified_txids rows survive indefinitely with no cleanup path at all, even outside of factory reset (normal single-user/wallet deletion leaks rows too). Fix: add ON DELETE CASCADE-capable cleanup — either an FK (tricky since wallet_id is polymorphic across wallet_kind) or explicit DELETE FROM notified_txids WHERE user_id = ? calls alongside deleteUser()/wallet deletion/resetInstance().
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Single-sig wallets never persist master_fingerprint/derivation_path — HW signing broken for all single-key wallets
description:
createWallet() (src/lib/server/wallets.ts:138-190) inserts only (user_id, name, type, xpub, script_type, device_type) — master_fingerprint and derivation_path (both declared in WalletRow, real DB columns) are never written, by ANY creation path, including the real hardware-connect flow: readSingleSigKeyFromTrezor() (src/lib/hw/trezor.ts:1346+) correctly reads {xpub, fingerprint, path} from the device, but src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte only captures d.xpub into validatedXpub — fingerprint/path are discarded, and the submitted form only sends xpub + deviceType. setWalletDevice() (wallets.ts:197) likewise only ever touches device_type.

Consequence: constructPsbt() (src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts:509) only emits bip32Derivation when params.origin is given, and src/lib/server/transactions.ts:299 only builds origin when wallet.master_fingerprint is non-null — which is NEVER true for a single-key wallet. Every exported/signed PSBT for a single-sig wallet therefore has no bip32Derivation. Cairn's own signPsbtWithTrezor() explicitly requires it and throws 'This transaction is missing the key-origin information the Trezor needs to sign. Re-create it from a wallet set up with its master fingerprint.' (trezor.ts ~line 176). The same requirement almost certainly applies to the Ledger/BitBox02/Jade signers (same PSBT, same guard pattern).

Reproduced live on the v0.1.3 Umbrel instance (http://umbrel.local:3211): confirmed via source read plus a live download+decode of a real 'QA Trezor (regtest)' wallet's unsigned PSBT (src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/transactions/[txId]/file/+server.ts output) — the input has witnessUtxo/nonWitnessUtxo but no bip32Derivation field at all.

Note: multisig keys (multisig_keys table / MultisigSignKey) DO store and validate fingerprint correctly (resolveMultisigSignKeys in trezor.ts requires it) — this bug is specific to the single-key  table/createWallet() path.

This may be a very recent regression: today's (2026-07-06) wallet-dedup refactor (see project memory 'cairn-wallet-dedup-refactor') left 'Ledger/BitBox/Jade signer pairs still unmerged' per its own notes — worth checking whether single-sig fingerprint capture regressed in that refactor or predates it.

Fix shape: thread {fingerprint, path} from the device-read result through the wallet-creation form (hidden inputs) and into createWallet()'s INSERT (and the paste-xpub path should leave both null, as today, since there's no device to trust).
close_reason: Duplicate of cairn-alw8 -- identical root cause (single-key wallets never persist master_fingerprint/derivation_path, breaking all HW signing), filed independently in another concurrent QA session against the same live instance (same pattern as cairn-tu0i, already closed as a dup of alw8 for this exact issue). See cairn-alw8 for the canonical writeup.

title: Add Dockerfile, compose file, and deployment docs
description:
No containerization exists at all — no Dockerfile, no docker-compose.yml. Self-hosters (this app's entire audience) commonly expect a Docker path, especially for Umbrel-style one-click deployment. Needs:
- Multi-stage Dockerfile (Node 22.5+ base per package.json engines, adapter-node production build)
- docker-compose.yml with a documented volume mount for CAIRN_DB persistence (currently ./data/cairn.db; a container restart without a mounted volume silently loses all users/wallets/invites)
- 'start' script in package.json (currently missing — only dev/build/preview exist; production convention is npm start -> node build)
- README 'Deployment' section covering the Docker path, the CAIRN_DB volume requirement explicitly, and documenting the full set of environment variables adapter-node/the app respects (PORT, CAIRN_DB, and whatever adapter-node exposes — HOST, SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT etc. — currently undocumented beyond a vague mention of two of them).
close_reason: Implemented in 393e119: multi-stage node:22-alpine Dockerfile (non-root, CAIRN_DB=/data, PORT, ADDRESS_HEADER=x-forwarded-for for the rate limiter, HEALTHCHECK, VOLUME /data), docker-compose.yml with documented ./data bind mount and data-loss warning, npm start script, README Deployment section incl. env table, proxy header trust caveat, and SSE buffering note. Verified beyond inspection: docker build succeeded, container ran healthy as non-root, /api/health 200 from host, compose config validates

title: Missing regression test: removing a contact doesn't revoke their multisig wallet shares (cairn-2oex)
description: cairn-2oex (P1, closed in 016acf2) fixed contact removal not revoking a contact's existing multisig wallet shares — a real access-control gap (a removed contact could retain viewer/cosigner access). Fix confirmed present, no test exists. Add a test to src/lib/server/contacts.test.ts: create a contact, share a multisig wallet with them (viewer or cosigner role with an assigned key), call removeContact(), and assert the multisig_shares row is gone / assigned_user_id is cleared and the (former) contact's access role for that multisig is now null.
close_reason: Committed in 8603ac6: multisigShares.test.ts (first coverage), contacts.test.ts share-revocation, notifications.test.ts stage isolation, broadcast route malformed-JSON 400 + log.error tests, walletsDraft.test.ts buildDraft/coin-control coverage.

title: hw_* feature flags don't gate the wallet-creation wizard's device picker (Key step)
description:
QA pass for bead cairn-1ytc (feature-flag matrix, my batch: hw_trezor/hw_ledger/hw_coldcard/hw_bitbox02/hw_jade).

BUG: turning off any hw_* flag in /admin/feature-flags has NO effect on the primary hardware-wallet entry point: the single-sig wallet creation wizard's Key step (/wallets/new, step "KEY").

Root cause: src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte defines its own METHOD_CARDS array (lines ~258-266: trezor/ledger/coldcard/bitbox02/jade/qr/paste) which is rendered unconditionally at lines ~791-826 ({#each METHOD_CARDS as m}) with NO feature-flag field and NO filter against page.data.flags. This is a SEPARATE device list from the flag-gated one in src/lib/components/DevicePicker.svelte (which correctly filters via `!o.flag || page.data.flags?.[o.flag] !== false` and is only used later, on the Finish step's "change device" sub-flow).

There is also no server-side enforcement: src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.server.ts reads `deviceType` from the submitted form (line ~72) and persists it with no requireFeature(event, `hw_${deviceType}`) check, so even a hand-crafted POST bypassing the client entirely would still succeed.

Net effect: an admin can toggle e.g. hw_trezor OFF, and a user (including a non-admin) can still pick "Trezor" as their key source in step 1 of wallet creation, fully unobstructed — the flag is silently inert for this surface.

REPRO (verified live in browser, both admin qa-matrix@test.local and plain user qa-matrix-user@test.local sessions, server on data/qa-matrix-b.db):
1. /admin/feature-flags -> toggle "Trezor" (hw_trezor) to Disable.
2. Navigate to /wallets/new.
3. Step 1 "KEY" still shows a fully-enabled "Trezor" card with description "Plug it in and connect with one click." and a "Buy one ->" referral link, identical to the enabled state.
4. Confirmed identically for Ledger/Coldcard/BitBox02/Jade after toggling hw_ledger/hw_coldcard/hw_bitbox02/hw_jade off together.
5. No console errors either way — this is a pure missing-gate, not a crash.
6. Toggled all 5 flags back on, confirmed admin UI reflects "Disable X" (enabled) state again.

FIX: filter METHOD_CARDS the same way DevicePicker.svelte does (add a `flag` field per entry, filter against page.data.flags in the {#each}), and add a requireFeature(event, `hw_${deviceType}`) guard in +page.server.ts's create action (only for device-sourced deviceType values; 'file'/paste stays ungated per registry.ts's userMessage design).

Evidence: HEARTWOOD_DB=data/qa-matrix-b.db, seeded via scripts/qa/seed-flagmatrix.mjs, dev server on port 5315, browser session isolated via http://qamatrixb.localhost:5315 (distinct *.localhost subdomain) to avoid httpOnly cairn_session collisions with other concurrent QA sessions sharing the bare localhost cookie domain.

title: Missing regression test: agreement page reserved-action crash (cairn-z6i1)
description: cairn-z6i1 (P0, closed in 46dd16f) fixed /agreement crashing with a 500 because a Svelte form action was named 'default', a reserved name. Fix confirmed still present in current code. No test exists guarding against this class of regression. Add a test that POSTs to the agreement page's accept action (or a route-level test on /agreement) and asserts a 200/redirect rather than a 500 — this would catch a future accidental rename back to a reserved action name. Filed from the 2026-07-06 process retrospective (docs/PROCESS-RETROSPECTIVE-2026-07-06.md), which found only 6% of bug fixes added a regression test.

title: Wallet wizard Step-1 'Paste public key' CTA sits below the fold at common 1280x800 desktop size
description:
On /wallets/new (single-sig wallet creation wizard, Step 1), the method-card grid (Trezor / Ledger / ColdCard / BitBox02 / Jade / Air-gapped QR / Paste public key) extends past the viewport bottom at a common desktop size (1280x800, viewport content area ~1280x720 under the app chrome). Measured via getBoundingClientRect(): the "Paste public key" card -- the ONLY option that doesn't require owning a hardware signing device, and the one the page's own copy recommends for "No hardware wallet yet?" -- sits at y=785-873, fully below the 720px-tall viewport, with no visible scroll affordance/indicator in the initial view.

Why it matters: a first-time, non-technical user without a hardware wallet is the exact audience this card is for, and the page gives no hint that more options exist below the fold (no visible partial card, no scroll cue). They may conclude their only options are hardware devices they don't own and abandon wallet creation. This also made the button materially harder to interact with in this QA pass (see cairn-61bh for the separate, more severe finding that clicks on these buttons don't work at all once reached).

Repro: navigate to /wallets/new at 1280x800, do not scroll -- "Paste public key" card is not visible or only barely visible at the very bottom edge.

Suggested fix: reorder so "Paste public key" (the no-hardware-required option) appears first/above the fold, or reduce vertical spacing of the method grid, or add a visible scroll cue.

title: Backup restore + account-reclaim chain allows registration-lockdown bypass to full admin
description:
Security audit 2026-07-05 (docs/SECURITY-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md), finding F7. Verified CONFIRMED by 3/3 independent adversarial reviewers.

Two independently-reasonable pieces of code chain into a full privilege escalation:

1. src/lib/server/backup.ts restoreBackup() (~lines 182-199): imports each user row from the decrypted backup file and writes u.is_admin verbatim with NO cap check against adminCount() and no distinct callout in RestoreSummary or the admin/restore route's log line. Imported rows have zero credentials by design (backups never carry credentials).

2. src/routes/api/auth/register/options/+server.ts (~lines 26-40) and register/verify/+server.ts (~lines 61-79): the account-reclaim path (reclaimableUserId() finds a credential-less user row) completely bypasses assertCanRegister() -- i.e. skips registrationMode (closed/invite/open) and invite-code checks entirely, by design, so a legitimate account can be reclaimed via passkey registration without an invite even when the instance is locked down.

Chain: attacker crafts/tampers with a backup file containing a row {email: attacker-controlled, is_admin: 1, no credentials}. An admin is social-engineered into restoring it via the admin-gated /api/admin/restore endpoint (looks like normal maintenance -- no preview of which imported rows are privileged). The attacker then visits the normal signup screen with that email; reclaimableUserId finds the credential-less admin row and routes around assertCanRegister entirely, regardless of registrationMode='closed'. Attacker completes a WebAuthn passkey ceremony with their own device, register/verify attaches the credential and immediately opens a live admin session. No invite code, no confirmation step, no admin-count cap, and no distinct audit-log/notification callout anywhere in the path (the only signup-notification, admin_new_signup, is never called on the reclaim branch).

Recommended fix (per verifier consensus):
- restoreBackup should force imported users' is_admin to 0, OR cap/require explicit admin re-confirmation for any is_admin=true row on import.
- The reclaim path should still honor registrationMode/invite-code for any account carrying is_admin=1, or require an additional out-of-band confirmation for admin-account reclaim.
- Fire a distinct, non-suppressible notification/audit entry whenever a restore imports an is_admin=1 row, and whenever any reclaim (not just admin) completes.
close_reason: Fixed in security-audit remediation pass; see commit. All 897 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: Unconfirmed tx detail page has no explicit confirmation count or speed-up CTA
description: Block-explorer elevation audit (2026-07-12): src/routes/(app)/explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.svelte (lines ~184-251) shows a burial-ring label + a fee-outlook sentence for an unconfirmed tx, but no explicit 'N of 6 confirmations' count and no bump/accelerate CTA, despite a fee_bumping feature already existing elsewhere in the app. Best-in-class: mempool.space puts an Accelerate button right next to the confirmation ETA. Fix: for an unconfirmed tx the viewing user owns, add a 'Speed this up' link into the existing RBF/CPFP send flow, and add an explicit confirmations count next to the burial-ring glyph. Related to (but distinct from) the UX-Elevation Blueprint's P0-3/P1-10 (plain confirmation-status wording on BurialRings labels generally) -- that's the wording fix; this is the missing count + missing CTA specifically on the Explorer tx-detail page. title: Federation (instance-to-instance PSBT coordination) — scoping epic
description: Scoping epic for Heartwood federation: instance-to-instance PSBT coordination for collaborative multisig custody. One Heartwood sends a PSBT to another for signing; peer discovery (invite codes/manual/Tor TBD); signature collection + broadcast; real-time cosigner notifications. NON-CUSTODIAL: each instance holds only its own keys; no e-cash, no Fedimint, no Lightning gateway. Reference model: Bastion's federation protocol (C:\dev\bastion\src\server\federation.ts + docs/COLLABORATIVE-CUSTODY-AUDIT.md). DESIGN CONSTRAINT: UI-first, never API-only (Bastion's lesson). Deliverable: docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md. Child beads labeled federation-scope.

NOTE: MobileTabRow only shows 4 destinations -- decide whether Federation replaces one, folds under Wallets, or extends the strip; document the choice in the bead when implementing. DESIGN CONSTRAINT (epic cairn-cz3q): UI-first -- the tab ships with real UI, never API-only. Full context: docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md.

title: DECISION: backup/export policy for federation e-cash secrets (bearer instruments)
description: Open product decision, blocks federation wallet implementation. Cairn's backup.ts (src/lib/server/backup.ts, BackupData lines 37-49) and accountData.ts deliberately exclude instance_secrets from backups -- safe for xpub-based wallets (nothing spendable is lost), but Fedimint e-cash notes/client seed ARE the funds (bearer instruments). Excluding them means backup != recoverable funds for federation balances, unlike every other wallet type. Options to evaluate: (a) include encrypted federation secrets in backup with explicit user consent + passphrase, (b) rely on Fedimint seed-based recovery from the federation itself (verify recovery guarantees first), (c) hybrid. Needs Alex's call. Also decide per-user/per-federation secrets storage: instance_secrets is a global k/v store and does not fit; likely new table federation_secrets(user_id, federation_id, key, value_enc) reusing encryptSecret/decryptSecret from src/lib/server/secretKey.ts with a new HKDF info label (structural template: src/lib/server/deviceKeys.ts).
close_reason: obsolete — scope changed 2026-07-12, Fedimint dropped; federation = inter-instance PSBT coordination (Bastion model)

title: DECISION: fedimint client packaging -- sidecar container vs embedded client
description: Open architecture decision, blocks most federation implementation beads. Cairn is a single Node 22 process with no child-process/sidecar precedent; its pattern for external services is probing sibling Umbrel apps over LAN (umbrelProbe.ts / umbrelCoreProbe.ts, hooks.server.ts init lines ~165-192). Options: (a) fedimint-clientd (REST/JSON-RPC daemon, github.com/fedimint/fedimint-clientd -- alive, default modules only) as a separate container -- either a second container in the heartwood-bitcoin Umbrel package or a dependency app; (b) @fedimint/core (official Fedimint SDK npm package, WASM -- formerly @fedimint/core-web) embedded in the SvelteKit server IF Node.js server-side support is verified (claimed 'multiple JS runtimes' since Oct 2025 rename, unproven -- needs a spike); (c) browser-side @fedimint/core (client state in IndexedDB per browser -- conflicts with Cairn's server-side multi-user model). Research verdict pending in docs/FEDERATION-SCOPE.md. Deciding this fixes where client lifecycle code lives (hooks.server.ts watcher vs container orchestration in heartwood-app-store repo).
close_reason: obsolete — scope changed 2026-07-12, Fedimint dropped; federation = inter-instance PSBT coordination (Bastion model)

title: Parallelize getTx's outspends fetch + stream stateless-signer page's fees
description:
Scoped by Fable consultation as the actual remainder of cairn-xlrm after
auditing every page route (see cairn-xlrm comment). Two small, independent
fixes:

1. src/lib/server/chain/index.ts:316-330, ChainService.getTx(): tx+tipHeight
   already fetch in Promise.all, but getTxOutspends() is awaited SEQUENTIALLY
   afterward in its own try/catch. This is the only awaited-on-paint chain
   call left in the app (explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.server.ts:76, deliberately
   awaited per cairn-2zxt.3 since it drives a 302-vs-404 routing decision) --
   folding outspends into the same Promise.all (with the existing
   catch-to-undefined degradation preserved) cuts it from 2 sequential esplora
   HTTP round trips to 1, on a Pi where each hop to a co-resident mempool
   instance can be 100ms-1s+ under load. Also speeds up api/tx/[txid] and
   explorer's classifySearch (which calls full getTx just to test existence).
   Update chain.test.ts mocks if any assert call ordering.

2. src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/stateless/+page.server.ts:15-20: fees
   are awaited synchronously (`fees = await getChain().getFeeEstimates()`),
   the last load in the app gating paint on a chain call it doesn't need --
   every other page with a fee estimate already streams it. Copy the send
   page's existing pattern exactly (src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte
   has a local `fees = $state<FeeEstimates | null>(null)` populated from an
   $effect that awaits the streamed promise): return
   `fees: getChain().getFeeEstimates().catch(() => null)` unawaited from the
   server load, and adapt +page.svelte's `data.fees?.economy` /
   `data.fees?.halfHour` / `data.fees?.fastest` / `{#if !data.fees}` references
   (lines ~176-182, 662, 694) to read from a local $state var populated the
   same way the send page does it.

Verified non-issues, don't re-investigate: ElectrumPool size (round-robin
dispatch, no checkout/exhaustion possible), the 15s connection timeout
(abandoned requests don't block new writes), and every OTHER page route
(already fully covered by the cairn-vknb streaming pass).

After landing, close cairn-xlrm referencing this bead + the premise-drift
note already on it.
close_reason: done

title: address_book flag OFF doesn't hide saved-address UI in send flow
description:
Found during cairn-1ytc flag-matrix QA pass (wallet-flags subset, port 5314, HEARTWOOD_DB=data/qa-matrix.db).

REPRO: Admin -> Feature flags -> toggle Address book OFF (address_book flag). As a plain user, visit /wallets/[id]/send. The RecipientCombobox still renders normally with no flag-off messaging; GET /api/address-book correctly 403s (server enforcement intact), but nothing on the client hides or disables the saved-address autocomplete/save-new-address UI.

ROOT CAUSE (code-read confirmed):
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.server.ts:167 calls `savedAddresses: listSavedAddresses(locals.user!.id)` unconditionally in load() -- no `requireFeature(event, 'address_book')` guard anywhere in this file (only `requireFeature(event, 'send')` at line 126).
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:162 sets `savedAddresses = data.savedAddresses` and passes it straight into <RecipientCombobox saved={savedAddresses} .../> (lines ~912, ~949) with no `data.flags?.address_book` check anywhere in the file.
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/_components/RecipientCombobox.svelte has a proper flag gate for qr_scan (`const flagEnabled = page.data.flags?.qr_scan !== false;` at line 144) but NO equivalent check for address_book anywhere -- the saved-address list and (per code at +page.svelte:650, `savedAddresses = [body.address as SavedAddress, ...savedAddresses]`) the save-new-address affordance are never gated client-side.

Same pattern likely affects the multisig send flow (src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/send/+page.svelte) -- not yet checked, worth a look when fixing.

IMPACT: with address_book turned off instance-wide, a user with existing saved addresses would still see and be able to select them from the send-flow autocomplete (the data only isn't reachable via the API if they navigate to a dedicated address-book page/route). This is the same class of regression the parent bead cairn-1ytc calls out ("History shows this area regresses: cairn-8dup, cairn-1x3w, cairn-jyh7") -- UI not following the flag even though the API is correctly gated.

FIX (not done here -- flagged only, owned by another session per current branch ownership): add a `data.flags?.address_book` check in +page.svelte (and the +page.server.ts load, mirroring the coin_control pattern at +page.svelte:1003) to hide the saved-address list/autocomplete and any save-new-address control when the flag is off, plus check the equivalent multisig send flow file.

Filed by: OPS QA session (cairn-1ytc wallet-flags subset), 2026-07-12.
