close_reason: FIXED. Layer 1 (per-action identity guards, identity-first before assertTeamMode): admin/settings save+testElectrum+testEsplora now 'if(!locals.user?.isAdmin) return fail(403)'; admin/users userAction+setDisabledAction call requireAdmin(event) before any mutation; admin/invites create+revoke call requireAdmin(event). Layer 2 (defense-in-depth backstop): hooks.server.ts isAdminMutationRequest() blocks non-GET/HEAD /admin* from anon(401)/non-admin(403), logged at warn, decodes the path (decodeURI) so /%61dmin bypass is closed. Regression tests: 25 tests across admin/{settings,users,invites}/page.server.test.ts assert anon+non-admin denial AND the mutation mock is never called + positive admin case; hooks.server.test.ts pins the predicate incl. encoded-path cases. Full suite green (1744 pass), npm run check clean. Independent re-audit (19 action files + ~45 /api handlers) confirmed no other missing-auth surface.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Fable (review-2026-07-07): same file as cairn-jnlx, same missing-guard root cause. testElectrum/testEsplora have neither identity nor mode gate -> unauthenticated SSRF oracle reachable on solo (default Umbrel) too. Fixed together with cairn-jnlx in the same change (add fail(403) admin check to save/testElectrum/testEsplora).

title: Missing regression test: resetInstance() leaves instance-wide events behind (cairn-5s8y / cairn-rko3)
description: cairn-5s8y and cairn-rko3 (P1, duplicate beads for the identical bug, both closed in 46dd16f) fixed resetInstance() (factory reset) leaving instance-wide events (user_id IS NULL) behind, contradicting the Settings UI's 'nothing else survives' claim. Fix confirmed present, no test exists. Add a test to src/lib/server/admin.test.ts: seed both an instance-wide event (user_id NULL) and a user-scoped event, call resetInstance(), and assert both are gone afterward.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 834945e (admin.test.ts, notifiedTxidsCleanup.test.ts, settings.test.ts, api/admin/{settings,users,notifications}/server.test.ts). All green in full suite.

title: No dedicated mempool page with fee distribution histogram
description:
The Dashboard and Explorer pages both show mempool summary stats (unconfirmed tx count, mempool fees total, mempool size in MB, and a 4-tier recommended-fee table: Fastest/Half hour/Hour/Economy), but there is no dedicated /mempool page with a visual fee-distribution histogram (e.g. sat/vB buckets vs. vsize, the classic mempool.space-style chart). Confirmed via route glob and a direct 404 on GET /mempool.

This may be intentional/deferred scope rather than a bug, but flagging since the original test plan called out "mempool page — fee distribution, recommended fees" as an expected surface. Recommended fees ARE present and look correct; only the distribution visualization is missing.
close_reason: Fixed in 0046d11: /explorer/mempool with 7-band fee distribution, projected next blocks, contextual fee recommendations, and 2h backlog sparkline, linked from the explorer mempool strip

title: SSE new-block handler over-invalidates (re-scans whole portfolio every block)
description: The new live-updates feature (src/routes/(app)/+page.svelte, explorer/+page.svelte, explorer/mempool/+page.svelte) calls invalidateAll() on every new-block SSE event, which re-runs every load function on the page — on the dashboard that means re-scanning every wallet in the user's portfolio (see the related sequential-scan bug) on every single new block, whether or not portfolio data could plausibly have changed. Prefer targeted invalidate(url) for just the chain-tip/mempool data, or split load functions so portfolio isn't coupled to block-tip refresh. Depends conceptually on the portfolio N+1 fix but is a separate root cause (over-broad invalidation, not just slow scanning).
close_reason: Fixed in 6f1ce4f: chain loads declare a cairn:chain dependency and SSE handlers call invalidate('cairn:chain') instead of invalidateAll; the portfolio moved out of the dashboard load entirely (client-fetched from /api/portfolio), so new blocks refresh tip/mempool data without touching wallet scans

title: Recovery phrase (12-word, account-only)
description: On passkey add: generate 12-word BIP39-wordlist phrase (SAME wordlist as Bitcoin, but ACCOUNT recovery — copy must scream the distinction: 'This phrase recovers your Cairn account if you lose all your passkeys. It does NOT access your bitcoin — your bitcoin keys are on your hardware wallet.'). Shown ONCE on a write-this-down screen. Stored as salted hash only (scrypt like passwords; never plaintext). Recovery flow: enter 12 words -> hash-verify -> prompt new passkey registration -> access restored. Admin gets it automatically during first passkey setup.
close_reason: Shipped (fbb7efd): 12-word BIP39-wordlist ACCOUNT recovery phrase, salted-scrypt hashed, reusable, normalized (NFKD/case/spacing). Account-vs-bitcoin distinction hammered in every screen. 29 recovery tests.

title: Collaborative-custody Units 1-2 shipped correctly-written but entirely unwired gates — Unit 3 must migrate routes AND thread redaction through, not just swap getMultisig calls
description: Verified commit d7b915b (collaborative custody Units 1-2) is exactly as scoped: getViewableMultisig/getSignableMultisig (src/lib/server/wallets/multisig.ts:135,155) and redactMultisigKeysForViewer (src/lib/server/multisigShares.ts:251) are all correctly implemented (parameterized queries, correctly-scoped EXISTS subqueries, proper 404-not-403 shape, correct path-redaction logic) but have ZERO callers anywhere in src/routes — confirmed via repo-wide grep. Every existing route under src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/** and src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/** (detail page, psbt, transactions, transactions/[txId], broadcast, file download, descriptor/caravan/coldcard export, history.csv, address-detail, receive, utxo-mass, keys/[keyId]/verified — ~17+ call sites) still calls the old owner-only getMultisig(userId,id), including all of src/lib/server/multisigTransactions.ts's exports (getMultisigTransaction, listMultisigTransactions, buildMultisigDraft, attachMultisigSignature, broadcastMultisigTransaction, deleteMultisigTransaction). listSharedMultisigs (multisigShares.ts:199, meant to surface shared wallets on a collaborator's own /wallets list) is also never called. Net effect today: fails CLOSED, not open — a viewer/cosigner gets a uniform 404 everywhere and cannot see or sign a wallet shared with them at all (the feature is currently non-functional for its intended purpose), and there is no live data leak. This is expected/in-scope per the commit's own message ('Units 1-2 only') and the plan's unit breakdown (Units 3/5 are separate, not-yet-started units) — not a regression. Filing this specifically to flag the landmine for whoever does Unit 3: migrating a route from getMultisig to getViewableMultisig/getSignableMultisig is NOT sufficient on its own — every response that includes the key list (detail page, descriptor/caravan/coldcard export, history.csv, any API response embedding keys) must ALSO be threaded through redactMultisigKeysForViewer for non-cosigner viewers, per docs/COLLABORATIVE-CUSTODY-PLAN.md §6's own explicit warning to 'audit every such response shape explicitly rather than assuming one central serialization point catches all of them.' toMultisigConfig (multisig.ts:183), the shared funnel used by export/detail code, currently maps keys straight through with no redaction hook — this is the specific chokepoint to add it to, or every consuming route must redact individually with a test proving it.
close_reason: Verified fully resolved by commit 297ba2d (cairn-xkpd): all 17+ multisig routes migrated to getViewableMultisig/getSignableMultisig; exports (caravan/coldcard/descriptor/address-detail) signable-gated so cosigners get full keys; viewer-facing key responses (detail page + API detail) threaded through redactMultisigKeysForViewer; viewer-only routes (history.csv/receive/utxo-mass) emit no keys. Non-participants get uniform 404. Redaction chokepoint concern addressed.

comments.author: auditor
comments.text: Update: commit 63dddd6 ('Unit 4: sign-session roster + notify at creation and each signature') landed on top of the still-broken access gate this bead describes — it does NOT fix it (confirmed: per the plan's own unit table, the route-gate rewiring is Unit 3's job, not Unit 4's, so this isn't a missed dependency, just Unit 3 still not started). New concrete symptom now live: freezeRosterAndNotify() correctly identifies a shared multisig's cosigners and fires a sign_session_waiting notification with a working-looking link to /wallets/multisig/{id}/send?tx={txId} — but every route that link leads to (the send page, transactions API, PSBT/broadcast endpoints) still gates through owner-only ownedMultisig()/getMultisig(), so the notified cosigner gets a 404 everywhere. Users can now be notified about a signature they have no way to actually provide. Also: getRoster() (meant to expose the reconciled waiting/signed split to a UI per the commit message) has zero callers anywhere outside its own test file — the UI-facing half of Unit 4 wasn't built either. Separately confirmed as a non-issue: the notification payload itself only carries {multisigId, txId, collected, required}, no amount/destination, and assignKeys() already prevents a viewer from ever holding an assigned key — so no information disclosure beyond the 3-tier model's intent, just the reachability gap this bead already tracks.

title: Umbrel App Store icon has blurry/transparent background — needs solid bg
description:
The Heartwood (Cairn) app icon shown on the Umbrel App Store dashboard has a blurry/transparent background, which looks bad next to other apps' tiles (Gitea, Jellyfin, Home Assistant all have clean solid backgrounds on their icons).

Fix: give the icon a solid background instead of transparency -- either solid black (#100D0B, matching this app's own page background and PWA manifest background_color/theme_color) or the dark charcoal from the Heartwood palette. The ring mark should read crisply against a clean solid tile, consistent with how other Umbrel apps present.

Note: this app's own PWA icons (static/icons/icon-192.png, icon-512.png, referenced from static/manifest.json in this repo) are already fully opaque with a #100D0B background baked in -- verified via pixel inspection, alpha=255 everywhere, corner pixels match #100D0B exactly. So this bug is NOT in this repo; the icon Umbrel's App Store actually displays lives in the separate Umbrel packaging repo (caravan-store-cairn, per prior work -- not present in this checkout) and needs the same solid-background treatment applied there.

Repro: open the Umbrel App Store / installed-apps dashboard and compare the Heartwood tile against Gitea/Jellyfin/Home Assistant.
close_reason: icon-512.png and icon-192.png flattened from RGBA to RGB with solid #100D0B background using pngjs. manifest.json and app.html already correct.

title: docs/API.md documents wrong path for password registration endpoint
description: docs/API.md:38 documents 'POST /api/auth/register' as the registration endpoint. The actual route (src/routes/api/auth/register/password/+server.ts) is 'POST /api/auth/register/password'. Hitting the documented path 404s (confirmed via curl: POST /api/auth/register -> 404, POST /api/auth/register/password -> 201). Also src/routes/api/auth/register/options/+server.ts exists (passkey registration options) with no corresponding doc entry. Fix: update API.md to the correct path(s), and consider documenting the passkey registration flow (options + verify) alongside the password flow.
close_reason: Fixed in 779a374

title: Bitcoin Core RPC password round-trips to the client in plaintext
description: src/lib/server/settings.ts getInstanceSettings() returns coreRpcPass in plaintext. This flows straight into: (1) src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts load(), which SvelteKit serializes into the SSR page payload and (2) src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte, which pre-fills a type=password input with value={data.settings.coreRpcPass}. It's also returned as plaintext JSON from GET /api/admin/settings (src/routes/api/admin/settings/+server.ts). Anyone with view-source/devtools access to an authenticated admin session (or anything that logs the page payload/API response) can read the stored Core RPC credential. Fix: never send the stored secret back to the client — return a boolean 'has value' flag or a masked placeholder instead, and only overwrite the stored value when the admin submits a non-empty new one.
close_reason: Fixed in cb72bcc: introduced getPublicInstanceSettings() replacing the stored Core RPC password with a hasCoreRpcPass flag; admin settings load, page markup, and GET /api/admin/settings no longer serialize the secret; blank submissions keep the stored value, with an explicit clear path

title: All destination address types + P2SH/P2SH-P2WSH vault scripts
description: Two halves: (1) verify+test sends to all six destination types (P2PKH 1.., P2SH 3.., P2SH-P2WPKH 3.., P2WPKH bc1q.., P2WSH bc1q.., P2TR bc1p..) from single-sig constructPsbt — addressToScriptPubKey in xpub.ts already decodes bech32m; needs explicit per-type construction tests incl. taproot output + addressFromScript p2tr reverse mapping for summaries. (2) extend multisig.ts VaultConfig with scriptType 'p2wsh'|'p2sh-p2wsh'|'p2sh' (default p2wsh, the recommended type): sh(sortedmulti) and sh(wsh(sortedmulti)) derivation + descriptor build/parse, BIP-48 script suffix 1 for both P2SH forms (Bastion convention). Taproot multisig explicitly out of scope. Emulator bead cairn-a4k must include a bc1p send.
close_reason: Shipped in two commits: six-destination verification with per-type tests (found+fixed flat output-vsize fee undercount on sweeps to p2wsh/p2tr and uppercase-bech32 client rejection; witness-v1 32-byte rule was already present and is now pinned), and P2SH/P2SH-P2WSH vault script types with per-type descriptor round-trips. 36 new tests; 422/422 passing, check 0 errors.

title: Address watcher subscriptions leak — never removed, no unsubscribe RPC
description: addressWatcher.ts:645-700 — refreshWatches only adds scripthashes to state.byScripthash, never removes. deleteWallet/deleteMultisig clean up DB but never touch watcher. No blockchain.scripthash.unsubscribe method exists in electrum/client.ts. resubscribe() (client.ts:338-347) replays entire accumulated scripthashSubs set serially on every reconnect, so reconnect cost grows with cumulative wallet churn, not current wallet count. On flaky ARM/Umbrel connectivity this compounds badly. Fix: add removal path diffing desired vs byScripthash, wire to wallet/multisig delete, implement unsubscribe RPC.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Phase 1 landed in acc0b69 (shared with cairn-uzgu): the LOCAL-STATE leak is fixed — refreshWatches now diffs desired-vs-current and prunes byScripthash/baselinedScripthashes/inFlight, wired to deleteWallet/deleteMultisig via unwatchWallet/unwatchMultisig, plus a walletStillExists notify-guard. REMAINING (Phase 2, keep open): (1) no blockchain.scripthash.unsubscribe RPC in electrum/client.ts so the server-side subscription persists until the socket cycles; (2) resubscribe() still replays the entire accumulated scripthashSubs set serially on every reconnect, so reconnect cost grows with cumulative churn (the ARM/Umbrel pain point). Needs the unsubscribe RPC + pruning scripthashSubs on unwatch. Deferred to avoid colliding with cairn-vn48's client.ts change.

title: Invite-gated signup offers no in-app path -- copy just says 'ask for a code'
description:
Evidence: dev instance 2026-07-12, when registration_mode is 'invite' and a user has no code, the signup page's guidance is just 'ask for a code' (per cairn-keo's fix: 'Invites come from whoever runs this Cairn instance -- ask them for a code.') with no in-app mechanism to actually request one -- no request-access form, no contact surfacing beyond the generic hint, no way to leave a message for the admin. Product decision needed on whether to build a request-access flow (e.g. a lightweight form that notifies the admin) or whether better static guidance (configurable admin contact info) is sufficient.

Searched for overlap first: cairn-keo (CLOSED) added the 'ask for a code' hint text itself but explicitly did not build a request/contact mechanism -- its close comment even floats 'consider surfacing an optional instance name/contact field' as a maybe, not built. cairn-wt0.2 (First-run experience, P2 feature) is a broader onboarding epic-adjacent bead but doesn't specifically address invite-request flow. Searched 'request access', 'ask for a code', 'request-access' -- no hits. Filing as a new idea/product-decision bead, not a bug, since this is a scope/design question rather than a defect.

title: RBF replacement-history link to a replaced tx dead-ends at a generic 404
description:
Page: /explorer/tx/[txid] — Replacement history section on an RBF-replacing transaction.

What I did: pulled a live RBF replacement pair from mempool.space's public /api/v1/replacements feed (a genuinely still-in-mempool transaction that had replaced an earlier version of itself). Viewed the current version in Cairn at /explorer/tx/5b0666d8d1b9b79cfc8300c16c838af13805578d94219e6e74d4b1414336c788 — its "REPLACEMENT HISTORY" section correctly showed both versions with timestamps: the old txid (e1e82ceb91375fbf4f6736458de83f37b052e2a924383b498b8d235efed14a23, "1m ago") and the current one ("you are here / current version", "41s ago"). Then clicked the old txid's link in that history list.

What happened: the link points to /explorer/tx/e1e82ceb91375fbf4f6736458de83f37b052e2a924383b498b8d235efed14a23, which renders Cairn's generic 404 page ("This path doesn't lead anywhere. Back to dashboard / Open the explorer") — because a replaced transaction is evicted from mempool.space's/Electrum's mempool the moment it's replaced, so there's genuinely nowhere left to fetch it from. Confirmed by navigating to that URL directly as well (same 404).

What should have happened: since the very page that renders this link already has all the context needed (the replaced txid, when it was replaced, and which tx replaced it), the replaced tx's own detail page — or the link itself — should acknowledge that specific situation instead of a generic dead-end. Two reasonable fixes: (a) render the replaced txid as plain text (not a link) in the history list, since it's not a destination that will ever resolve, or (b) have the /explorer/tx/[txid] route recognize a "replaced-away" txid (via the same replacement-history API data) and show a small explanatory page — "This transaction was replaced by RBF — view the current version" with a link forward — instead of the generic 404.

Priority: P3 — minor UX rough edge in an otherwise well-built feature; most users land on the current transaction and are unlikely to click through to a historical predecessor expecting it to render, but the current experience (rich context → dead-end 404) is jarring when they do.
close_reason: Fixed in 74eb35a: the tx not-found path consults the RBF index and 302-redirects evicted replaced txids to the current version with a banner explaining the replacement. Verified with the reporter's exact pair: e1e82ceb... redirected to 5b0666d8... with the banner rendered

title: Address watcher: yield between wallet/multisig derivation batches to stop event-loop stalls
description:
Load test (docs/LOAD-TEST-RESULTS-2026-07-05.md, Scenario 5) found periodic 1.1-4.3s latency spikes every 60-110s under sustained mixed load. Root cause confirmed by code read: src/lib/server/addressWatcher.ts.

The watcher's setInterval tick (startAddressWatcher, addressWatcher.ts:653-681, REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS=5*60_000 at line 53) calls refreshWatches() (546-589) -> enumerateAll() (159-196), which:
- Runs a synchronous node:sqlite DatabaseSync query for ALL wallets and ALL multisigs, all users, no LIMIT (163-165, 172-174).
- Per wallet: walletAddresses() (103-123) does a nested `for change of [0,1] x for i in 0..WATCH_WINDOW(30)` = 60 synchronous EC-derivation calls with zero await/setImmediate/setTimeout(0).
- Per multisig: multisigAddresses() (126-146) does the same 60-derivation nested loop, PLUS an extra synchronous DB query per multisig for its keys (N+1 query pattern, 176-178).

This entire (W wallets + M multisigs) * 60-derivation pass runs back-to-back inside one setInterval callback with no yielding anywhere. It scales linearly and unboundedly with total wallet count across all users. This is the prime suspect for the periodic spikes (fires every 5 min, but Node's single-threaded event loop means any in-flight HTTP request during that tick queues behind it).

Note: src/lib/server/notificationQueue.ts's tick() (214-232) was also investigated and is NOT implicated — it already awaits per-row (await processRow(row), line 227) doing real async I/O, so it naturally yields between iterations.

Fix: insert a yield (await new Promise(resolve => setImmediate(resolve)) or equivalent) between each wallet's/multisig's derivation batch inside enumerateAll() (after each walletAddresses()/multisigAddresses() call, addressWatcher.ts ~163-196), so a queued HTTP request can interleave between wallets rather than waiting for the entire enumeration pass. Also consider batching the N+1 multisig-keys query into a single upfront query to remove that inefficiency while touching this code.

Complexity: small. This is a localized, mechanical change to one file - add a yield point in the existing loop structure, no new dependencies or architecture change.

Risks/gotchas:
- Must confirm `setImmediate` (Node-only) is fine here since this is server-only code (it is, per existing addressWatcher.ts patterns using .unref()).
- Yielding mid-enumeration means enumerateAll()'s snapshot of "desired" scripthashes is no longer atomic across the whole pass - verify downstream logic (subscribe/unsubscribe diffing in refreshWatches) tolerates wallets list being read across multiple event-loop turns (e.g. a wallet added mid-scan). Low risk given watchers already tolerate eventual consistency (5-min refresh cadence), but worth a sanity check.
- Don't over-yield: yielding per-address (60x per wallet) would be excessive; yield per-wallet/multisig (i.e., once per ~60-derivation batch) is the right granularity per the load test's observed spike duration.
close_reason: Made enumerateAll async, yielding to event loop (setImmediate) after each wallet/multisig derivation batch in addressWatcher.ts to stop 5-min-tick event-loop stalls. Also batched the N+1 multisig_keys query into a single upfront query. Typecheck clean.

title: Server: optional dollar-denominated notification thresholds (client entry converts to sats; consider server-side $ semantics)

title: Vault wizard Back navigation doesn't reset device-selection state
description:
Reported by Alex, found immediately after the Ledger Buffer-error bug (see sibling bead): after a failed Ledger connection attempt, clicking Back returns to the same failed Ledger connect view instead of the device-selection picker -- the only way out is a full page refresh.

Root cause: src/routes/(app)/vaults/new/+page.svelte holds `method` (which signing/import method is active: trezor/ledger/coldcard/qr/paste) as page-level $state, not scoped to a step. There IS a working reset path -- the 'Different source' button inside the active-method view calls resetKeyForm() (line ~184), which correctly clears method, error state, and the QR scanner -- but the WIZARD'S OWN step-level Back buttons (e.g. the Review step's back-to-Keys button at line ~1297: onclick={() => (step = 'keys')}, and similar step transitions elsewhere in the file) just flip `step` without ever calling resetKeyForm(). Whatever Back control Alex actually hit (likely a step-level Back rather than the in-form 'Different source' link, possibly compounded by the crash happening before 'Different source' was reachable) lands back on step 'keys' with the stale method/addError state still set, re-rendering the same failed device view.

Fix: make sure returning to the Keys step -- by whichever Back control the wizard actually offers at that point, including any path reachable right after a device-connect error -- always lands on the device-selection picker (method === null) with a clean addError, not whatever method/error state was left over from before. Reuse resetKeyForm() rather than inventing a second reset path.
close_reason: Fixed: goToKeys() runs resetKeyForm() on every entry into the Keys step (Step 1 Continue + Review Back). Live-verified: stale Ledger view cleared, keys preserved.

title: Session cookie missing secure flag (challenge cookies have it, sessions don't)
description: Security review of the new dual-auth/passkey system found: setSessionCookie() in src/lib/server/auth.ts (~lines 85-92) sets httpOnly + sameSite:lax but never sets secure. Compare to the WebAuthn challenge cookie (src/lib/server/webauthn.ts:147), which correctly sets secure: event.url.protocol === 'https:'. Session cookies should match that pattern. Impact: in a misconfigured deployment or over plain HTTP, session tokens would be transmitted unencrypted and sniffable on the network -- the exact class of thing httpOnly/sameSite are meant to help guard, undermined by the missing secure flag. Fix: add the same secure: event.url.protocol === 'https:' check to setSessionCookie, matching the already-correct challenge-cookie pattern in the same codebase.
close_reason: Fixed: setSessionCookie takes the request URL and sets secure: protocol==='https:' explicitly at all six call sites (login/register password+passkey verify, settings password rotation), matching webauthn.ts's challenge-cookie pattern. Deliberately protocol-following rather than always-true so Umbrel http-LAN logins keep working per the auth-model constraint.

title: Signup: invalid invite code fails silently (no error shown) most of the time
description:
Submitting the signup form with an invalid/wrong invite code returns HTTP 400 from POST /api/auth/register/password, but the UI only shows the 'That invite code is not valid.' banner intermittently. Reproduced 2 of 3 identical attempts (different bogus codes each time) where the request 400'd but NO error message appeared at all -- the form just sat there, and in one case the already-typed name/email/password fields were silently wiped while the invite code field was kept. A first-time non-technical user would have no idea why account creation isn't working and might retype their info repeatedly with no feedback.

Repro: go to /signup, fill in name/email/password + a bogus invite code, submit. Repeat 3x with different bogus codes. Error banner appears ~1/3 of the time.
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Repeatable load-test harness: concurrent sessions + rapid-fire API + latency/event-loop report
description: All prior load testing was ad-hoc (the 2026-07-05 session that produced cairn-ggvt/fd56/ynfp); nothing scripted/reusable exists. Build a scenario runner: N parallel authenticated sessions doing dashboard->wallet->send-draft->explorer loops plus rapid-fire hits on /api/portfolio and label/history endpoints, against a prod build seeded with the scale fixtures, emitting p50/p95 latency and monitorEventLoopDelay histograms (reuses cairn-1q4b's measurement approach). Becomes the standing regression gate for the cairn-6xxa perf work.

Commit 8b7b110f384299bf11d1076de3dccadb8298ec82 on test/qa-wave-2026-07-12. Full results in docs/TEST-REPORT-2026-07-12.md.

title: Signup page doesn't prefill invite code from ?invite= URL query param
description:
Reproduced on Heartwood v0.2.0 at umbrel.local:3211 (browser QA).

Navigating to /signup?invite=CAIRN-XXXX-XXXX shows the signup form with the Invite code field completely empty -- the query param is silently ignored. The user has to manually copy-paste the code from wherever it was shared, even though the URL already carries it.

Root cause: src/routes/(auth)/signup/+page.svelte:9 initializes `let inviteCode = $state('')` with no read of the URL search params, and the load function in +page.server.ts (src/routes/(auth)/signup/+page.server.ts) never returns/reads an `invite` query value either.

This matters because the admin Invites page (/admin/invites) presents invite codes as bare strings to copy, but the natural way an operator shares one is a link (e.g. "join here: https://.../signup?invite=CAIRN-XXXX"). Right now that link degrades to "go to the signup page and then also separately paste this code."

Suggested fix: in +page.svelte, seed `inviteCode` from `page.url.searchParams.get('invite')` on mount (or thread it through the load function like registrationMode/needsInvite already are).

Repro: create any invite in /admin/invites, sign out, visit /signup?invite=<the code>, observe the Invite code field is blank.
close_reason: Fixed as part of the Track F merge (1a76aea). Signup's load function now reads ?invite= from the URL and threads it through the same way registrationMode/needsInvite already are, seeding the Invite code field instead of leaving it blank. npm run check 0/0.

title: Vault creation wizard doesn't warn when two keys share the same master fingerprint
description:
Page: /vaults/new (Add keys step)

What I did: created a 2-of-3 vault, pasting three extended public keys that
were all derived from the same master seed at different BIP-48 accounts
(m/48'/0'/0'/2', m/48'/0'/1'/2', m/48'/0'/2'/2') via the "Paste public key"
option, each with its origin annotation e.g. [73c5da0a/48'/0'/0'/2']xpub...
Named them Test Key 1/2/3 for clarity.

What happened: the wizard accepted all three with no warning, even though
all three share the identical master fingerprint (73c5da0a) -- clearly
visible right there in the key list next to each key's name. Nothing in the
"Add keys," "Review," or "Confirm" steps flagged this. The vault was created
successfully with all 3 keys effectively coming from the same underlying
seed, defeating the entire point of an M-of-N vault: compromising ONE device
(or seed backup) could compromise 2 or 3 of the "independent" keys at once.

What should happen: when two or more keys in the same vault share a master
fingerprint, the wizard should show a clear warning (e.g. "Test Key 1 and
Test Key 2 have the same master fingerprint — they likely come from the same
device or seed. For real protection, each key should live on a separate
device or in a separate place.") Whether this should be a hard block or just
a prominent, dismissible warning is a product call -- there could be
legitimate reasons (e.g. re-testing with different derivation paths off one
seed) -- but right now there's zero signal at all, and a user could build a
vault they believe is protecting them with 3 independent keys when it's
really 1.

Related: this exact scenario (duplicate fingerprints across vault keys) also
triggers a separate, more severe bug in the signing stepper -- see cairn-x54,
where it makes the vault impossible to fully sign at all once one key has
signed. That bug's impact is a direct consequence of this gap letting the
vault be created in the first place.

Priority: P2 -- doesn't corrupt anything by itself, but it's a real security-
transparency gap for a feature whose entire value proposition is "no single
point of failure," and it's the root enabler of cairn-x54's more serious bug.
close_reason: Fixed: sharedFingerprintGroups warning ('00000000' exempt) on Keys + Review steps, prominent but non-blocking, duplicated fingerprints highlighted on Review. Live-verified with exact wording.

title: Vault descriptor + multisig address library
description: wsh(sortedmulti(m, xpub1/../*, ...)) descriptor build/parse/export/import (Sparrow/Electrum-compatible, with checksum), M-of-N address derivation via @scure/btc-signer p2wsh+p2ms over sorted child pubkeys, test-address generation for wizard verification. Pure new files under src/lib/server/bitcoin/.

Byte-identical to Bastion's Core-verified format incl. #e2frtjsz pin; 4 checksum vectors; BIP-67 sort verified; SLIP-132 canonicalization; multi( and sh(wsh( rejected with clear messages. 366/366 suite, check 0 errors.

title: api/search bypasses the explorer feature-flag gate (requireFeature missing)
description:
SEVERITY: P2 (authz/feature-gate bypass; limited to public chain data + backend resource use).

SURFACE: api/search (cairn-jqss audit).

FINDING: src/routes/api/search/+server.ts:7 calls only requireUser(event) — it never calls requireFeature(event,'explorer'). Every explorer page is gated by src/routes/(app)/explorer/+layout.server.ts:9 (requireFeature(event,'explorer')). The 'explorer' flag now defaults OFF on fresh installs (registry.ts:112 + explorerDefaultMigration.ts), so on a default install the whole explorer UI 403s but /api/search stays fully live.

EXPLOIT: Any signed-in non-admin user on an instance where the admin disabled — or never enabled — the explorer flag can still GET /api/search?q=<64-hex|height|address>. classifySearch() then drives real backend chain RPC: getTip() (search.ts:63), getTx() (search.ts:83), getBlock() (search.ts:92). Effect: (1) a disabled feature's backend is reachable and consumes Electrum/Core resources; (2) existence of arbitrary txids/blocks is confirmable (public data, low sensitivity). This violates the exact invariant api.ts documents: 'the 403 here is what makes a disabled flag real against a direct API call.'

FIX: swap requireUser(event) for requireFeature(event,'explorer') in src/routes/api/search/+server.ts. Also audit other /api/* that reach chain data for the same gap.
close_reason: Fixed: /api/search now calls requireFeature(event,'explorer') instead of requireUser, matching the explorer layout gate (src/routes/(app)/explorer/+layout.server.ts:9). Flag-off now 403s before classifySearch runs. src/routes/api/search/+server.ts commit d76f8cb. Regression tests in src/routes/api/search/server.test.ts (flag off -> 403, classifier never called; flag on -> 200).

title: notifications.test.ts never exercises notify()/processRow()/tick() integration — only pure helpers tested
description: commit c9483a6 (Notify Unit 1) shipped notifications.test.ts (139 lines) covering resolveRecipients() and the pure backoffMs()/takeToken() helpers, but never calls notify() itself, and never drives notificationQueue.ts's processRow()/tick() state machine — no test asserts a notification_queue row is actually inserted with expected columns, no test confirms the dead-letter transition at attempts>=5 flips status to 'dead' (notificationQueue.ts ~line 192-201), and no test covers the unknown-channel-marks-failed path (~line 143-151) or corrupt-payload-marks-failed path (~line 155-161). These functions aren't even exported for testing (_internals only exposes backoffMs/takeToken/buckets/BACKOFF_MS/MAX_ATTEMPTS) so the integration surface is currently untestable without additional exports or a DB-level integration test. A regression breaking the wiring between processRow and its DB updates (wrong column/status name) would ship undetected despite 139 lines of tests. Fix: export what's needed for testing (or add a DB-integration test using the existing test DB helpers) and cover notify()'s actual INSERT, plus the dead-letter and failed-path transitions in processRow.
close_reason: Resolved by src/lib/server/notificationQueue.test.ts (new file, this session): exercises notify() -> queue insert -> tick()/processRow() end-to-end via _internals.tick, covering sent/failed/dead status transitions, retry backoff progression to MAX_ATTEMPTS, per-channel rate-limit bucket interaction, and preference-gated dispatch (disabled channel never sent, enabled+configured channel is). Also added src/lib/server/channels/email.smtp-integration.test.ts which exercises the real SMTP wire protocol (unmocked nodemailer) against a local TCP stub, including a real PGP encrypt/decrypt round trip.

title: resetInstance has no ROLLBACK — FK violation leaves open transaction permanently
description: admin.ts:107 — Factory reset runs db.exec('BEGIN; DELETE...; COMMIT;') with no try/catch/ROLLBACK. feature_flags.updated_by references users(id) with no cascade and is never cleared, so DELETE FROM users throws FK violation. With no ROLLBACK, the shared DatabaseSync connection is left with an OPEN TRANSACTION, breaking every subsequent BEGIN (restore, account deletion, recovery-code save) until process restart. Fix: wrap in try/catch→ROLLBACK, clear updated_by columns before deleting users.
close_reason: FIXED in 2e759e2 (admin.ts resetInstance). Nulls feature_flags.updated_by + user_feature_flags.updated_by before DELETE FROM users (deleteOwnAccount idiom), and wraps the sequence in explicit BEGIN/try/COMMIT/catch-ROLLBACK instead of a bare db.exec BEGIN;...;COMMIT. An FK error now rolls back cleanly instead of leaving a permanently-open transaction. Test: reset with a toggled feature flag succeeds and a subsequent BEGIN works (verified it threw FK before the fix).

title: Surface multisig as first-class on /wallets; link orphaned stateless route; honest sub-progress in Add-keys step
description: Three multisig-discovery/orientation gaps (Fable UX ranking c > b > d, all below the wizard-resilience #1 = cairn-1u41). (c, #2) Multisig is NOT a first-class choice on /wallets — to create one a user clicks 'Add a wallet' (single-sig flow) then must notice a small hand-off link near the bottom of the Key step: wallets/new/+page.svelte:787-794 'Several keys guarding one wallet? Set up a multisig wallet instead.' The funnel loses users inside the COMPETING flow before they know multisig exists. Add a top-level multisig option on /wallets/+page.svelte (which today only links /wallets/new, lines 95-125). (d, #4) /wallets/multisig/stateless (Caravan-compatible, nothing persisted server-side) has NO inbound link anywhere in src — orphaned/undiscoverable; link it from the multisig hand-off or a footer/docs (confirm with Alex whether it's deliberately advanced-only). (b, #3) The 'Add your N keys' step is ONE dot on a 4-dot progress bar but is internally an N-deep sub-wizard (per key: method -> read/paste/import -> repeat); for 5-of-7 that's 7+ screens hidden in 'step 2 of 4'. Each sub-screen is well-labeled ('Add key 2 of 5') so orientation exists, but expected EFFORT is understated — add a secondary sub-progress element. Do c+d before more users are funneled in; b is polish.

title: Signup submit fails silently -- no POST fired, no user feedback (valid invite code present)
description:
Evidence: dev instance 2026-07-12, filled a valid signup form (all fields including a VALID invite code) and clicked submit -- no network POST fired at all, no visible error, button appears dead. A brand-new user has no way to tell why registration isn't working. Likely a client-side validation check silently blocking submit on some field even though the tester believed all fields were valid (e.g. password-confirm mismatch, email format, or a hidden required field). Repro: fresh browser, /signup, fill all fields incl. valid invite code, submit -- zero network activity.

Note: state-dependent -- a separate QA run ~1h earlier successfully registered on a DIFFERENT fresh DB with the same general flow, so this may depend on specific field content, invite-required mode nuance, or DB/session state rather than being universal.

Searched for overlap first: cairn-4lp (CLOSED, 'native browser validation silently blocks submit with no visible error, required invite code field') and cairn-1qv7 (CLOSED, 'empty invite code gives no error message, just silent focus') both addressed the EMPTY-field case and were fixed via novalidate + styled inline errors (commits 1f94da6, 22ab66d). cairn-gy79 (CLOSED) covers invalid invite CODE returning 400 with an intermittent banner -- also not a match since this repro has zero network activity, not a 400 response. None of the closed fixes account for a fully-filled valid-looking form still producing zero POST -- filing as a new bug since this looks like either a regression of the closed fixes or a distinct silent-validation path they didn't cover.
close_reason: Root cause: the '(auth) layout secureRedirect HTTPS-probe could location.replace() mid-fill and silently orphan a signup submit ('dead button', no POST, no feedback); the interaction veto (0ff5d53) was only wired to focusin/input on .auth-col. Browser-verified on dev 5173 (invite mode) that the client form is now correct on every path: empty submit renders 'Enter a display name.' banner (no silent block); filled+invalid-invite fires POST /api/auth/register/password -> 400 and shows plain-language 'That invite code is not valid...' banner. Hardened the veto: added a window-level capture-phase pointerdown listener in onMount (marks interacted at the very START of a click gesture, before focus/click/submit) so a pointer-driven submit can never be orphaned by a probe resolving in the focus->submit gap; window listener avoids the a11y_no_static_element_interactions warning a div handler triggers, and is torn down on unmount. npm run check: 0 new errors/warnings from this change (6 pre-existing errors in recoverySetupFlow.test.ts untouched).

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Root cause: (auth)/+layout.svelte maybeRedirectToSecure HTTPS-probe can location.replace() mid-submit when CAIRN_HTTPS_EXTERNAL_PORT is set, orphaning the signup POST silently. Fixed 0ff5d53 (merged): redirect vetoed once the user touches any form field; layout-local, secureRedirect.ts untouched. Race not live-triggered in sandbox -- verification via code path + typecheck; watch on next Umbrel/HTTPS QA.

title: HTTPS listener (3212): first dashboard load after idle takes 10-30s (cold cache)
description:
On v0.2.1, the HTTPS listener (port 3212) dashboard is no longer permanently empty (the previously known issue) — but the very first authenticated GET / after the process has been idle took 10s+ (timed out a 10s curl, completed within 30s), while a repeat request right after returned in 48ms. HTTP port 3211 didn't show this — it was already warm from prior testing in the same session.

Likely just a one-time cache-warm cost (tip/fee-estimate/mempool-blocks TTL cache, cairn-vknb.5) paid independently per listener/process. Not spending more time root-causing since it self-resolves and isn't a hang — flagging so someone checks whether HTTPS and HTTP share the same ChainService cache instance or warm independently (if independent, every HTTPS-only user pays this once per cache expiry).

Found during v0.2.1 perf-fix QA pass, 2026-07-07/08 UTC, against live umbrel.local instance.

title: package.json's jadets '^1.1.18' is a caret range, not the pin the in-file comment claims
description: src/lib/hw/jade.ts's header comment (~line 9) says 'pin the version and never auto-update (foundation: jadets@^1.1.18)', but package.json actually specifies "jadets": "^1.1.18" — a caret range permitting any 1.x release up to (excluding) 2.0.0. The lockfile currently resolves to exactly 1.1.18 so today's installs are consistent, but that's an artifact of the lockfile being present/honored, not an actual pin — any workflow that regenerates or bypasses the lockfile (npm update, a fresh install after lockfile deletion, dependabot-style automation) would silently pull a newer 1.x release with no version-string change to flag it for review. Given jade.ts's own comment explicitly flags jadets as an unofficial single-maintainer library requiring extra caution, this undermines the stated mitigation. Fix: change package.json to the exact version "1.1.18" (no caret) so a real version bump requires an explicit, reviewable package.json diff.
close_reason: Pinned jadets to exact 1.1.18 in package.json (was ^1.1.18) and synced package-lock.json's dependency spec. A future bump to this unofficial single-maintainer library now requires an explicit, reviewable package.json diff, matching jade.ts's own header comment ('pin the version and never auto-update').

title: Vault key health check
description: Caravan-parity: per-key 'is this key accessible?' verification — for connected devices (Trezor/Ledger) re-read the key at its recorded path and compare fingerprint+xpub; for air-gapped/file keys, a guided address-verification prompt instead. Surface on the vault detail key list ('Last verified ...'). Scope after the Caravan research report.
close_reason: Committed: last_verified_at + markKeyVerified (ownership-checked), device re-read checks compared server-side with SLIP-132 canonicalization, guided manual checks for air-gapped keys, staleness rendering + half-year-windowed dismissible nudge (fresh vaults exempt 7 days). 12 new vaults tests; 581 total green. Caveat noted: device buttons not exercised against real hardware — emulator E2E covers that.

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Per Alex's Unchained/Casa research: model health checks on Casa's pattern — PERIODIC prompts to verify keys are still accessible (not just an on-demand button). Detail-page nudge when a key hasn't been verified in N months; connected devices re-read the key at its path and compare fingerprint+xpub, air-gapped keys get a guided address-verification prompt. 'Last verified' per key row.

title: single-sig receive action: QRCode.toDataURL outside try/catch causes unhandled 500
description:
src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.server.ts receive action (~lines 74-91): nextReceiveAddress is guarded by try/catch -> fail(502), but the subsequent await QRCode.toDataURL(next.address, QR_OPTS) was OUTSIDE the try, so any QR-encode failure is an unhandled 500 instead of a graceful form-level error. The multisig sibling (src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts, ~lines 138-150) already wraps both calls in one try -- mirror that pattern.

Caveat found while fixing: nextReceiveAddress (src/lib/server/wallets.ts:452) can return null, in which case the action calls error(404, 'Wallet not found') -- unlike the multisig sibling's nextMultisigReceiveAddress, which never returns null/throws. Folding the error(404) call inside the same try as QRCode.toDataURL would let the generic catch swallow the thrown SvelteKit HttpError and mis-report it as a 502 "Could not reach the Electrum server" instead of a real 404. Fixed by importing isHttpError from '@sveltejs/kit' and re-throwing when isHttpError(e) is true before falling through to fail(502).
close_reason: Fixed in 275f751 (worktree branch worktree-agent-a76bdb15881777d63). src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.server.ts receive action (lines 74-94): folded the QRCode.toDataURL() call into the same try as nextReceiveAddress(), mirroring the multisig sibling (wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts:138-150). Divergence from that sibling: nextReceiveAddress() (src/lib/server/wallets.ts:452) can return null, triggering error(404,'Wallet not found') inside the try -- added isHttpError(e) check (imported from '@sveltejs/kit') to re-throw that HttpError before falling through to fail(502), so a real 404 no longer gets mis-reported as a 502 Electrum failure. Full suite: 2032 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failed (npx vitest run). svelte-check: 0 errors, 0 warnings.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Fix landed on single-sig-full-wallet as 3fae3de (cherry-picked from worktree commit 275f751 referenced in the close comment above).

title: api/search has no rate limiting — authenticated chain-RPC amplification
description:
SEVERITY: P3 (authenticated DoS amplification against the shared chain backend).

SURFACE: api/search (cairn-jqss audit).

FINDING: src/routes/api/search/+server.ts applies no per-user/per-IP rate limit (unlike /api/auth/* and /api/contacts which use rateLimit.ts). A single GET /api/search?q=<64-hex> fans out to as many as three upstream chain calls: getTip (search.ts:63, for a height query) OR getTx then getBlock (search.ts:83, :92, for a 64-hex query that is not a known tx). CLASSIFY_BUDGET_MS (4s, search.ts:37) bounds per-request latency but NOT request rate or concurrency.

EXPLOIT: An authenticated user runs a tight loop of GET /api/search?q=<random 64-hex that is neither a real tx nor block>. Each request forces getTx()+getBlock() against the Electrum/Core pool and burns the full budget window. Enough concurrent requests exhaust the connection pool and stall chain-data reads (Home, Explorer, portfolio warm) for every other user on the instance — a low-cost self-DoS from any ordinary account.

FIX: add a per-user (or per-IP) fixed-window limiter around the search handler, mirroring rateLimit.ts's existing counters (e.g. the contacts-endpoint pattern). A modest ceiling (e.g. a few searches/sec) is invisible to the real type-ahead UI but caps the amplification. Note: fixing cairn-he4e (flag-gate) reduces but does not remove this — it still applies whenever the explorer flag is on.
close_reason: Fixed: added a per-user + per-IP fixed-window rate limiter (searchRetryAfter/noteSearchRequest, SEARCH_LIMITS = {user:120, ip:300} per the existing 15-min WINDOW_MS) in src/lib/server/rateLimit.ts, mirroring the contacts endpoint's every-request-counts pattern. /api/search now 429s with {code:'rate_limited'} once tripped, before classifySearch runs. commit d76f8cb. Regression tests in src/routes/api/search/server.test.ts (under limit -> 200 x5; at 120 -> 429, classifier not called; a fresh user/IP unaffected by another user's limit).

title: feat: Umbrel zero-config Electrum auto-detect via Docker-network probe (auto-connect Wave A)
description:
Wave A of the Umbrel zero-config Electrum auto-connect design: probeAndSeedUmbrelElectrum() in src/lib/server/umbrelProbe.ts. Strictly gated on CAIRN_PLATFORM==='umbrel' and connection_mode unset. Probes the well-known Umbrel Docker-network fixed IPs -- 10.21.21.10:50001 (electrs) then 10.21.21.200:50002 (fulcrum) -- with a real ElectrumClient.headersSubscribe() handshake, 2s timeout, credential-free (no manifest dependency declaration needed). On first successful handshake, seed-once writes electrum_host/electrum_port/electrum_tls, sets connection_mode='custom', and stamps chain_provisioned_by='umbrel-probe' for the settings-UI provenance card. Never throws; silent fall-through to the existing public-server default when nothing is reachable.

Builds on the already-closed cairn-loq7 (env-var seeding from Umbrel manifest dependencies, chainEnvSeed.ts). Wave A covers installs where the operator has electrs/Fulcrum running on Umbrel but Cairn's manifest doesn't declare a hard dependency on it (env vars therefore never arrive) -- the probe finds it anyway via the shared umbrel_main_network bridge.

Wiring: chainEnvSeed.ts now also stamps chain_provisioned_by='umbrel-env' (only when the env-provided host was actually adopted); settings.ts/types.ts expose chainProvisionedBy on InstanceSettings; hooks.server.ts calls the probe in init() right after seedChainConfigFromEnv() so env always wins over probe.

Design doc: docs/UMBREL-AUTOCONNECT-DESIGN.md.
close_reason: Shipped in commits b16eea1/1e311c3 on single-sig-full-wallet, pushed to origin. npm run check 0 errors; full suite 2315 passed/1 skipped. See comment for full evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Shipped. npm run check: 0 errors. Full suite: 2315 passed, 1 skipped (VAULT_E2E-gated regtest test, expected without live infra) -- targeted umbrelProbe.test.ts + chainEnvSeed.test.ts pass was 32/32 pre-ship. Reconstructed docs/UMBREL-AUTOCONNECT-DESIGN.md (original scratchpad design doc lost to a reboot) and repointed all code comments (umbrelProbe.ts, umbrelProbe.test.ts, hooks.server.ts) at it. Added MANUAL.md coverage: init() step 4 (Part I), CAIRN_PLATFORM env var + auto-connect section (§12), Umbrel test-target note (§16.3), and QA scenario 20.14 (Part II).

Commits on single-sig-full-wallet (pushed to origin):
- b16eea1 docs: add internal dev manual + QA runbook (MANUAL.md)
- 1e311c3 feat(umbrel): zero-config Electrum auto-detect via Docker-network probe (Wave A)

Also commented on cairn-2ldr noting Wave A's local-first probe confirms option (c) from that bead's decision list is viable without a manifest dependency; that bead remains open (product-positioning decision).

title: Docker build fails: native usb module needs node-gyp/Python, unused at runtime
description:
Reproduced: `docker build .` fails during `npm ci` inside the build stage with:

  npm error gyp ERR! find Python - "" could not be run
  npm error gyp ERR! stack Error: Could not find any Python installation to use
  ... cwd /app/node_modules/usb

Root cause: `usb@2.18.0` is a transitive HARD dependency (not optional) of @trezor/transport <- @trezor/connect <- @trezor/connect-web@9.7.3 (confirmed via `npm ls usb`). It is a native Node addon requiring node-gyp + Python + a C++ toolchain to build. The Docker base image has none of these.

The kicker: Cairn never actually needs this native binding. @trezor/connect-web only runs client-side in the browser via the Trezor Connect popup (see src/lib/hw/trezor.ts) -- there is no server-side Node USB access anywhere in the codebase. This native dependency is dead weight for how Cairn actually uses Trezor Connect, and it currently breaks the one deployment path (Docker) this project explicitly built and documented for self-hosters.

This is a hard blocker for anyone following the README Docker instructions -- the image cannot be built at all right now, not "builds but is degraded."

Options to fix, roughly in order of preference:
1. Check if a lighter Trezor Connect build target exists that does not pull in @trezor/transport native USB (e.g. an npm override/resolution pinning usb to a no-op stub, since the real transport used is always the browser popup, never the Node native path).
2. Add build tooling (python3, make, g++) to the Dockerfile build stage as a fallback if (1) is not viable -- heavier image, but functionally correct.
Verify whichever fix is chosen by re-running `docker build .` to completion, not just by reasoning about it -- this exact failure was only caught by actually attempting the build.
close_reason: Fixed + verified. Added python3/make/g++/linux-headers/eudev-dev to the Dockerfile build stage so npm ci can compile the usb native addon (hard transitive dep of @trezor/connect-web). Verified by actually running docker build to completion AND starting the container: it boots and serves /api/health 200. Chose the build-tools approach over stubbing usb because it's reliable and the multi-stage build keeps the runtime image toolchain-free (usb is never loaded server-side; Trezor Connect is browser-only).

title: Stale/unreachable SOCKS5 proxy config (192.168.50.134:9050) breaks Esplora lookups and likely explains the widespread portfolio/wallet-scan slowness
description:
Reproduced on Heartwood v0.2.0 at umbrel.local:3211 (browser QA), discovered while testing explorer error states.

Admin > Settings has a "Privacy: SOCKS5 / Tor proxy" section (Proxy host / Port) already filled in with 192.168.50.134:9050 -- I did not set this, it was pre-existing on the instance when I started this QA pass. This proxy is unreachable/broken: visiting /explorer/address/<any address> eventually surfaces "Can't reach chain data sources -- Esplora GET /address/<addr> failed: Proxy connection timed out" and "Couldn't load transactions -- Esplora GET /address/<addr>/txs failed: Proxy connection timed out" (after several seconds of skeleton loading with no error shown in between).

Per the settings copy: "Route all chain traffic (Electrum + explorer) through a SOCKS5 proxy... Applies whether you use the public servers or your own node." -- meaning if this proxy is broken, it degrades ALL chain data on the instance, not just the explorer.

This is very likely the root cause (or a major contributing cause) of the widespread slowness already filed as cairn-3gvb (dashboard /api/portfolio taking ~20s to resolve) and cairn-2ic5 (Rotate button hanging 30-40s+): every Electrum/Esplora round-trip on this instance may be eating a full proxy-connect timeout before falling back or erroring, rather than those pages being simply "slow to scan." The portfolio/wallet-scan code has legitimate optimization opportunities per cairn-3gvb/cairn-2ic5, but the proxy misconfiguration is likely inflating every number observed during this QA pass.

This isn't necessarily an app bug (a broken proxy is an operator configuration issue), but it's flagged P0 because: (1) it's silently degrading the entire instance with zero visible warning outside the explorer address page's specific error banner -- the dashboard and wallet pages just show generic skeleton-then-eventually-loads with no indication a proxy is the reason, and (2) it made every other perf finding in this QA pass hard to diagnose correctly.

Suggested actions:
1. Alex: check whether 192.168.50.134:9050 is intentional (was this set deliberately for privacy testing, e.g. via cairn-wgr8/Tor work) or leftover from an earlier session and should be cleared.
2. Product: surface a clear, instance-wide warning (not just buried in the explorer address page) when the configured proxy is unreachable, so this doesn't read as generic "everything is slow."
3. Re-run the cairn-3gvb / cairn-2ic5 timing repros with the proxy cleared/fixed to see how much of that slowness was proxy-timeout vs genuine gap-limit-scan cost.
close_reason: Fixed in 62ba92b. Added src/lib/server/chainHealth.ts (cheap in-memory chain-transport health signal, 2-consecutive-failure threshold, derived from Electrum connection outcomes in electrum/client.ts's ensureConnected -- no fresh probe), a read-only /api/chain-health endpoint, an instance-wide ChainHealthBanner.svelte mounted in the (app) layout next to SyncBanner (warns balances may be stale, links admins to connection settings), and a live-ish proxy status line in admin > Settings under the SOCKS5/Tor config. A broken proxy is now visible instance-wide instead of only surfacing as an unexplained skeleton on the dashboard. npm run check 0/0, full suite green. Alex still needs to independently verify whether 192.168.50.134:9050 is intentional or leftover.

title: Invite creation silently clamps out-of-range count/maxUses/expiresDays with no feedback to the admin
description: src/lib/server/admin.ts createInvites() clamps count to [1,50] and maxUses to [1,1000] via Math.min(Math.max(...)) with no error/warning returned. The form at src/routes/(app)/admin/invites/+page.svelte has no client-side max/min enforcement feedback either (the number inputs have min/max attributes but a user can still type an out-of-range value and submit). If an admin requests count=200 expecting 200 invite codes, they silently get 50 with no indication anything was capped, and the returned form.created array (len 50) doesn't explain why it's short of the requested count. Fix: either reject out-of-range values with a form error (consistent with how registrationMode/connectionMode/electrumPort are validated elsewhere in this same admin area) or return a note in the action result explaining the count was clamped.
close_reason: Duplicate of cairn-sh5h (same invite-clamp issue, same description). Fixed under cairn-sh5h in commit 22ab66d.

title: Filename-sanitizing slug() duplicated 5x across export routes with zero test coverage
description: The Content-Disposition filename sanitizer (lowercase, replace [^a-z0-9]+ with '-', trim dashes, slice(0,48), || 'wallet' fallback) is copy-pasted verbatim into at least 5 separate route files (src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/config/+server.ts, .../[id]/descriptor/+server.ts, and the three multisig export routes) instead of living once in src/lib/server/walletExport.ts (the module that was specifically introduced in 588aa0a to hold shared wallet-export logic). It is not currently a live vulnerability — all copies correctly neutralize quotes/CRLF/path separators/unicode for header-injection purposes and currently agree with each other — but there is zero test coverage of the sanitizer anywhere in the repo (walletExport.test.ts only covers the descriptor-building functions), so a future edit to loosen the regex or drop the empty-name fallback in just one copy could silently diverge or reintroduce a Content-Disposition header-injection path with nothing to catch it. Fix: move slug() into walletExport.ts as a single exported function, update all 5 call sites to import it, and add unit tests covering header-injection payloads (quotes, CRLF, path traversal, unicode, empty/whitespace-only names).
close_reason: Extracted the duplicated filename sanitizer into a single exported filenameSlug(name, fallback='wallet') in walletExport.ts. Updated all 6 call sites (single-sig config + descriptor, multisig caravan/coldcard/descriptor/backup-pdf) to import it; removed every local copy. Added header-injection unit tests in walletExport.test.ts (quotes, CRLF, path traversal, unicode, empty/whitespace names) — all 11 pass.

title: Explorer search bar rejects valid testnet/regtest addresses that the address page itself accepts
description: src/lib/server/search.ts:67 gates address recognition in the search bar through isValidAddress() (src/lib/server/bitcoin/xpub.ts:335), which is explicitly mainnet-only by design (see comment at xpub.ts:2). But the actual address page route (src/routes/(app)/explorer/address/[address]/) uses a separate, more permissive isExplorerAddress() (xpub.ts:264) that explicitly accepts bc/tb/bcrt HRPs and testnet/regtest base58 version bytes, specifically so testnet/regtest lookups work when Cairn is pointed at a non-mainnet backend. Net effect: on a testnet/regtest-configured instance, pasting a valid tb1.../bcrt1.../testnet-legacy address into the search bar falls through every branch and returns 'unknown' (no results), even though navigating directly to /explorer/address/<addr> works fine and shows the correct data. Fix: have classifySearch() in search.ts use isExplorerAddress() instead of (or in addition to) isValidAddress() for the address-recognition branch, matching what the address page itself accepts.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: classifySearch() now uses isExplorerAddress() (accepts bc/tb/bcrt + testnet base58) instead of the mainnet-only isValidAddress(), matching what the address page accepts (search.ts).

title: No security response headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options)
description: hooks.server.ts:143-181 — handle hook sets no Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, HSTS, or Referrer-Policy headers. A Cairn instance can be framed by a malicious site. Clickjacking a Sign/Send button could authorize a real Bitcoin transaction. Fix: add X-Frame-Options:DENY, X-Content-Type-Options:nosniff, Referrer-Policy:strict-origin-when-cross-origin, frame-ancestors 'none', and HSTS on HTTPS.
close_reason: Fixed via commit 25c9a8f (worktree agent-a7e8633eb8364b6f2). Added CSP (default-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'; ...), X-Frame-Options:DENY, X-Content-Type-Options:nosniff, Referrer-Policy:strict-origin-when-cross-origin to every response in hooks.server.ts; HSTS gated on cookieSecure() (HTTPS only, matches existing session-cookie HTTPS detection). Verified WebUSB/WebHID hw-signer flows unaffected (no fetch/WS usage in src/lib/hw). vitest 1880/1 skipped, svelte-check 0/0, build clean, manual curl smoke test confirmed headers present.

title: Umbrel: CAIRN_ORIGIN pins passkey expectedOrigin to http origin — WebAuthn on the HTTPS :4488 listener cannot verify (origin mismatch)
description:
Found during the cairn-wrph/cairn-9njl session-cookie review (2026-07-13).

On the Umbrel package, packaging/umbrel/heartwood/docker-compose.yml:33-42 sets:
  CAIRN_ORIGIN: http://${DEVICE_DOMAIN_NAME}:3211

This is deliberate and correct for the plain-HTTP app_proxy port (3211) — it keeps
the session cookie non-Secure there (cookieSecure() in auth.ts) and drives WebAuthn's
expectedOrigin for that listener.

But the SAME compose file also publishes a second, TLS-terminated listener for
hardware-wallet signing: host 4488 -> container 3443 (docker-compose.yml:16-18,
server.mjs's self-signed HTTPS listener, cairn-wgr8). src/lib/server/webauthn.ts's
getRp() (lines 47-51) is:

  const origin = env.CAIRN_ORIGIN ?? event.url.origin;
  const rpID = env.CAIRN_RP_ID ?? event.url.hostname;

Because CAIRN_ORIGIN is set (unconditionally, to the http://...:3211 value),
getRp()'s `origin` is PINNED to that http origin for every request regardless of
which listener served it. verifyRegistration/verifyAuthentication (webauthn.ts) pass
that pinned origin as `expectedOrigin` to @simplewebauthn's verify*Response() calls.
A WebAuthn ceremony actually performed in the browser against the HTTPS :4488
listener sends an attestation/assertion whose real origin is
https://<device>:4488 (or whatever the browser resolved) — which will NEVER match
the pinned http://...:3211 expectedOrigin, so @simplewebauthn's origin check fails
and passkey registration/authentication cannot complete on the HTTPS listener.

This is the exact listener Umbrel needs a secure context on for WebHID/WebUSB
hardware-wallet signing — passkeys are a plausible thing a user would also want to
set up/use from that same secure-context tab.

rpID itself is less broken (event.url.hostname is the same device hostname on both
ports, and CAIRN_RP_ID is unset in this compose file so it falls through to
event.url.hostname) — this bug is specifically about `origin`, not `rpID`.

Pre-existing bug, not introduced by the wrph/9njl fix (that fix only touches
server.mjs's protocol-header handling, not CAIRN_ORIGIN or webauthn.ts). Filing as a
separate P2 rather than folding into wrph/9njl's scope.

Suggested fix direction (not yet designed/reviewed): getRp() needs to derive origin
per-listener the same way cookieSecure() now correctly derives protocol, rather than
letting a single CAIRN_ORIGIN pin it across both ports — e.g. only use CAIRN_ORIGIN
when it matches the request's own scheme, or introduce a listener-aware origin
allowlist. Needs its own design pass before implementation.

Files: src/lib/server/webauthn.ts:47-51 (getRp), src/lib/server/webauthn.ts:90,126
(verifyRegistration/verifyAuthentication use of expectedOrigin),
packaging/umbrel/heartwood/docker-compose.yml:33-42.

title: Send amount entry: BTC/fiat toggle icon reads as refresh
description: Alex (phone testing): on the send screen, the button that swaps the amount display between BTC and fiat looks like a REFRESH/RELOAD button (circular arrow). Users read that icon as 'refresh', not 'switch currency'. Needs a clear swap affordance (horizontal two-arrow swap glyph, not circular) plus visible alternate-currency unit/value so the button is self-explanatory. Component: src/lib/components/send/AmountEntry.svelte, used by both single-sig (wallets/[id]/send) and multisig (wallets/multisig/[id]/send) flows.
close_reason: Implemented: Icon.svelte adds a non-circular 'swap-horizontal' glyph (two opposing horizontal arrows). AmountEntry.svelte replaces the refresh-icon toggle with a 3-way BTC/sats/USD cycle button (swap glyph + next-unit label, aria-label/title='Change amount unit'); canonical sats value never recomputed on cycle (render-only), so no drift. npm run check: 0 errors/0 warnings. Visual verification BLOCKED: spent well over the ~10min time-box across 4 running dev-server tabs (main 'cairn' server requires an invite code to register; documented QA creds for ports 5311/5322/55230 all returned bad_credentials, likely rotated since memory was written; port 55230 session also hit the known hard admin-recovery wall and dropped on hard-nav; port 5347 login succeeded but is stuck behind the setup-admin first-run wall, matching the documented 'hard permission wall, Alex must unblock' hazard; port 5366 not running). Code change verified by reading the rendered logic and diff only, not a live screenshot.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Live-browser cycle verification (BTC to sats to USD to BTC at 375x812, equivalents update, no drift) deferred to cairn-5fpb, which is blocked on cairn-wrph. Code shipped in commit db4fc29.

title: Multisig owner-only mutation gates never tested cross-actor
description: multisigTransactions.ts — broadcastMultisigTransaction, bumpMultisigTransaction, deleteMultisigTransaction are owner-only gates but every test runs as owner. Nothing asserts a cosigner role is refused. attachMultisigSignature roster gate (L355) also untested for off-roster denial. A privilege-escalation regression would be invisible. Fix: add cross-actor tests (cosigner denied broadcast/bump/delete, off-roster denied attach).
close_reason: Merged fc9453c: cross-actor tests confirm cosigner refused broadcast/bump/delete and off-roster signer refused attach. Gate logic was already correct — no bug found, test-gap only.

title: ntfy notification channel has ZERO SSRF protection — any authenticated user can probe internal network/cloud metadata
description: src/lib/server/channels/ntfy.ts: resolveConfig() (~57-79) applies no URL validation whatsoever to the user-supplied 'server' field, and publish() (~93-127) does fetch(normalizeServer(cfg.server), {...}) directly with no scheme check, no DNS resolution, no private/loopback/link-local/cloud-metadata IP blocklist — unlike the sibling webhook.ts channel shipped in the SAME commit (a0b8002), which does implement an SSRF guard (checkTargetUrl/isBlockedAddress). Any authenticated non-admin user can set their ntfy server to http://169.254.169.254 (cloud metadata), http://127.0.0.1:<port>, or an internal LAN address via PUT /api/notifications/channels/ntfy (requireUser only, no admin gate), then hit POST /api/notifications/channels/ntfy/test — publish()'s fetch runs server-side, and on any non-2xx response up to 500 chars of the response body is captured and returned verbatim as ChannelSendResult.error in the JSON response to the calling user. This is a full SSRF response-reflection oracle against the Cairn server's network position, reachable by any user account (registration can be 'open' mode per RegistrationMode in src/lib/types.ts). Fix: apply the same SSRF guard webhook.ts has (or share one implementation) to ntfy.ts's server URL before every publish()/test() call.
close_reason: Fixed in fc8da90

title: Signature-progress status updates lack aria-live (wallets and vaults send flows)
description: Both send flows use role="status" divs for live signing feedback but never pair it with aria-live="polite": src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:1167 and src/routes/(app)/vaults/[id]/send/+page.svelte:1056,1161,1189 (.sign-flash, .attach-status, .quorum-done). role="status" alone has an implicit aria-live="polite" per the ARIA spec in most browsers/AT combinations, but this is inconsistently honored -- explicit aria-live="polite" alongside role="status" is the robust, spec-recommended pairing. On the vault flow specifically this matters more than single-sig: a screen reader user collecting signatures from multiple cosigners over time ("2 of 3 signatures collected") needs to actually hear that update, not just see it. Pattern-wide fix, not vault-specific -- apply to both flows in one pass.
close_reason: Both halves committed: wallets flow (1c506ff) and multisig flow (this commit) — aria-live on attach status, quorum counts, signer confirmations, flashes.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: WALLETS half done: explicit aria-live=polite added alongside role=status on single-sig send flow status surfaces — .attach-status, .device-saved-note, .saved-note, mass-panel (aria-live matches its status/alert role), TrezorSigner/LedgerSigner .signed-ok, CoinControl .mass-loading. QrSigner already had the correct idiom. Vaults/multisig half untouched (other agent's scope).

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Wallets half committed (attach-status, signer confirmations, mass panel, coin-control loading all carry aria-live). Multisig half pending with the running stepper agent.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Multisig-flow half done: explicit aria-live=polite added alongside role=status on .sign-flash, .attach-status, .quorum-done (confirm step + new quorum-met notice on the sign step), the quorum-head count line (now a polite status region so 'N of M signatures collected' changes are announced), and the Trezor/Ledger signed-ok / save-warning confirmations. Wallets-flow half untouched (other agent's scope).

title: hooks.server.ts runs auth+flags queries before static asset skip check
description: hooks.server.ts:144,148,151 — getSessionUser() (1 SQLite query) and resolveAllFlags() (2 queries + 20+ key object) run unconditionally on every request including JS/CSS/fonts. isAsset(pathname) fast-path only fires at line 151, AFTER both queries. A SvelteKit page pulls dozens of sub-requests per navigation, each paying 3 wasted sync SQLite calls. Fix: move isAsset check to top of handle(), before touching locals.
close_reason: Landed in 3dd2b4a (with v84z). isAsset() check moved to the top of handle() so static-asset requests no longer pay getSessionUser()+resolveAllFlags() (3 SQLite reads each) before the fast-path. Independently reviewed clean. Full suite 1859 pass; svelte-check 0/0.

title: Default unstyled SvelteKit error page shown on 404 (no Cairn branding)
description:
Any unmatched route (e.g. /wallets right now, or any typo'd URL) renders SvelteKit's bare default error page: a plain "404" heading and "Not Found" paragraph, no styling, no Cairn logo/sidebar, no link back into the app.

Expected: a branded error page consistent with The Forge design system (warm charcoal background, copper accent, Cairn logo, a "Back to Dashboard" or "Back to sign in" link depending on auth state) — matching the quality bar set by other pages (e.g. the empty/closed-registration state on /signup which IS well designed).

Repro: navigate to any non-existent route, e.g. http://localhost:<port>/wallets or /explorer/foo/bar.
close_reason: Fixed in 1f94da6: branded +error.svelte on The Forge background with logo, status hero, and links back to the dashboard/explorer. Verified live on an unmatched route

title: Balance-over-time chart empty for imported wallets — no backfill from historical transactions
description: Importing a wallet with existing on-chain history shows an empty 'Balance over time' chart: balance_snapshots only records from the moment Cairn starts sampling, never backfilling from the wallet's historical transactions. Fix: derive historical balance points from the wallet's transaction history (running sum of tx deltas over confirmation time) on import/scan, or at minimum change the empty-state copy from 'Not enough history yet' (implies waiting helps) to 'History starts from when this wallet was added to Cairn'.
close_reason: Fixed: portfolio.ts backfills balance_snapshots from confirmed tx history on a wallet's first scan (buildBackfillPoints: running deltas, retention-aligned resolution, 13-month horizon carry-in; refuses when history can't reconstruct the confirmed balance), getBalanceSeries rewritten as carry-forward sum handling unaligned per-wallet timestamps + excluding deleted wallets' orphans, and BalanceChart empty-state copy no longer implies waiting helps. 11 new tests in portfolio.test.ts.

title: Admin/platform gaps vs. Gitea/Nextcloud, 2026-07-06 comparison audit
description: Comparison audit against Gitea and Nextcloud admin/platform UX (docs/ADMIN-COMPARISON-2026-07-06.md). Parent epic for the 6 gaps identified as most likely to make an experienced self-hoster say 'this feels unfinished' next to a mature multi-user app. See the doc for what's already ahead (backup/restore, per-user SMTP, notification breadth) and what's already tracked elsewhere (forced first-login reset, plaintext secrets, activity scoping).
close_reason: All 6 units (cairn-ivae.1 through .6) built and merged: API tokens (Bearer auth), update-availability notice, scheduled instance backups, PWA manifest, unified toast/banner component, operator name in app chrome. See docs/ADMIN-COMPARISON-2026-07-06.md for the original audit.

title: API tokens for scripting against a user's own instance
description:
Cairn's only auth mechanism is the browser session cookie (src/lib/server/auth.ts) -- there is no API token/key of any kind. Gitea has scoped Personal Access Tokens (per-resource read/write, since v1.23) plus OAuth2; Nextcloud has app passwords (unscoped) plus OAuth2. A Cairn user currently has no way to script against their own instance (pull balances into a spreadsheet, trigger a backup from cron, build a companion CLI) without replaying their session cookie.

Fix: add a personal-access-token system -- token hash stored server-side (same pattern as sessions), issued/revoked from /settings, Bearer-auth accepted alongside the existing cookie in requireUser()/requireAdmin() (src/lib/server/api.ts). Start unscoped (matches Nextcloud) rather than building Gitea-level per-resource scoping up front; scoping can follow later once real usage patterns show what needs restricting.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can create/name/revoke a token from /settings; token value is shown once, never stored in retrievable form (hash only, like sessions).
- Bearer <token> on any authenticated API route resolves to the same user/session context as the cookie.
- Revoked/expired tokens are rejected immediately.
- Rate limiting applies per-token the same way it applies per-session.
close_reason: Personal access tokens built. src/lib/server/apiTokens.ts: api_tokens table (hash-only via auth.ts hashToken, shown once at creation), create/list/revoke/resolve + per-IP invalid-Bearer throttle mirroring rateLimit.ts (20 fails/15min -> 429). requireUser/requireAdmin in api.ts accept 'Authorization: Bearer cairn_<secret>' alongside the cookie, populate locals.user+flags identically so downstream guards and per-user rate limits apply the same. Routes: GET/POST /api/tokens, DELETE /api/tokens/[id]; UI at /settings/tokens (name + optional 30/90/365d expiry, one-time reveal, revoke). Expired rows lazily deleted like sessions; disabled accounts rejected. Evidence: 16 unit+integration tests green (apiTokens.test.ts) incl. requireUser Bearer resolution, revoked->401, spray->429; live smoke on dev server: cookie create -> Bearer GET /api/tokens 200 (no cookie) -> Bearer POST /api/admin/backup 200 -> revoke -> immediate 401.

title: In-app update-availability notice in admin dashboard
description:
Cairn has zero update-awareness: no version check anywhere, no 'a new release is available' notice in /admin. Gitea ships an in-app update-checker cron job that at least surfaces a notice (even though the actual update is still an external image pull); Nextcloud goes further with a full in-app updater for non-container installs. Cairn's deployment target (Umbrel/Start9, container-based) means the update itself will always be an external image pull either way -- but the one-line 'you're on v0.1.4, latest is v0.1.6' notice costs little and both reference apps have it.

Fix: a background check (daily cron, similar shape to existing backupHealth.ts pattern) that compares the running package.json version against the latest GitHub release tag for the repo, surfaces a dismissible admin-dashboard notice when behind, and does nothing else (no auto-update -- Umbrel/Start9 own that lifecycle).

Acceptance criteria:
- Admin dashboard shows a notice when a newer release exists, with a link to the release notes.
- No notice shown when already on latest, or when the check fails (fail silent, log only -- don't nag on a network blip).
