Deleted the two temp settings rows + throwaway user/sessions/recovery rows after -- verified zero residue, left every pre-existing settings row untouched. Folds the Core-detected assisted-connect half into cairn-ylz5 B3 (see that bead).

title: Difficulty adjustment page
description: Difficulty overview at /explorer/difficulty: current difficulty with a plain-language explanation, epoch progress bar (x of 2,016 blocks), blocks remaining and estimated retarget date, projected adjustment percentage derived from this epoch's block arrival rate, average block time vs the 10-minute target, and a chart of recent adjustments when the backend provides history. Educational tooltips cover why difficulty exists and how retargeting keeps block times stable. Degrades gracefully on plain esplora backends.
close_reason: Implemented in 5148793: /explorer/difficulty with projected retarget hero (live -0.6%), 2,016-block epoch progress bar (1,021 in / 996 remaining verified), avg block time vs target with schedule badge, retarget ETA, 10-adjustment history chart, plain-esplora fallback computing pace from block timestamps. Live-verified against mainnet

title: PUT /api/admin/settings has no validation, unlike the equivalent admin settings form action - can silently misconfigure registration mode / node connection
description: src/routes/api/admin/settings/+server.ts PUT handler loops over KEY_MAP and calls setSetting(dbKey, String(body[key])) for whatever JSON the caller sends, with zero validation. Compare the admin settings form action (src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts save), which whitelists registrationMode to open/invite/closed, connectionMode to public/custom, and validates electrumPort as an integer 1-65535. The JSON API has none of that: posting a typo'd or garbage registrationMode is accepted verbatim. Traced through src/routes/(auth)/signup/+page.server.ts: needsInvite = not firstUser and mode is invite, closed = not firstUser and mode is closed - a garbage registrationMode value matches neither check, so the instance silently falls through to fully OPEN registration with no invite requirement and no error surfaced anywhere. electrumPort similarly accepts non-numeric strings that become NaN via parseInt() in getInstanceSettings(), breaking the configured node connection. This route is live and admin-session-authenticated (requireAdmin) even though the current UI never calls it (grepped src/ - zero fetch('/api/admin/settings') call sites) - reachable today by anyone scripting against the API with an admin cookie. Fix: apply the same enum/range validation as the form action, ideally via one shared validation function.
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Polymorphic (wallet_kind,wallet_id) FK integrity — add wallet_entities mapping + user_version migrations + collision audit
description: Strategic follow-up to the balance_snapshots orphan bug. Six child tables reference wallets/multisigs via (wallet_kind,wallet_id) with no FK; wallets.id and multisigs.id are disjoint AUTOINCREMENT spaces BOTH starting at 1, so wallet_id=5 exists in both — cross-kind collision is LIVE not latent: any query filtering wallet_id without a wallet_kind companion silently returns cross-kind rows (user_id scoping doesn't save it — one user owns both kinds). Fable's recommendation (option b, decisively — rejects status-quo-patching which failed 4x, and rejects full parent-merge as highest-risk): create wallet_entities(id INTEGER PK, kind TEXT CHECK(kind IN('wallet','multisig')), ref_id INTEGER, UNIQUE(kind,ref_id)) maintained by AFTER INSERT/DELETE triggers on both parents; children then get a composite FK (wallet_kind,wallet_id) REFERENCES wallet_entities(kind,ref_id) ON DELETE CASCADE with NO new column and NO data backfill (works because the parent cols are UNIQUE). Each child needs a 12-step table rebuild (foreign_keys=OFF outside txn, rebuild, foreign_key_check, VACUUM INTO backup first). Also: adopt PRAGMA user_version + ordered migrations NOW (set atomically inside the DDL txn) — the current probe-PRAGMA-table_info-and-patch approach cannot order the rebuild this needs and is the mindset that produced the orphan bug. AUDIT TASK: grep every 'wallet_id = ?' predicate lacking a wallet_kind companion. wallet_entities is NOT a dead end — it is the shared header table a future parent-merge would grow into.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: SPLIT (Fable roadmap + red-team): the cheap/immediate half of this bead has moved to cairn-97ui as TRIGGERS + introspective test (ships v0.2.2 with the orphan fix). What REMAINS here is ONLY the structural end-state, deferred until after the test-coverage batch and bundled with the next schema-touching change: wallet_entities(id,kind,ref_id,UNIQUE) mapping table + composite FK (wallet_kind,wallet_id) REFERENCES wallet_entities(kind,ref_id) ON DELETE CASCADE from every child (no new column/backfill) + PRAGMA user_version ordered migrations + the 'wallet_id=? lacking wallet_kind' grep audit. The 97ui triggers are ~20 lines that die naturally inside this migration — cheap insurance, not wasted work. Do NOT fast-track this many-table migration on a live funds DB while security P0s (jnlx/fame) are open.

title: Contacts anti-enumeration is incomplete: timing side-channel + no rate limiting on POST /api/contacts
description:
src/lib/server/contacts.ts's requestContact is explicitly documented (file header, JSDoc, db.ts schema comment) as anti-enumeration: an unknown-email request and a known-email request both return an identical 200 {ok:true}. However this only covers response SHAPE, not two other channels:
1. Timing: an unknown email hits 1 read-only query then returns immediately; a known email runs a reciprocal SELECT + either an UPDATE or a SELECT+INSERT — up to 4 sequential SQLite operations including a write. An attacker with any authenticated account (registration can be 'open' mode per RegistrationMode in src/lib/types.ts, so this doesn't require special privilege) can statistically distinguish account-exists vs not by averaging response latency over repeated requests against a wordlist.
2. No rate limiting: src/lib/server/rateLimit.ts already exists and is wired into login and invite-redemption specifically to stop enumeration ('Invalid invite codes per IP — stops invite-code enumeration'), but POST /api/contacts has no rate limit at all, making bulk wordlist-based enumeration practical regardless of the timing issue.
Fix: add a rateLimit.ts bucket for POST /api/contacts (per-user and/or per-IP), and consider normalizing the unknown-email path to perform an equivalent-cost dummy read/write (or add a constant artificial delay) so timing no longer correlates with account existence.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: POST /api/contacts now rate-limited per-user (20) and per-IP (60) per 15-min window counting every request (rateLimit.ts + route), and the unknown-email path does equivalent-cost dummy reads so latency no longer correlates with account existence (contacts.ts). Rate limiting is the primary enumeration defense.

title: Match Caravan's HW-connect posture: Trezor popup + BitBox02 bridge work over plain HTTP
description: Alex's finding: Caravan on the same Umbrel (plain HTTP, same store) connects hardware wallets fine. Investigated Caravan source (AlexM223/caravan#umbrel + upstream caravan-wallets): (1) Trezor — Caravan runs the TrezorConnect popup with NO secure-context gate; the popup at connect.trezor.io holds the transport (WebUSB/Bridge) in its own secure context, so the host page scheme doesn't matter. Cairn's isTrezorConnectAvailable() wrongly requires window.isSecureContext — the cairn-4b2b 'popup needs secure context' claim was circular (it was Cairn's own gate reporting unavailable). (2) BitBox02 — Caravan calls bitbox02ConnectAuto (WebHID, falling back to BitBoxBridge on 127.0.0.1:8178); the bridge prompts the user to approve unknown origins (AllowedHosts confirm dialog), so http://umbrel.local works after one native confirmation. Cairn deliberately restricted to bitbox02ConnectWebHID. (3) Ledger — Caravan uses plain WebUSB, equally unavailable over HTTP; nothing to copy, file/paste stays the Ledger fallback. Fix: drop the isSecureContext gate in trezor.ts, switch bitbox02.ts to bitbox02ConnectAuto with bridge-aware availability + error guidance, update wizard/signer copy.
close_reason: Implemented Caravan parity. trezor.ts: isTrezorConnectAvailable no longer requires window.isSecureContext (the connect.trezor.io popup is its own secure context and holds the transport — verified no gate exists in @trezor/connect-web itself); bitbox02.ts: connectAndPair now uses bitbox02ConnectAuto (WebHID → BitBoxBridge fallback), new isBitbox02Available() browser probe, bridge-aware error copy when a bridge-only browser can't connect; UI copy updated (TrezorSigner/BitboxSigner/signMethods/deviceRead x2). Ledger unchanged — Caravan uses plain WebUSB there and is equally unavailable over HTTP, so file/paste remains the Ledger fallback. All 1604 tests pass incl. new isBitbox02Available + bridge-error tests; svelte-check clean; wizard smoke-tested in preview. Needs a real-device retest on the Umbrel instance.

title: TTL-cache the remaining uncached Dashboard/Explorer chain-data calls (recent blocks, mempool summary, hashrate, difficulty, mempool blocks)
description:
## Reported symptom

"Pages reload on every visit — Dashboard, Explorer, and Wallets all reload from scratch every time. There's no client-side caching."

## What's actually true

SvelteKit re-runs `load()` on every navigation by design — there's no page-level "keep alive"/component-cache primitive in SvelteKit (unlike e.g. Vue's `<keep-alive>`), so "stop reloading on revisit" isn't achievable by keeping pages alive. The two available levers are (a) request-level result caching server-side, and (b) SvelteKit's built-in preload-on-hover, both of which are already partly in place:

- `data-sveltekit-preload-data="hover"` is set globally on `<body>` in `src/app.html`, so any nav link the user hovers before clicking has its data fetch already in flight/done by click time — this already hides most of the latency for mouse-driven nav (not touch/mobile, and not back/forward/direct navigation).
- The genuinely expensive paths are already TTL-cached: `getFeeEstimates` (30s) and `getTip` (10min + header-event invalidation) in `src/lib/server/chain/cache.ts`; wallet/multisig gap-limit scans via `ScanCache` (60s TTL, `gapLimitScanner.ts`). A repeat Wallets visit inside 60s, or repeat fee/tip reads, are already served warm.

## What's NOT cached (the actual gap)

These have zero request-level memoization and pay a fresh esplora HTTP round-trip on every single Dashboard/Explorer visit:
- `getRecentBlocks` (esplora HTTP loop)
- `getMempoolSummary`
- `getHashrate`
- `getDifficultyInfo` (2-4 esplora calls, explorer/difficulty page)
- `getMempoolBlocks`

None of these are per-user or fast-changing enough to need live-freshness on every navigation (blocks arrive every ~10min, mempool summary/hashrate/difficulty change slowly). All streamed already (not blocking first paint), so this isn't urgent, but adding the same TTL-cache pattern from `chain/cache.ts` to these would cut repeat-visit cost, especially for back-navigation, direct URL visits, and mobile (no hover-preload).

## Scope

Add TTL caching (a reasonable default: 30-60s, matching the existing fee-estimate cache) to the five calls above, following the pattern already established in `src/lib/server/chain/cache.ts`. Low priority — none of this blocks first paint today, it's a "make the warm path even warmer" cleanup, not a fix for a broken/blocking experience. The genuinely blocking-feeling issue (rapid nav stutter) is tracked separately at cairn-xlrm.
close_reason: Superseded by cairn-2zxt.8.2 — persisted chain_snapshot + refresh-on-invalidate replaces the proposed TTL-cache-the-remaining-calls approach with a strictly better cache-first pattern that also survives cold start.

title: No address-level labels/tags (only per-transaction labels exist)
description:
Found during a privacy-tools parity audit against Bastion (2026-07-06). Bastion has BOTH address-level tags and transaction-level tags (src/server/routes/tags.ts, address_tags + tx_tags tables). Cairn only has transaction labels (src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/labels/+server.ts, tx_labels table per src/lib/server/db.ts) — there is no way to label an individual address (e.g. 'exchange deposit address', 'donation address') independent of any specific transaction.

Impact: moderate. Users doing coin-control or reviewing address reuse/history have no way to annotate WHY a given address exists, only individual transactions that touched it. Lower severity than the sharing/Tor gaps, but a real UX gap for anyone actively managing privacy/labeling hygiene across many addresses.

Recommended fix: add an address_labels table (wallet_id or multisig_id, address, label) mirroring the existing tx_labels pattern, and surface it on the address-detail view (src/routes/(app)/wallets/.../address-detail equivalents).
close_reason: Built: address_labels table + addressLabels.ts (get/set/delete, wallet_kind for single-sig+multisig); GET/PUT address-labels endpoints (ownership-gated); inline-editable Label column in both wallet + vault Addresses tabs; cleanup on delete. 6 tests, svelte-check 0 errors.

title: Paginate address transaction history past 50 txs
description: src/routes/(app)/explorer/address/[address]/+page.svelte and the underlying chain.getAddressTxs (src/lib/server/chain/index.ts) already support paging via afterTxid, but the address detail page only fetches/renders the first page (up to 50 txs from esplora's /address/{addr}/txs) with no 'load more' control. Addresses with more than 50 transactions are truncated with no way to see older history. Wire up a 'load more' button using the existing afterTxid cursor. v1.1 polish, not a v1 blocker.
close_reason: Implemented in e79e22f: /api/address/[address]?after=<txid> cursor (400 on malformed) and a load-more control on the address page accumulating pages with dedupe, full-list running balance, and count-driven hasMore. Live-verified end to end: 176-tx address walked in pages of 50/25/25/25/25/25/1 with zero overlap, firstSeen appeared at full history, counter tracked loads, and navigating to a 5,562-tx address reset to a fresh first page

title: Recovery code burned before passkey capability check on Umbrel
description: recover/+page.svelte:52-136 — verify step burns a single-use recovery code (recovery.ts:194 sets used_at immediately) before checking whether the browser can complete passkey registration. On non-secure context (Cairn's default plain-HTTP Umbrel deployment), the code is spent and the flow dead-ends at the passkey step. Repeated attempts can exhaust all 8 recovery codes without ever recovering the account. Fix: gate verify screen on real passkey capability up front, or defer code consumption until registration completes.
close_reason: FIXED in c753fc2 (Fable-designed, Opus-reviewed). Kept burn-at-verify (deferring consumption would break the atomic single-use grant); added POST /api/auth/recover/password as a second recovery-completion path that validates password length BEFORE consumeRecoveryGrant (weak pw 400s without burning the grant), then setUserPassword + real session + warn-level security_password_changed 'was this you?' alert (stolen-secret parity). recover/+page.svelte gates the passkey button on browserSupportsWebAuthn() && isSecureContext so plain-HTTP Umbrel always gets the password form and can't dead-end. Both paths share consumeRecoveryGrant's atomic single-use guard. Tests: validate-before-burn, race, capability gating.

title: Signup invite-code error hint text regression: dynamic guidance dropped from register route (found auditing cairn-keo)
description: cairn-keo (closed via commit 6373511) added dynamic guidance to invite_required/bad_invite signup errors: 'Invites come from whoever runs this Cairn instance — ask them for a code.' appended at error time. While auditing cairn-keo for missing-regression-test coverage on 2026-07-06, found this dynamic append is now GONE from current code: src/routes/api/auth/register/password/+server.ts and src/lib/server/auth.ts throw raw AuthError messages with no hint suffix. Only a static hint span in signup/+page.svelte (lines ~119-121, always visible regardless of error state) survives — it appears to have been dropped when the passkey refactor (commit 119d5e4) replaced the old form-action flow with a client-side fetch call. Restore the dynamic hint-on-error behavior (or confirm the static hint fully substitutes for it and close as intentional), and add a regression test this time so it can't silently disappear a third time.
close_reason: Dynamic ask-your-admin hint restored on invite_required/bad_invite in the register password route; regression test asserts hint presence and absence on non-invite errors. Commit d353f7a.

title: Hostile-input sweep: unicode/RTL/emoji, XSS payloads, SQLi strings across user-writable fields
description: No test feeds hostile strings into wallet names, labels, contacts, invite fields, announcement text, or export paths. Notes from code inspection: zero {@html} in src so Svelte auto-escaping likely holds (unproven), and node:sqlite prepared statements are presumed (unproven). One matrix test asserting round-trip integrity and no markup/SQL interpretation. Complements — does not duplicate — cairn-b1rg/cairn-mf68, which cover only CSV formula injection in historyExport.ts.
close_reason: Hostile-input sweep covered by hostileText.test.ts (85 tests, test/qa-wave-2026-07-12), merged in 7bda1d1.

comments.author: qa-fable
comments.text: QA hostile-input sweep 2026-07-12 (isolated copy: qa-sweeps.db, port 5308). PAYLOAD SET (combined into single fields): , , SQLi '; DROP TABLE announcements;-- , RTL override U+202E, emoji, CJK (你好), accented (café), and a javascript: URI in link fields. SURFACE 1 — Admin announcement (Title + Message; renders as an instance-wide banner to every user, plus the admin list). RESULT: CLEAN. - Rendering: payload shown as literal escaped text in the live preview, the persisted admin list, AND the real top-of-page banner across pages. No /"> + emoji. RESULT: CLEAN. /terms renders it as literal escaped text; no execution; exact round-trip in settings.user_agreement_operator. CONCLUSION: cairn-nohi's two open questions are answered for these surfaces — (1) Svelte auto-escaping DOES hold (confirmed live, consistent with "zero {@html} in src"), and (2) node:sqlite prepared statements ARE parameterized (DROP TABLE proven inert). Two distinct render paths tested (authenticated banner/list + unauthenticated public page). No XSS or SQLi found. COVERAGE NOTE: The QA account had 0 wallets, so wallet-name/tx-label/address-label/recipient fields and settings/contacts were not exercised this pass. They share the same Svelte-escaping + node:sqlite stack, but a regression test should still assert round-trip integrity on at least one wallet-scoped field and one contacts field to fully close this bead. Recommend keeping cairn-nohi open until those are covered. All test data cleaned up (announcement deleted, operator name restored).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Hostile-input sweep DONE: 85 tests in hostileText.test.ts (test/qa-wave-2026-07-12). SQLi inert (parameterized everywhere), XSS stored as literal text, unicode/RTL/zalgo round-trip clean. 4 new beads filed for the real defects found (NUL truncation, displayName unbounded, surrogate mangle, UTF-16 cap). Candidate for closure once branch merges.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Merge commit 7bda1d1 (test/qa-wave-2026-07-12 into single-sig-full-wallet) confirmed: hostileText.test.ts (85 tests, unicode/RTL/emoji/XSS/SQLi sweep) present and passing in the merged suite (verified via npm test: 2662/2670 tests pass). Closing per the merge-candidate note.

title: Access-control gaps in collaborative custody + admin visibility
description:
docs/DATA-AUDIT-2026-07-06.md sections 2/3/5/6/7: the multisig viewer role (meant to be
read-only, less-privileged than cosigner) can fetch full unbroadcast PSBTs via the transaction
detail/file/list routes because they gate on getViewableMultisig instead of a signable-tier
check; ledger_multisig_registrations is backwards (owner-only, blocking legitimate cosigners);
tx_labels/address_labels have no defense-in-depth ownership check; the multisig address-labels
route lets a viewer role write, not just read; /admin/activity's API returns every user's full
untruncated detail JSON by default; users.last_login exposes exact behavioral timestamps to
every admin; and raw email/IP are logged outside logger.ts's redact list at four call sites,
readable by any admin via /admin/logs. Children: C1-C7 below.
close_reason: All 7 children done: viewer PSBT gate, ledger-registration tier, label defense-in-depth, label write tier, admin activity detail opt-in, last_login bucketing, email/ip log redaction.

title: Multisig viewer role can fetch full unbroadcast PSBT (detail/file/list routes)
description:
src/lib/server/multisigTransactions.ts's getMultisigTransaction (lines 135-145) and the
transaction-list function (~line 150) both gate via the internal viewableMultisig wrapper
(lines 86-88, which calls getViewableMultisig, src/lib/server/wallets/multisig.ts:148 -- owner
OR any share role including 'viewer'), not the stricter getSignableMultisig (multisig.ts:168
-- owner OR role='cosigner'). This means a read-only 'viewer' share can fetch the full raw
PSBT (recipient addresses, amounts, BIP32 derivation) for in-flight, unbroadcast draft
transactions via:
- GET /api/wallets/multisig/[id]/transactions/[txId] (detail)
- GET /api/wallets/multisig/[id]/transactions/[txId]/file (raw .psbt download) --
  src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/[id]/transactions/[txId]/file/+server.ts
- GET /api/wallets/multisig/[id]/transactions (list) --
  src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/[id]/transactions/+server.ts

This is inconsistent with the app's own stated intent elsewhere -- key-path redaction and
descriptor withholding already applied to viewers via multisigShares.ts's
redactMultisigKeysForViewer -- which implies viewers should see LESS than cosigners, not the
same level of transaction detail. The wallet-overview page,
(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts:116-121, is unaffected: it already projects saved
transactions down to {id, txid, status, feeRate} correctly for viewers.

Fix: swap the gate on these three routes' underlying data functions from
viewableMultisig/getViewableMultisig to getSignableMultisig (owner-or-cosigner). If a
pure-viewer-can-see-drafts behavior turns out to be an intentional product decision, note that
as an open question in the PR rather than silently keeping the looser gate.

Acceptance criteria:
- A viewer-role share gets a 403/redacted response from the transaction detail, file-download,
  and list endpoints for draft/unbroadcast transactions.
- A cosigner-role share and the owner still get full PSBT data via all three routes.
- Add a test case to the existing 3-tier access-gate test suite (multisigAccess.test.ts or
  equivalent) asserting a viewer is denied on all three routes.
close_reason: getMultisigTransaction/listMultisigTransactions now gate on getSignableMultisig; viewer overview list preserved via new PSBT-free listMultisigTransactionSummaries; detail route comment/gate aligned; viewer-denial test added to multisigAccess.test.ts. Commit 341fb5e.

title: Align ledger_multisig_registrations access tier with cosigner needs
description:
src/lib/server/multisigRegistrations.ts's ownsMultisig (lines 67-73) queries
`multisigs WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?` directly -- strictly owner-only, with no
multisig_shares check at all (unlike getViewableMultisig/getSignableMultisig). This is
backwards relative to the sibling PSBT-exposure finding (this epic's other bead): a cosigner
share genuinely NEEDS the Ledger policy-HMAC registration to co-sign on a Ledger device, but
currently cannot fetch it through this table's routes at all, while a viewer (who has no
business signing) currently CAN see full transaction PSBTs elsewhere in the app.

Fix: change ownsMultisig's gate (or add a sibling function) to allow owner OR cosigner-role
share, mirroring getSignableMultisig's logic in src/lib/server/wallets/multisig.ts:168. Do NOT
extend access to viewer-role shares -- registration data has no read-only use case.

Acceptance criteria:
- A cosigner-role share can fetch the Ledger registration for a shared multisig.
- A viewer-role share still cannot.
- Add a test case to multisigRegistrations' test file covering both roles.
close_reason: canSignMultisig (owner-or-cosigner) replaces ownsMultisig across get/list/save; viewer still uniform-404; cosigner+viewer roles test-pinned. Committed.

title: Add defense-in-depth ownership check to tx_labels/address_labels
description:
src/lib/server/db.ts's tx_labels (lines 92-98: wallet_id, txid, label, created_at) and
address_labels (lines 467-474: wallet_kind, wallet_id, address, label, created_at) have no
user_id column -- access is enforced entirely by callers checking wallet/multisig ownership
BEFORE calling into the labels functions, with no check inside the label functions
themselves. Not broken today (all current call sites gate correctly, confirmed by tracing
every read/write path), but a latent risk: any future route that forgets the ownership check
would leak labels wallet-wide with no second line of defense. Contrast with
src/lib/server/addressBook.ts's saved_addresses, which is already strictly scoped by
user_id at the query level.

Fix: inside the label read/write functions (addressLabels.ts / wherever tx_labels functions
live), add an assertion that re-verifies the caller-supplied wallet_kind/wallet_id belongs to
the requesting user (via the same ownership/share-role check the callers already perform),
even though it's currently redundant -- cheap insurance against a future regression.

Acceptance criteria:
- A unit test calls the label function directly with a wallet_id belonging to a different
  user and confirms it throws/denies, independent of any route-level gating.
close_reason: getAddressLabels/setAddressLabel now take userId and re-check access internally (LabelAccessError); cross-user + role-tier denial test-pinned; tx_labels already had the internal getWallet gate. Committed.

title: Restrict multisig address-labels write access to cosigner/owner
description:
The multisig address-labels route currently lets a 'viewer' share PUT (write) labels, not
just GET (read) them -- broader access than a role named "viewer" implies. Reading shared
annotations is explicitly intentional for viewers (see (app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/
+page.server.ts:78's "shared annotations for this vault" comment) -- this bead is about
WRITE access specifically, which was never explicitly designed, just inherited from using the
same gate for both GET and PUT.

This is a policy decision, not purely a bug -- confirm the intended behavior with product/Alex
before shipping (default assumption below is the more conservative reading: viewers read,
cosigner/owner write).

Fix: gate the PUT handler in the multisig address-labels route on getSignableMultisig (or an
explicit owner-or-cosigner check) instead of getViewableMultisig, while leaving GET on the
current viewer-inclusive gate.

Acceptance criteria:
- A viewer-role share gets 403 on PUT to the address-labels route but still 200 on GET.
- Cosigner-role share and owner are unaffected (PUT still works for them).
- Add a test case covering all three roles x both HTTP methods.
close_reason: PUT gated on getSignableMultisig (403 for viewer, 404 for outsider), GET unchanged; 3-role x 2-method route test added. Shipped the bead's conservative default (viewers read, cosigner/owner write). Committed.

title: Scope /admin/activity's unfiltered cross-user detail exposure
description:
src/lib/server/activity.ts's listAllActivity (lines 276-329) returns every user's event
stream -- including the full, untruncated `detail` JSON column (e.g. full txid/multisig id
for a broadcast) -- with no user_id filter applied unless the caller explicitly passes
filters.userId (lines 290-295). The rendered /admin/activity page happens to truncate the
displayed message, but GET /api/admin/activity hit directly returns the full unfiltered
payload to any admin -- more than the UI implies is available. On a multi-admin instance,
every admin (not just the operator) can pull every other user's detail-level activity via the
API even if they'd never click through the truncated UI.

Fix: truncate/redact the `detail` field server-side in listAllActivity by default, to match
what the admin UI actually displays, and require an explicit opt-in query param (e.g.
?includeDetail=true) for the full payload if there's a genuine support/debugging need for it.

Acceptance criteria:
- Default GET /api/admin/activity response matches what the admin UI displays (truncated
  message, no raw detail JSON) unless the new explicit param is passed.
- Add a test asserting the default response shape excludes/truncates detail, and that the
  opt-in param restores it.
close_reason: detail nulled by default in listAllActivity; ?includeDetail=true opt-in on the API; default-vs-opt-in shape test-pinned. Committed.

title: Reduce users.last_login granularity in admin user list
description:
src/lib/server/admin.ts's listUsers() (~lines 7-36) returns exact last_login timestamps to
every admin via /admin/users -- more granular behavioral tracking than a "who has an account"
admin page needs, especially since on a multi-admin instance every admin sees it, not just
the operator.

Fix: replace the exact timestamp in the admin users list response with a coarser bucket (e.g.
"active in last 30 days" / "inactive 30+ days" / "never logged in"), or gate the exact
timestamp behind a per-row expand action instead of showing it in the default table.

Acceptance criteria:
- /admin/users default view shows a coarse activity signal, not an exact timestamp.
- Update admin.test.ts to assert the coarse-bucket behavior.
close_reason: lastActivity bucket (recent/inactive/never, 30-day window) replaces exact lastLogin in AdminUserInfo + /admin/users UI; bucketing + no-raw-timestamp test-pinned in admin.test.ts. Committed.

title: Redact email/IP from auth, recovery, and rate-limit logs
description:
Four call sites log raw PII outside src/lib/server/logger.ts's REDACT_KEYS list (lines
180-205, which already covers password/token/secret/xprv/mnemonic/etc. but not email or ip):
- src/lib/server/auth.ts:191 -- log.warn({ event: 'password_login_failed', email:
  normalizedEmail }, ...) on every failed login attempt.
- src/lib/server/auth.ts:194-199 -- log.warn({ event: 'password_login_denied', email:
  normalizedEmail, userId: row.id }, ...) on disabled-account login.
- src/lib/server/recovery.ts:387 -- log.warn({ userId, email }, 'Admin recovery login via
  environment variable') inside recordBreakGlassLogin.
- src/lib/server/rateLimit.ts:219 -- log.warn({ ..., ip }, 'invalid invite code submitted')
  inside noteInviteFailure (line 217).

These are all readable by any admin via /admin/logs (admin-gated per requireAdmin, but
visible to every admin on a multi-admin instance -- not just the operator) just from normal
login/recovery/invite traffic.

Fix: add `email` and `ip` to logger.ts's REDACT_KEYS array (lines 180-205) so pino's redact
mechanism strips them at every call site automatically (simpler and more future-proof than
hand-editing each of the four sites, and protects against new call sites making the same
mistake). Confirm all four sites still log userId where available (already true at 3 of 4)
so events remain traceable to an account without the raw PII.

Acceptance criteria:
- A fresh log run through all four code paths shows "[redacted]" in place of email/ip values.
- Add/extend a logger test asserting REDACT_KEYS includes 'email' and 'ip'.
close_reason: email+ip added to REDACT_KEYS (pino redaction covers all four sites + future ones); logger.test.ts pins all four call-site shapes with userId preserved. Committed.

title: RBF signaling boundary 0xfffffffe not pinned in tests
description: feeBump.ts:58/210 — tests cover 0xfffffffd (accept) and 0xffffffff (reject) but never the exact BIP-125 threshold 0xfffffffe. If >= were refactored to >, both tests pass but a 0xfffffffe tx is wrongly treated as replaceable. Cairn builds a replacement the network silently ignores — user thinks stuck tx was bumped. Fix: add three-fixture boundary test (0xfffffffd accept, 0xfffffffe reject not_rbf, 0xffffffff reject).
close_reason: DONE (test coverage added; NO code bug — false alarm corrected). Added src/lib/server/feeBump.rbfBoundary.test.ts (10 tests) pinning the BIP-125 boundary. IMPORTANT: a Sonnet subagent initially misread BIP-125 and flagged an off-by-one in feeBump.ts:210 (>= vs >). VERIFIED AGAINST Bitcoin Core policy/rbf.cpp SignalsOptInRBF (MAX_BIP125_RBF_SEQUENCE=0xfffffffd) and Cairn's own consistent convention (psbt.ts RBF_SEQUENCE=0xfffffffd, chain/index.ts rbf = seq<0xfffffffe, types.ts): a tx signals RBF iff nSequence<=0xfffffffd (<0xfffffffe). feeBump.ts (reject when seq>=0xfffffffe) is CORRECT. 0xfffffffe does NOT signal RBF. Changing >= to > would have been a real money-path bug. Corrected the TEST to pin 0xfffffffe on the non-signaling side; feeBump.ts unchanged. DO NOT 'fix' the >= — it is spec-correct. All 10 tests pass.

title: Nostr instance private key (nostr_sender_privkey) stored in plaintext with no encryption at rest or rotation
description: src/lib/server/channels/nostr.ts's getOrCreateSenderSecretKey() persists a freshly generated secp256k1 secret key via setSetting('nostr_sender_privkey', bytesToHex(sk)) into the flat, unencrypted settings table (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT NOT NULL — same pattern as core_rpc_pass). Unlike a rotatable relay password, this is a permanent signing/identity key: publishEncryptedDM() feeds it directly into nip44 conversation-key derivation and event signing. Any read access to the SQLite file (backup copy, disk image, misconfigured file permissions, path traversal, or another compromised in-process component) yields the raw key, letting an attacker impersonate the instance's Nostr identity indefinitely (send DMs as 'Cairn' to any recipient) with no rotation mechanism (only regenerated if absent/corrupt). This mirrors an existing project convention (core_rpc_pass is stored the same way) so it's not a new class of bug, but it's newly applied to a signing/identity key rather than a rotatable credential, which is a materially higher-value target if the DB ever leaks. Consider at minimum a rotation admin action, and evaluate whether instance secrets broadly warrant at-rest encryption given this precedent is now used for an identity key, not just relay passwords.
close_reason: nostr_sender_privkey is now stored via encryptSecret (AES-256-GCM at-rest envelope, same scheme as per-user SMTP passwords) instead of plaintext 64-hex, so a leaked settings DB is inert without the instance key file. Backward-compatible: a legacy plaintext key is adopted once and re-persisted encrypted (identity preserved, silent upgrade); an undecryptable stored key fails CLOSED (null) rather than silently minting a new identity. Added rotateSenderSecretKey() + admin-only POST /api/admin/nostr-identity rotation action for suspected-leak response. Tests cover encryption-at-rest, migration, fail-closed, and rotation (19 pass).

title: Caravan round-trip loses unknown-origin key paths
description: multisigExport.ts — export masks unknown-origin keys to fabricated m/0/0/0/0 for Caravan compatibility. Import reads literally with no recognition of the masking. Round-tripping Cairn's own export of an unknown-origin key silently turns it into a concrete (wrong) path. No test exercises the full round-trip. Fix: add sentinel or metadata marking masked paths, or normalize m/0/0/0/0 back to unknown on import.

title: Wallet-to-explorer links honor the explorer feature flag
description: With the 'explorer' flag disabled, tx/address links on wallet detail, send-sent, and activity surfaces still point into the gated route — closed cairn-8dup covered hiding UI elements on flagged pages, not inbound links from other surfaces. Either hide the links or swap to a non-explorer detail fallback, with cairn-8dup-style 'disabled by administrator' messaging on direct navigation.
close_reason: Fixed in commit (pending): guarded the 5 remaining unguarded explorer links (activity/+page.svelte:347,352 tx/block links; wallets/[id]/+page.svelte:820,1121,1141 tx/address/saved-tx links) using the svelte:element degrade-to-span pattern with a derived explorerEnabled = data.flags?.explorer !== false, matching RecentActivity.svelte's approach. npm run check: 0 errors, 0 warnings.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Wallet-side explorer links flag-gated in 6 files (ed873e6, merged). Deferred: wallets/[id]/+page.svelte L797/L1099/L1120 and activity/+page.svelte L340/L342 — files temporarily owned by double-spend track. Post-merge visual QA pending.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Follow-up code survey 2026-07-12 (infra session, single-sig-full-wallet branch): remaining unguarded links re-located at CURRENT line numbers (previous comment's L797/L1099/L1120/L340/L342 have since shifted): - src/routes/(app)/activity/+page.svelte:347 (tx link) and :352 (block link) — no flags.explorer guard. - src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte:820 (tx link), :1121 (recipient address link), :1141 (saved-tx link) — none guarded. Reference implementation already in the codebase - wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.svelte:771-772 gates its equivalent tx link: {#if data.flags?.explorer !== false} ... {:else} ... {/if} Preferred pattern (degrade, not hide) - RecentActivity.svelte:21,31-33 uses a derived flag and to swap for
when the flag is off: const explorerEnabled = $derived(page.data.flags?.explorer !== false); ... This is preferred over hiding the link outright since the layout guard already 403s direct navigation to /explorer/** when the flag is off - degrading to a non-interactive element (rather than omitting the row) keeps the surrounding info intact. Re-verified the links already marked as gated in prior work are still intact (no regression): wallets/multisig/stateless/+page.svelte:880, wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:1540, and wallets/multisig/[id]/send/+page.svelte:1534 all still carry the flags?.explorer !== false guard. Handoff note: this infra session deliberately did NOT edit activity/+page.svelte or wallets/[id]/+page.svelte - the concurrent UX orchestrator session has uncommitted changes in both files. Wrote docs/HANDOFF-o90e-wallet-explorer-links.md (untracked, not committed) with this same detail for that session to fold into its in-flight edits and close this bead with evidence once done.

title: No server-side logging anywhere -- errors vanish once caught
description:
Checked directly: Cairn has zero server-side logging infrastructure.

- No logging library in package.json (no pino/winston/anything).
- Zero console.* calls anywhere in src/ -- confirmed by grep across the whole tree.
- src/hooks.server.ts is 7 lines (session lookup + resolve) -- no request logging (method/path/status/latency), nothing.
- No handleError hook defined anywhere -- the dedicated SvelteKit mechanism for catching otherwise-unhandled request exceptions does not exist in this codebase. The only safety net is SvelteKit adapter-nodes own unconfigurable default behavior.
- The real problem is upstream of that default, though: 24 separate API-route catch-alls (grep for "status: 502" / "status: 500" under src/routes/api) catch an unexpected error, extract e.message for the client response, and let the actual error object -- and its stack trace -- fall out of scope. These never reach even the (nonexistent) handleError hook or SvelteKits default logging, because application code already fully handled the exception before that could happen.

Net effect: when something genuinely breaks -- a bug, a DB write failure, an Electrum client fault, a broadcast that fails for a reason none of the typed error classes anticipated -- there is currently NO server-side record of it. The self-hosting admin (who has no support line but themselves) finds out only if a user happens to report it, with nothing to debug from afterward.

What Cairn does well and should keep: typed domain errors (PsbtError, BroadcastError, AuthError, LedgerError/TrezorError) with good user-facing messages -- this is a UX strength, not the problem. The gap is purely on the "what does the operator see when something goes wrong" side.

Scope the fix for a self-hosted, privacy-first app -- NOT third-party error-reporting SaaS (Sentry etc. would contradict the self-sovereignty positioning; do not add telemetry that phones home by default). Instead:
1. Add a lightweight structured logger (pino is the standard, low-overhead choice) writing to stdout in a format self-hosters can pipe into whatever they already use (journald, Docker logs, a file, their own log aggregator if they want one).
2. Add a handleError hook in hooks.server.ts that logs any exception that reaches it with enough context (route, method, a request id) before SvelteKit returns its generic error response.
3. Audit the 24 API-route catch-alls: at minimum, log the underlying error server-side before converting it to a client-facing message, rather than only ever surfacing .message and discarding the rest.
4. Consider basic structured request logging (method, path, status, duration) in hooks.server.ts -- valuable for a self-hoster wanting to know whats hitting their instance, independent of whatever a reverse proxy in front of it might separately log.
close_reason:
3 of 4 fix items landed solidly, verified directly: (1) pino structured logger (src/lib/server/logger.ts) with dev-pretty/prod-JSON output, size-rotated local file (10MB x5, bounded), explicit no-secrets-logged discipline and comment. (2) handleError hook in hooks.server.ts logs 5xx exceptions with a short error ID, full context (route/method/userId), and path redaction (txids/addresses truncated) before returning a generic message + error ID to the client. (3) request logging is real: every non-asset request gets method/path/status/duration/userId logged at a severity matching its outcome. Admin log viewer (src/routes/(app)/admin/logs) is properly admin-gated and reads the same rotated file with a bounded tail (2MB) -- no unbounded growth risk. This is a strong, well-designed implementation, not a token fix.

Item 4 (audit the 24 API-route catch-alls) is partially done -- ~30 routes now call log.error() explicitly (multisig broadcast, stateless ops, auth, etc.) but a handful of single-sig routes still have bare catch-and-respond blocks with no log.error() call, most importantly src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/transactions/[txId]/broadcast/+server.ts. Note for the record: these are NOT fully silent anymore -- hooks.server.ts's request-level logging still records a '502 in Nms' line for every request regardless, since that logging happens unconditionally after resolve() -- but the underlying stack trace/exact error is lost for these specific routes since the exception is fully handled locally and never reaches handleError. Filing a precisely-scoped follow-up bead for the remaining routes rather than leaving this one open unchanged, since the overwhelming majority of the original ask is genuinely done.

title: Explorer no-chain state is a dead end: raw '000,000 blocks' placeholder + 'height range' jargon
description:
Evidence: dev instance 2026-07-12, with no chain backend configured, the Explorer page shows a raw unformatted '000,000 blocks' placeholder and jargon like 'height range' instead of a friendly disconnected/setup state. This is a dead end for a brand-new user -- no explanation of what's missing or what to do next. Should follow the same calm 'not connected yet' pattern proposed for Home (see companion bead cairn-7zjo, item 2 of this QA pass) rather than leaking raw placeholder values and internal terminology.

Searched for overlap first: no existing bead matches this specific placeholder/jargon dead-end (searched 'explorer', '000,000 blocks', 'height range' -- only tangential hits like cairn-p2ol (chart axes/labels) and cairn-oqg (P1, explorer jargon tooltips, block/tx/address terms) which covers detail-page jargon, not this empty/disconnected-state placeholder). Filing new; recommend implementing alongside cairn-7zjo for a consistent disconnected-state component reused across Home and Explorer.
close_reason: Admin CTA added to the explorer no-connection banner (Check connection settings -> /admin/settings), live-verified admin vs non-admin rendering desktop+mobile. See comments for detail.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Fixed 0ff5d53 (merged): 3 sub-bugs -- eternal 000,000 skeleton on failed load, wrong 'Nothing found at this height range' message when never-synced, dead Retry on paged views (now invalidate('cairn:chain')); calm plain-language banner, no raw error strings. Never-synced branch verified by code (sandbox had live Esplora).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Follow-up fix (this pass): added an admin-only secondary action on the explorer index no-connection banner. src/routes/(app)/explorer/+page.server.ts now returns isAdmin (locals.user?.isAdmin ?? false, same pattern as tx/block/mempool routes). src/routes/(app)/explorer/+page.svelte:398-425 wraps Retry + a new 'Check connection settings' link (href=/admin/settings) in .chain-error-actions, shown only when data.isAdmin. Non-admins see Retry only, unchanged.

QA: live-verified on the shared dev instance (localhost:5173), which was genuinely Electrum-disconnected during this pass (real ECONNREFUSED, not simulated) -- seeded two throwaway DB users (qa-obg6-admin, qa-obg6-user; scrypt-hashed passwords, recovery/agreement rows inserted directly to clear onboarding gates) to log in as each role, confirmed via read_page + get_page_text at both 1280x800 and 375x812:
