- Pulled the in-container app-store checkout from 376de2d -> 66b79f5.
- Pre-update: apps.list.query showed heartwood-bitcoin version 0.2.16, state
  "ready". Pre-update DB: users=1, wallets=0, settings table empty (no
  electrum/core_rpc provenance rows).
- Ran `umbreld client apps.update.mutate --appId heartwood-bitcoin` -> true.
- Post-update (after ~45s async settle — apps.list.query and the rendered
  compose file both lagged briefly behind the actual container swap):
  apps.list.query version = "0.2.17". Rendered compose pins
  `ghcr.io/alexm223/cairn:0.2.17@sha256:8868f814...e30870` (exact digest
  match) and carries CAIRN_PLATFORM=umbrel.
- Running container: docker inspect heartwood-bitcoin_web_1 Image = exact
  digest match. docker ps: "Up ... (healthy)" for both web and app_proxy.
- Health: `curl -sk https://127.0.0.1:5588/api/health` -> HTTP 200
  {"status":"ok"}.
- UI serves: `curl -sk https://127.0.0.1:5588/` -> HTTP 302 -> /login
  (expected unauthenticated redirect, not an error page).
- Startup log: {"tag":"startup","version":"0.2.17","node":"v22.23.1",...,
  "chain":{"electrumHost":"electrum.blockstream.info","electrumPort":50002,
  "electrumTls":true,"coreRpcUrl":null,"coreRpcUser":null},
  "adminBootstrapped":true,"instanceMode":"solo",...}

WAVE B4 PROBE OBSERVATION: Bitcoin Core is NOT installed on umbrel-s15 (only
heartwood-bitcoin app-data exists; no bitcoin app-data dir, no bitcoin
container in docker ps). Post-update startup log shows coreRpcUrl: null,
coreRpcUser: null — no core_rpc_detected marker, no provenance card data
written. This is the documented negative-path case: the Wave B detect probe
correctly stays silent when no Core RPC is reachable at the fixed Umbrel
Docker-DNS address, rather than erroring or showing a false-positive card.
Wave A Electrum probe was also negative-path this pass (no electrs/stub
container present on umbrel_main_network this time — fell back cleanly to
electrum.blockstream.info, matching the documented Wave A negative-path
behavior from cairn-x26e). Neither is a defect; both are the designed
silent-fallback behavior with no local chain backend present.

PERSISTENCE CHECK: users count 1 -> 1, wallets count 0 -> 0 across the
update (identical DB via node:sqlite query pre/post). Data survived.

CLOSING: CI shipped the real multi-arch image, app-repo packaging mirror
bumped and committed (0c54b78), store repo bumped and pushed by a concurrent
session (66b79f5, verified matching), and a live umbreld-managed update
0.2.16 -> 0.2.17 was exercised end-to-end on umbrel-s15 with a healthy,
digest-verified, data-persisted result. B4 probe behavior confirmed correct
for the no-Core-RPC-installed case.

title: B4 positive-path verification: install Bitcoin app on umbrel-s15 and verify core_rpc_detected marker fires
description: Wave B Core-RPC follow-up. Install the Bitcoin Core app on the umbrel-s15 local umbrelOS test container and verify the core_rpc_detected marker actually fires (positive-path check; prior Wave B work only verified detect-and-surface / negative path, evidence-verified as silent auto-connect being impossible). See MEMORY.md Cairn infra session 2026-07-12 for context.

title: Nostr relay URLs have no SSRF protection
description: channels/nostr.ts:263 — user-supplied relay URLs open WebSocket connections via SimplePool.publish() with no IP-range validation. A user configuring wss://169.254.169.254/ causes server to open WebSocket to cloud metadata endpoint. safeFetch is HTTP-only and doesn't cover WebSocket. Fix: validate relay hostnames against blocked IP ranges at config-save time, translating wss:// to https:// for the DNS/IP check using existing checkTargetUrl.
close_reason: Stale duplicate of cairn-zn7z (fixed and merged in 4418db9 before this bead was even filed). See comment for full evidence.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
TRACKER: `br show cairn-zn7z` = CLOSED 2026-07-09 (merge 4418db9). `br show cairn-p9c3` = OPEN P1, created 2026-07-08 — one day BEFORE the fix merged. Same labels (review-2026-07-07, security), same location (channels/nostr.ts:263), identical finding (user relay URLs → SimplePool.publish WebSocket, no IP-range validation). p9c3's requested fix ("validate at config-save time … using existing checkTargetUrl") is literally what zn7z implemented, plus more. Classic stale reviewer duplicate.

FIX IS LIVE: 4418db9 (merge) → efce11d, confirmed `git merge-base --is-ancestor 4418db9 HEAD` on branch single-sig-full-wallet, HEAD 197072a. Working-tree code read directly.

SEND-TIME GUARD (the exact function p9c3 cites, formerly nostr.ts:263, now publishEncryptedDM): src/lib/server/channels/nostr.ts:295-309 runs filterAllowedRelays() → checkRelayUrl() on EVERY relay before any socket opens; if all blocked it returns {ok:false, retryable:false} and never dials. filterAllowedRelays defined at nostr.ts:105-116. Both entry points funnel through it: send() nostr.ts:415, test() nostr.ts:432. Grep confirms SimplePool + pool.publish() appear ONLY in nostr.ts (pool.publish at line 344, createPool at 336 inside publishEncryptedDM) — there is no sibling socket-opening path. The test route api/notifications/channels/[channel]/test/+server.ts:42 calls plugin.test → publishEncryptedDM (guarded).

SAVE-TIME GUARD: src/routes/api/notifications/channels/[channel]/+server.ts:119 validateOutboundTargets() (defined 278-306) calls checkRelayUrl() per nostr relay (and checkTargetHost for SMTP), returning 400 before the INSERT at line 124 — exactly the config-save-time check p9c3 asked for.

GATE IMPL: ssrf.ts:241 checkRelayUrl = checkTargetUrl(rawUrl,{scheme:'ws'}); checkTargetUrl:214 enforces ws:/wss: then checkTargetHost:175 resolves every A/AAAA record and rejects if ANY is blocked; isBlockedIPv4:127 covers 127/8,10/8,172.16/12,192.168/16,169.254/16, plus 100.64.0.0/10 CGNAT (cairn-pihb) and 0/8; isBlockedAddress handles IPv4-mapped IPv6. Tests exist: ssrf.test.ts ("checkRelayUrl — Nostr relays"), nostr.test.ts ("relay SSRF guard (cairn-zn7z)" — private-resolve drop, all-blocked non-retryable, CGNAT literal, non-ws scheme), server.test.ts ("save-time SSRF guard").

SIBLING-PATH CHECK (divergence found, but NOT live): admin route src/routes/api/admin/notifications/+server.ts:106-116 saves nostr_default_relays with a regex-only scheme check (/^wss?:\/\//) and NO checkRelayUrl — diverging from the per-user PUT path's save-time SSRF check. However this does NOT create a live SSRF: instanceDefaultRelays() (nostr.ts:251) feeds those defaults into publishEncryptedDM, whose send-time filterAllowedRelays backstops ALL relay sources (user config, instance defaults, built-in defaults) before any socket opens. It is also admin-only and off-by-default. So it is at most a minor defense-in-depth inconsistency for a friendlier early rejection, not the user-supplied-relay vulnerability p9c3 describes, and not exploitable.

RECOMMENDED TRACKER ACTION: close cairn-p9c3 as a duplicate of cairn-zn7z (fixed in 4418db9). Optional low-priority follow-up (separate from p9c3): add checkRelayUrl to the admin nostrDefaultRelays save path in api/admin/notifications/+server.ts:106-116 for save-time parity.

title: Mobile nav (horizontal scroll tab bar) has no visual affordance hinting more items are off-screen
description: On viewports <=768px, src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte:338-359 turns the sidebar into a horizontally-scrollable row of nav items (overflow-x: auto). At 375px width, 'Settings' (and part of 'Activity') sit fully off-screen with no fade/gradient/arrow cue that the bar scrolls. Functional (verified via scrollWidth vs clientWidth: 556 vs 375) but not very discoverable -- a first-time mobile user may not realize Settings exists. Low priority polish: consider a subtle edge-fade mask or scroll-shadow indicator.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: mobile nav strip now has a dynamic edge-fade scroll cue (bg covers pinned local + fades pinned scroll) so off-screen items are discoverable ((app)/+layout.svelte).

title: Referrals: tests for fallback precedence + flag gating
description: Test getDeviceBuyUrl() precedence (admin override present -> use it; blank -> fall back to official store constant). Test multisig_service_referrals CRUD + active-row filtering. Test that all referral UI (not troubleshooting links) disappears when referral_links flag is off.
close_reason: Built + committed: schema/flag/tabs in 024542f, full feature (referrals constants + server helpers, /admin/referral-settings, wizard buy links, managed-service card, signer buy/troubleshooting links, wallet-detail Need-help expandable, 19 tests) in 8c974f7. Full suite + svelte-check green.

title: SSRF guard missing Tailscale CGNAT range 100.64.0.0/10
description: channels/ssrf.ts:113-126 — isBlockedIPv4 checks 127/8, 10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16, 169.254/16, 0/8 but falls through for 100.64.0.0/10. Cairn is explicitly deployed with Tailscale. ntfy echoes up to 500 bytes of non-2xx response back to user, making this a partial-readback SSRF oracle against tailnet services. Also covers Alibaba metadata (100.100.100.200). Fix: add if (a === 100 && b >= 64 && b <= 127) return true.
close_reason: Merged 4418db9: 100.64.0.0/10 added to isBlockedIPv4 in ssrf.ts. Test coverage in ssrf.test.ts.

title: admin deleteUser throws raw FK error when target created invites or updated feature flags
description: deleteUser (src/lib/server/admin.ts:98-107) does a bare DELETE FROM users with no pre-cleanup of invites.created_by / feature_flags.updated_by / user_feature_flags.updated_by — unlike deleteOwnAccount (accountDeletion.ts:66-68) and resetInstance (patched in cairn-hl87). Empirically confirmed: throws raw "FOREIGN KEY constraint failed" (not AuthError), victim row survives, admin UI would surface the raw SQLite string. Fix: mirror accountDeletion.ts pre-cleanup. Evidence: probe test in src/lib/server/destructiveOps.test.ts (Group 1) on branch test/qa-wave-2026-07-12, worktree C:\dev\cairn-testqa.
close_reason: Fixed in 6aff163: server-side user-deletion path hardened (FK-clean purge, robust last-admin guard incl. disabled sole admin, shared-multisig owner-deletion guard + cosigner notification). 46 targeted tests green.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: FIXED in 6aff163 (branch single-sig-full-wallet). Root cause: admin.ts deleteUser did a bare DELETE FROM users with no pre-cleanup of the three non-cascade user FKs (invites.created_by, feature_flags.updated_by, user_feature_flags.updated_by), so a target who created an invite or touched a feature flag triggered a raw 'FOREIGN KEY constraint failed'. Fix: both deleteUser and deleteOwnAccount now route through userDeletion.ts purgeUserRow(), which DELETEs the invites and NULLs the two updated_by authors inside the same transaction as the user delete, then clears the no-FK notified_txids rows. Schema audit confirmed these three are the ONLY non-cascade user FKs (all others ON DELETE CASCADE; multisig_keys.assigned_user_id is SET NULL). Evidence: destructiveOps.test.ts PROBE flipped to green ('deleteUser cleans up no-action FKs instead of throwing'), plus userDeletion.test.ts covers both entry points. Targeted vitest: 46 passed across userDeletion/accountDeletion/admin/deleteCascade/destructiveOps.

title: NotificationPanel SSE missing reconnect hardening from d266294
description: NotificationPanel.svelte:169-194 — opens its own separate EventSource for /api/notifications/stream with none of the visibility-change/stale-timer reconnect hardening that commit d266294 added to liveBlocks.ts. This component is mounted once in the persistent sidebar. The exact bug class (OS kills connection while backgrounded, auto-reconnect fails) is still live for the unread notification badge. Fix: use liveBlocks.ts pattern or extract shared reconnect logic.
close_reason: Fixed via commit 60c867e (worktree agent-a7cef3e1ee4439962). Extracted liveBlocks.ts's visibility/staleness SSE reconnect pattern into shared src/lib/sseReconnect.ts (createResilientEventSource), NotificationPanel now uses it. Verified: vitest 1880 passed/1 skipped, svelte-check 0/0.

title: Missing regression test: notify() dropping external-channel delivery on partial failure (cairn-s0p5)
description: cairn-s0p5 (P1, closed in 46dd16f) fixed notify() so a failure partway through (e.g. the in-app activity DB insert succeeding but external-channel enqueue throwing) doesn't silently drop tx_large/security_failed_login/admin_restore/backup_missing/key_health_due alerts. Current src/lib/server/notifications.test.ts does not cover this specific partial-failure scenario. Add a test to notifications.test.ts: mock recordActivity() (or the in-app write) to throw partway through notify(), and assert external channels are still enqueued/attempted independently rather than the whole call aborting silently.
close_reason: Committed in 8603ac6: multisigShares.test.ts (first coverage), contacts.test.ts share-revocation, notifications.test.ts stage isolation, broadcast route malformed-JSON 400 + log.error tests, walletsDraft.test.ts buildDraft/coin-control coverage.

title: Mandatory recovery setup UX after first passkey
description: After first passkey add: MANDATORY interstitial — 'Before we continue, save your recovery phrase' with 'I've written this down in a safe place' checkbox, then recovery codes with Download. Admins CANNOT skip; regular users may skip but get a persistent warning until set up.
close_reason: Shipped (659e24c): mandatory /recovery-setup wizard after first passkey (phrase behind write-it-down gate, then downloadable codes), admin no-skip, persistent settings banner until complete, secrets shown once. Caveat: forced first-passkey redirect emits next:'/recovery-setup' but no passkey-signup UI consumes it yet — enforcement rests on the banner + wizard; noted for when passkey signup lands.

title: Signing-mass device profile constants duplicated between client and server
description: src/lib/server/bitcoin/signingMass.ts defines DEVICE_PROFILES (base/perInput/rate constants per hardware device, used to estimate signing time for large multisig parent transactions); src/routes/(app)/vaults/[id]/send/+page.svelte separately hardcodes a client-side MIRROR_PROFILES (~lines 289-293) matching the same values, presumably so the UI can render an estimate without a round trip. These will silently drift if one is updated without the other -- there's no test or type-level guarantee keeping them in sync. Either export the server constants for client-safe reuse (if they don't depend on server-only code) or add a test that asserts the two constant sets stay identical.
close_reason: Fixed in 0072dc7: signing-mass device profiles + estimator math extracted to /shared/signingMass (environment-neutral), imported by both the server module and the client mirror; pure extraction, no numeric change (signingMass.test.ts still green).

title: wallet_config_export flag OFF doesn't hide Wallet config/Descriptor links on wallet page
description:
Found during cairn-1ytc flag-matrix QA pass (wallet-flags subset, port 5314, HEARTWOOD_DB=data/qa-matrix.db).

REPRO: Admin -> Feature flags -> toggle "Export wallet config" OFF (wallet_config_export flag). As a plain user, visit /wallets/[id] (single-sig wallet detail page). The "Wallet config (JSON)" and "Descriptor (.txt)" links are still rendered and clickable -- confirmed live via DOM query (configLinkPresent: true, descLinkPresent: true) right after toggling the flag off. The underlying endpoints DO correctly enforce the flag server-side (confirmed live: GET /api/wallets/1/config -> 403, GET /api/wallets/1/descriptor -> 403), so clicking either link produces a dead/broken download (raw 403) instead of the link being hidden.

ROOT CAUSE (code-read confirmed): src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte has NO check for `data.flags?.wallet_config_export` anywhere in the file (grep confirms zero matches). Compare to the neighboring csv_export flag in the SAME file, which IS properly gated: `{#if data.flags?.csv_export !== false}` at line 1313 hides the CSV download link when off. wallet_config_export has no equivalent guard around the "Wallet config (JSON)" / "Descriptor (.txt)" links (and likely also src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/[id]/backup-pdf, caravan, coldcard export links, all of which share the wallet_config_export requireFeature() gate server-side per src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/[id]/*.ts).

Same missing-gate pattern confirmed via code grep on the multisig wallet detail page: src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.svelte also has csv_export gated (line 1082) but no wallet_config_export check -- so the multisig wallet's config/descriptor/caravan/coldcard/backup-pdf export links are equally unguarded client-side. Not live-verified on multisig (no multisig test wallet seeded this session), only single-sig was live-confirmed.

IMPACT: same class of regression the parent bead cairn-1ytc calls out ("History shows this area regresses: cairn-8dup, cairn-1x3w, cairn-jyh7") -- API correctly gated, UI not following the flag. Minor severity (no data leak, just a dead-link/confusing-error UX) but a real functional gap.

FIX (not done here -- flagged only, owned by another session per current branch ownership): wrap the "Wallet config (JSON)" / "Descriptor (.txt)" links (and any other wallet_config_export-gated export affordances) in both src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte and src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.svelte in a `{#if data.flags?.wallet_config_export !== false}` check, mirroring the existing csv_export pattern in the same files.

Filed by: OPS QA session (cairn-1ytc wallet-flags subset), 2026-07-12.

title: Explorer index missing forward-looking projected-blocks strip (buried at mempool/blocks)
description: Block-explorer elevation audit (2026-07-12): the Explorer index (src/routes/(app)/explorer/+page.svelte) shows only past 'rings' plus one dashed pending row. The mempool.space-style forward-looking projected-next-blocks view -- the signature move users open an explorer to check -- lives two clicks deep at /explorer/mempool -> 'Visualize' (src/routes/(app)/explorer/mempool/blocks/+page.svelte), which already has an excellent keyboard-navigable treemap with fee legend, but it's buried. Fix: surface a compact projected-next-rings strip on the index itself, fed by the same mempoolBlocks snapshot already loaded, linking into the full viz page. Best-in-class: mempool.space puts this front-and-center on its homepage. Searched for overlap first: br search for 'projected' returned no matches -- filing new. Distinct from cairn-zoz8.14 (Build mempool-blocks projection via Bitcoin Core RPC), which is the backend data-source migration, not this index-surfacing/IA gap.
close_reason:
Shipped: compact "Up next" projected-blocks strip on the explorer index (commit 2abfe1d).

- src/routes/(app)/explorer/+page.server.ts: ExplorerChainData now also
  carries mempoolBlocks (the full projected-blocks array), reusing the
  existing chain_snapshot.mempoolBlocks field already read for the
  dashed pending row -- no new data pipeline.
- src/routes/(app)/explorer/+page.svelte: new "Up next" section between
  the hero and the historical chain strip, showing up to 4 upcoming
  blocks (next ring / ~20/30/40 min, median fee, fee range), each in a
  compact chip; "Full view" links to /explorer/mempool/blocks (the
  treemap viz, matching the bead's "link into the full viz page").
  Hidden entirely (no placeholder/error string) when chain is null or
  mempoolBlocks is empty/absent -- verified by loading the index against
  an empty chain_snapshot (dev cairn.db had none): "Up next" did not
  render, only the existing "can't reach the Bitcoin network" banner
  showed, and Latest rings fell back to its own empty state. No new
  error path introduced.

Visual verification (built-in browser, own tab, throwaway QA account
pw3u-qa@example.com registered via /api/auth/register/password with a
manually-issued invite code since the signup form has the known
cairn-hmi4 silent-fail bug; agreement gate accepted via its ?/accept
form action):
- Seeded dev data/cairn.db's chain_snapshot with a real projected-blocks
  payload (copied from data/dev-fresh.db, a QA fixture db) to render the
  populated state, since the shared dev cairn.db had no chain backend
  reachable.
- 1280x800: accessibility tree + getBoundingClientRect show a "region
  Upcoming blocks" with heading "Up next", a "Full view" link to
  /explorer/mempool/blocks, and 4 chips (next ring / ~20 min / ~30 min /
  ~40 min with fee + range each). Strip box 836x113.75px, positioned
  y=377-491, all 4 chips fit with no horizontal scroll needed
  (rowScrollWidth === rowClientWidth == 836).
- 375x812 (mobile): strip box 339x121px at y=448.6-569.9; the 4 chips
  overflow to a horizontal-scroll row (rowScrollWidth 414 > clientWidth
  339, matching the existing .proj-row pattern used elsewhere in the
  app) rather than stacking vertically. "Latest rings" section head
  starts at y=587.9, well inside the 812px viewport -- core content is
  NOT pushed below the fold.
- Screenshot pixel capture was unavailable this session (known
  environment hazard, browser-QA-hazards memory note); verified instead
  via the accessibility tree + live DOM measurements (getBoundingClientRect),
  which is more precise for the fold/overflow check this bead cares about.

npm run check: 6 pre-existing errors in
src/routes/api/auth/recovery/recoverySetupFlow.test.ts (unrelated,
known, other sessions' in-flight work) -- zero errors/warnings in the
two files this bead touched.

title: Wallet detail backup-status indicator regressed for single-sig wallets (found auditing cairn-xvze)
description: cairn-xvze (closed in fd7427e) fixed wallet/multisig detail pages checking backup status via localStorage instead of the server-tracked wallet_backups table (isBackedUp()). While auditing cairn-xvze for missing-regression-test coverage on 2026-07-06, found that a LATER same-day commit (61e1b9d, 'feat(coinbase): coin-control maturity UI...') silently deleted the isBackedUp wiring from the SINGLE-SIG wallet detail page's load(), incorrectly describing it as dead state in that commit's diff. The multisig detail page is unaffected — only single-sig regressed. Currently the single-sig wallet page shows no server-tracked backup indicator at all, meaning a user could believe they've backed up a wallet (or vice versa) with no accurate signal. Restore the isBackedUp() wiring to the single-sig wallet detail page's load(), matching the multisig page, and add a regression test this time (e.g. asserting data.backedUp reflects the wallet_backups table) so a future refactor can't silently drop it again.
close_reason: Restored isBackedUp() wiring to single-sig detail load() + downloaded badge/optimistic overlay in the page; regression tests in page.server.test.ts pin data.backedUp to wallet_backups (incl. kind scoping). Commit d353f7a.

title: Missing regression test: Bitcoin Core RPC password no longer round-trips to client (cairn-g7a)
description: cairn-g7a (P2, closed in cb72bcc) fixed the Bitcoin Core RPC password round-tripping to the client in plaintext via admin settings, replacing it with getPublicInstanceSettings()'s hasCoreRpcPass flag. Fix confirmed present (settings.ts strips coreRpcPass; the admin settings page/route never serializes the secret; blank submissions preserve the stored value with an explicit clear path). No test covers this. Add a src/lib/server/settings.test.ts: seed a core RPC password via setSetting(), call getPublicInstanceSettings(), and assert the result has no coreRpcPass key and hasCoreRpcPass === true (and false when unset). Also worth a route-level assertion that GET /api/admin/settings's response body never contains the raw password string.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 834945e (admin.test.ts, notifiedTxidsCleanup.test.ts, settings.test.ts, api/admin/{settings,users,notifications}/server.test.ts). All green in full suite.

title: Minor hardcoded values: button text color and border-radius bypass tokens
description:
Two small token-bypass instances found during the design-system audit, safe to batch:
1. src/lib/components/Stepper.svelte and src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte both hardcode color: #201409 (the dark-brown text-on-copper color) instead of a named token -- give this the same treatment as other on-accent text colors, or add an --on-accent token if it does not exist yet.
2. src/routes/(app)/vaults/stateless/+page.svelte (~line 1528, 1537) and src/routes/(app)/vaults/[id]/send/+page.svelte (~1817, 1824) use border-radius: 3px literally instead of var(--radius-chip) (4px) -- align to the existing token unless 3px is deliberately a distinct new size, in which case it should get its own token too rather than staying a magic number.
close_reason: Fixed: --on-accent token added and used in app.css/.btn-primary, Stepper, wallets/new stepper dots; stateless quorum-bar radii use var(--radius-chip). The bead's multisig-send 3px instances no longer exist post-rename; 3px uses in portfolio/explorer charts were outside the bead's scope and may be deliberate. Browser-verified: button renders identical rgb(32,20,9) via the token.

title: Redundant operator-name phrasing on /agreement when admin hasn't set a custom operator name
description: DEFAULT_OPERATOR in src/lib/server/disclosures.ts:17 is 'the operator of this Cairn instance'. The template at src/routes/agreement/+page.svelte:35 renders "This instance is operated by {operator}." which becomes "This instance is operated by the operator of this Cairn instance." for every fresh install until the admin sets a custom operator name in Admin > Settings. Awkward/redundant phrasing shown to every new user by default. Low severity, cosmetic copy issue. Fix options: reword DEFAULT_OPERATOR to something like 'its operator', or restructure the sentence to omit/rephrase gracefully when no custom name is set.
close_reason: Fixed in 779a374

title: Multisig import panel button unresponsive to click (native + JS-dispatched)
description:
While QA-testing Caravan-format wallet import on qa-caravan2.localhost:5388 (multisig wizard step 1, /wallets/multisig/new), the button labeled \"Already have this wallet in another app? Import it\" did not open the paste/upload panel on repeated attempts:

- Native computer left_click on the correct ref (verified via read_page, correct bounding rect ~x628,y427) — no visible change, panel stayed collapsed.
- Direct DOM .click() via javascript_tool on the located <button> element — document.querySelector('textarea') still null afterward.
- Full synthetic pointerdown/mousedown/pointerup/mouseup/click MouseEvent sequence dispatched at the button's exact center coordinates — still no textarea appeared.
- One earlier attempt DID appear to expand the panel momentarily (a textbox + \"Read it\"/\"Upload a wallet file\" buttons were read back once via read_page), but the very next read showed the panel collapsed again with no user-visible cause, and a console error was observed: \"[cairn:0330729c] Internal Error [object Object]\" (exact object payload not captured — DevTools showed only the tag).

CAVEAT: this browser tab was shared with other concurrent QA agents per the orchestrator's notes (background-tab throttling was suspected and worked around for read_page/computer calls elsewhere in the same session), so there is some chance this is a throttling/rendering artifact rather than a genuine app bug. However .click() and full synthetic event dispatch are JS-level and shouldn't depend on paint/focus, which weighs toward a real issue with the accordion/panel toggle state (possibly a Svelte reactive-state race, or the \"Internal Error\" swallowing the intended state change).

Impact if real: this is the ONLY UI entry point for Caravan/Unchained wallet-config import (POST /api/wallets/multisig/import backs it) — if it's broken for real users (not just this sandbox), nobody can import an existing multisig wallet from the browser at all. The API endpoint itself works correctly (verified separately: 2-of-2 P2WSH import, export round-trip, and testnet-network rejection all passed cleanly via direct fetch()).

Repro steps:
1. Go to /wallets/multisig/new as a logged-in user.
2. Click \"Already have this wallet in another app? Import it\".
3. Expect: textarea + \"Read it\" + \"Upload a wallet file\" to appear. Observed: panel stays on the step-1 education content; a couple of attempts intermittently seemed to flash the panel state before reverting.

Recommend: re-verify in a dedicated non-shared browser tab/session before triaging severity; if confirmed, check the accordion/import-panel toggle state logic in the multisig wizard's +page.svelte and the console \"Internal Error\" source.

title: Extract shared TxRow.svelte + EmptyState.svelte components (dedup wallet/multisig/explorer/activity row + empty-state markup)
Devices & sessions page always shows 'expires just now' regardless of actual session expiry
description:
Reproduced on Heartwood v0.2.0 at umbrel.local:3211 (browser QA), Settings > Devices & sessions (/settings/devices).

Every session row shows "expires just now" no matter how old the session is or how far in the future it actually expires -- verified across 8 sessions ranging from "Signed in 12m ago" to "Signed in 1d ago", all showing the identical "expires just now".

Root cause: src/routes/(app)/settings/devices/+page.svelte:58 calls `since(s.expiresAt)` where `since()` (line 11) wraps `timeAgo()` from src/lib/format.ts:29. timeAgo() computes `diff = now - unixSeconds` and is designed for PAST timestamps ("X ago"). expiresAt is a FUTURE timestamp, so diff is always negative, and timeAgo's first branch `if (diff < 5) return 'just now'` catches every negative diff -- so the expiry column is permanently stuck on "just now" and never conveys real information (a session could expire in 5 minutes or 30 days and this would look identical).

Suggested fix: add a forward-looking formatter (e.g. `expiresIn(unixSeconds)`) that computes `unixSeconds - now` and renders "in Xm" / "in Xh" / "in Xd" etc., and use that instead of timeAgo() for the expiresAt column. Don't reuse timeAgo() for future timestamps.

Repro: sign in on a couple of devices/browsers, visit /settings/devices, compare the "expires" text across rows with different sign-in ages.
close_reason: Fixed as part of the Track F merge (1a76aea). Added expiresIn() to src/lib/format.ts (forward-looking mirror of timeAgo -- computes unixSeconds - now instead of now - unixSeconds) and switched the devices page's expires column to it; timeAgo() stays reserved for genuinely past timestamps (createdAt/lastSeen). 12 new test cases in format.test.ts, all pass.

title: W2A- multisig balance summary lags actual confirmation status for multiple refresh cycles
description: Repro: fund a fresh 2-of-2 collaborative multisig (W2A-vault, multisigId=1 on data/vault-e2e-wave2a.db), mine a confirming block, then poll GET /api/wallets/multisig/:id (after POST .../refresh) repeatedly. Observed the utxos[] array correctly show height=<tip> (i.e. confirmed) while the top-level balance object simultaneously reported {confirmed:0, unconfirmed:<full amount>} for 6+ polls over ~18s, even after explicitly calling the /refresh endpoint each time. Same pattern repeated after a spend's confirming block: utxos/tx status showed confirmed immediately via electrs (GET :3002/tx/<txid>/status), but GET .../multisig/1 balance stayed at the PRE-spend total for one refresh+poll cycle, and only settled to the correct post-spend amount after a second explicit refresh ~5s later. Both users (owner and cosigner) saw the same stale value simultaneously, so it isn't a per-viewer cache split. Expected: balance.confirmed reflects the same confirmation status the utxos list and electrs already show, ideally within one refresh call. Actual: can take 2+ manual refreshes and 10-15s to converge, which would read as 'my money disappeared' or 'my spend didn't go through' to a real user watching the wallet page. Verified 2026-07-13 on branch single-sig-full-wallet, port 5399, txids 6292bb579d1ae56bf48e4895f2ea82c4ead59511a613bf23bed409ca89c491ed and ccfc025c1281ea328316698fe0b26a8af98c927e6576623c026b32a5e4d7057d.

title: First-run onboarding session drop: recovery codes POST 401 while phrase POST 200 moments earlier
description: Seen during automated first-run signup on dev-fresh2.db; original signup session dropped mid-onboarding, re-login fixed it; possibly a test-harness artifact (JS requestSubmit signup + browser-tool httpOnly-cookie quirk) -- needs one clean manual signup repro before treating as real.
notes: Downgraded P2 -> P3 after server-side investigation: could not reproduce a session/auth-gate bug through the real per-request pipeline (see comment log). Likely a QA-automation hard-navigation cookie-drop artifact, not a product bug. Pending one manual real-browser click-through confirmation before further downgrade/close.
close_reason: Not-a-bug: confirmed distinct from cairn-wrph/cairn-9njl (different code path, unaffected by that fix). Server-side investigation + recoverySetupFlow.test.ts (3/3 passing) already showed the real per-request pipeline is correct; symptom matches a known browser-automation hard-navigation cookie-drop artifact, not a product bug. See bead comment for full reasoning.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: REPRODUCED INDEPENDENTLY on a second instance (dev-fresh.db, preview build, different agent, different automation path): POST /api/auth/recovery/phrase 200 (user=2) then immediate POST /api/auth/recovery/codes 401 persistently until a fresh password login. First admin's identical flow succeeded; second admin's failed. Hypothesis: the phrase endpoint rotates/invalidates the session cookie. Blocks a real user from finishing mandatory admin recovery setup without re-login. Priority raised P3 -> P2 and title updated to drop NEEDS-REPRO since it is now independently reproduced. Server auth code = other session's turf; this bead is the handoff.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
INVESTIGATION RESULT (server-side, cairn-qlxs): could not reproduce a server-side session/auth-gate bug on this path -- recommend downgrading/relabeling as a QA-tooling artifact rather than a product bug, pending one more manual real-browser confirmation.

What I checked (server-side only; territory src/lib/server/**, src/routes/api/**, hooks.server.ts):
- POST /api/auth/recovery/phrase and POST /api/auth/recovery/codes (src/routes/api/auth/recovery/phrase/+server.ts, .../codes/+server.ts) both call plain requireUser(event) (src/lib/server/api.ts:77-82) and otherwise only write to account_recovery_phrases / account_recovery_codes (src/lib/server/recovery.ts). Neither touches the sessions table, destroyUserSessions(), or the session cookie in any way.
- hooks.server.ts's (app) route-group gate (appGateRedirect, src/lib/server/appGate.ts) only runs when event.route.id starts with '/(app)'. These two routes live at top-level /api/auth/recovery/*, NOT under the (app) group, so that gate (including the "admin recovery incomplete -> redirect" rule) never fires for them -- confirmed both by reading hooks.server.ts:566 and empirically in the new test below.
- No feature flag, rate limiter (rateLimit.ts), or admin-bootstrap step touches sessions on this path.
- Audited every session-mutating call site in the repo (createSession/destroySession/destroyUserSessions/dataRetention sweep/deviceTracking/admin.ts instance-reset) -- none is reachable from generateRecoveryPhrase().store() or generateRecoveryCodes().store().

Empirical repro attempt: wrote src/routes/api/auth/recovery/recoverySetupFlow.test.ts, which drives the REAL per-request pipeline (hooks.server.ts's handle(), re-resolving locals.user fresh from the session cookie via getSessionUser() for each call -- exactly like two independent real HTTP requests sharing one browser's cookie jar) rather than hand-injecting locals.user the way most route-handler tests here do. Result: POST phrase (200) immediately followed by POST codes (200) in the SAME session cookie -- no 401, session survives intact. A sanity-check case confirms the gate is still real (absent/garbage cookie legitimately 401s both routes). 3/3 tests pass: `npx vitest run src/routes/api/auth/recovery/recoverySetupFlow.test.ts`.

No code fix applied -- there is nothing reachable in this path to fix; changing requireUser/appGateRedirect here would either be a no-op or would loosen the gate for a route that's currently correctly enforcing "must be signed in."

Likely real explanation: this session's own memory (browser QA hazards, 2026-07-12) already documents that this wave's browser-automation tool drops httpOnly cookies specifically on HARD navigations ("stay in SPA or curl+cookie-jar"). generatePhrase() fires automatically on page mount; codes generation requires a user-driven click. If the automation path that reproduced this used anything resembling a hard navigation/reload between the phrase and codes steps (rather than staying in the SPA), that would explain a genuinely cookie-less second request against this server -- which legitimately 401s, as confirmed by the sanity-check test above. This matches the original bead text's own hypothesis ("possibly a test-harness artifact") before it was "independently reproduced" on a second automated run -- the second run used a different automation path but the memory note suggests this class of harness quirk is not path-specific.

Security implications: none -- no gating code was changed. The routes remain correctly closed to unauthenticated callers (verified by the sanity-check test).

Recommendation: keep P2 open but relabel/reframe as suspected QA-harness artifact; the fastest way to fully close this out is one manual click-through in a real browser tab (not the automation tool) through Settings/first-run -> generate phrase -> generate codes, watching Network tab for the Cookie header on both requests. If that manual pass is clean, this can close as "not a product bug, tooling artifact" with the regression test above kept as permanent coverage for the real end-to-end flow.

Test evidence: src/routes/api/auth/recovery/recoverySetupFlow.test.ts, 3 passed / 3 total. --actor worker-qlxs

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Confirmed DISTINCT from cairn-wrph/cairn-9njl (both closed 2026-07-13): those two were
a real server bug (session cookie stamped Secure over plain HTTP on unconfigured
deployments, now fixed via server.mjs PROTOCOL_HEADER default +
scripts/serverProto.mjs). This bead's symptom (recovery codes POST 401 immediately
after a 200 phrase POST, same onboarding flow) is unrelated to that cookie-Secure
mechanism — it is not the same code path, and the wrph/9njl fix does not touch
anything on this bead's territory (src/routes/api/auth/recovery/**,
src/lib/server/recovery.ts).

This bead's own prior investigation (2026-07-13 00:31 UTC comment) already reproduced
the real per-request pipeline end-to-end via
src/routes/api/auth/recovery/recoverySetupFlow.test.ts (phrase POST 200 -> codes POST
200 in the same session, 3/3 passing) and could not find any session-mutating code
reachable from either route. Re-confirming that conclusion now with the wrph/9njl fix
in hand: the wrph/9njl bug would have made the SESSION COOKIE ITSELF unusable
(Secure-over-http, dropped by the browser) on a bare/unproxied deployment — a
uniform failure across the whole session, not a phrase-succeeds/codes-fails split
within one flow. That shape doesn't match this bead's symptom, so the wrph/9njl fix is
not expected to and does not change this bead's behavior. Server-correctness stands:
harness artifact (browser-automation hard-navigation cookie drop, per the existing
hypothesis and this session's own QA-hazard notes), not a product bug.

Closing as not-a-bug per the existing investigation's recommendation, with the
regression test (recoverySetupFlow.test.ts) kept as permanent coverage of the real
flow.

title: Balance-history chart shows stale snapshot after switching node connection, contradicting live 0 balance
description:
Observed live on the v0.1.3 Umbrel instance after switching Admin > Settings > Node connection from the default public servers to a custom regtest Electrum server: the Dashboard's "Portfolio" confirmed balance correctly showed 0.00 BTC / 0 sats (live, correctly re-scanned against the new regtest chain), but the "Balance over time" chart directly below it kept showing a flat line labeled 0.0000186 BTC on both the start and end of the visible range, contradicting the live number.

Root cause: balance history is stored in an append-only balance_snapshots table (src/lib/server/portfolio.ts), written by recordSnapshot() (throttled to ~hourly) and backfilled on wallet import by buildBackfillPoints() (added in commit 64c47af, cairn-ittq). Nothing invalidates or re-derives existing snapshot rows when the node/chain connection changes (or generally whenever a wallet's live balance changes to contradict its last snapshot) -- src/lib/server/dataRetention.ts only downsamples/prunes by age, never by correctness. getBalanceSeries() simply carries forward whatever was last snapshotted.

Impact: low severity, self-corrects within about an hour once the next snapshot is recorded, and this specific trigger (switching Electrum backend to a different chain) is a power-user action rather than routine flow -- but it does mean the chart can show a materially wrong number that visibly contradicts the live balance card immediately above it for up to an hour, which reads as a real correctness bug to anyone who notices, especially if they'd just switched to their own node for the first time (exactly the "point it at your own node" flow the settings page invites new users to try).

Fix shape: either (a) invalidate/re-derive balance_snapshots when the node connection settings change, or (b) have the chart refuse to show a snapshot whose value has drifted from a fresher live-scanned confirmed balance, falling back to its empty state instead of a stale number.
close_reason: Chose a variant of option (b), implemented entirely in src/lib/server/portfolio.ts (the node-connection settings path was out of this task's file scope): recordSnapshot() now bypasses the hourly throttle whenever any wallet's live, fully-scanned balance contradicts that wallet's latest snapshot (new contradictsLatestSnapshot() helper). Since getPortfolioDetail records the snapshot BEFORE building the series, the chart's newest point agrees with the live balance card on the very next portfolio fetch instead of up to an hour later — covering the chain-switch case observed on Umbrel and ordinary balance changes alike. Only complete scans reach recordSnapshot (existing guard), so a partial Electrum outage can never fake a contradiction. Growth stays bounded: extra rows only on real balance changes, and dataRetention's existing downsampling applies. portfolio/dataRetention tests pass.

title: First-boot HTTPS window: 3212 accepts-and-drops while the TLS key generates
description: Field report v0.1.6 (Alex, 2026-07-07): https://umbrel.local:3212 gave ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE immediately after updating. Root cause verified live against the device the same night: NOT an Umbrel networking problem — raw ports: publishes are the sanctioned mechanism (umbrel-package-app skill: 'integrations that must connect without app_proxy'; bitcoin/electrs/adventurelog all do it) and by probe time the port served TLS1.3 + full pages to curl, openssl, and real Chrome (cert interstitial reached via both umbrel.local and LAN IP; device cert had correct SANs, generated 04:12:12 UTC). The failure window: server.mjs starts the HTTP listener first, then awaits ensureCert; node-forge 2048-bit RSA keygen is pure JS and can take tens of seconds on ARM, and during image pull + boot + keygen the published port 3212 has docker-proxy accepting connections with no backend -> accept-then-close = ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE. First boot after update only (cert persists in /data/tls). Fix ideas: (1) generate the key with node:crypto generateKeyPairSync (native, <1s even on ARM) and keep node-forge only for cert assembly, or switch cert generation off selfsigned entirely; (2) SecureContextHelp copy: 'just updated? give it a minute' note. No store/compose change needed or wanted.
close_reason: server.mjs binds listeners before app import (503 placeholder), ensureCert degrades to in-memory cert; boot-hammer verified zero empty replies; ships in 0.1.7

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Root-caused and fixed. The dead-port window was NOT keygen (selfsigned v5 uses native crypto.subtle — full local suite generates in <1s; the node-forge assumption was stale): it was server.mjs importing ./build/handler.js (DB open, migrations, Electrum pool) BEFORE binding any listener, while Docker forwards the published port from container start. Fix: server.mjs now binds HTTP+HTTPS immediately with a self-refreshing 503 'Cairn is starting' placeholder and hot-swaps in the SvelteKit handler when the import completes; ensureCert() additionally degrades to an in-memory cert if /data/tls is unwritable (previously: swallowed error -> listener disabled -> permanently dead port, the exact ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE signature). Verified: boot-hammer against the built image on the 0.1.6 volume — first HTTPS 200 at 379ms from docker run, zero empty replies (was: empty replies until full app init). Also verified live against the device pre-fix: curl/openssl/Chrome all reached Cairn on https://umbrel.local:3212 once booted — compose raw-port publish is architecturally correct, NO store/compose change needed.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: SHIPPED in v0.1.7: public c0b73af, tag v0.1.7, image ghcr.io/alexm223/cairn:0.1.7@sha256:bfb32b52632998129aca1448ace1cefa4736e15a12ce6f048ea74d014dc23723 (amd64+arm64 verified via ghcr manifest API), store commit a0d40c8, release https://github.com/AlexM223/cairn/releases/tag/v0.1.7. CI caveat: first tag build failed on svelte-check (err unknown in checked .mjs + selfsigned types have notAfterDate not days) — fixed in private 0bc8e87, public release commit amended + tag moved. Published-digest update-path re-test was NOT possible this session (host DNS outage, see below) — the locally built image from the identical source commit passed the full matrix instead. NOTE for the session log: the entire GitHub leg (push/API/release) was done through docker run --dns 1.1.1.1 because Tailscale MagicDNS points at a dead tailnet resolver (100.109.113.98) and ALL host DNS is down — Alex needs to fix the tailnet nameserver.

title: Wallets empty-state 'Restore from a backup' link is identical to 'Add your first wallet'
description: In src/routes/(app)/wallets/+page.svelte (added in a816047, cairn-lun6), the empty-state now shows two buttons: 'Add your first wallet' and 'Restore from a backup', both linking to the same href="/wallets/new". Both land on step 0 (wallet-type picker) — clicking 'Restore from a backup' does not skip ahead to the restore-file-upload affordance (which only appears on step 1, the Key step, for single-sig). The two buttons are currently functionally indistinguishable despite different labels, which is confusing and doesn't actually shortcut the restore journey as the cairn-lun6 bead intended ('make the entry point discoverable from login/onboarding'). Fix: either deep-link to step 1 with the restore box pre-focused (e.g. /wallets/new?restore=1 read on mount), or open the file picker directly from the empty-state link before navigating.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: the wallets empty-state 'Restore from a backup' link now targets /wallets/new?restore=1, which lands directly on the Key step where the restore box is the first affordance (wallets/+page.svelte, wallets/new/+page.svelte).

title: Unify buildDraft/broadcast behind the feeBump spec engine before CPFP forks it a 3rd time
description: transactions.ts (single-sig) and multisigTransactions.ts (multisig) carry near-verbatim buildDraft vs buildMultisigDraft and broadcastTransaction vs broadcastMultisigTransaction. The atomic broadcast-claim UPDATE (transactions.ts:618-627 vs multisigTransactions.ts:473-482) is copy-pasted, differing only by table name + owner column — the multisig file's own comment says 'identical idiom to transactions.ts'. Fable (arch advisor, verified feeBump.ts:91 in-repo): this is the codebase's OWN proven pattern left half-applied — feeBump.ts already genericized RBF-bump + CPFP across both paths via executeRbfBump<TDraft,TDetails>/executeCpfpDraft parameterized by spec:{table,ownerColumn} + a buildReplacement callback, with ZERO lifecycle divergence lost. The copy-pasted broadcast-claim UPDATE is the double-broadcast guard — 'the most dangerous line in the codebase to have two of'; a timeout/predicate fix applied to one table and not the other is a money-losing bug no test on the edited file catches. Rule-of-three trips now: cairn-u9ob CPFP/package-relay would fork it a third time (tryPackageRescue at transactions.ts:509 is already single-sig-only). ABSTRACTION: do NOT build around 'single-sig = M=1 multisig' (leaky — script type, PSBT finalization, signer coordination genuinely differ, breeds if(n===1) branches). Build around 'a spend record has one lifecycle' (draft->claimed->broadcast->confirmed) parameterized by storage location, construction/signing injected as callbacks — exactly what feeBump embodies. SCHEMA stays split (deliberate). SEQUENCE: (1) extract broadcast-claim UPDATE + broadcastTransaction into the spec engine, (2) unify buildDraft persistence, (3) THEN build CPFP against the engine once, (4) do NOT start CPFP on the duplicated code.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: CORRECTION 2026-07-07: cairn-u9ob (CPFP/package-relay) is ALREADY CLOSED (built+shipped 2026-07-06), and it was built ON the generic feeBump engine (executeCpfpDraft/executeRbfBump), so CPFP did NOT fork buildDraft/broadcast a third time. The 'unify before CPFP forks it again' timing driver is therefore MOOT. This bead remains VALID as latent-safety tech-debt — the copy-pasted broadcast-claim UPDATE (the double-broadcast guard) still exists in two places and a fix to one and not the other is a money-losing bug — but it is NO LONGER time-gated by an incoming feature. Reclassify: background / when-touching-the-broadcast-path, and pair it with the next change that lands on transactions.ts or multisigTransactions.ts. Priority stays P2 on latent-risk grounds, but it does NOT jump the security/robustness backlog.

title: resetInstance() factory reset leaves instance-wide events (user_id IS NULL) behind, contradicting the 'nothing else survives' claim in the Settings UI
description: src/lib/server/admin.ts resetInstance() runs DELETE FROM wallets/invites/sessions/users/settings in a transaction and relies on ON DELETE CASCADE (users(id)) to clean up everything else. But src/lib/server/db.ts L424 defines events.user_id as nullable with a comment 'NULL = instance-wide' — rows with user_id IS NULL have no FK target to cascade from, and resetInstance() never issues a DELETE FROM events. Confirmed by reading src/lib/server/activity.ts: recordActivity() writes instance-wide rows (userId omitted/null) for things like chain/electrum switches, backups, restores. src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte's danger-zone copy tells the admin a reset 'deletes all users, sessions, wallets, and invites... nothing else survives' — that's false for instance-wide activity history, which a NEW operator (or the same operator post-reset) will see in /admin/activity, including entries like 'instance restore applied' from the prior operator/session. Fix: add DELETE FROM events; to resetInstance()'s transaction (or DELETE FROM events WHERE user_id IS NULL if any events should be considered structural rather than history).
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Factory reset leaves instance_secrets + global feature flags intact
description:
resetInstance (src/lib/server/admin.ts:115-148) never deletes the instance_secrets table (which holds smtp_pass, core_rpc_pass, telegram_bot_token, nostr_sender_privkey; see db.ts:1160). feature_flags rows also survive the reset, just with updated_by nulled out. UI copy and docs/RECOVERY.md:49 both claim a factory reset wipes everything.

This is a confidentiality leak on operator handover / device resale scenarios: SMTP credentials, Core RPC password, bot tokens, and Nostr private keys persist on a 'reset' instance that the next operator believes is clean.

Fix: resetInstance must also clear instance_secrets and feature_flags (or explicitly document what factory reset does and does not wipe, and fix the UI/RECOVERY.md claims to match).
close_reason: Fixed: resetInstance() now deletes instance_secrets and feature_flags outright. Commits 6be9373 (fix + tests), 41a9f39 (MANUAL.md). 26/26 targeted vitest passing. See evidence comment for full root cause + test detail.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: RUNTIME CONFIRMED (2026-07-12, cairn-preview-fresh port 4199, dev-fresh.db, preview build), twice: instance_secrets.smtp_pass survived two consecutive factory resets while all smtp_* settings rows were wiped. Worse: the fresh admin's SMTP settings UI shows "A password is stored. Leave blank to keep it, or clear it." -- actively inviting the new operator to reuse the prior operator's orphaned secret (silently reused if a host is saved with password blank). feature_flags row (explorer, enabled=0) also survived with original seed timestamp.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Priority raised P2 -> P1: the SMTP settings UI copy ("A password is stored. Leave blank to keep it, or clear it.") actively invites the new operator to silently reuse the prior operator's orphaned secret post-reset, turning a confidentiality leak into an active credential-reuse trap on operator handover / device resale.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Pinned as KNOWN GAP in destructiveOps.test.ts: readSecretSetting('smtp_pass') survives resetInstance; feature_flags rows survive. Test flips when fixed.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
FIXED (commits 6be9373 admin.ts/destructiveOps.test.ts, 41a9f39 MANUAL.md, branch single-sig-full-wallet).

Root cause: resetInstance() (src/lib/server/admin.ts) deleted users/sessions/wallets/invites/settings/events/notified_txids/announcements/multisig_service_referrals but never touched instance_secrets (no user_id to cascade from) and only nulled feature_flags.updated_by instead of deleting the rows.

Fix: resetInstance() now runs 'DELETE FROM feature_flags' (before DELETE FROM users, to satisfy the NO-ACTION updated_by FK -- doing it after throws FOREIGN KEY constraint failed, caught during implementation) and 'DELETE FROM instance_secrets' inside the same transaction. This wipes smtp_pass, core_rpc_pass, telegram_bot_token, nostr_sender_privkey, and scheduled_backup_pass (backup.ts) in one shot since they all live in the same table. Per-user secrets (personal SMTP password embedded in notification_channel_config.config, secretKey.ts envelope) were already correctly cascaded via notification_channel_config.user_id ON DELETE CASCADE -- confirmed via new test, not a missed store.

Tests: destructiveOps.test.ts -- flipped the pinned KNOWN GAP test (now asserts instance_secrets/feature_flags are 0 rows post-reset) and added a new table-driven test enumerating all 5 instance_secrets keys plus the per-user encrypted-SMTP case, run via targeted 'npx vitest run src/lib/server/destructiveOps.test.ts src/lib/server/admin.test.ts': 26/26 passed.

Docs: MANUAL.md gets a new Factory reset (resetInstance(), admin.ts) section under 6. Server: Database Layer, documenting the fixed wipe behavior and the delete-order constraint.

Not closing per orchestration instructions -- leaving open for integration review.

title: Missing regression test: admin self-disable/self-demote server-side guard (cairn-zl6d / cairn-vljv)
description: cairn-zl6d and cairn-vljv (P2, duplicate beads for the identical bug, both closed in 46dd16f) fixed the admin users route not blocking an admin from disabling or demoting their own account server-side (only client-side JS prevented it, unlike self-delete which already had a server check). Fix confirmed present at the admin users route (checks body.id === admin.id for both disabled:true and isAdmin:false, returns 400 before mutation). No test exists for this HTTP-layer guard — admin.test.ts only covers the lower-level setUserAdmin/setUserDisabled/deleteUser functions. Add a route-level test: POST to the admin users route as an admin targeting their own id with disabled:true (and separately isAdmin:false), and assert 400 + no mutation occurred, mirroring the existing self-delete guard's test if one exists.

Fix confirmed still present post-warm-cache refactor at portfolio.ts:169-176 (wallet) and :187-190 (multisig). No portfolio.test.ts exists at all (portfolioWarm.test.ts covers a different function/scenario). Add portfolio.test.ts: mock one of several wallet/multisig scans to reject, call the portfolio aggregation function, and assert (1) log.warn fires with wallet/multisig id context, and (2) the failure is excluded from the total while other wallets' balances are still included correctly.

title: Per-user SMTP host has no internal-range validation — blind LAN port scanner
description: channels/email.ts:237-247 — per-user SMTP host validated only for port range/TLS mode. SMTP isn't HTTP so ssrf.ts doesn't apply. No range check before nodemailer connects. Any user can set host=10.x.x.x, hit test-smtp (unthrottled, doesn't require saving) repeatedly — timing gives blind LAN port scanner. Not an open relay (recipient pinned to user's own address). Fix: validate smtp.host through same range policy as ssrf.ts, set connectionTimeout, rate-limit test-smtp.
close_reason: Merged 4418db9: per-user SMTP host validated via checkTargetHost before test-connect and save, closing the blind-LAN-port-scanner oracle.

title: Docs: reverse-proxy/TLS-termination section — X-Forwarded-Proto requirement + ORIGIN/CAIRN_ORIGIN escape hatches
description:
Follow-up from cairn-wrph/cairn-9njl (session cookie stamped Secure over plain HTTP
on unconfigured deployments, fixed 2026-07-13).

The fix makes bare/unproxied server.mjs deployments correct by default (defaults
PROTOCOL_HEADER=x-forwarded-proto per listener, fill-when-absent via
scripts/serverProto.mjs), but a deployment that puts its OWN TLS-terminating reverse
proxy in front of Cairn's plain HTTP port now has a hard requirement: that proxy MUST
set X-Forwarded-Proto itself, or the operator must set ORIGIN (adapter-node) /
CAIRN_ORIGIN (Cairn's own app-level override, src/lib/server/auth.ts cookieSecure())
to their externally-visible https URL. Getting this wrong now fails LOUD (missing
Secure attribute) rather than the old silent failure mode, which is the right
trade-off but needs to be documented somewhere a self-hosting operator will actually
find it before they hit it.

Currently this is only documented in code comments (server.mjs header comment,
Dockerfile note near the ADDRESS_HEADER note, scripts/serverProto.mjs header) and in
the cairn-wrph/cairn-9njl bead history — nothing in user-facing docs.

Scope: add a "Reverse proxy / TLS termination" section to the operator-facing docs
(check docs/ and MANUAL.md — see also memory note "Cairn MANUAL.md" for where the
docs live and the rule that manual/behavior disagreement is itself a bug) covering:
  - The X-Forwarded-Proto requirement for any TLS-terminating proxy in front of the
    plain HTTP port.
  - The ORIGIN (adapter-node) and CAIRN_ORIGIN (Cairn app-level) escape hatches, and
    when to use each.
  - ADDRESS_HEADER / HOST_HEADER's existing analogous requirement (already
    documented in the Dockerfile — cross-reference or fold in for one coherent
    "behind a proxy" section rather than three scattered notes).
  - Umbrel's own docker-compose.yml as the worked example (it already sets
    PROTOCOL_HEADER=x-forwarded-proto, ADDRESS_HEADER=x-forwarded-for,
    HOST_HEADER=x-forwarded-host, and CAIRN_ORIGIN — a concrete reference
    config for anyone building their own reverse-proxy setup).

No code changes — docs only.

title: Extract shared gap-limit scan engine (gapLimitScanner.ts)

title: All commits carry an AI co-author trailer — confirm this is intentional for the public repo
description: Every commit so far (14/14) ends with 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>'. This is a defensible, transparent choice (comparable to how Claude Code itself attributes commits) and I'm not flagging it as a defect — but since this pass was asked to specifically check for 'no AI mentions,' I want it surfaced as a decision point rather than silently left or silently scrubbed. Rewriting it now would mean rewriting all existing commit messages (destructive, history-altering), so I'm not doing that unilaterally. If the intent is to keep attribution, no action needed. If the intent is to drop it going forward, that's a one-line change to how commits are authored from here on (existing history would need an explicit rebase decision, separately).
close_reason: Confirmed intentional: Co-Authored-By trailers are deliberate, factual attribution on AI-assisted commits and are required by the contributing tooling's policy; they are commit metadata, not repo artifacts. Whether to strip them via history rewrite before first publication is the owner's call — flagged in the session summary

title: validateCosignerKeyPath does not verify hardened bit on BIP-48 levels
description: multisig.ts:307-330 — checks BIP-48 coin/account/script-type levels by value only via unhardened(), never confirming they are actually hardened. m/48'/0/0'/2' (unhardened coin) and m/48'/0'/0'/2 (unhardened script-type) both pass. Every acceptance test uses fully-hardened paths so the hole is invisible. Impact: recovery/interop confusion with strict external tools. Fix: add hardened-bit check on levels 1-3, add test with unhardened variants.

title: Wallet-detail scanner-error state shows the same message twice back to back
description:
On a wallet detail page when the wallet scanner/Electrum is unreachable, the error panel shows two near-identical lines in a row:
  "Can't reach the wallet scanner"       <- hardcoded heading
  "Could not reach the wallet scanner"   <- refreshError body text
Reads like a copy-paste leftover to a non-technical user (duplicate content, no added information in the second line). Repro: navigate to any wallet detail page (/wallets/[id]) while the Electrum/chain backend is unreachable -- confirmed live at http://qa-a2.localhost:5344/wallets/2 during QA session qa-2026-07-12b.

Source: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte:70,76 (refreshError = 'Could not reach the wallet scanner') and :614 (hardcoded heading "Can't reach the wallet scanner"). Same duplicate pattern in src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.svelte:66,72,531.

Suggested fix: either drop the hardcoded heading and let refreshError be the single message, or differentiate the two lines (e.g. heading + a more specific/actionable body like a Retry hint or timestamp of last successful sync).

Severity: P3 -- cosmetic/copy polish, not blocking. The surrounding error state is otherwise good (clear top-level banner "Heartwood isn't connected to the Bitcoin network yet... Ask your instance operator", a working Retry button).

title: notify() can silently drop external-channel delivery after in-app record succeeds
description: src/lib/server/notifications.ts, notify() (around lines 172-206): the outer try/catch wraps both the in-app activity write and the entire external-channel enqueue loop. If the DB insert throws partway through enqueueing, the in-app record already succeeded but no external channel (email/telegram/ntfy/nostr/webhook) ever receives the alert -- visible only as one generic log.error line, and notify() returns void so callers have no way to detect partial failure. Affects tx_large, security_failed_login, admin_restore, backup_missing, key_health_due. rateLimit.ts's noteLoginFailure() calls notify() with no local guard, compounding the risk. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

The centerpiece of v1: every piece of data should be transparent and educational, so that someone new to Bitcoin understands what they are looking at after a few minutes of browsing.

Includes an introductory 'what is a block' panel, miner/pool identification from the coinbase, fee statistics (total, average rate, range) with an explanation of why fees exist, a highlighted coinbase transaction with subsidy + fees = reward breakdown, and a confirmations count explaining why more confirmations mean more settlement assurance. Unconfirmed transactions get an ETA based on current fee rates.

title: Esplora fetch errors swallow the real cause (DNS/TLS/refused) behind generic 'fetch failed'
description:
src/lib/server/chain/esplora.ts's fetchPath() caught fetch() failures and used only e.message, which for Node's global fetch (undici) is always the generic 'TypeError: fetch failed' -- the actual underlying reason (DNS ENOTFOUND, ECONNREFUSED, TLS cert error, timeout) lives in e.cause and was being discarded. This made 'Can't reach chain data sources' on the Explorer/Home/mempool/difficulty pages, and the Admin Settings 'Test connection' badge for the Esplora backend, diagnostically useless -- impossible to tell DNS failure from blocked egress from a bad URL.

Found while investigating a real user report of the Explorer page failing to reach chain data on their Umbrel install even with electrs already connected. Electrum and Esplora are separate dependencies -- electrs alone does not satisfy the Esplora HTTP dependency the Explorer/Home/mempool/difficulty pages need (see src/lib/server/chain/index.ts header comment). Default esplora_url is the public https://mempool.space/api; default electrum host is electrum.blockstream.info:50002 -- two separate endpoints, and the user reported both public servers unreachable.

Fix: added a fetchErrorDetail() helper in esplora.ts that walks the .cause chain (up to 3 levels) and joins each level's message, so errors like 'Esplora GET /blocks/tip/height failed: fetch failed: getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND mempool.space' now surface the real cause instead of bare 'fetch failed'.
close_reason: Fixed in src/lib/server/chain/esplora.ts (uncommitted working-tree change): added fetchErrorDetail() helper that walks e.cause up to 3 levels and joins each level's message, replacing the old 'e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)' extraction in fetchPath()'s catch block. Verified live: started cairn-fresh dev server, logged in, set Admin Settings esplora_url to a nonexistent hostname, clicked Test connection -- badge changed from what would have been bare 'fetch failed' to 'Esplora GET /blocks/tip/height failed: fetch failed: getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND this-host-does-not-exist-cairn-perf-test.invalid'. Ran full chain.test.ts suite (18/18 pass) and tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json (no new errors, only a pre-existing unrelated chainEpochs.ts svelte-module-typing error). NOTE: this fix makes the error message diagnosable going forward -- it does not by itself fix the user's actual Umbrel connectivity problem (both electrum.blockstream.info and mempool.space reported unreachable from their container), which still needs the real error text from their box (now obtainable via Admin Settings > Test connection or the explorer page's error line) to diagnose as DNS/egress/proxy.

title: Missing regression test: Trezor driver silently swallows multisig-change/cosigner-xpub parse failures (cairn-yaw1)
description: cairn-yaw1 (P2, closed in 7ebd68c) added console.warn logging (confirmed present at src/lib/hw/trezor.ts:1081,1202,1211) when multisig-change verification or cosigner-xpub parsing fails, instead of silently discarding the exception. No test asserts these warnings fire — trezor.test.ts only checks the resulting wrong_device error path. Add a test: spy on console.warn (or the logger, if migrated), feed a malformed cosigner xpub into selectMultisigKeyForDevice and a bad multisig-change scenario, and assert a warning is logged in each case.

title: Legacy umbrel-community-app-store is stale at v0.2.9 while heartwood-app-store shipped v0.2.15
description: 0.2.15 smoke test (cairn-4pio follow-up) found two competing Umbrel community-store repos both distributing Cairn/Heartwood: (1) C:/dev/umbrel-community-app-store (caravan-store-cairn / caravan-store-heartwood app ids, port 3211) — last touched 2026-07-11, manifest still pinned at v0.2.9, README still tells users to install 'Cairn (caravan-store-cairn)'; (2) github.com/AlexM223/heartwood-app-store (heartwood-bitcoin app id, port 3217) — the actual current store, already at v0.2.15 with clean linear history 0.2.9->0.2.15. There is no cross-link, deprecation notice, or migration note in the old repo pointing users at the new one. Anyone who added the old community-store URL to their Umbrel (documented in older release notes / this repo's own README) is stuck on 0.2.9 forever with a stale app id/port, unaware a maintained v0.2.15 store now exists elsewhere. Recommend: either archive/redirect the old umbrel-community-app-store repo's Cairn/Heartwood entry with a README note pointing at heartwood-app-store, or restore it to a synced/tracking state. Confirm with Alex which repo is meant to be canonical going forward.

title: Instance backup omits multisig_shares -- restore loses all collaborative-custody sharing
description:
buildBackup (src/lib/server/backup.ts:64-83) captures 8 tables but not multisig_shares (db.ts:956), the table that records which cosigners a multisig wallet is shared with. restoreBackup never recreates rows in this table, and the omission is silent -- it is not surfaced in RestoreSummary.

Failure scenario: an owner takes an instance backup, later restores it (new instance, disaster recovery, or migration), and every multisig wallet that was shared with cosigners comes back as unshared -- collaborative custody relationships are silently severed with no warning during or after restore.

Fix: add multisig_shares to buildBackup's captured tables and to restoreBackup's recreation logic, and surface it in RestoreSummary. Cross-reference cairn-a857 (mid-operation disruption tests) and cairn-684u (cascade test-gap) as related but distinct.

NOTE: multisigs + multisig_keys DO round-trip -- the wallet config itself is preserved; only shares are lost.
