title: Add-wallet method list: surface 'Paste public key' above the six hardware options on mobile
description: Visual audit (2026-07-12): on mobile, the Add-wallet wizard Key step (src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte, METHOD_CARDS) stacks: restore box -> multisig hand-off card -> Trezor -> Ledger -> ColdCard -> BitBox02 -> Jade -> Air-gapped QR -> 'Paste public key' (last). A keyless beginner -- the person most likely to paste an xpub from another app -- must scroll past six hardware-brand options first. Fix: on mobile, surface 'Paste public key' and 'Connect a device' as the top two choices, or group the six device brands behind a single 'Connect a hardware wallet' expander. Searched for overlap first: br search for 'paste public key'/'METHOD_CARDS'/'hardware wallet options' returned no matches -- filing new.
close_reason:
Implemented: added data-method attribute to each .method-cell in src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte (line ~798) and a CSS rule '@media (max-width: 900px) { .method-cell[data-method=paste] { order: -1; } }' (line ~1855, the app-wide mobile breakpoint convention used across 34+ other files). This is a grid-item order override: CSS Grid auto-placement sorts items by order value before document position, so on any viewport <=900px wide (confirmed 375x812 target) the Paste public key card is placed first, ahead of the six hardware-brand cards (Trezor/Ledger/ColdCard/BitBox02/Jade/Air-gapped QR), matching the bead's ask that the most keyless-friendly option leads on mobile. Above 900px (confirmed 1280x800 desktop target) the media query does not match, all cells keep the default order:0, and the original DOM order (device brands first, paste last) renders unchanged -- desktop is untouched. METHOD_CARDS is a fixed, unfiltered constant array (verified via grep -- no conditional add/remove of entries before the #each), so paste is deterministically the last DOM child and this rule's target is stable.

Verification: npm run check = 0 errors/0 warnings (1876 files). Live browser visual verification was attempted (registered a throwaway user via POST /api/auth/register/password with existing dev invite code CLAUDE-TEST-2026 against the running 'cairn' preview server on :55467, confirmed /api/auth/me 200) but blocked by the same session-persistence bug tracked in cairn-wrph (fetch()-based login/agreement-accept returns success but the session cookie does not survive -- /api/auth/me flips back to 401, and the agreement accept action redirects to /login instead of persisting, confirmed empty via direct sqlite read of user_agreement_acceptances for the throwaway user). This is the identical blocker already tracked against cairn-5fpb (QA visual re-verification blocked on cairn-wrph). Did not spend further time routing around it; falling back to deterministic code verification (CSS Grid order semantics + confirmed-static DOM order) per orchestrator instruction. Recommend a real screenshot pass of this change ride along whenever cairn-5fpb/cairn-wrph unblocks QA auth.

title: notifyConfig credential stripping only proven for ntfy channel
description: notifyConfig.ts — redactChannelConfig feeds 4 call sites (settings loader, channel API, admin API, account export). Only the ntfy branch is proven by accountExport.test.ts. Webhook branch (secret/secretEnc -> hasSecret) and email branch (smtp.passEnc -> smtp.hasPass) untested. Legacy plaintext fields also untested. getPublicInstanceNotificationSettings (smtp_pass, telegram_bot_token) entirely untested. A regression leaks webhook HMAC secret, personal-SMTP password, or Telegram bot token to browser. Fix: add notifyConfig.test.ts.

title: Mobile: app freezes after backgrounding/foregrounding — SSE stream has no visibilitychange handling
description:
## Repro (reported by Alex)

On mobile, switch away from the browser to another app, then switch back to Heartwood. The app freezes / shows an error page — nothing is responsive.

## Root cause

App is SSE-only (no WebSocket usage anywhere — grepped, confirmed). Two SSE endpoints:
- `src/routes/api/events/+server.ts` — new-block stream, 25s server heartbeat.
- `src/routes/api/notifications/stream/+server.ts` — notification badge stream.

Client-side EventSource setup:
- `src/lib/liveBlocks.ts:18` — `const source = new EventSource('/api/events');` inside `onNewBlock()`. **No `source.onerror` handler at all.** Only a `block` event listener (L31) and `.close()` on unsubscribe (L66). A code comment on L10 claims "Native EventSource handles reconnects" — true for ordinary network drops, but the browser's own retry timer is itself throttled/frozen while the tab is backgrounded on mobile, so it doesn't reliably fire on its own.
- `src/lib/components/NotificationPanel.svelte:176` — `source = new EventSource('/api/notifications/stream')`. Same pattern: no `onerror`, no reconnect/backoff logic.
- Consumers wiring `onNewBlock` in `onMount` with no visibility-aware re-subscribe: `src/routes/(app)/+page.svelte:53-54`, `explorer/+page.svelte:70`, `explorer/mempool/+page.svelte:43`, `wallets/[id]/+page.svelte:64`, `wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:787`, `wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.svelte:67`, `wallets/multisig/[id]/send/+page.svelte:78`.

**`document.visibilitychange` / `pagehide` / `pageshow` / `online`/`offline`: not used anywhere for SSE lifecycle** (grepped the whole client codebase). The only `document.hidden` reference at all is `explorer/mempool/blocks/+page.svelte:53`, which just skips a `setInterval` poll while hidden — unrelated to EventSource, and still has no visibilitychange listener (checks `document.hidden` inline inside an existing timer tick).

No global error/crash boundary either — no `window.addEventListener('error'|'unhandledrejection')` anywhere in `src`; only SvelteKit's routing-level `+error.svelte`, which doesn't fire for a stuck runtime connection.

## Theory

Mobile Safari/Chrome suspend background tabs and tear down the SSE stream's underlying connection. `EventSource`'s native auto-reconnect is timer-driven and gets throttled along with the rest of the backgrounded tab's JS — it doesn't reliably reconnect until the tab regains a full scheduling slice, which may never happen without some other trigger. On foreground, since nothing listens for `visibilitychange`/`pageshow`, nothing proactively checks `source.readyState` or forces a fresh subscription/data refetch. State only updates via `invalidate('cairn:chain')` triggered from the `block` SSE event, so once the passive channel goes silent the UI simply stops receiving updates and appears frozen — no fallback poll, no manual reconciliation, no forced reconnect on resume.

## Fix

Add a `document.visibilitychange` (or `pageshow`) listener wherever EventSource connections are created (`liveBlocks.ts`, `NotificationPanel.svelte`) that, on becoming visible again: closes any existing EventSource, opens a fresh one, and triggers an immediate data refresh/invalidate so state catches up even if some `block` events were missed while backgrounded. Also add `source.onerror` handlers so a dead connection is detected and can be proactively replaced rather than silently relying on the browser's own (throttled) retry.
close_reason: liveBlocks.ts: visibilitychange reconnect + 90s staleness timer + reconnect() helper. Preserves throttle logic across reconnects. Cleanup tears down all listeners/timers.

title: Code-organization consistency: partial subdomain folders + confusable backup.ts/backups.ts naming
description: Low-priority cleanup found during the arch audit. (1) src/lib/server/ has SIX subdomain folders (bitcoin/, chain/, electrum/, channels/, featureFlags/, wallets/) but wallets/ holds ONLY multisig.ts while its single-sig sibling wallets.ts sits flat one level up, and 8 other multisig-domain files (multisigTransactions/Export/Scan/Shares/Roster/Registrations/BackupPdf) + 6 notify-domain files stay flat — a newcomer sees folders, assumes full grouping, then hits 100+ flat files. Either commit to folder-per-domain (multisig/*, notify/*, wallets/singlesig.ts) or drop the partial folders; the half-measure is worst. (2) backup.ts (instance backup/export/restore + scheduled-backup watcher) vs backups.ts (per-wallet 'has this been backed up?' reminder tracking) — singular/plural is the ONLY distinguishing signal for two unrelated responsibilities; rename backups.ts -> backupReminders.ts. (3) wallets-multisig.test.ts breaks the camelCase test-file convention (walletsMultisig.test.ts). CORRECTION for memory: the prior review's 'Ledger/BitBox/Jade signer pairs still unmerged' is STALE — they ARE merged (one component per device, shared via optional multisig prop); gap-limit scanning is also already deduped via bitcoin/gapLimitScanner.ts runGapScan/scanChainAddresses<T>.

title: Coin control: manual UTXO selection
description: Coin selection in constructPsbt (src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts, selectUTXO call) is always automatic/greedy -- there is no way for a user to pick specific UTXOs to spend from. Useful for privacy (avoiding linking unrelated deposits) and deliberate dust consolidation. Small-medium effort: ConstructParams would need an optional UTXO allowlist, and the Create step needs a UTXO picker UI (checkbox list, probably behind a disclosure toggle so it doesn't clutter the default flow).
close_reason: Shipped: onlyUtxos allowlist in constructPsbt (restriction of candidate set, not exactInputs; distinct no_utxos/insufficient_funds errors; send-max over subset tested), CoinControl.svelte disclosure with per-coin checkboxes and selected totals, UTXO list loaded from the cached scan. Live-preview verified: only the allowlisted coin was spent.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Verified with a real emulator round trip (commit 38679b2), per standing instruction to run PSBT-construction-touching changes through the regtest+emulator infra.

Funded wallet 4 with 3 distinct regtest UTXOs (30,000 / 68,530 / 20,000 sats). Two checks:

1. Restriction is real, not a preference: built a draft allowlisting only the 30,000 and 20,000-sat UTXOs. Selection correctly picked just the 30,000-sat one (sufficient alone) rather than needing both -- normal minimal-selection semantics preserved within the allowed subset, as designed.
2. Proved the allowlist is a hard restriction: built a draft allowlisting ONLY the 20,000-sat UTXO and requested 25,000 sats -- correctly rejected ("The selected coins don't cover that amount plus the network fee"), even though the untouched 68,530-sat UTXO sitting in the same wallet could easily have covered it. Coin control never silently reaches outside the allowed set.
3. Signed the resulting coin-controlled draft with the real Trezor emulator (genuine device round trip, same as the RBF verification) and broadcast for real -- confirmed via the real regtest chain.

No issues found. Full suite: 366/366 passing.

title: disabled sole admin can self-delete leaving zero admin rows
description: Last-admin guard (accountDeletion.ts:38 + admin.ts adminCount()) filters is_admin=1 AND disabled=0, so a DISABLED sole admin is unguarded — deleteOwnAccount succeeds leaving no is_admin=1 rows at all; recovery only via direct DB intervention. Pinned as KNOWN GAP in destructiveOps.test.ts Group 2. Evidence on branch test/qa-wave-2026-07-12, worktree C:\dev\cairn-testqa.
close_reason: Fixed in 6aff163: server-side user-deletion path hardened (FK-clean purge, robust last-admin guard incl. disabled sole admin, shared-multisig owner-deletion guard + cosigner notification). 46 targeted tests green.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: FIXED in 6aff163 (branch single-sig-full-wallet). Root cause: the last-admin guard in accountDeletion.ts and admin.ts adminCount() counted only is_admin=1 AND disabled=0, so a DISABLED sole admin was unguarded and deleteOwnAccount/deleteUser left zero admin rows. Fix: new deletionOrphansAdmins() in userDeletion.ts refuses to delete an admin when (a) no OTHER admin ROW of any kind would remain (covers the disabled sole admin), OR (b) the target is itself a usable admin and no OTHER usable (enabled) admin remains (preserves the pre-existing 'can't delete the last active admin' behavior even when a disabled admin row also exists). Both entry points now use it. Evidence: destructiveOps.test.ts Group 2 KNOWN GAP flipped to green ('a DISABLED sole admin can NOT self-delete or be deleted into a zero-admin instance'), plus a predicate matrix test in userDeletion.test.ts. Targeted vitest: 46 passed.

title: backup.ts restoreBackup() silently skips malformed/duplicate rows, zero logging
description: src/lib/server/backup.ts lines 241, 284, 305, 325 (per-row insert loops for wallets/multisig keys/Ledger registrations/addresses in restoreBackup) all catch and skip a malformed/duplicate row with only a code comment, no log call. An admin performing a break-glass restore has zero visibility into which rows were silently dropped or why -- the RestoreSummary just shows lower counts than expected. Fix: log.warn each skipped row with enough context (table, reason) to diagnose after the fact. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed in 7ebd68c

title: Missing regression test: multisig PSBT-substitution guard untested on the multisig path (cairn-973)
description: cairn-973 (P1, closed in 40cf0d8) added assertSameTransaction() to guard against a signed PSBT with different outputs than the reviewed draft being accepted/broadcast — wired into both single-sig and multisig upload/broadcast paths. src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.test.ts covers the single-sig path (recipient-swap, amount-change, signing-only-doesn't-trip-guard), but src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisigPsbt.ts's identical call site (around combineMultisigPsbts, confirmed still wired post the 881ee90 shared-handler-plumbing refactor) has NO dedicated substitution test — multisigPsbt.test.ts only covers an unrelated script-type-mismatch guard. Add a test: construct two multisig PSBTs from the same UTXO set with different recipients/amounts, and assert combining/finalizing draft A with a co-signer's PSBT for draft B throws the mismatch error. This is a fund-safety-critical gap on the higher-complexity (M-of-N) code path.
close_reason: Committed in 26af83d: multisig substitution guard tests, legacy p2pkh/p2sh-p2wpkh spending-source tests, all 6 SPV reason codes, describe.runIf trap doors removed (multisig suites unconditional; regtest E2E skips loudly), coinbaseScan.test.ts fail-closed coverage.

title: [UX-QA f0d423f] Raw ECONNREFUSED/scan error string leaks to UI via scanError (server sanitization)
description:
Follow-up to the client-side patch on ux/receive-fix. The raw chain error string (e.g. 'connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:60401') ORIGINATES server-side and is surfaced verbatim to multiple UI surfaces via the snapshot's scanError field. Per cairn-s17j, raw socket/DNS/refused detail must never reach the user.

RAW-STRING ORIGIN (server, off-limits to the ux session):
- src/lib/server/wallets.ts:439  getWalletDetail catches the scan failure and re-throws:  new Error(e instanceof Error ? e.message : 'Wallet scan failed', { cause: 'unreachable' })  -- e.message is the raw Electrum/socket message, propagated verbatim.
- src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.server.ts:90-91  catches that and does scanError = e.message  -> raw string into the streamed live snapshot.

CLIENT LEAK (already patched on ux/receive-fix): src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.svelte:851-855 interpolated the raw {scanError} after a plain-language lead. Patched to drop the raw suffix and show plain language only. BUT scanError is consumed by other surfaces too (the send banner was one; audit the wallet detail page and any portfolio/explorer error strips that read snapshot.scanError), so the durable fix belongs at the server boundary.

PROPOSED FIX: sanitize at the throw site. In src/lib/server/wallets.ts:439 (and/or a shared helper), map raw connectivity errors (ECONNREFUSED / ETIMEDOUT / ENOTFOUND / socket / TLS) to a stable plain-language message BEFORE they become scanError, so every consumer inherits clean copy. This mirrors the 7fcb734 error-copy Wave B standard which covered broadcast/build paths but not the scan/scanError path. Consider reusing the same error-copy helper introduced in 7fcb734.

NOTE: files are .server.ts / $lib/server -- off-limits to the ux/receive-fix session, hence this bead rather than an inline fix.

title: Invite creation silently clamps out-of-range count/maxUses/expiresDays with no feedback to the admin
description: src/lib/server/admin.ts createInvites() clamps count to [1,50] and maxUses to [1,1000] via Math.min(Math.max(...)) with no error/warning returned. The form at src/routes/(app)/admin/invites/+page.svelte has number inputs with min/max attributes but no enforcement of them (a user can still type an out-of-range value and submit). If an admin requests count=200 expecting 200 invite codes, they silently get 50 with no indication anything was capped - the returned form.created array (length 50) doesn't explain why it's short of the requested count. Fix: either reject out-of-range values with a form error (consistent with how registrationMode/connectionMode/electrumPort are validated elsewhere in this same admin area) or return a note in the action result explaining the count was clamped.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: admin invites create action now validates count/maxUses/expiresDays ranges and returns a form error instead of silently clamping (src/routes/(app)/admin/invites/+page.server.ts).

title: test issue please ignore
Wallet wizards: remove top 'Wallets' back-link that exits the flow
description: Umbrel feedback from Alex: the wallet wizards show too many back controls. The '< Wallets' back-link at the top of both the single-sig wizard (src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte ~line 595) and the multisig wizard (src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte ~line 788) exits the flow entirely, losing context alongside the step-level Back buttons. Remove the top back-link in both wizards; keep only the step-level Back buttons.
close_reason: Removed the top '< Wallets' back-link (and its now-unused .back-link CSS) from both the single-sig wizard (src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte) and the multisig wizard (src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte). Only step-level Back buttons remain; the multisig step-1 'Cancel' ghost button was kept as the deliberate, clearly-labeled exit. Verified in dev preview: .back-link absent on both wizards, step-level Back present on step 2 of each, sidebar 'Wallets' nav unaffected.

title: Referrals: schema + hardcoded resource constants
description: Add settings keys referral_device_<trezor|ledger|coldcard|bitbox02|jade>_url (generic settings k/v table). Add dedicated multisig_service_referrals table (id, name, url, description, logo_url, active, display_order, created_at, updated_at) since it's an admin-managed repeatable list. Add src/lib/referrals.ts with hardcoded OFFICIAL_STORE_URLS and OFFICIAL_SUPPORT_URLS maps per device (verify exact URLs before shipping: trezor.io, shop.ledger.com/support.ledger.com, store.coinkite.com/coldcard.com/docs, shiftcrypto.ch/support, blockstream.com/jade + help.blockstream.com). Export getDeviceBuyUrl(device) that reads settings override else falls back to constant.
close_reason: Built + committed: schema/flag/tabs in 024542f, full feature (referrals constants + server helpers, /admin/referral-settings, wizard buy links, managed-service card, signer buy/troubleshooting links, wallet-detail Need-help expandable, 19 tests) in 8c974f7. Full suite + svelte-check green.

title: Electrum reconnect backoff bypassed by ambient requests
description: client.ts:144-148 vs L311-327 — ensureConnected() never checks reconnectTimer. Any request() call triggers a fresh connect attempt, ignoring the scheduled backoff delay. During outage, every user request hammers the dead server. Fix: ensureConnected() should respect the backoff timer and fail fast if a reconnect is already scheduled.

title: Multisig PSBT combine never enforces SIGHASH_ALL on incoming co-signer signatures
description:
Security audit 2026-07-05 (docs/SECURITY-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md), finding F1. Verified CONFIRMED by 3/3 independent adversarial reviewers.

src/lib/server/bitcoin/multisigPsbt.ts combineMultisigPsbts() (~lines 603-662) validates incoming partialSig entries only for (a) same-transaction commitment via assertSameTransaction (psbt.ts ~lines 682-733, which compares serialized input/output lists and never reads signature bytes) and (b) that the signing pubkey belongs to the multisig's own keys. The signature bytes themselves -- including the trailing sighash-type byte -- are copied through unexamined. multisigPsbtProgress (quorum counting) and finalizeMultisigPsbt/the underlying @scure/btc-signer finalize path likewise never inspect the sighash byte before assembling and broadcasting the final witness. No test in multisigPsbt.test.ts exercises non-SIGHASH_ALL signatures (its signWith test helper always signs with the library default, SIGHASH_ALL).

Impact: a co-signer (or a buggy/malicious hardware wallet) could supply a signature using a different sighash flag (SIGHASH_SINGLE, SIGHASH_NONE, or an ANYONECANPAY variant) and Cairn would accept, count toward quorum, finalize, and broadcast it without complaint. This does not enable direct fund theft by an outside attacker (assertSameTransaction still pins down the reviewed inputs/outputs before combine, and pubkey membership is checked), but it is a real, unmitigated malleability/scope-creep gap: a SIGHASH_SINGLE|ANYONECANPAY-class signature commits to less than the full transaction and could legally be replayed onto a different transaction spending the same input with different outputs, defeating the very guarantee assertSameTransaction is meant to provide for that input. Verifier consensus rates this medium-to-medium-high severity -- real and worth fixing, but requires a cooperating malicious or buggy signing device to trigger, not remotely exploitable by a pure network attacker.

Fix: in combineMultisigPsbts (and ideally also finalizeMultisigPsbt as defense in depth), reject any partialSig whose trailing sighash byte is not 0x01 (SIGHASH_ALL), with a clear error message mirroring the existing foreign-signature guard style. Add regression test coverage for non-ALL sighash rejection.
close_reason: Fixed in security-audit remediation pass; see commit. All 897 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: ColdCard/QR signer flows don't warn when wallet lacks key-origin data
description:
Page: /wallets/[id]/send (Sign step) — found while live-testing the newly-wired
ColdCardSigner/LedgerSigner/QrSigner components (verifying cairn-5i3 once
patched in locally).

What I did: on wallet 2 ("Legacy wallet", imported via bare xpub with no
master_fingerprint recorded), walked through Create -> Review -> Sign and
tried each hardware-signer method.

What happened:
- Ledger: clicking "Connect Ledger & sign" immediately shows a clear error
  before ever touching WebHID: "This transaction is missing the key-origin
  information the Ledger needs to sign. Re-create it from a wallet set up
  with its master fingerprint." (ledger.ts's accountOriginFromPsbt correctly
  detects the PSBT has no bip32Derivation, since constructPsbt only attaches
  it when wallet.master_fingerprint is known — see src/lib/server/transactions.ts
  ~line 107 and src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts ~line 178.)
- ColdCard: walks the user through all three steps (download unsigned PSBT,
  "sign on your ColdCard", upload signed file) with zero indication that a
  real ColdCard has no bip32Derivation to match against its own keys and
  will very likely be unable to identify/sign the inputs. The user only
  finds out after physically moving an SD card back and forth.
- QR/Animated: same gap — displays the QR immediately and walks through
  "approve on the device" with no upfront warning, even though a real
  SeedSigner/Passport/Jade would hit the identical missing-derivation
  problem.

What should happen: ColdCardSigner.svelte and QrSigner.svelte should perform
the same upfront check LedgerSigner already benefits from (via
accountOriginFromPsbt or an equivalent "does this PSBT have bip32Derivation"
check) and show the same clear message — something like "This wallet doesn't
have key-origin data recorded, so air-gapped/hardware signing isn't
available for it. Use the Generic wallet/file method instead." — before
walking the user through a physical device round-trip that's doomed to fail.

Priority: P2 — not a security issue (no wrong signature could be produced;
the guard downstream still requires valid signatures), but a real UX gap
that sends users through a multi-minute physical hardware dance for a
predictable, detectable failure. Affects any xpub-only imported wallet
without a stored master_fingerprint (at least "Legacy wallet" id 2 in this
instance).
close_reason: Fixed in 0d191b2: shared psbtHasKeyOrigin helper (src/lib/hw/keyOrigin.ts, checks bip32Derivation/tapBip32Derivation on every input, 5 unit tests); ColdCardSigner and QrSigner now show a clear pre-flight warning with a 'Choose another method' action instead of walking the user through a doomed physical round trip. Connected signers (Ledger/Trezor) already failed fast in their drivers.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Verified fixed by commit 0d191b2 "fix: warn upfront when a wallet lacks key-origin data". Added src/lib/hw/keyOrigin.ts + keyOrigin.test.ts, and both ColdCardSigner.svelte and QrSigner.svelte now check the PSBT's bip32Derivation before walking the user through the physical round trip.

Live-tested on wallet 2 ("Legacy wallet", no master_fingerprint recorded):
- ColdCard: selecting it now immediately shows "This wallet can't sign on a ColdCard directly. It was added without key-origin data (no master fingerprint recorded)... Use the Generic wallet / file method... or re-import the wallet with its master fingerprint." No more walking through download/sign/upload steps first.
- QR: same upfront message, correctly device-specific ("a SeedSigner, Passport, or Jade won't recognize these coins").

Full suite: 280/280 tests passing. Closing.

title: Ledger's custom BIP-388 wire protocol has zero unit tests
description: src/lib/hw/ledger.ts's hand-rolled BIP-388 wire protocol (serializeMultisigPolicy, makeDeviceWalletPolicy, exchangeInterruptible, primeInterpreterWithPolicy) is the most protocol-fragile custom-implemented code in any hardware wallet driver and has no unit tests at all, unlike the rest of ledger.ts's well-tested pure logic. Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Exported the four internal BIP-388 wire-protocol functions (serializeMultisigPolicy, makeDeviceWalletPolicy, primeInterpreterWithPolicy, exchangeInterruptible) — matching ledger.ts's 'exported for unit testing' convention — and added tests in ledger.test.ts (4 new, 49 total pass): exact v2 named-policy byte layout incl. sha256(template) and the keys Merkle root over hashLeaf(ascii(key)); wallet_id = sha256(serialization); interpreter priming order; and the interruptible-APDU loop (serves merkle requests until 0x9000, strips the status word) driven by injected stub transport/interpreter. No device or ledger-bitcoin needed.

title: Shared node_modules/.vite optimizer cache causes perpetual full-reload/500s across concurrent dev servers
description:
Observed on cairn-qa-twin (port 5347, data/qa-twin-2026-07-12.db) during QA Track F (2026-07-12).

Symptom: /wallets/multisig/new (and later every route including /) 500'd on ~every load. Client console showed hooks.client.ts's handleError firing repeatedly with distinct errorIds, each preceded by "[vite] The next HMR update will cause the page to reload". Network tab showed node_modules/.vite/deps/bbqr.js?v=<hash> returning 504 "Outdated Optimize Dep" / net::ERR_ABORTED — the client's module graph referenced an optimizer browserHash that no longer matched node_modules/.vite/deps/_metadata.json's current browserHash on disk.

Root cause: node_modules/.vite (the Vite dependency pre-bundle cache) is shared across every `npm run dev` process running against this one checkout (each QA track/session runs its own dev server on its own port, but they all point at the same node_modules). When one process's optimizer re-bundles (new dep discovered, config/tsconfig change, etc.) it bumps the cache's browserHash out from under every OTHER already-running process, which keeps serving stale hashed URLs until it also re-optimizes -- and if multiple processes are re-optimizing concurrently they can keep invalidating each other faster than any one converges. Server stdout also showed a `changed tsconfig file detected: C:/dev/cairn/devcairn-explorer-idx/tsconfig.json - Clearing cache and forcing full-reload` line, i.e. an unrelated directory/tool inside this checkout (devcairn-explorer-idx) was independently triggering full-reloads for every sibling dev server during this session.

Impact: blocks browser-driven QA of any multi-step flow (wizards, forms) for minutes at a time, appears identical to a real product 500 (same generic error page / errorId), and can strike ANY route, not just the one that changed.

Mitigation tried and confirmed working (temporarily): stop the affected dev server, delete node_modules/.vite entirely (`cmd /c rmdir /s /q`), restart. Works, but is shared infrastructure -- any concurrently-running sibling dev server will start re-triggering the same loop again, and deleting the cache while others are mid-optimize makes things worse for them too. Not a safe repeatable fix while other tracks/sessions are active.

Suggested follow-up (not fixed here, needs an infra decision, not a code fix): give each QA/dev worktree or session its own Vite cache dir (e.g. `optimizeDeps.cacheDir` per launch.json config, or run genuinely separate worktrees instead of N `npm run dev` processes sharing one node_modules), OR document this as a known hazard in the QA runbook so agents recognize "generic 500 + [vite] HMR reload spam in console + *.vite/deps 504" as an infra flake to route around (kill+restart own server) rather than a product bug to file against feature code.

No product code change indicated by this finding -- purely a shared-checkout dev-server hazard.

title: Wallet Addresses tab: Unused filter omits change-chain addresses
description:
Page: /wallets/[id] (Addresses tab)

What I did: imported a watch-only wallet from the known BIP84 test-vector zpub
(81 total addresses: 60 receive m/0/x, 21 change m/1/x). Checked the
Used/Unused filter tabs on the Addresses list.

What happened: "Used 38" correctly mixes both chains (37 used receive + 1
used change, confirmed the list includes m/1/0). But "Unused 23" only shows
receive-chain addresses (m/0/x) -- all 20 unused change addresses (m/1/1
through m/1/20) are completely absent from both tabs. 38 + 23 = 61, but the
wallet has 81 addresses total; the 20 missing ones are simply never shown
anywhere in this view. Confirmed via GET /api/wallets/1/addresses directly:
change: {used: 1, unused: 20}, receive: {used: 37, unused: 23}, and the
"Unused" tab's rendered <td> paths are 100% m/0/* with zero m/1/*.

What should happen: the Unused filter should include unused change
addresses too (matching how the Used filter already mixes both chains), or
if change addresses are deliberately excluded from Unused for some reason,
the "Unused 23" badge and "Addresses 81" total should be reconciled so the
counts actually add up -- right now a user has no way to see 20 of their
own wallet's addresses through this UI at all.

Priority: P3 -- not a fund-safety issue (balances/history are still correct
elsewhere), but a real gap in address-list completeness and a count that
visibly doesn't add up (38 + 23 ≠ 81) if anyone does the math.
close_reason: Fixed: Unused filter includes both chains (receive-first sort); comment documents why. Change rows remain distinguishable by derivation path.

title: Difficulty explorer page shows nonsensical far-future retarget/halving dates with no sanity bound
description: On /explorer/difficulty, when the backing chain has abnormally slow block times (e.g. a local test/regtest chain with blocks hours apart instead of ~10 min), the page extrapolates literal wall-clock estimates with no upper bound: observed 'ESTIMATED RETARGET: Nov 6, 2255' and 'NEXT HALVING: ~Oct 5, 27506' on a 127-block test chain. CURRENT DIFFICULTY also displays as a bare '0' (likely a formatting/precision issue for regtest's near-zero real difficulty value, not an actual zero). On real mainnet with normal block timing this wouldn't manifest, but the estimate math should probably clamp or show 'unknown'/'>100 years' past some threshold rather than a literal 5-digit-year date, and difficulty formatting should avoid rendering true tiny values as a bare 0. Low priority, cosmetic/edge-case only.
close_reason: Fixed in 22ab66d: estimatedRetargetDate now clamps the projection pace to the consensus 4x band (chain/index.ts); the difficulty page caps the halving-date pace the same way and shows tiny difficulty as '<0.01' instead of 0 (explorer/difficulty/+page.svelte).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Root cause found: src/lib/server/chain/index.ts:495 -- 'estimatedRetargetDate: tip.timestamp + base.blocksRemaining * avg' has no sanity bound. Note that projectedChangePercent two lines above (line 490) IS correctly clamped to the consensus [-75%, +300%] range, but estimatedRetargetDate is not clamped at all, so on a slow chain 'avg' (this epoch's average seconds/block) balloons and blocksRemaining * avg produces an absurd far-future date. Fix: clamp avg (or the resulting date) the same way projectedChangePercent is clamped just above it.

title: Nav header/avatar unresponsive during page load: TTL-cache layout's per-user SQLite reads
description:
QA (v0.2.2 live, Alex): user icon in top-right nav unresponsive during page load.

Root cause (matches cairn-xlrm's own fix-plan item 3, left undone by f0dd421):
(app)/+layout.server.ts runs 3 synchronous node:sqlite reads on every navigation
and every full document load -- listUnbackedWallets(), shouldShowBackupReminder(),
listActiveAnnouncementsFor() -- all real queries (joins/subselects), not the
single-keyed-SELECT fields f0dd421 already fixed. Node is single-threaded, so
these block the event loop, delaying TTFB on full loads (avatar button in
HWRail.svelte is a pure client-side menuOpen toggle -- unresponsive only because
JS hasn't hydrated yet) and serializing behind abandoned navigations on
client-side nav (xlrm's core complaint).

Note: the orchestrator prompt described this as "Electrum tip/fee TTL caching
still needed" -- that's already shipped (cairn-vknb.5, chain/cache.ts). Not the
same bug. This bead is specifically the 3 per-user SQLite reads above.

Fix: short (15s) in-process TTL cache, keyed by userId, wrapping the 3-read
bundle. Safe to serve stale: all 3 dismiss flows (backup banner, backup
reminder, announcement) already hide optimistically client-side (sessionStorage
/ component state), so server staleness within the TTL window is invisible to
the user in-session.

Out of scope / not attempted: Electrum pool abort-signal threading (xlrm fix-plan
item 2) -- SvelteKit's client router itself never sends an abort signal for
superseded navigations (see xlrm's root-cause writeup, client.js load_data),
so there's no signal to thread through without patching node_modules. Left as a
known limitation.
close_reason: done

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Fixed + committed e00cdef (cherry-picked from worktree agent acdaf4023ac667e4b -- worktree branched from main's rewritten/squashed history, not single-sig-full-wallet, so merge failed with unrelated-histories; cherry-pick applied cleanly since file content was identical). 15s TTL cache (navBundleCache.ts) wraps the 3 sync node:sqlite reads in (app)/+layout.server.ts. Verified on single-sig-full-wallet directly: npm run check 0 errors/0 warnings, new tests 5/5 passing. cairn-xlrm stays open -- item 2 (Electrum abort-signal threading) is infeasible without patching SvelteKit's own client router, noted as a known limitation.

title: Nonexistent tx shows generic app-wide 404 instead of contextual 'Transaction not found' like block/address pages
description:
Reproduced on Heartwood v0.2.0 at umbrel.local:3211 (browser QA of explorer error states).

Visiting /explorer/block/<64 zero hex chars> (a well-formed but nonexistent block hash) renders a friendly in-page "Block not found / No block matches this height or hash." message with a "Back to explorer" button -- the page chrome stays intact.

Visiting /explorer/address/<nonexistent address> behaves the same way (graceful in-page notFound state).

But visiting /explorer/tx/<64 hex chars, well-formed but nonexistent txid> instead falls through to the generic app-wide error page: "404 / This path doesn't lead anywhere." with only "Back to dashboard" / "Open the explorer" buttons -- no transaction-specific messaging, and it reads like a broken URL rather than "this transaction doesn't exist."

Root cause: block's and address's +page.server.ts (src/routes/(app)/explorer/block/[id]/+page.server.ts, src/routes/(app)/explorer/address/[address]/+page.server.ts) both catch a not-found chain error and return `notFound: true` as page DATA, which +page.svelte renders in-page. But src/routes/(app)/explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.server.ts:88 calls `error(404, 'Transaction not found')` -- a route-level SvelteKit error that bubbles to the root src/routes/+error.svelte, which (by design, to avoid leaking info on truly garbage routes) hardcodes "This path doesn't lead anywhere." for ANY 404 regardless of the thrown message. So the string 'Transaction not found' is computed but never actually shown to the user.

Suggested fix: bring the tx route in line with block/address -- return a `notFound` flag from loadTxDetails/the tx load function instead of throwing error(404), and have +page.svelte render an in-page "Transaction not found" state consistent with the other two explorer detail pages.

Repro: visit /explorer/tx/ffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffff (or any well-formed but nonexistent txid), compare against /explorer/block/<64 zeros>.
close_reason: Fixed as part of the Track F merge (1a76aea). explorer/tx/[txid] now returns notFound:true from its load function instead of throwing error(404,...), and +page.svelte renders the same in-page 'Transaction not found' state block/address already use. npm run check 0/0.

description: Addresses tab shows only receive-chain addresses. Change addresses (internal chain, m/1/*) must be viewable too — separate filter/toggle — so users can verify where change goes and audit for recovery. Applies to single-sig and multisig wallet pages if both have address lists.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Both wallet pages get a third Addresses filter chip 'Change' showing the whole internal chain (…/1/*, used + gap window) sorted by index; single-sig also gains the change badge on m/1/* rows (multisig already had it) and the explanatory change-note, with shared CSS moved into wallet-detail.css. Backend needed no changes — scan/watcher already track chain 1. Verified in Chrome: BIP84 test-vector wallet shows Change 23 with balances/txs on m/1/0-2 and badges in the Used view; fresh imported 2-of-2 multisig shows Change 20 gap-window rows with badges + note. svelte-check 0 errors, vitest 1445 passed.

title: Update Umbrel package description copy for forced first-login reset
description: caravan-store-cairn's umbrel-app.yml description (C:\dev\umbrel-community-app-store) currently says: 'Log in with the email and password shown after install, then change the password (and set your real email address, so notifications reach you) from Settings.' Once a Cairn release containing the forced /setup-admin first-login reset (cairn-49xi.2, built 2026-07-06) ships (any tag >0.1.4), that copy is stale — the app forces the password+email step itself on first login. Update the description at the next package version bump (follow the umbrel-update-app skill checklist). Split from cairn-49xi.1.

Updated alongside the version bump and release notes in the same commit.

title: normalizePsbt's magic-byte check is too shallow for good error messages
description: src/lib/server/transactions.ts hasPsbtMagic() only checks the first 5 bytes match the PSBT magic. A truncated or structurally corrupt PSBT (magic bytes intact, everything after is garbage) passes normalizePsbt() and only fails later, at assertSameTransaction() or finalizePsbt(), with a generic mismatch/incomplete-signing error rather than a clear 'this file is corrupted' message. Not a security hole -- assertSameTransaction still catches any real substitution attempt regardless -- purely an error-message-quality issue for users who upload a truncated/corrupted file. Low priority: add a basic structural sanity check (e.g. attempt Transaction.fromPSBT and catch parse errors specifically) so the error path can say 'that file looks corrupted' instead of a substitution-guard message that implies something more alarming than 'bad file.'
close_reason: Fixed in 8717710 + follow-up route fix: normalizePsbt structurally parses past the magic bytes and throws InvalidPsbtError ('truncated or corrupted'), surfaced on both broadcast and upload PATCH paths.

title: Single-sig wallets never persist master_fingerprint/derivation_path -- HW signing broken for all single-key wallets
description:
createWallet() (src/lib/server/wallets.ts:138-190) inserts only (user_id, name, type, xpub, script_type, device_type) — master_fingerprint and derivation_path (both declared in WalletRow, real DB columns) are never written, by ANY creation path, including the real hardware-connect flow: readSingleSigKeyFromTrezor() (src/lib/hw/trezor.ts:1346+) correctly reads {xpub, fingerprint, path} from the device, but src/routes/(app)/wallets/new/+page.svelte only captures d.xpub into validatedXpub — fingerprint/path are discarded, and the submitted form only sends xpub + deviceType. setWalletDevice() (wallets.ts:197) likewise only ever touches device_type.

Consequence: constructPsbt() (src/lib/server/bitcoin/psbt.ts:509) only emits bip32Derivation when params.origin is given, and src/lib/server/transactions.ts:299 only builds origin when wallet.master_fingerprint is non-null — which is NEVER true for a single-key wallet. Every exported/signed PSBT for a single-sig wallet therefore has no bip32Derivation. Cairn's own signPsbtWithTrezor() explicitly requires it and throws "This transaction is missing the key-origin information the Trezor needs to sign. Re-create it from a wallet set up with its master fingerprint." (trezor.ts ~line 176). The same requirement almost certainly applies to the Ledger/BitBox02/Jade signers (same PSBT, same guard pattern).

Reproduced live on the v0.1.3 Umbrel instance (http://umbrel.local:3211): confirmed via source read plus a live download+decode of a real "QA Trezor (regtest)" wallet's unsigned PSBT (src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/transactions/[txId]/file/+server.ts output) — the input has witnessUtxo/nonWitnessUtxo but no bip32Derivation field at all.

Note: multisig keys (multisig_keys table / MultisigSignKey) DO store and validate fingerprint correctly (resolveMultisigSignKeys in trezor.ts requires it) — this bug is specific to the single-key wallets table/createWallet() path.

This may be a very recent regression: today's (2026-07-06) wallet-dedup refactor left Ledger/BitBox/Jade signer pairs still unmerged per its own notes — worth checking whether single-sig fingerprint capture regressed in that refactor or predates it.

Fix shape: thread {fingerprint, path} from the device-read result through the wallet-creation form (hidden inputs) and into createWallet()'s INSERT (and the paste-xpub path should leave both null, as today, since there's no device to trust).
close_reason: Duplicate of cairn-alw8 -- same root cause (single-key wallets never persist master_fingerprint/derivation_path, breaking all HW signing), found independently in a concurrent QA session against the same live instance. See cairn-alw8 for the canonical writeup; my source-level analysis matched it exactly (createWallet() INSERT, constructPsbt() origin gate, trezor.ts's missing-key-origin throw).

title: MANUAL.md Part II missing-scenario expansion (backup/restore, notification channels, flags, auth, explorer, batch send, deletion)
description: Part II has no QA scenarios for: admin instance backup/restore round-trip (open bugs cairn-0dg4/cairn-lka5 sit exactly there, plus closed P1 cairn-cpb5 history); notification channels beyond tx-events (telegram/ntfy/nostr/webhook config+test, quiet hours, security_* events — cairn-sask relevant); feature-flag admin flow (regression-prone: cairn-8dup/1x3w/jyh7); auth beyond password happy-path (passkeys, recovery, rate limiting, device revoke, API tokens — compounds cairn-x7vk); explorer as a feature; batch sending (cairn-6s6); CSV export (cairn-b1rg); stateless multisig route (cairn-e8de/cairn-hla1); labels/address book; wallet/account deletion completeness (cairn-vop2/cairn-684u). Write scenarios in MANUAL.md's existing Part II format with PASS bars.

title: Caravan export/import silently drops startingAddressIndex, causing address reuse on re-import
description:
src/lib/server/multisigExport.ts:138 (caravanExport) always hardcodes 'startingAddressIndex: 0' in the exported JSON, never reading the wallet's actual receive cursor (tracked as receiveCursor / receive_cursor in src/lib/server/wallets/multisig.ts:75, bumped via bumpReceiveCursor). The CaravanImport interface/parseCaravanImport (multisigExport.ts:149-155) has no field for startingAddressIndex at all, so it's discarded on import too. createMultisig's INSERT (wallets/multisig.ts:250-254) never sets receive_cursor, taking the schema default of 0 (db.ts:50).

Concrete failure: create a multisig, receive to addresses 0-49 (receiveCursor=50), export the Caravan JSON backup, then re-import it (fresh instance, or after editing the key set in the wizard which the codebase already has logic to 'revert imported->created' for). The re-created wallet's receive cursor resets to 0 and hands out already-used addresses 0-49 again as if fresh -- silent address reuse. Not a fund-loss bug (balances are found by rescanning UTXOs by scriptPubKey, not by trusting the cursor), but a real privacy/UX round-trip data loss for exactly the kind of backup-and-restore workflow this feature exists for.

Fix: have caravanExport read the real receiveCursor into startingAddressIndex, add the field to CaravanImport/parseCaravanImport, and have the import path seed receive_cursor from it.
close_reason: Fixed in c110211

title: Single-sig PSBT finalization does not enforce SIGHASH_ALL
description: psbt.ts:871-875 — finalizePsbt just calls tx.finalize() with no SIGHASH check. The multisig path (multisigPsbt.ts:588-594) already enforces SIGHASH_ALL (cairn-srte). A buggy/malicious signer returning SIGHASH_SINGLE/NONE/ANYONECANPAY is not caught by assertSameTransaction (which only pins current draft). Fix: enforce the same trailing-byte check as multisig combine.
close_reason: Fixed on track/signing @ f6a6837, merged @ 5f5aa72, final SHA 66f0c36.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Fixed on track/signing @ f6a6837 (fix(bitcoin): enforce SIGHASH_ALL at finalize and validate cosigner finalization, commit d6dcade within that branch). finalizePsbt in bitcoin/psbt.ts now enforces the SIGHASH_ALL trailing-byte check on all inputs (mirroring the existing multisig combine check), with broadcastTransaction surfacing rejection. New/expanded tests in psbt.test.ts (+71 lines). Merged into single-sig-full-wallet at 5f5aa72 (merge(bitcoin): integrate SIGHASH_ALL + cosigner finalization validation, cairn-u2r5, cairn-vo6z). Full suite green post-merge (2411 passed / 1 skipped). Final pushed SHA: 66f0c36. Residual: validation is structural (SIGHASH byte + script binding), not full ECDSA signature verification -- filed as a separate P3 follow-up task, tagged hardening-followup.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Reprioritized P2->P1 per 2026-07-12 beads audit (docs/BEADS-AUDIT.md sec 2/7): spend-path safety issue (SIGHASH_ALL not enforced at finalization), small funds-adjacent fix, underweighted at P2.

title: Rapid triple-click on invite Create button creates duplicate invites
description:
Repro: on Admin > Invites, filled the label field and called the Create button's click handler 3 times back-to-back synchronously (simulating a fast real-world triple-click/double-click). Result: 3 separate invite codes were created (CAIRN-JGZE-7ZH7, CAIRN-CARB-3M8T, CAIRN-SQ86-78W3), all with the same label "race-test", instead of 1.

src/routes/(app)/admin/invites/+page.svelte:71 does guard the button with `disabled={creating}`, and the handler sets `creating = true` (line 48) before the request — but Svelte's reactive DOM patching for the `disabled` attribute isn't synchronous with the state assignment, so clicks that land within the same tick (before the DOM's `disabled` attribute is actually flushed) still fire the handler again. A real double-click or a stuck/repeating key can trigger this.

Low severity here — worst case is a few extra unused invite codes, easily revoked. Flagging because the same `disabled={submitting}`-during-request pattern is used elsewhere in the app (e.g. wallet creation wizards), and it's worth checking whether any of those paths could produce a more consequential duplicate (the send/broadcast flow appears to have its own server-side atomic claim marker per src/lib/server/db.ts's `broadcast_started_at` comment, which should prevent an actual double-broadcast, but duplicate-submit races on other mutating forms haven't been audited).

Fix direction: disable the button synchronously in the click/submit handler itself (or via `event.currentTarget.disabled = true`) rather than relying solely on a reactive state variable's DOM flush timing, OR add a simple in-flight guard (`if (creating) return;` at the very top of the handler) so a same-tick re-entrant call is a no-op regardless of DOM state.
close_reason: Fixed in ef5d8f5

title: Electrum client never sends a keepalive ping -- connection flaps every ~90-120s
description:
The Electrum client (src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts) supports server.ping via a ping() method (line 471) and pool.ts exposes a wrapper (pool.ts:123), but nothing in the codebase ever calls it on an interval -- grepped the whole of src/lib/server for setInterval + ping(: the only setInterval calls are in addressWatcher's 5-min refresh, backupHealth, keyHealth, dataRetention and notificationQueue, none of which ping the Electrum socket.

Reproduced live on the v0.1.3 Umbrel instance: Admin > Logs (filtered on "electrum") shows a clean, continuous "connected" / "connection lost" cycle against electrum.blockstream.info:50002 roughly every 90-120 seconds, for the entire ~24 minutes of log history checked (16:37-17:01). The Admin > Activity log shows the same pattern as repeated network_up/network_down entries ("Connected to the Bitcoin network" / "Network connection lost").

Root cause hypothesis (consistent with the observed cadence): public Electrum servers commonly enforce a short idle-socket timeout; with no periodic keepalive, Cairn's persistent connection sits idle between real requests and gets dropped by the server, triggering client.ts's onDisconnect/reconnect-backoff path (lines ~255-274). The backoff resets to RECONNECT_MIN_MS on every successful reconnect (line 158), so the cycle repeats indefinitely rather than settling into a longer, stable connection.

Impact: constant reconnect churn under normal operation (not just on a bad connection), noisy activity/log feeds, and -- per cairn-<addressWatcher-notification-flood-bug, filed alongside this one> -- may be contributing to a false "payment received" / "transaction confirmed" notification flood by repeatedly re-triggering header/scripthash resubscribe events during addressWatcher's baseline pass.

Fix shape: add a periodic (e.g. every 45-60s) keepalive that calls pool.ping() (or per-connection ping()) only while idle, unref'd like the other interval timers in this codebase.
close_reason: Added idle keepalive to ElectrumClient (src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts): a 45s unref'd setInterval started after each successful handshake sends server.ping via rawRequest only while connected and idle (pending.size===0), stopped on disconnect/close. Covers every pooled connection since it lives in the client, not the pool. Existing client/pool tests pass (21/21); full suite green (1445).

title: Spend unconfirmed outputs + CPFP fee bumping
description: Epic: let users spend their own unconfirmed change/received outputs and build CPFP child transactions to rescue a stuck low-fee incoming tx, for both single-sig and multisig. Full scoping doc: docs/CPFP-UNCONFIRMED-PLAN.md. Research basis: Sparrow/Electrum/BlueWallet all spend unconfirmed change by default and offer both RBF (sender) and CPFP (receiver) bump paths; Cairn currently hard-filters ALL unconfirmed coins out of coin selection (psbt.ts:247, multisigPsbt.ts:272) and has no CPFP builder at all (only read-only explorer display via mempool.space's /v1/cpfp endpoint).
close_reason: Epic complete: all 8 units (.1-.8) closed. Unconfirmed-spend coin selection + trust classification, stuck-tx detection, CPFP builder, Speed up UI + RBF/CPFP routing, chain-depth warnings, multisig RBF/CPFP parity, coin-trust badges, and opportunistic package-relay broadcast all built + tested.

title: Allow spending unconfirmed own/received UTXOs (core coin selection)
description: Replace the blanket confirmed-only filter (u.height > 0) in constructPsbt (psbt.ts:247-249) and constructMultisigPsbt (multisigPsbt.ts:272) with a policy that admits unconfirmed coins, classified as safe (own change) vs risky (received from elsewhere) per docs/CPFP-UNCONFIRMED-PLAN.md section 5. Automatic selection must still prefer confirmed coins first, then unconfirmed own-change, then unconfirmed received — never silently depend on someone else's unconfirmed tx without explicit coin-control opt-in. Preserve exactInputs (RBF) and onlyUtxos semantics unchanged. Full spec: docs/CPFP-UNCONFIRMED-PLAN.md Unit 1.
close_reason: Fixed in commit ba5b099: constructPsbt/constructMultisigPsbt now admit unconfirmed coins classified as own-change (safe) vs received (risky). Auto-selection prefers confirmed, then own-change, never received; coin-control opt-in allows any coin; exactInputs unchanged. SpendableUtxo.unconfirmedTrust populated by both draft builders. check + 976 tests green (9 new cases).

title: Wallet-scoped stuck/incoming-tx detection (own change vs received, RBF-signaling)
description: Backend query (single-sig + multisig) over a wallet's live UTXOs + tx history returning: which unconfirmed coins are our own change vs received from elsewhere, and whether the underlying tx still signals BIP-125 RBF. This is the data the RBF-vs-CPFP decision table (docs/CPFP-UNCONFIRMED-PLAN.md section 3) needs — no UI in this unit. Feeds the Speed Up UI unit.
close_reason: Built in bfb1ef1: detectUnconfirmedInflows (transactions.ts) classifies a wallet's unconfirmed coins as own-change vs received and resolves each stuck tx to an RBF-vs-CPFP recommendation per plan §4, reusing TxDetail.rbf/.confirmed. Single-sig (detectWalletUnconfirmedInflows) + multisig (detectMultisigUnconfirmedInflows) wrappers. No UI (feeds Unit 4). Tests cover own-RBF vs received-CPFP routing, non-signaling own tx, confirmed/raced drops, multi-output aggregation, lookup-failure fallback. check clean.

title: CPFP fee-math + PSBT builder (buildCpfpDraft)
description: Add buildCpfpDraft() (transactions.ts) and its multisig equivalent (multisigTransactions.ts), implementing child_fee_sats = ceil(target_rate * (parent_vsize + child_vsize)) - parent_fee_sats (docs/CPFP-UNCONFIRMED-PLAN.md section 2), sourcing parent_vsize/parent_fee_sats from EsploraTx.weight/fee (no mempool.space-only dependency). Forces the wallet's unconfirmed output on the stuck parent as a required input and routes through the EXISTING constructPsbt/constructMultisigPsbt builders — do not hand-roll a second transaction constructor. Clamp target rate to MAX_FEE_RATE; refuse a zero/negative child fee; pull in extra confirmed UTXOs if the qualifying coin can't cover fee+non-dust payout. Depends on the unconfirmed-spend coin-selection change.
close_reason: Fixed in commit f6a784c: buildCpfpDraft (single-sig) + cpfpChildFee implement child_fee = ceil(target*(parent_vsize+child_vsize)) - parent_fee, sourcing parent vsize/fee from chain tx detail (not mempool.space-only). Forces the wallet's unconfirmed output as a required input (coin-control) and routes through constructPsbt via estimateTxVsize (factored out of psbt.ts). Clamps target to MAX_FEE_RATE, floors child to 1 sat/vB, refuses not_needed/coin_too_small. New draft (no replaces_txid). cpfp.test.ts covers fee math + builder e2e. check + 984 tests green. NOTE: the 'Speed up' route/UI is Unit 4 (cairn-u9ob.4, P2) and multisig CPFP parity is Unit 6 (cairn-u9ob.6, P2) per the plan's unit split.

title: 'Speed up' UI + RBF-vs-CPFP routing
description: Add a 'Speed up' action on unconfirmed transactions in the wallet page (mirrors the existing RBF 'Bump fee' button placement/style) that applies the RBF-vs-CPFP decision table in docs/CPFP-UNCONFIRMED-PLAN.md section 3: routes to the existing bump-fee flow when the wallet originated the tx and it still signals RBF, otherwise opens a CPFP review (pre-filled amount/fee) built on the new draft — NOT a replaces_txid resume, since a CPFP child doesn't replace anything. Reuse the existing CPFP/RBF badge copy from /explorer/tx/[txid] (the cpfp-hint block) instead of inventing new language. Depends on the stuck-tx detection query and the CPFP builder.
close_reason: Built (single-sig + multisig): Speed up button routes RBF (reuse bump flow) vs CPFP (buildCpfpDraft review) per §4; server loads detect*UnconfirmedInflows; fixed multisig load error-branch to keep savedTxs/speedUp defined. svelte-check 0 errors, CPFP suites green.

title: Ancestor/descendant/cluster chain-depth warnings
description: Cluster mempool (Bitcoin Core 31.0, released April 2026) replaced the old fixed 25-ancestor/25-descendant/101kvB policy with a whole-cluster cap (64 tx, 101,000 vB per cluster) and removed the CPFP carve-out — the two regimes coexist in the wild since node upgrades take time. Before building a send or CPFP child that spends an unconfirmed coin, walk its ancestor chain (reuse ChainService.getCpfpInfo's ancestors array where available) and warn (do not hard-block) as the count/package-vsize approaches whichever limit applies to the connected node — default to the legacy, more conservative 25/25/101kvB numbers when the node's regime can't be determined (Cairn has no reliable signal for this via Electrum or esplora today). Degrade silently (no warning) on backends without the v1 CPFP endpoint, same pattern every other v1-only EsploraApi method already follows. Full spec: docs/CPFP-UNCONFIRMED-PLAN.md section 5 (rewritten for cluster mempool) and section 2 (cluster mempool background).
close_reason: Built: chainDepth.ts (checkUnconfirmedChainDepth/checkSelectedInputsChainDepth) walks ancestor/descendant chains via getCpfpInfo, warns near legacy 25/25 cap (conservative default), degrades silently without v1. Wired into buildDraft/buildCpfpDraft + multisig equivalents + endpoints; non-blocking Review banner on both send flows. 9 tests + full CPFP suite green.

title: Multisig RBF + CPFP parity
description: Build bumpMultisigTransaction (closes cairn-mklv) mirroring bumpTransaction's BIP-125 checks exactly, THEN extend the unconfirmed-spend and CPFP-builder units to multisigPsbt.ts/multisigTransactions.ts. Do not start the CPFP-parity half before multisig RBF lands — the RBF-vs-CPFP routing table needs multisig RBF to exist to route to it at all. Full spec: docs/CPFP-UNCONFIRMED-PLAN.md Unit 6.
close_reason: Built in bfb1ef1: buildMultisigCpfpDraft brings CPFP to multisig at parity with single-sig (multisig RBF already landed via cairn-mklv). Shares cpfpChildFee/CpfpError with single-sig; new estimateMultisigTxVsize mirrors psbt.ts. Fresh draft (no replaces_txid), re-enters roster sign/broadcast flow, cosigner-gated. POST /api/wallets/multisig/:id/transactions/cpfp added (fee_bumping-gated). Tests: package averages target rate, no-unconfirmed-output refusal, already-confirmed refusal, detection routing. NOTE (from alexl comment): Core 31.0 feerate-diagram RBF acceptance may differ from BIP-125 bumpMultisigTransaction — flagged as follow-on, not fixed here.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Heads up for whoever picks this up: Bitcoin Core 31.0 (released April 2026, see docs/CPFP-UNCONFIRMED-PLAN.md section 2) overhauled RBF acceptance to a feerate-diagram comparison, replacing the older 'pays enough more' rule. bumpTransaction()'s BIP-125 checks (and this unit's planned bumpMultisigTransaction mirror of them) implement the older rule. A bump that passes today might be accepted/rejected differently by a cluster-mempool node. Not asking you to fix this now — just flagging so it isn't mistaken for a bug if it surfaces during testing against a 31.0+ node; file a follow-on issue if you hit it.

title: Coin trust UX: badge safe vs risky unconfirmed coins
description: Badge unconfirmed coins in CoinControl.svelte as own-change (neutral tone, 'Change - unconfirmed') vs received-from-elsewhere (warning tone, 'Unconfirmed - could still be replaced', with a Term tooltip explaining double-spend risk in plain language per Cairn's UX conventions). For multisig, coordinate with cairn-zcui (no coin-control UI exists yet for multisig send) rather than duplicate — if zcui is unbuilt when this starts, build the minimal coin list it needs and note the overlap on both issues. Full spec: docs/CPFP-UNCONFIRMED-PLAN.md section 5 / Unit 7.
close_reason: Built: CoinControl.svelte badges unconfirmed coins own-change (neutral) vs received (warning + Term tooltip); send + multisig-send server loads now include+classify unconfirmed coins via classifyUnconfirmedTrust + ownBroadcastTxids/ownMultisigTxids. svelte-check clean.

title: Opportunistic package-relay broadcast (Electrum broadcast_package / esplora /txs/package)
description: Probe (once, cached, degrade-silently, same pattern as EsploraApi.probeV1()) whether the connected Electrum server supports blockchain.transaction.broadcast_package and/or the configured esplora backend supports /txs/package. When available, use it for the one case sequential broadcast can't cover: a Cairn-built draft whose fee rate is too low to be accepted alone (below the node's minimum relay/mempool-floor fee) — submit as a package with a fee-paying child instead of broadcasting a doomed parent. Explicitly NOT required for baseline CPFP (Units 1-7 target an already-broadcast, already-propagated parent, which sequential broadcast handles fine) — pure enhancement, safe to defer or drop. Full research + rationale: docs/CPFP-UNCONFIRMED-PLAN.md section 2 (cluster mempool / package relay) and Unit 8.
close_reason: Built: Electrum broadcast_package primitive + packageRelay.ts orchestrator (probe once, cache, degrade-silently); wired into single-sig + multisig broadcast reject path as guarded parent+child package rescue (below-floor/missing-parent), fetching unconfirmed parent hexes from chain; verdict reset on reconfigureChain. 5 tests; broadcast suites green (57).

title: db.ts notified_txids UNIQUE rebuild migration untested
description: db.ts:739-766 — second untested destructive migration: ALTER TABLE RENAME TO old, CREATE TABLE new schema, INSERT...SELECT, DROP TABLE old. Widens UNIQUE constraint to include user_id. Zero test coverage — same fresh-DB bypass as the vault rename. Fix: add test seeding old notified_txids shape, importing db.ts, asserting rebuilt table has new UNIQUE and 1:1 row copy.

title: Missing regression test: SMTP cleartext cross-field validation (cairn-es32)
description: cairn-es32 (P2, closed in c110211) fixed admin SMTP settings allowing tls='none' while smtp_user/smtp_pass were configured, which would send credentials in cleartext with no cross-field validation. Fix confirmed present (effectiveTls/effectiveUser/effectivePass cross-field check in the admin notifications settings route). No test covers this specific validation. Add a test: POST {smtpTls: 'none'} while smtp_user/smtp_pass are already set and assert 400 with settings unchanged; also verify the clear-credentials-then-set-tls-none path succeeds once creds are actually cleared.
close_reason: Regression tests committed in 834945e (admin.test.ts, notifiedTxidsCleanup.test.ts, settings.test.ts, api/admin/{settings,users,notifications}/server.test.ts). All green in full suite.

title: Replace esplora tx-detail fetch on scan path with local Electrum-only parsing
description:
collectScanTxs's chain.getTx (chain/index.ts) does esplora HTTP fetches per tx on the wallet scan hot path (~2 fresh WAN round-trips x up to 50 unique txids/scan after accounting for the 10s tipHeight cache; outspends/.spent is fetched but NEVER READ on this path -- pure waste, only the explorer page needs it).

Fix: fetch raw hex via existing getTransaction(txid,false) (works against electrs, not just Fulcrum/ElectrumX -- do NOT depend on verbose=true, electrs doesn't support it), parse locally with already-in-tree @scure/btc-signer, take confirmation height from history already in hand, get block timestamp via existing blockchain.block.header(height) (client.ts:582, decode timestamp from 80-byte header). Spent-side delta resolves with zero extra fetches in the common case (wallet-owned inputs are by construction already in that scripthash's history, cacheable per-scan). Fee needs prevout resolution only for non-wallet inputs (rare) -- extra fetch or display a dash as fallback. Drop outspends fetch from the scan path entirely (explorer page can fetch it separately if needed).

Sequence after the sync-engine Phase 1 bead (both touch scan internals; avoid concurrent-session merge conflicts, same lesson as the BitBox02 incident).

title: Test gap still open: single-sig buildDraft/coin-control (wallets.ts) has zero test coverage
description: The 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md, section 1.5) noted as a Note-level (not filed-as-bead) finding: 'Single-sig buildDraft/coin-control (wallets.ts) has zero coverage — wallets.test.ts only covers tx-labels; createWallet, listWallets, getWalletDetail, nextReceiveAddress are untested anywhere.' Re-verified 2026-07-06: still true — src/lib/server/wallets.test.ts contains only a 'tx labels' describe block; no test exercises buildDraft, coin-control/UTXO selection, createWallet, listWallets, getWalletDetail, or nextReceiveAddress for the single-sig path. This is fund-moving code (transaction construction) with no direct unit coverage at the wallets.ts layer (only indirectly via psbt.test.ts's constructPsbt tests, which don't cover wallets.ts's own orchestration). Add tests for each of these functions, particularly buildDraft's coin-control/UTXO-filtering paths.
close_reason: Committed in 8603ac6: multisigShares.test.ts (first coverage), contacts.test.ts share-revocation, notifications.test.ts stage isolation, broadcast route malformed-JSON 400 + log.error tests, walletsDraft.test.ts buildDraft/coin-control coverage.

title: CoreRpcRequiredNotice dead-ends non-admin users (no back/secondary action)
description: Block-explorer elevation audit (2026-07-12): CoreRpcRequiredNotice.svelte (shipped via cairn-zoz8.9, closed 2026-07-10) is the shared 'needs a Bitcoin Core node' component shown on tx/[txid] and block/[id] detail pages when only Electrum/Esplora is configured. For a non-admin viewer it dead-ends: there's no 'Back to explorer'/'Search again' button, only the tiny '< Explorer' breadcrumb link which is scrolled off-top on mobile. The notice correctly tells an admin what to configure but gives a non-admin no escape. Fix: add a back/secondary action for non-admin viewers specifically. Files: src/lib/components/heartwood/CoreRpcRequiredNotice.svelte, src/routes/(app)/explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.svelte, src/routes/(app)/explorer/block/[id]/+page.svelte. Searched for overlap first: cairn-zoz8.9's closure notes only mention feature/isAdmin/compact props, not this navigation dead-end -- filing new as a gap in the already-shipped component.
close_reason: CoreRpcRequiredNotice non-admin branch now includes a Back to explorer link; live-verified on tx/block detail. See comments for detail.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Fixed: src/lib/components/CoreRpcRequiredNotice.svelte:80-92 -- the non-admin else-branch of the full-panel variant now includes a "Back to explorer" link (href=/explorer, chevron-left icon, same btn-secondary btn-sm styling as the admin's "Configure Bitcoin Core" link) below the existing "Ask your instance operator..." hint. Admin branch unchanged. The compact chip variant is unaffected (it's a single inline cell, not a dead end). This is a shared component so the fix applies to all three callers (explorer/tx/[txid], explorer/block/[id], explorer/mempool/blocks) without per-route changes.

QA: live-verified as a throwaway non-admin DB user against /explorer/block/800000 on the shared dev instance (Bitcoin Core RPC not configured there), read_page confirmed link "Back to explorer" href="/explorer" present at both 1280x800 and 375x812. Re-verified as a throwaway admin user that the admin branch still renders only "Configure Bitcoin Core" (no regression, no duplicate link). Screenshot tool was unavailable this session; used read_page/get_page_text fallback. Throwaway users deleted after QA.

title: Branding: default operator name still says 'Cairn instance'
description: In v0.2.0 (post-Heartwood-rebrand), admin > Settings > Operator name defaults to 'the operator of this Cairn instance' -- this string wasn't updated during the rebrand and is shown to end users on the terms-acceptance screen ('This instance is operated by ...'). Also spotted: the SMTP 'From address' field placeholder in admin > Notifications is 'cairn@example.com', and admin > Logs shows the log file path as /data/logs/cairn.log (lower priority, internal-facing). Also: activity log error messages (admin > Activity, e.g. 'Bitcoin node connection down — Cairn has been unable to reach its Bitcoin backend...') still say 'Cairn' instead of 'Heartwood'. Sweep the codebase for remaining literal 'Cairn' user-facing strings post-rebrand.
close_reason: Fixed in 392f6a2, merged to single-sig-full-wallet. Rebranded 2 remaining user-facing Cairn strings: BitBox02 pairing-failure error message, admin SMTP From-address placeholder. 2 of the 4 originally reported instances (operator-name default, chain-health activity messages) were already fixed by an earlier same-day commit -- QA was testing a build that predated them. Log-file path label left alone (it's the real CAIRN_LOG_FILE-backed constant, not cosmetic). Invite-code CAIRN- prefix flagged separately for Alex's branding call, not changed. npm run check 0/0, 85 tests pass.

title: Admin Settings: 'Test connection' buttons hang forever, never submit
description:
Page: /admin/settings (Node connection > Custom)

What I did: switched Node connection to "Custom", entered a real, working
Electrum server (127.0.0.1:60401, TLS off) and Esplora URL
(http://127.0.0.1:3002) -- both independently verified reachable and correct
(raw electrum JSON-RPC calls to server.version and blockchain.headers.subscribe
both respond in well under 100ms; the Esplora REST endpoint is also live).
Clicked "Test connection" under Electrum server.

What happened: the button shows a spinner and becomes disabled (client state
briefly correct -- `testing = 'electrum'` fires), but it never resolves.
Waited 40+ seconds on one attempt, reproduced again after a server restart
(13+ seconds, well past testElectrum()'s own 8-second internal timeout in
src/lib/server/chain/index.ts). Checked the browser's network log across both
attempts: zero POST requests to `?/testElectrum` ever left the browser. The
dev server's own stdout shows no incoming request either.

Confirmed the button itself is correctly wired at the HTML level: it's a real
`type="submit"` button, `formaction="?/testElectrum"`, genuinely inside the
settings `<form>` (whose own action is `?/save`). Since it's a proper submit
button with a valid formaction and no request ever fires, the most likely
cause is the form's `use:enhance` (or equivalent submission-interception
logic) swallowing/failing the submission before it reaches fetch() -- possibly
throwing before calling `preventDefault`'s paired submit, or awaiting a
promise that never resolves/rejects. The exact same symptom applies to the
mirror "Test connection" button under Esplora (same code shape, not
separately reproduced but very likely to share the bug).

What should happen: clicking "Test connection" should hit the server (the
network tab should show a POST to `?/testElectrum`), and within ~8 seconds
either show "Connected — tip N" or a red failure badge with the error message
-- never hang indefinitely with no visible way to cancel or retry (the button
stays disabled forever, `disabled={testing !== null}` never gets reset since
`testing` state has no path back to null).

Impact: an admin configuring their own node has no way to verify their
Electrum/Esplora settings actually work before saving -- the one feature
built specifically for that (pre-save validation) is completely non-
functional. I worked around it entirely by writing directly to the settings
table via node:sqlite, which is not something a real admin could do.

Priority: P2 -- doesn't block saving/using custom settings (I verified those
still take effect once actually saved, requiring a server restart to
pick up in this dev environment specifically -- reconfigureChain is
presumably meant to hot-apply on Save, separate from this bug), but the
verification UX is completely broken, and a stuck-forever spinner with no
error and no way to retry is a real dead end for the person using it.
close_reason: Fixed: root cause was self-disabling submit buttons cancelling their own form submission. Pending state moved into use:enhance, 20s watchdog, mutual disable. Verified via direct action POST (6ms response) + SSR 200.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Verified fixed: clicking 'Test connection' on Admin > Settings now correctly shows 'Connected -- tip 127' immediately. Retested during the final polish QA pass.

title: Mid-broadcast delete race erases record of real broadcast
description: transactions.ts:912-920 and multisigTransactions.ts:389-399 — delete gates on status but not broadcast_started_at. Between atomic claim and trailing status update, concurrent delete succeeds. Funds moved on network but DB has zero record. Also: deleteMultisigTransaction doesn't exclude superseded status (unlike deleteTransaction). Fix: add broadcast_started_at IS NULL check to delete guard, add superseded exclusion to multisig delete.
close_reason: Landed in 94ddd83. Both deleteTransaction and deleteMultisigTransaction replaced check-then-delete with a single atomic conditional DELETE: WHERE id=? AND wallet_id/multisig_id=? AND status NOT IN ('completed','superseded') AND broadcast_started_at IS NULL, returning changes>0 — closes the TOCTOU against broadcastTransaction's atomic broadcast_started_at claim. Added the missing 'superseded' exclusion to the multisig delete. Verified: broadcast-claim mechanism is broadcast_started_at TEXT on both tables (db.ts:132-137, 346), claimed by broadcast*/released on failure. Tests: transactions+multisigTransactions 55 pass (new: in-flight-claim refusal single+multisig, superseded multisig refusal); svelte-check 0/0.

title: Deleted wallets keep firing notifications via address watcher
description: addressWatcher.ts:645-700 — refreshWatches only adds new subscriptions, never removes them. deleteWallet/deleteMultisig dont call back into watcher. Deleted wallet's ~60 scripthash subscriptions live forever until restart, manufacturing new orphaned notified_txids rows and sending notifications linking to 404 pages. Fix: add unwatchWallet/unwatchMultisig methods called from delete functions, or check wallet existence in handleScripthashChange before notifying.
close_reason: Landed in acc0b69. Deleted wallets/multisigs no longer fire notifications: unwatchWallet/unwatchMultisig strip scripthashes from watcher state on delete, refreshWatches now prunes stale entries (covers accountDeletion's FK-cascade path too), and handleScripthashChange guards on walletStillExists() (fail-closed) before notifying + self-prunes. Tests: addressWatcher/wallets 85 pass incl. removal-on-delete (single+multisig) and notify-guard-blocks-and-self-prunes; full suite 1843 pass in-worktree; svelte-check 0/0.

title: addressWatcher.ts reintroduces the exact Tier-0 cairn-j6fv bug in fresh code: tx amounts computed by address-string match, not scriptPubKey
description: src/lib/server/addressWatcher.ts (~line 317, handleScripthashChange): 'if (out.address && walletAddrs.has(out.address)) receivedSats += out.value' — walletAddrs is built from Cairn's own MAINNET-ONLY derived addresses (deriveAddress()/deriveMultisigAddress()), while out.address is whatever encoding the configured chain backend reports (bcrt1.../tb1... on regtest/testnet). This is the IDENTICAL bug already diagnosed and fixed today in commit ebcd945 ('fix(cairn-j6fv): compute tx delta by scriptPubKey, not address string') for walletScan.ts/multisigScan.ts — but addressWatcher.ts is NEW code (commit 0a42065, shipped in the same session AFTER the fix) that does not reuse scriptPubKeyHex()/the scriptPubKey-matching pattern at all, reintroducing the exact defect from scratch despite TxVout already exposing a network-independent scriptPubKey field (chain/index.ts) that could have been used directly. Effect: on any non-mainnet backend (the same regtest setup that originally surfaced cairn-j6fv), receivedSats stays 0 for every genuine deposit, tx_large never fires (requires receivedSats>0), and every real payment silently downgrades to a generic 'New wallet activity' notification with no amount — the notification-plan equivalent of the original bug, in new code that shipped without applying the lesson from the fix made hours earlier in the same session. Fix: change addressWatcher.ts to match tx.vout[].scriptPubKey against a Set of scriptPubKeyHex(address) for each watched address, exactly as walletScan.ts/multisigScan.ts now do, including matching prevScriptPubKey for spent-input detection if relevant here.
close_reason: Fixed in fa4baec

title: Layout runs 13-15 sequential SQLite queries per navigation, none cached
description: +layout.server.ts — every nav fires: mustResetPassword (1q) → disclosure/agreement gate (getUserAgreement 3q + acceptedVersion 1q) → recovery gate (2q) → getInstanceSettings (full table scan + decrypts RPC password) → getUserAgreement AGAIN (3q duplicate) → listUnbackedWallets → shouldShowBackupReminder (2q) → listActiveAnnouncementsFor. None cached, unlike chain/cache.ts TTL pattern used elsewhere. getUserAgreement runs twice. Most likely single largest contributor to nav stutter.
