Fix: short-TTL cache for instance settings and user agreement, dedupe getUserAgreement, skip RPC password decrypt when only instanceMode needed.
close_reason: Landed in 3dd2b4a; independently reviewed (no blockers/majors). Root cause was the (app) layout load reading url.pathname, forcing a full server re-run of ~13-15 sequential SQLite queries on every client nav. Fix: moved all four gates into a pure appGateRedirect() in hooks.server.ts (scoped by event.route.id startsWith '/(app)'); layout load is now ({ locals }) with zero tracked deps so SvelteKit caches it across navs. Reviewer verified gate order/semantics are a byte-for-byte match, the hooks gate is a strict superset of the old protection (form actions now gated too), no redirect loops, no reachability gap, and caching is UI-data-only (not a security boundary). Full suite 1859 pass; svelte-check 0/0.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: ARCH-AUDIT 2026-07-07 (Opus orchestration + Fable advisor): Found the STRUCTURAL root cause behind the 13-15 queries, and it's a near-free fix superior to per-query caching. The layout load re-runs on EVERY navigation ONLY because it reads url.pathname (line 19, for the /login?next= redirect target). A SvelteKit 2 server layout load that reads a url/params property is re-invoked (server round-trip included) whenever that property changes = essentially every nav; a load that reads only untracked locals is cached and does NOT re-run. Fable's recommended fix: (1) wrap the read as untrack(() => url.pathname) OR drop url entirely and move the auth/disclosure/recovery GATING into hooks.server.ts (which already runs per-request and already computes locals.user) — then flags/instanceMode/operatorName/announcements load ONCE per session and only refresh on an explicit invalidate('cairn:settings') after a mutation. (2) For the reads that must stay, stream the non-critical ones (unbackedWallets, showBackupReminder, announcements) as un-awaited top-level promises after an await setImmediate() so the shell flushes first; keep the redirect gates awaited (they're control flow). Caveat: a non-rerunning layout redirect is UX, not security — keep real authz in hooks + per-endpoint (already the case). This makes cairn-v84z's caching approach a fallback, not the primary fix.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: RECOMMENDED FIX DESIGN (Fable, verified vs hooks.server.ts + layout.server.ts). Move ALL FOUR gates (auth+?next=, mustResetPassword, disclosure/agreement, recovery) out of the layout load and into hooks.server.ts. Then the layout load signature becomes ({ locals }) only — no url/params/depends access — so it has NO tracked dependencies and SvelteKit genuinely CACHES it across client-side navs (the layout node returns type:'skip', body never executes; re-runs only on invalidateAll/matching invalidate/full document load). SHAPE: new pure fn $lib/server/appGate.ts returning a redirect target or null; call it in handle AFTER the isAsset early-return (synergy with cairn-isda) and scope it via event.route.id?.startsWith('/(app)') — NOT pathname string-matching — which auto-excludes /login,/disclosure,/agreement,/setup-admin,/api/*,SSE,assets. TRAP + BONUS: a thrown redirect() in handle becomes a raw 302 that use:enhance's applyAction chokes on for POST form actions, so branch: for non-GET/HEAD return error(401) (same pattern as the existing isAdminMutationRequest backstop). The BONUS is structural: gates now cover FORM ACTIONS, which the layout load NEVER did (actions don't run parent loads — the exact hole documented in hooks.server.ts's isAdminMutationRequest comment and the root cause behind the admin-action P0s cairn-jnlx/cairn-fame). DON'T split the layout data — use:enhance's default invalidateAll() already self-heals the volatile slices (unbacked/announcements/reminder); TTL/streaming is over-engineering once URL-tracking is gone. So the caching approach originally noted on this bead is the FALLBACK; the primary fix is stop-being-URL-tracked via the hook move.

title: Send flow a11y/mobile polish sweep
description:
Batch of smaller independent fixes found auditing the new send/sign flow -- fine to do together:
1. Fee-tier buttons and BTC/Max mode toggle (send/+page.svelte .seg-btn, .fee-card) have short vertical padding (~24-32px effective height) -- under the ~44px WCAG 2.5.5 touch-target guidance, worth padding out on mobile specifically.
2. DeviceCard.svelte disabled state uses opacity:0.6 on the whole card rather than a proper muted color token -- text contrast on the disabled state likely dips below AA; use --text-muted-style tokens instead of blanket opacity.
3. The 'coins being spent' disclosure toggle (send/+page.svelte .utxo-toggle) has no aria-expanded/aria-controls -- screen reader users can't tell it's a toggle or what it controls.
4. QrSigner's animated BBQr frame display has no aria-live status announcing frame progress (e.g. 'frame 2 of 5') for screen reader users, and no text-based alternative for users with prefers-reduced-motion who may have animation-dependent cues suppressed.
close_reason: All four items done: 44px touch targets on .seg-btn/.fee-card via max-width/pointer:coarse media rule and aria-expanded/aria-controls on the utxo disclosure (7c944da); DeviceCard AA-contrast disabled state (40c7417); QR scan aria-live progress + prefers-reduced-motion manual frame stepping (bb33a3c).

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Items 2 and 4 done (bb33a3c, 40c7417): DeviceCard AA contrast, QR scan aria-live + reduced-motion manual stepping. Items 1 and 3 (touch targets, utxo-toggle aria) in flight with the send-page agent.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Verified items 2 and 4 (commits bb33a3c, 40c7417):

Item 2 (DeviceCard AA contrast) — live-tested: forced a disabled state by patching navigator.hid to undefined so Ledger renders "Unavailable" with its reason text, then measured actual contrast against the card's real background (rgb(26,22,20)): hint text rgb(146,133,122) = 5.0:1, title text rgb(168,158,148) = 6.8:1. Both clear WCAG AA passes (>=4.5:1 for normal text). opacity is 1 on the card now, confirming the blanket-opacity approach is gone.

Item 4 (QR aria-live + reduced motion + paste discoverability) — verified by reading the current QrSigner.svelte source (couldn't drive it live end-to-end, see caveat below):
- "Paste the signed transaction instead" now sits beside "Start camera" up front (scan-actions div), not just after a camera failure.
- aria-live="polite" status region is always mounted in the scan phase, announcing frame progress.
- prefers-reduced-motion is read via Svelte's MediaQuery reactive class; when set, the auto-cycling frame timer is skipped (checked in the same effect that starts the interval) and manual Previous/Next frame stepping buttons appear instead.
This all matches the commit message description and looks correctly wired.

Caveat: I could not drive the actual QR scan/paste UI live end-to-end in the browser this round. psbtHasKeyOrigin() now gates the entire QrSigner render (cairn-sta's fix), and the only wallet with a real spendable balance in this dev environment (wallet 2, "Legacy wallet") has no master_fingerprint, so it always hits the key-origin warning before reaching the scan phase. The only wallet with key-origin data (wallet 4, test vector) has zero balance and can't build a draft. Same test-data gap I've flagged before for the hardware-signer happy path — not a new issue, just noting why this is a code-review pass rather than a click-through for item 4.

Items 1 (touch targets) and 3 (utxo-toggle aria) not yet landed — will test when they land.

title: Accessibility & mobile polish sweep
description:
Batch of smaller, independent a11y/mobile fixes found during audit — fine to do together in one pass:
1. --text-faint (#4a4038 on #1a1614 bg, src/app.css) is ~3.6:1 contrast, fails WCAG AA (4.5:1) for normal text. Used for secondary labels in wallets, admin/invites, dashboard, explorer pages. Either lighten the token or restrict its use to large/decorative text only.
2. Wallet detail page's banner-dismiss button (src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.svelte, .banner-dismiss) relies on opacity shift for focus with no :focus-visible ring — inconsistent with the app's global :focus-visible rule in app.css.
3. Admin tables (admin/users, admin/invites) have overflow-x:auto wrappers but no visual affordance that they're scrollable on mobile (no breakpoint-specific hint/shadow).
4. Icon-only buttons missing aria-label in several places (e.g. the sidebar logout button in src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte only has a title attribute, which isn't reliably announced by screen readers).
5. Admin invite-creation form (admin/invites/+page.svelte .create-form) has no mobile breakpoint — fields compress awkwardly on narrow viewports instead of stacking.
6. Mempool visualizer tooltip (explorer/mempool/blocks/+page.svelte) uses fixed positioning that can still overflow on very narrow (<256px) viewports.
close_reason: Fixed in 95a585c, all six items plus one the audit underestimated: measured contrast in-browser showed --text-faint at 1.78:1 AND --text-muted at 2.9:1 (worse than the filed 3.6 estimate), so muted was lightened to a verified 4.56:1 on the darkest surface and faint became decorative-only with its four informative call sites promoted; focus-visible ring on the wallet banner dismiss; scroll shadows on all overflowing tables (local-attachment cover technique, appear only when scrollable); aria-label added to the clear-recent control (the flagged logout button already had one); invite form stacks below 560px (verified at 375px); visualizer tooltip clamps to >=8px left and viewport-capped width. svelte-check 0/0, 195 tests green, mobile verified live

title: Admin notifications settings: saving crashes with '.trim is not a function' if SMTP Port field is touched
description: src/routes/(app)/admin/notifications/+page.svelte: smtpPort is declared '$state(init.smtpPort)' (a string from the server load) and bound via 'bind:value={smtpPort}' to '<input id="smtpPort" type="number" ...>' (line ~180). Svelte's bind:value on a <input type=number> always writes back a JS number, not a string, once the user edits the field — but currentBody() (line ~60) unconditionally calls 'smtpPort.trim()' when building the PUT payload for /api/admin/notifications. The moment an admin changes the port (e.g. typing a custom SMTP port), smtpPort becomes a number, and clicking 'Save settings' or 'Save & send test email' throws 'smtpPort.trim is not a function' (surfaces client-side as an uncaught-error banner reading roughly $.get(...).trim is not a function under Svelte 5's compiled runes). Because the exception is thrown synchronously inside currentBody(), which save() calls before the fetch(), the fetch to /api/admin/notifications never happens — so the SMTP settings are silently NOT persisted, and 'Save & send test email' then correctly reports back SMTP is not configured on this instance (a confusing secondary symptom of the same root cause). Reproduced live: started a fresh instance (npm run dev with a clean CAIRN_DB), navigated to /admin/notifications, filled Host/From, changed Port from 587 to 2599 via the Port input, clicked 'Save & send test email' -> got the client-side crash banner and the settings were not saved. Fix: coerce smtpPort to a string in currentBody() (e.g. 'String(smtpPort).trim()'), or declare smtpPort with an explicit string type guard / parse it as a number consistently end-to-end instead of mixing string state with a number-typed input.
close_reason: Fixed: currentBody() in admin/notifications/+page.svelte now uses String(smtpPort).trim() instead of smtpPort.trim(), so editing the Port field (which makes Svelte bind:value write back a number) no longer throws 'smtpPort.trim is not a function' and silently skips the save.

title: Possible race condition: rapid 'Test connection' clicks in admin node settings can silently drop results / reset form
description:
While testing Admin > Settings > Node connection (Custom mode), I observed the following once and could not reliably reproduce it on retry, so filing at low confidence for someone with more time to investigate:

Sequence: switch connection mode to "Custom" (client-side only, not yet saved) -> click "Test connection" next to Electrum server (got "Connected — tip N") -> immediately click "Test connection" next to the Esplora base URL field -> the whole Node connection section reverted to "Public servers" and the Custom-mode fields (Electrum server, Esplora URL, Bitcoin Core RPC) disappeared entirely, discarding the unsaved mode selection.

On a clean retry of the same two-rapid-clicks sequence, the mode did NOT fully reset, but the second ("Esplora") test-connection's result text was silently dropped (button reverted to plain "Test connection" instead of showing "OK — tip N") — suggesting a race between the two forms' `use:enhance` submissions where a slower response overwrites a faster one, or an `invalidateAll()`/reload triggered by one form's success handler stomps the other's in-flight local state.

Suggested investigation: check whether the "Test connection" buttons' form actions call `invalidateAll()` or otherwise trigger the parent `+page.server.ts` load to re-run and overwrite local $state (connectionMode, per-field test results) with stale server data, especially when two test-connection requests are in flight concurrently.
close_reason: Fixed in 1f94da6: per-backend test results are copied into local state as they arrive, so the shared ActionData slot can no longer drop or overwrite the other test's badge; buttons stay disabled while a test is in flight. The one-off full mode reset was not reproducible; local-state preservation removes the suspected stomp path

title: multisig name length cap counts UTF-16 code units not glyphs
description: createMultisig name.length > 60 rejects emoji-heavy names that look ~16 chars. UX nit. Pinned in hostileText.test.ts on branch test/qa-wave-2026-07-12, worktree C:\dev\cairn-testqa.

title: Perf: stream blocking wallet/send loads + chain-data TTL cache (Umbrel lag fix)
description: Architectural fix for persistent Umbrel/ARM navigation lag. Root cause: 4 load() functions still block full page render on synchronous Electrum/esplora round-trips instead of streaming (established pattern already used elsewhere in the app). Full scope, design, and per-track complexity in docs/PERF-ARCHITECTURE-PLAN.md. Sub-beads are file-disjoint and safe to build in parallel.
close_reason: All 6 children (vknb.1-.6) closed; QA-verified on live Umbrel/ARM hardware 2026-07-08 (vknb.6). br epic status --eligible-only confirms eligibility. Closed per 2026-07-12 beads audit recommendation #3.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: ARCH-AUDIT 2026-07-07: Two additional levers beyond the streamed loads. (1) Dashboard portfolio bypasses SvelteKit's load cache entirely — (app)/+page.svelte:78-86 fetches /api/portfolio in onMount, so every return to Home re-runs the full portfolio recompute (~6 sync SQLite queries + all-wallet scan), no ETag/staleness reuse. Consider moving it to a streamed depends('cairn:portfolio') promise in load (keeps it OUT of the cairn:chain block-invalidation path while gaining SSR-stream + client cache). (2) Fable flags that the biggest sustained event-loop blocker on ARM is likely NOT request-path reads but the BACKGROUND WORKERS (addressWatcher, notificationQueue) doing synchronous DB work in the web process under concurrent load — batching/yielding their DB access or moving them to a worker_thread is worth more than any request-path DB worker. Also: page loads chaining await parent() create a serial layout->page waterfall; once gating moves to hooks (see cairn-v84z), drop await parent() where it was only for auth ordering.

title: Stream wallets/[id] detail load (single-sig wallet page)
description: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/+page.server.ts currently awaits getWalletDetail (full gap-limit scan) -> peekReceiveAddress -> Promise.all([getWalletUtxos, getChain().getTip()]) -> detectWalletUnconfirmedInflows before the page can render at all. Apply the same deferred-promise streaming pattern already used in (app)/+page.server.ts (loadChainSnapshot) and wallets/+page.server.ts (scans): return the Electrum-dependent fields as an unawaited async function, add depends('cairn:wallet:{id}'), keep the existing try/catch degrade-gracefully behavior. Mirror (app)/+page.svelte's $state + $effect + onNewBlock -> invalidate() pattern on the client for a skeleton + live refresh on new blocks. See docs/PERF-ARCHITECTURE-PLAN.md Track 1 for full detail. Complexity M. File-disjoint from the other three page beads -- safe to build in parallel.
close_reason: Merged commit 52a6d1a (worktree agent) + fb8876f (test-mock fix). Streamed Electrum-dependent chainData; svelte-check clean; full suite green (1684 passed).

title: Stream wallets/multisig/[id] detail load (multisig wallet page)
description: src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/+page.server.ts currently awaits getMultisigDetail (full scan) -> peekMultisigReceiveAddress -> Promise.all([getMultisigUtxos, getChain().getTip()]) -> detectMultisigUnconfirmedInflows before rendering. Apply the same deferred-promise streaming pattern as the dashboard/wallet-list (see docs/PERF-ARCHITECTURE-PLAN.md Track 1): stream the Electrum-dependent fields, add depends('cairn:multisig:{id}'), keep existing degrade-gracefully catch blocks (base fields like role/collaborators/descriptor already computed synchronously and must stay that way). Wire the .svelte with the same skeleton + onNewBlock -> invalidate() pattern as (app)/+page.svelte. Complexity M. File-disjoint from the other three page beads -- safe to build in parallel.
close_reason: Merged commit d4e4586. Streamed loadMultisigScan promise; svelte-check clean (0 errors); full suite green.

title: Stream wallets/[id]/send load (single-sig send page)
description: src/routes/(app)/wallets/[id]/send/+page.server.ts sequentially awaits getWalletDetail (full scan) -> getWalletUtxos -> getChain().getFeeEstimates() -> getChain().getTip() before rendering. Stream these using the established deferred-promise pattern (docs/PERF-ARCHITECTURE-PLAN.md Track 1); the ?tx=N resume branch (getTransaction + summarizePsbt) is already cheap/local (DB read + pure PSBT parsing, confirmed in Track 4 audit) and should stay synchronous so it renders instantly once fees/utxos/tip stream in behind it. Add depends('cairn:send:{id}'). Complexity M. File-disjoint from the other three page beads -- safe to build in parallel.
close_reason: Merged commit ba6e310. Streamed live send-page data (fees/utxos/tip) via unawaited promise; resume branch stays synchronous per Track 4; svelte-check clean; full suite green.

title: Stream wallets/multisig/[id]/send load (multisig send page)
description: src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/[id]/send/+page.server.ts sequentially awaits getChain().getFeeEstimates() -> getMultisigUtxos -> getChain().getTip() before rendering. Stream these using the established deferred-promise pattern (docs/PERF-ARCHITECTURE-PLAN.md Track 1). The ?tx=N resume branch (getMultisigTransaction + summarizePsbt + multisigTransactionProgress + getRoster) is already cheap/local and should stay synchronous so it renders instantly once fees/utxos/tip stream in behind it. Add depends('cairn:multisig-send:{id}'). Complexity M. File-disjoint from the other three page beads -- safe to build in parallel.
close_reason: Merged commit ac73e2e. Streamed loadSendChain promise for multisig send page; resume branch stays synchronous; svelte-check clean; full suite green.

title: Add tip + fee-estimate TTL cache to ChainService
description: ChainService.getTip() and getFeeEstimates() (src/lib/server/chain/index.ts) currently have no cache -- every call is a fresh esplora HTTP round-trip. Once the four wallet/send pages stream (other sibling beads), concurrent tabs/pages still redundantly re-fetch the same tip/fees. Add a tipCache (10min TTL ceiling, actively invalidated the instant a new block arrives via a hook into wireChainEvents' electrum.on('header', ...) handler in chainEvents.ts) and a feeCache (flat 30s TTL, no event invalidation needed). Reset both in reconfigureChain() alongside the existing resetChainHealth()/resetPackageRelaySupport() calls. Wrap the two ChainService methods in place so no call site changes. See docs/PERF-ARCHITECTURE-PLAN.md Track 2. Complexity S. Touches chain/index.ts + chainEvents.ts only -- file-disjoint from the four page beads, safe to build in parallel with them.
close_reason: Merged commit aa2f695. New src/lib/server/chain/cache.ts: tipCache (10min ceiling, invalidated on header event), feeCache (30s flat TTL); both reset in reconfigureChain(). Drop-in wrap of getTip/getFeeEstimates, zero call-site changes. svelte-check clean; test suite green (2 new cache tests, 17 total in chain.test.ts).

title: Verify perf fix on Umbrel/ARM hardware
description: Manual QA pass once the streaming + cache beads land: confirm navigation to all four migrated pages (wallet detail, multisig detail, single-sig send, multisig send) feels instant on Umbrel/ARM hardware, no skeleton flash/jank on revisit, SSE-triggered background refresh works on new block, and the existing degrade-gracefully paths (Electrum killed mid-scan) still behave correctly under streaming. See docs/PERF-ARCHITECTURE-PLAN.md bead 6.
close_reason: done

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Partial verification done via local dev server (cairn-fresh launch config, scratch dev-fresh2.db, Electrum unreachable so degrade-gracefully paths were exercised, not the happy-path timing win):

- wallets/[id] detail (vknb.1): shell (name, xpub, nav) renders immediately; streamed scan resolves to "Can't reach the wallet scanner" banner + Retry, never a 500. GET /wallets/246 200 373ms (ECONNREFUSED fails fast so this isn't a real ARM-latency signal).
- wallets/multisig/[id] detail (vknb.2): same pattern -- shell (keys, collaborators, backup banner) renders immediately, scan degrades to the same graceful banner.
- wallets/[id]/send (vknb.3): shell renders with "Loading live fee estimates..." skeleton, then resolves to "Live fee estimates are unavailable -- set a custom sat/vB rate." + spendable-coins error alert. Never hangs, never 500s.
- wallets/multisig/[id]/send (vknb.4): same degrade pattern, plus correct 2-of-3 multisig fee note.
- No console errors on any of the four pages.

NOT yet verified: real Umbrel/ARM hardware timing (this bead's actual scope), SSE-triggered live refresh on a new block (no live Electrum/regtest stack was running), no-skeleton-flash-on-revisit. Full test suite (1684 tests) and svelte-check both pass on the merged code. Leaving this bead open pending an actual Umbrel/ARM pass.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text:
Umbrel/ARM hardware pass complete (live instance, umbrel.local:3211, v0.2.1, real Electrum backend electrum.blockstream.info, NOT local dev with ECONNREFUSED).

CONFIRMED FAST (this bead's actual scope):
- Dashboard, Explorer, Activity, Settings, Admin/Node all navigate instantly with proper skeleton -> content transitions. No jank on revisit.
- Notification panel opens instantly with real data.
- Send flow for a healthy wallet (wallets/1/send): TTFB 47ms, full resolution <1s once scan cache warm (first cold hit ~11s, which is the gap-limit scan cost the streaming shell already hides behind a skeleton -- not a freeze).
- No console errors on any tested page.
- Charts render correctly (balance history, block/difficulty chart).

NEW P1 FOUND (filed separately, not blocking this verification): cairn-vn48. On real hardware there's a QA wallet ("QA Trezor (regtest)") whose Electrum backend is unreachable at the network level (packets silently dropped, NOT ECONNREFUSED). That distinction matters: Alex's local dev pass exercised the ECONNREFUSED degrade path (fails fast, shows "Can't reach the wallet scanner" banner correctly). But a true network black-hole never fails at all -- ensureConnected() in electrum/client.ts has no connect-level timeout for direct (non-proxy) TCP/TLS, so the connection attempt hangs forever, which hangs the wallet's streamed chainData indefinitely and, intermittently, the whole /wallets list page (Promise.all never settles). This is likely the root cause behind the "Electrum-freeze" symptom tracked under cairn-2zxt. Root-caused down to the exact missing timeout; see cairn-vn48 for details and fix approach.

Also filed cairn-hoee (P3): HTTPS port 3212 dashboard is no longer permanently empty (prior bug fixed) but pays a one-time 10-30s cold-cache cost on first load after idle.

NOT verified (no safe way to trigger on this instance without mining/instructing infra changes): SSE-triggered live refresh on a new block.

Closing this verification bead -- the streaming/TTL-cache perf fix itself is confirmed working on real Umbrel/ARM hardware for the happy path. The freeze Alex is hitting in practice is the unreachable-backend connect-timeout gap now tracked at cairn-vn48.

title: W2A- admin recovery-setup phrase step stuck on 'Generating your phrase...' despite 200 API response
description: Repro: seed a fresh admin user (password_hash NULL, session minted directly, no passkey), accept /disclosure, then GET /recovery-setup. The page fires POST /api/auth/recovery/phrase via its Svelte component; network tab shows 200 OK with a real phrase in the JSON body, and a duplicate POST also fires (200). But the UI never advances past 'Your phrase... Generating your phrase...' — read_page/get_page_text confirm the DOM never renders the 12 words or the Continue button. This is a HARD gate (appGate.ts: admin cannot reach ANY other route, including /admin/settings, until hasRecoverySetup() is true) with no visible escape hatch in the UI. Workaround used for QA: call POST /api/auth/recovery/phrase and POST /api/auth/recovery/codes directly via curl with the session cookie, which flips hasRecoverySetup() to true and unblocks navigation immediately. Expected: the phrase step renders the returned words and lets the admin continue. Actual: stuck forever on the loading state; admin is fully blocked from the instance without a curl workaround. Verified 2026-07-13 on branch single-sig-full-wallet, instance data/vault-e2e-wave2a.db port 5399.

title: Self-disable and self-demote are only blocked client-side; unlike self-delete, the server actions don't check id === admin.id
description: src/routes/(app)/admin/users/+page.svelte disables the Disable/Demote buttons via disabled={user.id === me?.id} — a client-side-only guard. The server side (src/routes/(app)/admin/users/+page.server.ts actions.disable/actions.demote, and src/routes/api/admin/users/+server.ts POST) call setUserDisabled/setUserAdmin directly with no id===admin.id check; src/lib/server/admin.ts only blocks disabling/demoting when adminCount()<=1 (the LAST admin), not self specifically. Compare the DELETE handler in src/routes/api/admin/users/+server.ts, which explicitly checks 'if (body.id === admin.id) return json({ error: \'You cannot delete your own account.\' }...)' — the codebase clearly considers self-action guards worth enforcing server-side for destructive-to-self operations (see also the resetInstance comment: 'the admin layout already gates this route, but a factory reset deserves a second, explicit check'), but disable/demote didn't get the same treatment. With 2+ admins, an admin can self-disable via a raw POST to /api/admin/users (devtools, curl, or a stale/tampered DOM) and setUserDisabled immediately calls destroyUserSessions(id), killing their own session instantly with no confirmation step, recoverable only by asking another admin to re-enable them. Fix: mirror the DELETE handler's self-id check in the disable/demote actions and API route (at minimum for disable, which is destructive to the caller's own session).
close_reason: Fixed in 46dd16f

title: Heartwood solo mining — Tessera-solo sidecar (MVP)
description: Epic for building first-party solo-mining support in Heartwood: a Mining tab where a home user points a Bitaxe/small ASIC at their own node and, on a win, the full coinbase pays directly into their Heartwood wallet. Architecture is a 'Tessera-solo sidecar': a solo-only engine extracted from C:\dev\raffle (Tessera), run as a separate process/container, with Heartwood providing config, payout addresses, stats UI, and notifications. Solo-only, non-custodial, Stratum V1; the raffle layer is NOT ported. Full scoping doc: docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md.

title: mining: extract Tessera-solo engine (stratum/wire/TipPoller port + solo job builder + coordinator)
description: MVP unit 1 (L) of epic cairn-vn43. Port pool/src/stratum.ts, pool/src/wire.ts, TipPoller (pool/src/rpc.ts), and pool/src/types.ts from C:/dev/raffle nearly verbatim into a standalone solo-mining engine. Rewrite pool/src/job.ts as a solo coinbase builder (keep BIP34 height push, extranonce placement, coinb1/coinb2 split, witness-commitment output; single payout output, no RAFFLE/RAFFND OP_RETURNs). Rewrite pool/src/pool.ts as a thin solo coordinator (keep the serialized tip/solve promise queue; drop RaffleEngine/draw/finder/reorg-void plus flat-file persistence). Must be standalone runnable via a pool-runner entrypoint. See docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md Tessera reuse map section.

title: mining: admin settings section (enable, bind, port, vardiff, engine health)
description: MVP unit 10 (M) of epic cairn-vn43. Add an admin settings section following the per-section save/test pattern in src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte: enable toggle, bind address (loopback default, LAN opt-in requiring an explicit consent step per open question 2), stratum port, target wallet, vardiff bounds, and engine health/restart controls. Plain kv for port/bind/vardiff/enabled; instance_secrets for the engine API auth token.

title: mining: Umbrel packaging (second container, port 3333, bitcoin dependency)
description: MVP unit 11 (M) of epic cairn-vn43. Package the engine as a second container from the same Heartwood image (pool-runner command as entrypoint), publish port 3333 directly in docker-compose (app_proxy is HTTP-only, same mechanism as the existing 4488:3443 HTTPS mapping), add the bitcoin app as a dependency in umbrel-app.yml (currently dependencies: []), and digest-pin the image. See docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md architecture overview section.

title: mining: dev-mode child-process supervisor for the engine
description: MVP unit 12 (S) of epic cairn-vn43. In local dev (no Docker), run the Tessera-solo engine as a supervised Node child process instead of a second container, using the server.mjs shutdown() seam and the hooks.server.ts init()/start pattern already used for other background services. Must be Windows-friendly (dev box is Windows 11).

title: mining: docs (MANUAL.md sections, help panel, release notes)
description: MVP unit 13 (S) of epic cairn-vn43. Write MANUAL.md sections covering setup, the honest-odds framing, and troubleshooting for the mining feature, add an in-app help panel, and draft release notes once the feature ships. Companion doc: docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md.

title: mining: HARD GATE - legal review required before any multi-user/shared-reward mode
description: Hard gate from docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md Key decisions item 2. Heartwood mining scope is solo-only and non-custodial for the MVP (epic cairn-vn43); the raffle/multi-user layer from Tessera (C:/dev/raffle) is explicitly NOT ported because operator-run prize draws for third parties amount to gambling (consideration plus chance plus prize; licensing; some US states ban online raffles) and multi-user PPS/PPLNS creates reserve liability, custody of others funds, and money-transmission exposure. This bead exists to block any future multi-user or shared-reward mining mode from being coded until Alex has obtained legal review. Do not close by shipping code; close only when a review process is documented or the feature is formally deferred.

title: mining scope Q: is the raffle layer confirmed dead for Heartwood?
description: Open question 1 from docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md. Recommendation: yes, the raffle/prize-draw layer from Tessera (C:/dev/raffle) is dead for Heartwood, for legal reasons (see the HARD GATE bead) and because it is dead weight at home mining scale (the shared block essentially never comes). Tessera continues to exist and develop separately as its own product; this only concerns what gets ported into Heartwood. Needs Alex sign-off to close.

title: mining scope Q: LAN bind default for the stratum port
description: Open question 2 from docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md. Recommendation: loopback-only bind by default, with an explicit LAN opt-in consent step in admin settings (unit cairn-vn43.10) before the stratum port 3333 is exposed to the LAN where a Bitaxe or ASIC needs to reach it. Needs Alex sign-off to close.

title: mining scope Q: external payout addresses in MVP or phase 2?
description: Open question 3 from docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md. Recommendation: phase 2. MVP payout wiring (unit cairn-vn43.6) pays only the user's own Heartwood wallet via peekReceiveAddress/nextReceiveAddress; supporting arbitrary external payout addresses would require validateAddressEncodable on untrusted input and is deferred. Needs Alex sign-off to close.

title: mining scope Q: mainnet posture - ship mainnet-enabled day 1 or testnet4-first soak?
description: Open question 4 from docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md. Choice is between shipping the mining feature mainnet-enabled from day 1 with honest-odds UI, versus requiring a testnet4-first soak period before mainnet is allowed (mirroring the 33-hour real-Bitaxe testnet4 soak Tessera already ran). No recommendation given in the scoping doc; needs an explicit decision from Alex. Related to the forced-solve CI gate, unit cairn-vn43.2.

title: mining scope Q: Tessera licensing - relicense/vendor decision needed
description: Open question 5 from docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md. Verified facts (2026-07-12): C:/dev/raffle/package.json declares license GPL-3.0 (package name raffle-pool; there is no separate pool/package.json, the pool code lives under raffle/pool/ but is covered by the root manifest). Cairn/Heartwood's own C:/dev/cairn/LICENSE is MIT. Alex owns the Tessera code so relicensing or vendoring it is his call. Needed action: Alex must decide whether to relicense the extracted solo-engine code before vendoring it into the MIT-licensed Cairn/Heartwood repo (unit cairn-vn43.1), or keep it in a separate GPL-3.0 sidecar package/container so the two license terms are not mixed in one source tree/image.

title: mining: forced-solve regtest harness plus CI gate (P1 schedule long pole)
description: MVP unit 2 (M) of epic cairn-vn43. Build a maintained CI harness that exercises getblocktemplate to job to share to submitblock on regtest and verifies the block is actually confirmed on-chain. Adapt Tessera existing regtest e2e (which already exercises accepted submitblocks). This is the one code path that must be flawless for the rare real win, and is a required gate before mainnet enablement of the mining feature. Schedule long pole for the whole epic, should start first. See docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md Key decisions item 4 and Risks item 1.

title: mining scope Q: engine fallback trigger - how long to timebox Tessera-solo extraction?
description: Open question 6 from docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md. If the Tessera-solo engine extraction (unit cairn-vn43.1) stalls badly, the documented fallback is bundling Public Pool (GPL-3.0, NestJS, proven mainnet blocks including 920440) as an unmodified sidecar container, relying on mere aggregation for the GPL boundary. No specific timebox duration has been set; needs a concrete decision (e.g. N days/weeks of stalled extraction effort) from Alex or the orchestrating lead before the build wave starts.

title: mining scope Q: naming/branding - Mining, Solo Mining, or lottery-flavored framing?
description: Open question 7 from docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md. Candidate names for the feature/nav tab: plain Mining, Solo Mining, or something with lottery-flavored framing (consistent with the honest lottery-ticket odds framing used elsewhere in the UI per Key decisions item 2). Affects nav copy (unit cairn-vn43.5) and the dashboard/onboarding copy (unit cairn-vn43.7). Needs a decision before UI copy is finalized.

title: mining: engine stats/control HTTP API
description: MVP unit 3 (M) of epic cairn-vn43. Add a minimal HTTP API to the Tessera-solo engine (replacing pool/src/api.ts, not reusing it as-is): status, workers, shares/best-share, blocks endpoints; config push for payout-address/port/vardiff; health check. Auth via bearer token stored through instance_secrets/setSecretSetting. This is what the Heartwood stats bridge (unit 4) polls. See docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md architecture diagram and Integration points section.

title: mining: Heartwood stats bridge plus aggregate persistence
description: MVP unit 4 (M) of epic cairn-vn43. Server-side client in Heartwood that polls the engine stats/control API (unit 3) with a light cache, and persists only low-rate aggregates (worker rollups, best_share, blocks_found, hashrate history) via batched writes to new tables (mining_workers, mining_stats rollups, mining_blocks) added CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS style in src/lib/server/db.ts. Per-share state must stay in the engine process (in-memory plus periodic flush); do not let per-share work leak into the wallet sync node:sqlite (cairn-xlrm hazard). See docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md Integration points section.

title: mining: feature flag plus nav entry plus /mining route scaffold
description: MVP unit 5 (S) of epic cairn-vn43. Add a mining feature flag in src/lib/server/featureFlags/registry.ts (defaultEnabled true is compiler-enforced), enforce with requireFeature() in src/lib/server/api.ts, add a one-line nav tab in src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte around line 29 following the absent-when-flag-off convention, and scaffold src/routes/(app)/mining/ modeled on explorer/. See docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md Integration points section.

title: mining: payout wiring (peek-hold plus advance-on-block)
description: MVP unit 6 (M) of epic cairn-vn43. Wire a dedicated labeled mining address using peekReceiveAddress(walletId) (does not advance the cursor) held stable across template rebuilds in src/lib/server/wallets.ts, with nextReceiveAddress() advancing the cursor only when a block is actually found (withLock). Push the resulting address to the engine as job payout config. MVP pays own-Heartwood-wallet only; external addresses are phase 2 (see open question 3). See docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md Key decisions item 6.

title: mining: dashboard UI (setup card, worker status, hashrate chart, odds calculator)
description: MVP unit 7 (L) of epic cairn-vn43. Build the /mining dashboard: connection setup card (host/port 3333/username), worker status list, hashrate chart via CairnChart.svelte, best-share high score, honest odds calculator (measured hashrate to expected time to block, framed like at roughly 1 TH/s expect a block roughly every 15000 to 35000 years), and empty states for flag-off, Core missing (CoreRpcRequiredNotice.svelte), and no workers. Follows the MVP user journey in docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md.

title: mining: notifications (block_found, worker_offline, best-share milestone)
description: MVP unit 8 (S) of epic cairn-vn43. Add block_found, mining_worker_offline, and best-share-milestone event types to NOTIFICATION_EVENT_TYPES in src/lib/server/notifyTypes.ts, wire through notify() in src/lib/server/notifications.ts, add a celebration UI for block_found, and log via recordActivity() to the activity feed. See docs/MINING-POOL-SCOPE.md Integration points section.

title: mining: MiningRewards.svelte wiring for the mining address
description: MVP unit 9 (S) of epic cairn-vn43. Wire the existing MiningRewards.svelte (already renders coinbase UTXOs with the 100-conf maturity countdown, using src/lib/shared/coinbase.ts maturity logic) to the dedicated mining address from unit 6, so the block_found to maturing to spendable journey shows correctly in the wallet UI. Mostly-existing component, small wiring task.

title: Electrum ensureConnected() has no connect-level timeout — unreachable backend hangs page forever
description:
## What's broken

Any wallet whose Electrum backend is unreachable (dead host, dropped packets, no RST) causes the wallet's chain-data fetch to hang **indefinitely** — not slow, never resolves. Confirmed via curl with a real authenticated session against the v0.2.1 Umbrel instance (umbrel.local:3211):

- GET /wallets/2 (wallet pointing to a dead "QA Trezor (regtest)" backend): hung past 20s, streamed shell painted but `chainData:__sveltekit_ywkcgt.defer(1)` never resolved.
- GET /wallets (the list page): intermittently hangs (15s+, incomplete response) whenever this wallet's 60s scan cache (`scanCache` in walletScan.ts) has expired and `listWallets()` re-scans it — `Promise.all([...])` in `(app)/wallets/+page.server.ts` never settles because one of the awaited scans never rejects, just hangs.
- In the browser this manifests as: clicking into Wallets/Explorer/any nav after visiting the bad wallet leaves the tab permanently stuck on the previous page (client-side nav awaits the same hung promise before swapping the view), and DevTools Runtime.evaluate itself times out — the tab looks fully frozen.

Root cause: `src/lib/server/electrum/client.ts` `ensureConnected()` has no timeout on the initial TCP/TLS connect for direct (non-proxy) connections:
- `net.connect({ host, port }, onReady)` (line ~259) — no connect timeout.
- `tls.connect({...}, onReady)` (lines ~201-209, used at line ~256) — no connect timeout.
- Only the SOCKS5 proxy path passes `timeout: this.timeoutMs` to `SocksClient.createConnection` (line ~238).
- `this.timeoutMs` (via `rawRequest`'s `setTimeout`, line ~407-408) only covers a request sent on an *already-established* socket — it never fires while still dialing.
- `s.setTimeout(0)` (line 214) explicitly disables the socket idle timeout too (intentional, for keepalive per cairn-u7bw), so nothing bounds a stalled connect.

So: dead host that silently drops SYN/never completes TCP handshake → `ensureConnected()`'s promise never settles → every caller up the chain (`scanWallet` → `getWalletDetail`/`listWallets` → the page's streamed `chainData`/`scans` promise) hangs forever, even though those callers have "never rejects" try/catch wrappers — those can't help because the underlying promise doesn't reject, it just never resolves.

## Why this matters now

This is NOT fixed by the cairn-vknb streaming perf work that just shipped in v0.2.1. Streaming makes the shell paint instantly, but once the deferred chainData promise hangs, the affected wallet's card/page/list never fills in and (via client-side nav blocking on the same await) can make the whole tab look frozen. This is very likely the same "Electrum-freeze" symptom tracked at a high level under cairn-2zxt (post-Heartwood-launch fixes).

## Fix

Add a connect-level timeout to `ensureConnected()` for the direct `net.connect`/`tls.connect` paths (e.g. wrap in `Promise.race` with a timer, or use `net.connect(...).setTimeout(timeoutMs, ...)` on the connecting socket before it's ready), matching the SOCKS5 path's behavior — reject with a clear "connect timed out" error so the existing try/catch degrade-to-empty-state logic in the load functions actually gets a chance to run.

## Repro

1. Have a wallet configured against an unreachable Electrum host (regtest node that's down, wrong IP, firewalled with silent drop).
2. Wait for its scanWallet 60s cache to be cold (or first load).
3. GET /wallets/<id> or /wallets (list) while authenticated — request hangs past any reasonable timeout, confirmed to 20s+ with curl, never completes.

Verified against live umbrel.local:3211 instance during v0.2.1 perf-fix QA pass (2026-07-07 / 2026-07-08 UTC).
close_reason: Landed in ed95397. Added a standalone connect-timer (this.timeoutMs) to ensureConnected(), armed on the two DIRECT branches (wrapTls + net.connect) only; on expiry it destroys the dialing socket and rejects via the existing fail() so scanWallet/getWalletDetail/listWallets/streamed loads stop hanging on a black-holed SYN. Disarmed in onReady (success) and fail() (error/close/timeout); settled flag prevents double-reject; timer unref'd. Idle timeout (s.setTimeout(0), cairn-u7bw) untouched. SOCKS5 path untouched (already bounded by SocksClient timeout). Verified: fail() rejects, attach() sets this.connectingSocket synchronously. Tests: electrum 26 pass (2 new, deterministic via mocked socket + fake timers, load-bearing); svelte-check 0/0.

title: Fiat equivalents across wallet, send, and tx surfaces (price-feed-aware)
description: Verified: 'usd' appears only in src/routes/(app)/+page.svelte (dashboard hero); zero fiat in src/routes/(app)/wallets/** (balances, send entry, confirm, tx lists). Show USD alongside BTC on wallet balances, send amount entry (bidirectional entry ideally), confirm screen, and tx history rows. Must degrade gracefully when the price feed is unconfigured/off — depends on open cairn-zoz8.18, which makes the standalone price feed opt-in and OFF by default.

title: combineMultisigPsbts adopts finalScriptWitness from untrusted PSBT
description: multisigPsbt.ts:652-661 — unlike partialSig path (key membership + SIGHASH_ALL enforced), finalization fields are copied verbatim once !curFinal. A cosigner can attach garbage finalScriptWitness with zero real signatures. assertSameTransaction passes (inputs/outputs unchanged). Draft is durably marked ready-to-broadcast but network rejects it. Honest signers can't recover — only owner deleting/rebuilding. Availability DoS on shared funds. Fix: validate finalization fields or re-derive from partialSig.
close_reason: Fixed on track/signing @ f6a6837, merged @ 5f5aa72, final SHA 66f0c36.

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Fixed on track/signing @ f6a6837 (fix(bitcoin): validate pre-existing finalization at the multisig finalize entry, commit f6a6837 within that branch). combineMultisigPsbts and finalizeMultisigPsbt no longer adopt finalScriptWitness/finalScriptSig verbatim from an untrusted PSBT: both combine entry points and the finalizeMultisigPsbt pre-loop now validate structural binding (script + DER SIGHASH_ALL + quorum count) before treating an input as already-finalized. New tests in multisigPsbt.test.ts (+130 lines). Merged into single-sig-full-wallet at 5f5aa72. Full suite green post-merge (2411 passed / 1 skipped). Final pushed SHA: 66f0c36. Residual: validation is structural, not full cryptographic signature verification against descriptor pubkeys -- filed as a separate P3 follow-up task, tagged hardening-followup (same follow-up bead as cairn-u2r5's residual, covers both).

comments.author: alexl
comments.text: Reprioritized P2->P1 per 2026-07-12 beads audit (docs/BEADS-AUDIT.md sec 2/7): untrusted finalScriptWitness adopted in multisig combine, contradicts MANUAL.md sec 18.9's tampered-PSBT-rejected claim, underweighted at P2.

title: Wallet/account deletion cascades away broadcast records, bypassing the up0q delete guard
description: Found by independent review of 94ddd83 (cairn-up0q). cairn-up0q hardened per-transaction delete (deleteTransaction/deleteMultisigTransaction) against erasing a mid/post-broadcast record. But deleteWallet (wallets.ts:283-291) does an unconditional DELETE FROM wallets WHERE id=? AND user_id=?, and transactions.wallet_id has ON DELETE CASCADE (db.ts:83), so deleting a whole wallet removes every transaction row regardless of status — including rows with a live broadcast_started_at claim or a completed/superseded broadcast record. accountDeletion.ts has no guard either. Same likely applies to deleteMultisig + multisig_transactions cascade (unverified). NUANCE for triage: unlike deleting one pending tx, deleting a WHOLE wallet is a deliberate total-removal intent, so cascading its tx history is arguably correct — the residual concern is only the narrow race where a wallet delete lands concurrently with an in-flight broadcast, erasing a just-broadcast record. Decide whether to (a) block wallet/account deletion while any child tx has broadcast_started_at IS NOT NULL, or (b) accept it as intended destructive behavior. SECONDARY nit from the same review: db.ts:337 multisig_transactions.status column comment still reads 'draft | awaiting_signature | completed' and omits 'superseded'.

title: Test hygiene: electrum/client.test.ts uses a real-timer timeout assertion (CI-flaky)
description: src/lib/server/electrum/client.test.ts:151-162's timeout test uses real Date.now() and a real 200ms timer with a 150ms-1500ms assertion window -- a classic CI-flake shape where a slow runner can tip either bound. Related real-timer waits at lines 168, 239. Fix: use vi.useFakeTimers(). Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Replaced the CI-flaky wall-clock assertion window (elapsed >=150ms && <1500ms) in the request-timeout test with a deterministic fake-timer drive: warm the connection under real timers so handshake IO completes, then vi.useFakeTimers({toFake:['setTimeout','clearTimeout']}) and advanceTimersByTimeAsync(200) to fire the timeout exactly. Restricted faking to setTimeout only (leaving setImmediate/IO real) so the loopback socket keeps working and vitest's own tick loop isn't disturbed; attach the .rejects assertion before advancing so the timer-driven rejection is never momentarily unhandled. Added vi.useRealTimers() safety-net in afterEach. Lines 168/239 real-timer waits are event-sequencing delays (not tight assertion windows) and were left as-is — forcing fake timers onto their real-socket IO reintroduces tick collisions. 12 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: Multisig vaults (local M-of-N)
description: Local multisig where one user controls multiple keys — NOT collaborative custody. Terminology: 'Vault' not 'multisig wallet', plain language, educational tooltips (what a vault is, why multiple keys, what if one is lost). Scope: guided creation wizard, vault detail page, M-of-N PSBT construction, per-key signing sub-stepper reusing the existing device signers, wsh(sortedmulti(...)) descriptor export/import portable to Sparrow/Electrum, emulator-verified end to end.

title: Desktop nav rail: add persistent text labels (icon-only rail illegible to first-timers)
description: Visual audit (2026-07-12) confirmed by screenshot + zoom: the desktop left rail in src/lib/components/heartwood/HWRail.svelte is a ~55px strip of stacked icons (logo-ring, grid, target, wallet, activity-squiggle, gear, a second ring-mark, a second squiggle, avatar) with no visible text labels. A non-technical first-timer cannot tell that the 'target' icon is Explorer or what the ring-marks do, and two pairs of icons look nearly identical at that size. Sharp inconsistency with the mobile shell, which has an excellent labeled tab row (Home / Explorer / Wallets / Activity). Best-in-class (Muun/Cash App/Strike) all use labeled tabs. Fix: add persistent text labels beside the rail icons (always-on, not expand-on-hover -- hover is insufficient for first-timers) on desktop >= the 940px content width, and differentiate the two look-alike icon pairs. Searched for overlap first: br search for 'rail'/'icon-only'/'HWRail'/'text label' returned no matches -- filing new.
close_reason: Fixed in eba0b38 (src/lib/components/heartwood/HWRail.svelte): rail widened 68px->92px, each nav icon-btn changed to flex-direction:column with a new .icon-label span (10px, --font-ui, always-on, no hover-to-reveal) rendering item.label under the icon, matching the plain-language labels already used in MobileTabRow.svelte. npm run check: 7 pre-existing errors, all in unrelated test files (userDeletion.test.ts, recoverySetupFlow.test.ts) untouched by this change and present before it -- HWRail.svelte itself is clean. Visual verification in the built-in browser was blocked: every QA dev-server login path hit either a silent client-side submit failure on /login (suspected same root cause as known P1 cairn-hmi4, being fixed concurrently by another agent) or the forced /setup-admin first-run wall on shared QA databases, and the safety layer correctly declined an attempt to complete that wall on another agent's shared instance. Re-verify visually next QA pass once cairn-hmi4 lands or a byte-fresh QA DB is available.

title: Unexplained jargon on auth + nav surfaces: 'passkey', 'node', 'descriptor', TIMECHAIN/rings metaphor unlabeled
description: Sweep found unexplained technical jargon on user-facing auth and nav surfaces: 'passkey' appears on login/signup with no explanation of what it is or why it's offered; 'node' and 'descriptor' appear without plain-language definitions; the TIMECHAIN/rings visual metaphor used in nav/explorer is unlabeled so its meaning isn't discoverable. Searched existing beads for overlap: cairn-oqg (No educational tooltips anywhere in the explorer, P1, block/tx/address jargon) covers explorer-specific jargon only -- this item is broader (auth + nav surfaces, passkey/node/descriptor/rings metaphor) and is a copy/plain-language sweep, not explorer tooltips specifically. No existing bead covers the auth-surface or nav-metaphor jargon. Suggested fix: plain-language alternatives or inline tooltips/help text for each term, consistent with Cairn's no-exposed-Bitcoin-internals UX philosophy.

USB mode: src/lib/hw/jade.ts via Web Serial (navigator.serial — Chromium-only, availability-gated like WebHID), JadeSigner component, picker entry 'Jade (USB)', wizard key-import read (note Bastion gotcha: Jade can't display a path during xpub export), multisig registration in the device store, tests.

Jade air-gapped QR (BC-UR) is a documented separate follow-on — filed as its own bead (see jade-qr follow-on).

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Scout verdicts: (1) USB: jadets@1.1.20 exact-pin (de-facto standard, Caravan ships it; Blockstream has no official JS SDK). Vite: alias-stub tiny-secp256k1/bip32/crypto, never call getMasterFingerPrint (use scure xfpFromXpub client-side), Buffer global needed, optimizeDeps.include jadets+cbor2. Web Serial gate; pinserver blind-oracle relay for PIN-locked devices (jadepin.blockstream.com; already-unlocked = zero network — say so in copy). Registration: 16-slot device store, deterministic name = sanitized prefix + 6-hex sha256 of canonical descriptor (re-register same name silently REPLACES), firmware >=1.0.27 for getMultiSigName dedupe. All three script types supported. get_xpub is silent by protocol — no path-confirm exists. (2) QR MODE: Jade speaks BC-UR crypto-psbt ONLY, NOT BBQr — existing QrSigner does not cover it. Per Alex both modes are wanted: add urPsbt.ts with @ngraveio/bc-ur@1.1.13 (+@keystonehq/bc-ur-registry for crypto-psbt CBOR; avoid the 2.0 beta) and a QrSigner format toggle. (3) COPY BUG today: DevicePicker.svelte:26 + wallets send +page.svelte:630 list Jade under the BBQr QR blurb — move Jade to its own tiles, drop from that blurb. Full brief in scratchpad/bitbox-jade-brief.md.

title: Backup filename convention not applied to ColdCard/descriptor exports — inconsistent with the new dated cairn-<name>-backup-<date> pattern
description: commit 588aa0a introduced a standard cairn-<name>-backup-<date>.json filename for the Caravan export (src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/[id]/caravan/+server.ts) and the new single-sig config export (src/routes/api/wallets/[id]/config/+server.ts), but left src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/[id]/coldcard/+server.ts:37 and src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/[id]/descriptor/+server.ts:38 on the old cairn-multisig-<name>-coldcard.txt / -descriptor.txt pattern — no date segment, no '-backup-' token. A user backing up one multisig wallet via the wallet detail page's three adjacent download buttons (Wallet config JSON, ColdCard file, Descriptor) ends up with three inconsistently-shaped filenames, two of which carry no date and can't be freshness-compared after a key rotation or re-download — undermining the commit's stated goal of a standard, comparable naming convention. Fix: apply the same dated cairn-<name>-backup-<date>-<kind>.<ext> pattern to the coldcard and descriptor endpoints.
close_reason: ColdCard and multisig-descriptor exports now use the standard dated cairn-<name>-backup-<date>-<kind>.<ext> filename (coldcard.txt / descriptor.txt), matching the caravan/config/backup-pdf exports. Also aligned the single-sig descriptor export to -backup-<date>-descriptor.txt. All export buttons on a wallet now emit consistent, freshness-comparable filenames.

title: Missing regression test: broadcast routes swallow malformed JSON body instead of 400 (cairn-1yw7)

Add a test in src/lib/server/api.test.ts (or a route-level test) asserting a malformed JSON body to either broadcast route returns 400 'Invalid JSON body', not a silent no-op.

title: createMultisig is not transactional — partial failure leaves corrupt state
description: wallets/multisig.ts:328-366 — multisigs row INSERT and N separate multisig_keys INSERTs are independent auto-committed statements. No db.transaction() wrapper. A failure partway through key-insert loop leaves a multisigs row whose threshold/key count doesnt match actual multisig_keys rows. Every downstream consumer assumes this cant happen. Grep shows zero uses of transaction wrapper in entire src/lib/server. Fix: wrap multi-row writes in db.transaction().

title: Copy-to-clipboard buttons fail on HTTP deployments (Umbrel)
description: navigator.clipboard.writeText requires a secure context (HTTPS or localhost). Umbrel serves Cairn over plain HTTP at umbrel.local, so navigator.clipboard is undefined and every copy button silently fails. Affected call sites: src/lib/components/CopyText.svelte, src/routes/(app)/admin/invites/+page.svelte, src/routes/(app)/recovery-setup/+page.svelte, src/routes/(app)/explorer/tx/[txid]/+page.svelte. Fix: shared copyToClipboard() util with document.execCommand('copy') textarea fallback for insecure contexts; use it at all call sites.
close_reason: copyToClipboard() fallback shipped and verified in both secure and insecure contexts

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Verified end-to-end in Chrome against the dev server: (1) simulated Umbrel HTTP by defineProperty navigator.clipboard=undefined + isSecureContext=false, real trusted click on 'Copy all 12 words' in /recovery-setup -> execCommand('copy') returned true and pasting into a scratch textarea reproduced the exact 12-word phrase; (2) regression check without overrides (secure localhost) -> navigator.clipboard path still copies the exact phrase. Fix: new src/lib/clipboard.ts copyToClipboard() with textarea+execCommand fallback (preserves prior selection, restores it after); swapped in at all 4 call sites: CopyText.svelte, admin/invites, recovery-setup, explorer/tx. svelte-check: 0 errors.

title: auth.ts, webauthn.ts, and rateLimit.ts have zero logging -- security events invisible to admin logs
description: src/lib/server/auth.ts and src/lib/server/webauthn.ts have no logger import at all, so failed login attempts and WebAuthn ceremony failures never reach the structured log pipeline. src/lib/server/rateLimit.ts (brute-force throttling for login/recovery/invites) also has zero logging -- trips are tracked only in an in-memory map and surfaced solely via in-app notify() to the affected user, never to the operator-facing log. An operator watching /admin/logs during an active credential-stuffing or recovery-brute-force attempt would see nothing beyond generic 4xx request noise. Fix: add log.warn calls on failed auth attempts, WebAuthn ceremony errors, and rate-limit trips (with hashed/truncated key plus count). Found in the 2026-07-05 tech-debt audit (docs/TECH-DEBT-AUDIT-2026-07-05.md).
close_reason: Fixed in d56e77f

title: Sync engine Phase 1: wire Electrum status-hash into dirty-tracking, skip clean rescans
description:
gapLimitScanner.ts always full-rescans up to 400 addrs/chain on every 20s-throttled refresh (doWalletScan/doMultisigScan in walletSync.ts) -- never checks whether anything changed. addressWatcher.ts:1021 onScripthash literally discards the Electrum status-hash argument it receives -- the subscription infra and the balance/data path are completely disjoint despite the wallet already holding live scripthash subscriptions for every watched address.

Bonus bug found during audit: addressWatcher.ts:45 WATCH_WINDOW=30 is a FIXED window from index 0, not gap-trimmed -- wallets with >30 used addresses on one chain have addresses beyond index 30 that are never subscribed at all. Fix this in the same bead (live notification-correctness bug, not just perf).

Fix (Phase 1, ~150-250 LOC -- rejected the full normalized wallet_addresses/wallet_txs/wallet_utxos rewrite as Phase 1; that's ~2000-2500 LOC with a hard tx-eviction/refcounting risk under reorg/RBF and a scan-storm-on-migration-backfill problem; gate that as Phase 2, its own bead, opened only if Phase 1 proves insufficient): persist last-seen status per scripthash, mark wallet dirty on real status change, skip scanWallet/getMultisigDetail entirely when clean, use subscribeScripthash's return value as a free reconciliation checkpoint on every (re)connect, not just a periodic sweep.

Acceptance criteria MUST include: reorg, RBF replacement, and reconnect-after-outage all correctly mark dirty (status-hash changes on confirmations too, not just new txs). Send/spend path (getWalletUtxos/getMultisigUtxos) stays on live Electrum unchanged -- already narrow, already guarded (walletSync.ts:24), do not touch.

Medium risk -- a false-clean bug silently shows stale balances. Do NOT run concurrently with the derivation-memoization bead (both touch addressWatcher.ts) -- sequence after it, single-session only (concurrent-session branch hazard is a repeat offender in this repo).

title: Vault creation wizard a11y/mobile polish sweep
description:
Batch of independent a11y/mobile findings from auditing the vault creation wizard and send flow -- fine to fix together:
1. Cosigner slot-remove buttons (vaults/new/+page.svelte ~1887-1898, .slot-remove) are ~26x26px, under the ~44x44px touch-target guidance, with no mobile breakpoint bump.
2. Custom M-of-N threshold input fields (vaults/new/+page.svelte ~679-689, .custom-inputs) have no mobile breakpoint to stack vertically -- may overflow at 375px.
3. The signing-method card grid (Trezor/Ledger/ColdCard/QR/Paste, vaults/new/+page.svelte ~1923-1947) uses repeat(auto-fit, minmax(150px, 1fr)) with no explicit mobile rule -- may produce cramped single-wide cards with hard-to-read labels at 375px.
4. QR scanning video element (vaults/new/+page.svelte ~1011) has a svelte-ignore a11y_media_has_caption pragma suppressing the linter without actually addressing it -- add a live-region announcement ("Camera scanning in progress...") so screen reader users know a camera feed is active and what to do.
5. Vault signer components' file upload/download controls (VaultFileSigner.svelte ~156-201) rely on surrounding paragraph text for context rather than descriptive button text or aria-describedby -- icon+short-label buttons like 'Download registration file' don't carry enough standalone context for screen reader users navigating by control.
6. Device-unavailable explanations (VaultTrezorSigner.svelte ~141-150) aren't semantically grouped with their disabled control (no aria-describedby linking the reason text to the button), so screen reader users navigating by control may not discover why a device option is greyed out.
close_reason: All six items: wizard touch targets/stacked inputs/single-column grids/QR live region (146ebfb), signer download/input accessible names + described-by fallbacks (this commit).

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Items 1-4 committed (touch targets, stacked custom inputs, single-column grids, QR live region — live-verified at 375px). Items 5-6 target the signer components under wallets/multisig/[id]/send/_components, owned by the running stepper agent — relayed there.

comments.text: Items 5-6 done in the multisig send flow signer components: (5) MultisigFileSigner download/upload/paste controls got standalone accessible names (registration + .psbt download aria-labels with wallet/key context, stable aria-label on the file input, aria-label on the paste textarea); same treatment for the QR register-callout download on the send page. (6) Trezor/Ledger device-unavailable reasons now carry ids and the 'Use the file method' fallback buttons reference them via aria-describedby.

title: Vault quorum signing-time warning
description: Vault send flow: each of M signers processes the full mass — total time = M x per-device estimate. Casa-summary Impact line gains 'approximately 15-20 minutes across all N signing devices' when mass is high, per-key device-specific estimates on the stepper chips. Blocked on the Trezor/Ledger stepper wiring task finishing (owns the vault send page).

Vault coin control doesn't exist so the live line attached to the Review coins list — documented adaptation.

comments.author: fable
comments.text: Scope extension from Alex: (1) vault SEND — Casa Impact line shows totalSeconds (per-signer x M) with amber (>10min) / red (>30min or timeout risk) tones from signingMass.warnLevel; red includes the split suggestion; per-key device-specific estimates on stepper chips. (2) vault COIN CONTROL — live estimate line updating as UTXOs are (de)selected: 'Selected 3 UTXOs - Estimated signing mass: HIGH - ~8 min per signer x 4 signers = ~32 min', using the pure estimateSigningSeconds export from signingMass.ts (client-callable math on utxo-mass data) so no server round trip per click; suggest removing high-mass UTXOs when extreme. Depends on cairn-194's v2 contract + the stepper wiring agent releasing the vault send page.

title: wallet_config_import flag OFF doesn't hide 'Import it' entry point in multisig wizard
description:
Found during cairn-1ytc flag-matrix QA pass (wallet-flags subset, port 5314, HEARTWOOD_DB=data/qa-matrix.db). Third instance of the same pattern as cairn-de7e (address_book) and cairn-puyb (wallet_config_export) -- API correctly gated server-side, client UI doesn't follow the flag.

REPRO: Admin -> Feature flags -> toggle "Import wallet config" OFF (wallet_config_import flag). As a plain user, visit /wallets/multisig/new. The "Already have this wallet in another app? Import it" button is still present and clickable (confirmed live via DOM query right after toggling the flag off -- button text/existence unchanged). Server-side, requireFeature(event, 'wallet_config_import') IS correctly wired at src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.server.ts:297 and src/routes/api/wallets/multisig/import/+server.ts:75, so an actual import submission should fail -- but the entry point isn't hidden or disabled first, so a user can walk through the whole paste/scan-config flow before hitting a late failure.

ROOT CAUSE (code-read confirmed): src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte has no `data.flags?.wallet_config_import` (or similar) check anywhere -- the only flags reference in the file is `cameraDisabled={page.data.flags?.qr_scan === false}` at line 1999 for the camera-scan sub-feature. The "Import it" toggle buttons (lines 1216 and 1526) and the "Have this wallet in Sparrow, Caravan, or another app? Import its wallet config" panel (line 1294) are unconditionally rendered.

IMPACT: minor/P3 -- late failure instead of a hidden entry point (not a data leak), but a confusing dead-end UX and the same regression class the parent bead cairn-1ytc calls out.

FIX (not done here -- flagged only, owned by another session per current branch ownership): gate the "Import it" buttons/panel in src/routes/(app)/wallets/multisig/new/+page.svelte behind `data.flags?.wallet_config_import !== false`, mirroring the qr_scan camera gate already in the same file.

Filed by: OPS QA session (cairn-1ytc wallet-flags subset), 2026-07-12.

title: Umbrel secure-context fix: serve self-signed HTTPS so WebHID/WebUSB/camera work
description: umbrel.local over plain HTTP is not a secure context, so WebHID (Ledger/BitBox02), Web Serial (Jade USB), WebUSB, camera QR-scan-back, and WebAuthn are all unavailable — blocking hardware signing on the primary deployment target. Bridge-based workarounds are fragile (Caravan/BitBoxBridge 'worked once then stopped' — session-scoped origin approval + Chrome LNA gating). Chosen fix: Cairn terminates TLS itself on a second port with a self-signed cert generated at first boot and persisted in the data dir; Umbrel compose maps the extra port; the UI detects !isSecureContext and offers the https:// address with plain-language one-time-warning guidance. Evaluated and rejected: WebHID (same secure-context rule as WebUSB), app_proxy TLS (unsupported), Chrome flag (docs-only), custom bridge daemon (fragile, LNA-gated). Pending verification: WebHID/WebUSB must actually function after a bypassed cert interstitial.
close_reason: Built and verified. Cairn serves a self-signed HTTPS listener (server.mjs + scripts/tls-cert.mjs, CAIRN_HTTPS_PORT default 3443 in the image, cert persisted 10y in <data>/tls) and the UI offers the secure address from all USB-signing surfaces via SecureContextHelp. Live-browser verification confirms WebHID/WebUSB/Web Serial + clipboard + camera all function after the one-time (weekly) cert bypass; WebAuthn/service workers do not — copy directs users to password login there. Committed cd887bb + copy follow-up; Umbrel compose (3212→3443) staged in caravan-store-cairn for the 0.1.6 release. Remaining: real-device pass on the Umbrel box after 0.1.6 ships.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Implemented. (1) scripts/tls-cert.mjs — standalone self-signed cert module (selfsigned pkg, 10y validity, SANs umbrel.local/localhost/*.local/127.0.0.1, persisted key.pem+cert.pem, regenerates when <30d left or unparsable; unit-tested in src/tests/tlsCert.test.ts). (2) server.mjs — new prod entry: adapter-node handler on HTTP (PORT) + HTTPS listener when CAIRN_HTTPS_PORT is set; cert dir defaults to <CAIRN_DB dir>/tls; a cert failure logs and degrades to HTTP-only, never takes the app down. adapter-node's get_origin falls back to 'https' when x-forwarded-proto is absent, so cookies/CSRF are correct on the direct TLS port. (3) Dockerfile: CMD node server.mjs, CAIRN_HTTPS_PORT=3443 default, EXPOSE 3443. (4) (app) layout exposes httpsPort (CAIRN_HTTPS_EXTERNAL_PORT wins) to the client. (5) New SecureContextHelp.svelte — renders ONLY on insecure pages when the listener is advertised; links to https://<hostname>:<port><same path> with plain-language one-time-cert-warning guidance; wired into LedgerSigner/JadeUsbSigner/BitboxSigner, the send-page method grid, and both wizards' device panes (Ledger/Jade/BitBox02 connect boxes + camera-unavailable QR states; Trezor excluded — its popup works over HTTP). Umbrel compose in caravan-store-cairn updated (host port 3212 → container 3443 + CAIRN_HTTPS_EXTERNAL_PORT), takes effect with the 0.1.6 image. Verified: prod build boots both listeners, TLS handshake serves CN=umbrel.local, /login + /api/health 200 over https; 1610 tests pass; svelte-check clean. Pending: background research on whether Chrome fully enables WebHID after a bypassed cert interstitial (Home Assistant ecosystem precedent says yes); real-device test on the Umbrel box.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Verification verdict (live experiment: Chrome 149/Win11, real self-signed cert on a LAN-IP origin, interstitial bypassed via certificateErrorPageController.proceed(), APIs probed under trusted user activation; 2 clean runs + Chromium source evidence): (1) isSecureContext=true after bypass — scheme-based per W3C spec. (2) WebHID/WebUSB/Web Serial WORK — choosers open normally, and Chromium source has NO cert gate in content/browser/{usb,hid,serial} (unlike WebAuthn and Service Workers, which were simultaneously blocked on the same test page — proving the state was genuinely cert-error). (3) navigator.clipboard works; (4) getUserMedia (camera QR) works — passed the security layer. (5) Bypass persists 7 days per host+cert-fingerprint+error (kCertErrorBypassExpirationInSeconds=604800), survives browser restart, resets if the cert changes — Cairn's persisted 10y cert is exactly right; never regenerate per boot. (6) WebAuthn BLOCKED (AuthenticatorStatus::CERTIFICATE_ERROR, IsSecurityLevelAcceptableForWebAuthn) — passkeys can never work on the self-signed port; password login is the path (SecureContextHelp copy updated to say so, and to set the ~weekly re-warning expectation). (7) Service workers blocked on that port (no PWA install there). (8) Brave caveat: Web Serial off by default + shields interfere with WebUSB identity; Chrome/Edge behave as tested. Full sources + raw results in the session transcript.
